## D377 — CROSS KEYS / PORT REPUBLIC SPEC: TWO FIELDS, A ROLE FLIP, AND COARSE-GRAIN HONESTY — [CODEX 5.6 Sol Ultra, LANE-003 DRIVE] (2026-07-11)

D377 commits the fifth LANE-003 planning contract:
`docs/design/cross-keys-port-republic-battle-build-spec.md` plus
`tools/probe-cross-keys-port-republic-plan.mjs` (11/11, dual-mode and bind-tested). This slice
is spec/probe only. It creates no data file, registry/menu entry, schema row, Army Register row,
generated-game behavior, or combat change.

**Playable decision:** use the packet's lead two-day shape, not the campaign as a whole and not
Port Republic alone. Phase 1 is `Cross Keys - Ewell Holds the Ridge` (weight 1, US attacker /
CS defender, cautious doctrine, fog off). Phase 2 is `Port Republic - The Coaling` (weight 3
decisive, CS attacker / US defender, standard doctrine, fog off). Weights sum 4. Phase-level
home edges change with the two fields. The Valley Campaign's interior lines, Massanutten screen,
Kernstown, McDowell, First Winchester, and Front Royal remain teaching, not extra phases.

**Source/OOB decision:** the packet supports dates, outcomes, army-present anchors, side-total
casualties, senior grades, and landmarks. It does not support Fremont's committed subtotal, a
brigade-grain Cross Keys OOB, named batteries, exact gun counts, or the exact regiments that took
The Coaling. The future model therefore keeps Cross Keys CS at 5,500-6,100 around the 5,800 anchor
and labels the US 6,000-9,500 commitment as an Inferred simulation envelope below the Verified
11,500 present. Port Republic stays inside 5,700-6,300 CS / 3,300-3,700 US around the fetched
6,000/3,500 anchors. Every lower split and gun envelope is Inferred; the Coaling battery uses a
generic Unpinned identity; 7th/9th Louisiana or a battery name requires a committed citation
amendment first.

**Rank and history law:** Thomas J. Jackson, Richard S. Ewell, and John C. Fremont are major
generals; Erastus B. Tyler, Richard Taylor, and Charles S. Winder are brigadier generals; Samuel
S. Carroll is a colonel. Jackson's rejection is scenario-scoped because lieutenant general is
correct after October 10, 1862. Turner Ashby is absent after his June 6 death. The packet's
candidate direction teeth control: P1 CS holds, P2 CS seizes, aggregate CS wins, and P1 US losses
exceed CS losses, each at least 5/8. There is no phase-2 or aggregate casualty-direction tooth and
no casualty magnitude. D74 rejects hesitation, genius, speed, commitment, flank, damage, casualty,
winner, or score controls.

**D378 integration contract:** tactical id/file `crossKeysPortRepublic` /
`data/cross-keys-port-republic.json`; menu rank 12 between Bull Run and Gaines' Mill; schema
47→48; scenarios 17→18; T10 E/false/anv with the flag family labeled Inferred; weather/flags/
Intel/media 17→18; suite 122→123; Army Register `1125 + unique side-unit ids × 3` with every
whole-registry pin moved in the same commit; Gaines' runtime chronology updated; frozen Classic
`crosskeys` and `portrepublic` ids remain separate with no rail route.

**Bind and focused gate:** the exact Jackson rank heading was changed from Maj. Gen. to Lt. Gen.
The plan probe exited 1 with exactly HISTORY red (10/11); all ten other steps stayed green. The
spec then restored md5-identically to `bbe53c90c2cbb39045d3bc90f7d52518` and reran 11/11. Final
gate: node checks green; build GATE OK with `civil_war_generals.html` byte-identical at
`3cab1dae6acc4e97101de7e196f613d9`; schema 47/47; research 15/15; Cross Keys plan 11/11;
Gaines 8/8; New Market Heights 10/10; Stones River 11/11; Cedar Creek 11/11; roster 8/8;
builder 15/15; every produced JSON read; 0 pageerrors; `git diff --check` clean. The full
`npm run vet:noreg` battery was not run. Planned baselines remain 17 / 47 / 1125 / 17 / 122.

LANE-003 returns to CONTRACT/unowned. D378 playable runtime starts only after a new committed
DRIVE lock.

## D376 — PLAYABLE CEDAR CREEK: THE ROLE REVERSAL IS LIVE, DIRECTION-NEUTRAL HISTORY IS EXECUTABLE, AND THE CROSS-TOOL RELAY HOLDS — [CODEX 5.6 Sol Ultra, LANE-003 DRIVE] (2026-07-11)

Cedar Creek is the seventeenth registered historical scenario. `data/cedar-creek.json` implements
the D375 contract as two T8 phases over one field: `Gordon's Dawn Assault` (weight 1, CS attacks
under real river fog) and `Sheridan's Counterattack` (weight 3, US attacks in the clear). The
same side-keyed home edges survive the role reversal. Nineteen unique unit ids produce 57 new
Army Register rows and move the whole registry from 1068 to **1125**. Every unit carries the
exact `Verified identity; Inferred strength` provenance label; Lomax is not fielded; The Burning,
Ramseur's wound, and the fatal-halt dispute remain teaching only. The Classic lowercase
`cedarcreek` layer remains three frozen references with no rail route, separate from tactical
`cedarCreek`.

- **Playable shape and integration:** phase 1 totals US 30,200 / CS 13,900 with 78 / 40 guns;
  phase 2 totals US 24,500 / CS 14,000 with 60 / 32 guns. T1 registers menu rank 72 in the exact
  Kennesaw → Cedar Creek → Franklin order; T10 is explicit `E/false/anv`; schema is 47/47;
  flags/weather/Intel/media cover all 17 scenarios; Kennesaw's 17 opening units retain the
  largest-scene crown over Cedar Creek's 8/15 phase openings; `vet --list` is 122. Roster,
  custom-builder, every whole-registry pin, the generated deliverable, and the Franklin
  adjacency tooth move in the same commit. The roster DOM also gains the previously omitted
  existing Stones River button while adding Cedar Creek.
- **No D74 shortcut:** no Cedar-specific damage, firepower, morale, casualty, rout, capture,
  plunder, straggle, blame, scripted-death, winner, or score key exists. The dawn surprise is
  fog + camp posture + flank geometry + the sourced arrival sequence. The afternoon result is
  the strength edge + fresh cavalry + timing. The focused probe enforces the two-primary fatal
  halt, Gordon-only attribution for “glory enough for one day,” count-free pursuit, and the
  absence of playable Fort Pillow.

**Honest A/B log — every post-battery simulation input move and all five 8-seed guards:**

1. **Attempt 0, first authored inputs:** phase-1/top hold 95s; objective `(640,390,r125)`;
   Belle Grove marker `(640,390)`; all 14 phase-1 opening units present. Result:
   P1 CS **0/8**, P2 US **8/8**, aggregate US **8/8**, P1 US>CS losses **0/8**, P2 CS>US
   losses **8/8**. The P1 winner sequence was `US,US,US,US,US,US,US,US`; no result-derived
   multiplier was added.
2. **Attempt 1, honest geometry/hold correction:** hold `95→80`; objective
   `(640,390,r125)→(760,430,r115)` at both top and phase levels; marker
   `(640,390)→(760,430)`; US Wheaton `(350,260)→(300,130)`, Getty
   `(420,230)→(420,200)`, Keifer `(300,290)→(240,170)`, artillery
   `(470,315)→(320,120)`; CS Gordon `(850,550)→(800,520)`, Ramseur
   `(790,570)→(740,535)`, Pegram `(915,580)→(880,540)`, Kershaw
   `(720,570)→(690,530)`, artillery `(650,690)→(700,600)`. Result was byte-for-byte the
   same five guard counts as Attempt 0: `0/8, 8/8, 8/8, 0/8, 8/8`.
3. **Attempt 2, sourced sequential Union re-formation:** phase-1 opening `14→8`; Dwight moved
   from opening `(570,385)` to `@105 (520,160)`, Grover `(500,370)` to
   `@125 (600,145)`, artillery `(320,120)` to `@145 (320,80)`, Wheaton
   `(300,130)` to `@170 (300,90)`, Getty `(420,200)` to `@190 (420,100)`, and Keifer
   `(240,170)` to `@210 (240,110)`; cavalry timings Merritt `82→230`, Custer `102→250`,
   Powell `122→270`. Total strengths and guns did not move. Result: P1 CS **4/8**, P2 US
   **8/8**, aggregate US **8/8**, P1 US>CS losses **4/8**, P2 CS>US losses **8/8**;
   P1 winners by seeds 1/7/21/33/49/101/202/303 were
   `US,US,CS,US,CS,US,CS,CS`.
4. **Attempt 3, final narrow timing correction:** only Dwight `@105→115` and Grover
   `@125→135` moved. Result: **8/8 on all five guards**. Every seed resolved
   `CS/US=US`; phase-loss samples were
   `3756-699/2330-14000`, `4090-647/2329-14000`, `1903-543/2236-14000`,
   `5116-732/2749-14000`, `2114-705/2172-14000`, `5166-690/2168-14000`,
   `4871-738/2145-14000`, and `5100-707/2203-14000` (P1 US-CS / P2 US-CS).

**Negative binds, both restored byte-identically:** removing the exact T1 registry line made
the plan probe's REGISTRY step red and exactly eight focused teeth red: registry/menu,
landmarks/home edges, launch, same-seed replay, passive completion, historical direction,
Army Register pin, and menu/side choice. The other seven focused teeth stayed green. Tampering
only phase-1 Emory from `Brig. Gen. William H. Emory (Bvt. Maj. Gen.)` to
`Maj. Gen. William H. Emory` made the plan REGISTRY step and exactly the focused RANK tooth red;
the other fourteen focused teeth stayed green. Restored hashes matched the pre-bind values:
T1 `2245384f4046c4307cd7697e7e0207bb`, Cedar data
`08863fd0bdfaacc5ad90d3fabfe2ca2d`, generated HTML
`3cab1dae6acc4e97101de7e196f613d9`.

**Final focused gate, serialized with no competing browser:** touched-file `node --check`
green; build **GATE OK**; schema **47/47**; Cedar plan **11/11**; Cedar runtime **15/15**;
roster **8/8**; builder **15/15**; loot **12/12** at 1125; flags **46/46**; weather
**30/30**; Intel **26/26**; media **13/13**; vet list **122**; Stones River **13/13**;
Kennesaw **11/11**; Franklin **10/10**; `git diff --check` clean. Every required JSON was
parsed after the run: all `ok:true`, no failed steps, 0 pageerrors. Franklin's first pass found
only its stale pre-Cedar assumption that Franklin immediately followed Kennesaw; its battle
simulation remained green. The tooth was corrected to require Kennesaw → Cedar Creek →
Franklin, syntax-checked, and rerun 10/10. The docs-only lane release then exposed Gaines'
Mill plan's stale live-owner tooth; it now asserts CONTRACT/unowned while retaining the D362
historical handoff, and all four relay-sensitive plans rerun green (Gaines 8/8, New Market
Heights 10/10, Stones River 11/11, Cedar Creek 11/11). Two cooked-string syntax errors in the new browser
probe were caught and fixed before the first simulation battery; they were harness failures,
not tuning attempts. New browser probes must preparse embedded SETUP/DOM programs because
`node --check` alone does not inspect those strings.

The D375→D376 spec/runtime relay required **zero** ambiguity questions, ratifying the planned
cross-tool contract seam. The session crossed a context-compaction boundary during the long
serialized gate, so the S-04 ≥40% threshold cannot be defended; Cross Keys/Port Republic does
not start here. LANE-003 returns to CONTRACT/unowned at this clean pushed boundary. The next
slice is Cross Keys/Port Republic **spec only** from the committed Shenandoah-1862 packet. The
full `vet:noreg` battery remains deferred until the agreed 2-3-battle release boundary.

## D373 — LANE-003 RELEASE CHECKPOINT GREEN: 121/121, ONE ROOT-CAUSED HARNESS FLAKE, NOTHING DEFERRED — [CLAUDE Fable 5 main loop, LANE-003 DRIVE] (2026-07-11)

The full serialized `npm run vet:noreg` battery owed since D366 ran locally (full-access session,
`CODEX_SANDBOX` unset, one shared :8765 server, `TMPDIR=$PWD/.tmp`, foreground-artifact discipline)
in two fail-fast segments with `--from=` resume: commands 1-18 green, then `--from='hard war'`
103/103 green, final `VET NO-REGRESSION OK`. Every artifact was read, not assumed.

- **The one red, root-caused — a harness/scheduling flake, no probe or product defect:** `probe-hard-war` timed out at its 360s budget in segment 1. Timestamp forensics proved the probe finished ALL 8 steps green and wrote its screenshot + JSON at **84s** (matching the prior green run's 80.9s profile), then hung in post-write `browser.close()` until SIGTERM. Cause: Fable had launched an 11-agent Cedar Creek research workflow CONCURRENTLY with the battery; Chrome teardown starved under that load on the 8 GB Mac. Standalone focused rerun on an idle machine: green in 92s; in-battery rerun: green at 108.1s. **Lesson locked: nothing runs concurrently with the release battery on this machine — workflows/helpers wait or run between segments.** The probe was not weakened; no blind retry occurred.
- **Artifact readback (120 fresh `tools/shots/*.json`):** every artifact `ok:true`, 0 pageerrors, 0 realErrors, no failed step, no fatal key. `bootprobe`'s 7 filtered 404 console lines are the documented expected absent-optional-assets probe (`assets/3d/` .glb/HDRI; `tools/bootprobe.mjs:55`; `realErrors:[]`). `sweep-all-battles`: all **16 registered scenarios × 8 seeds, failures=[]**. `diag-classic`: ok, m3dActive=false, nonBlank above floor. Known-accepted 2.42MB raw-embed soft warning unchanged.
- **Lane state:** LANE-003's release obligation is discharged; the lane stays Fable-owned DRIVE for its ratified stretch order (Cedar Creek → Cross Keys/Port Republic → Five Forks → Fort Donelson → Elkhorn Tavern, 2-3 at full depth beating 6 shallow). The stale pre-D372 Sol-status sentence in the lane's resume pointer is reconciled in this commit per D372's relay order.
- **Ridden-along research (docs impact only, committed as its own next slice):** two Cedar Creek research workflows (11 + 8 agents, 0 errors; Sonnet gather → Opus default-refute → Opus critic) resolved the shenandoah-1864 packet's §9 unknowns — including an approved-source CMH staff-ride strength table (Kershaw 3,071 at last), the DISPUTED fatal-halt framing with both primaries pinned, and a C73-class correction: **Emory's substantive Oct 19 1864 grade was Brig. Gen. USV** (Bvt. MG July 23 1864; full MG Sept 25 1865) — the packet's "Major General (NPS-confirmed)" line reflects NPS courtesy usage and will be corrected in the packet addendum slice.

Gate for this docs-only boundary: build **GATE OK** with the generated HTML unchanged; `git diff --check` clean; the 121-command battery result above is the release evidence.

## D374 — CEDAR CREEK RESEARCH ADDENDUM COMMITS THE WORKFLOW YIELD AS DURABLE PACKET LAW — [CLAUDE Fable 5 main loop, LANE-003 DRIVE] (2026-07-11)

The shenandoah-1864 packet now carries §12, the 2026-07-11 pre-spec research pass (19 agents across
two workflows, 0 errors; Sonnet gather → Opus default-refute → Opus critic; Fable adjudication),
resolving every Cedar Creek §9 unknown so the D375 spec can be written from committed prose alone.

- **Strength anchor found in an approved source:** the US Army CMH staff-ride OOB appendix (Akamai-403s bots; Wayback mirror recorded in §3) gives US per-corps AND CS per-division engaged strengths — including Kershaw's 3,071, absent from every other approved source — with the honest caveat that the CS table is a 31-Oct field-inspection reconstruction ("No official report is available"). Encoding law: "Verified identity; Inferred strength" per unit; the ~14,000-attacking vs ~21,000-total split stated as a range; no exact battle-morning count claimed.
- **§4 rank correction (C73-class):** Emory's substantive Oct 19 1864 grade was **Brig. Gen. USV (Bvt. Maj. Gen.)**, not the packet's prior "Major General (NPS-confirmed)" — NPS's label is courtesy usage; two independent verification passes converged. Torbert's and Rosser's "Maj. Gen." labels on NPS/CCBF likewise refute-killed as substantive grades (both Brig. Gen. at the battle; Rosser's MG is Nov 1 1864). Thoburn/Hayes/Powell/Keifer/Lowell all Colonels; Lowell's brigadier star posthumous; the Thoburn "commission made out the day of his death" claim DROPPED (NPS bio doesn't state it).
- **The Fatal Halt ships as an explicit DISPUTE:** Early's plunder account and Gordon's "fatal halting" counterclaim both pinned to the NPS Fatal Halt article's quotations; "glory enough for one day" exists only via Gordon's Reminiscences and must be attributed, never stated as fact (an ABT-page attribution was default-refuted). No mechanic may encode either commander's blame theory.
- **Count-free pursuit law:** the circulating 43-gun/24-recaptured/200-wagon/1,000-prisoner figures failed default-refute against fetched approved pages; pursuit prose stays count-free. The CS afternoon west-to-east line order is Inferred-grade (no probe tooth). §9's Cedar Creek bullets are annotated RESOLVED with the audit trail retained (probe's ≥2-bullet READY_FOR_SPEC guard respected); Third Winchester items stay open.
- **Gate:** `probe-battle-build-research` **15/15**; build **GATE OK** with the generated HTML unchanged; `git diff --check` clean. Docs only — no data/runtime/registry/probe change.

## D375 — CEDAR CREEK SPEC + PLAN PROBE: THE ROLE-REVERSAL BATTLE, THE DIRECTION-NEUTRAL LAW, THE KITCHING RESOLUTION — [CLAUDE Fable 5 main loop, LANE-003 DRIVE] (2026-07-11)

The fourth LANE-003 contract is committed: `docs/design/cedar-creek-battle-build-spec.md` +
`tools/probe-cedar-creek-plan.mjs` (**11/11**, bind-tested), written entirely from the committed
packet §12 prose per the D363-D366 spec-first pattern. No runtime data, registry, menu, or combat
change; the generated HTML is byte-identical.

- **Locked shape:** two-phase T8 with the game's first ROLE REVERSAL — P1 `Gordon's Dawn Assault` (w1, CS attacker / US defender, per-phase `defaultFog:true` — the real pre-dawn river fog as a D92 input) → P2 `Sheridan's Counterattack` (w3 DECISIVE, US attacker / CS defender, fog OFF). **Weights sum 4, never 5** (probe-asserted). Menu rank `cedarCreek:72` (Kennesaw 70 → Cedar Creek → Franklin 75, by full date). Objectives: the Belle Grove camp line → the high ground north of Middletown. Home edges side-keyed (US north / CS south) and constant across the reversal.
- **The direction-neutral casualty law:** US bled MORE and WON (ABT 5,764/3,060 · Wikipedia 5,665/2,910 · NPS ~7,682 no split — same direction, three totals). NO aggregate casualty tooth in EITHER direction; the only guards are the phase-scoped D92 system validations (P1 US > CS phase losses, P2 CS > US, both direction-only, ≥5/8) beside the three outcome guards (P1 CS seizes, P2 US seizes, aggregate US). Pursuit prose count-free (the 43-gun/24/200-wagon/1,000-prisoner figures failed D374 refute).
- **Strength contract from the CMH staff-ride table (§12.1), every unit `Verified identity; Inferred strength`:** P1 CS 12,500-14,500 (the ~14,000-attacking anchor; Lomax's 3,121 NOT fielded — Front Royal road, teaching only) vs US 27,000-31,610; P2 US 22,000-28,500 vs CS 13,000-19,000; guns P1 CS 30-48 / US 60-90, P2 US 40-90 / CS 15-48. The CS west-to-east afternoon order stays Inferred-grade with NO probe tooth.
- **The §12.5 "verify at spec time" item is RESOLVED:** a 3-agent D375 workflow (2 Sonnet gathers → 1 Opus default-refute, 0 errors) CONFIRMED **Col. J. Howard Kitching** (Provisional Division) from three institutional sources (NPS people page, CCBF OOB, NY State Military Museum), and documented the trap: his brevet BG was awarded POSTHUMOUSLY (died of the Cedar Creek wound Jan 11 1865) but BACKDATED to Aug 1 1864 — a date that predates the battle and must never be encoded; he is also never killed-in-action. Two new source rows added to the spec register.
- **Rank table encoded with format law** (brevet parentheticals follow the name so prefix-scan rejections stay clean): Sheridan Maj. Gen. USV (never backdated Regular grades) · Early Lt. Gen. (temporary grade) · Emory Brig. Gen. (Bvt. Maj. Gen.) — the §12 correction · Crook/Torbert/Custer/Merritt/Getty/Wheaton/Ricketts/Dwight/Grover Brig. Gen. · Thoburn/Hayes/Powell/Keifer/Lowell/Kitching Col. (Lowell's star posthumous; Thoburn commission claim dropped) · Gordon/Kershaw/Ramseur/Lomax Maj. Gen. · Pegram/Wharton/Rosser Brig. Gen. (the NPS OOB "Maj. Gen. Rosser" is an ERROR). Ramseur's death is teaching flavor only.
- **Teaching contract (8 cards, ≥2 URLs each; codex Eastern/Union victory):** the DISPUTED `cc_fatal_halt` centerpiece with BOTH primaries attributed and "glory enough for one day" existing ONLY via Gordon's Reminiscences (probe-enforced: the line may live only in the attributed card); the ride/poem split; "The Burning" as this lane's own dignity line (teaching-only, never scored); Ramseur; Gordon's march; Early as Lost Cause architect; the 1864 election; Lowell optional.
- **Plan probe (11 steps):** SPEC/LANE/SOURCES/SHAPE/STRENGTH/HISTORY/TEACHING/D74+GATES/CLASSIC-LAYER/DIGNITY/REGISTRY — the REGISTRY step is dual-mode (today it proves NO half-registration exists anywhere: T1/roster/builder/schema/T10/flags 16/intel 16/media 16/suite 121/sweep 16/loot 1068 all at planned-only baselines; once `data/cedar-creek.json` exists it enforces the FULL integration contract including the glory-enough attribution walk, the count-free rejections, and the 1068+units×3 pin law).

## D369 — GENRE-ELITE AUDIT RATIFIES LEGIBILITY, SESSION RESPECT, AND PORTABLE TEACHING AS THE NEXT QUALITY BAR — [CODEX 5.6 Sol Ultra, LANE-004 Phase A] (2026-07-10)

The web-grounded genre audit is now binding at `docs/design/genre-elite-audit-2026-07.md`. It compares the live game against Ultimate General: Gettysburg/Civil War, Total War, Grand Tactician: The Civil War, Scourge of War, Field of Glory II, Paradox onboarding/tooltips, Old World events, and the Xbox game-accessibility bar. Primary developer, publisher, manual, and platform sources were checked live on 2026-07-10; older manuals are used as documented feature bars, never as a claim about an uninspected 2026 runtime.

- **The adjudication:** do not answer the genre gap with another combat subsystem. The current universal tactical model is credible, the strategic political economy is unusually broad, and the sourced teaching stack is the product's strongest differentiator. The weak connective tissue is the higher-return target: reactive onboarding, camera recovery/overview, deterministic cause feedback, accessible audio/input, session-length disclosure, and portable AAR/evidence output. A continuous operational map remains the largest genre gap, but is design-only until a bounded node-and-edge seam proves it will reuse the current logistics/command stack instead of becoming a second game.
- **Four pillars weighted equally:** 22 features carry a sourced elite bar, live repository evidence, an explicit gap, recommended slice shape, D74/citation/performance risks, and a scored priority. The mandatory unknown-feature pass adds camera recovery, causal ribbons, learning routes, AAR export, replay capsules, audio mono/buses, evidence packets, battle-commitment forecasts, friendly-intent display, and accessibility preview.
- **Ratified queue:** `REVIEW-QUEUE.md` GEA-01 through GEA-14. GEA-01 stale Quick Start roster copy is FIX-NOW. GEA-02 AAR Copy/Download is a cleared presentation-only quick win. GEA-03 Home / Shift+Home camera recovery is conditionally cleared after Phase B and a narrow probe contract; `F` remains Charge. GEA-04 is the already-live LANE-002 5b contract from the 918-row remainder. GEA-05 through GEA-14 require the stated design/accessibility/metadata dependency and do not self-clear.
- **Live evidence, not roadmap inference:** 73 Codex entries, 20 primary-source records, 12 executive decision cards, 39 citation-grade Soldier's Story replacements, 16 historical scenarios, and 80 scenario teaching cards were read from live data. Runtime wiring and focused-probe contracts were inspected across the tactical, strategic, QoL, and teaching stacks. The Classic hex surface has a display-only minimap; the modern real-time surface has no radar, reset, or frame-selected command. The theater map explicitly remains a readout and the save envelope explicitly excludes live battle state.
- **Phase A runtime gate, one existing shared server, serialized, every artifact read:** tutorial **15/15**, accessibility **25/25**, order-feel **22/22**, audio-ambience **27/27**, save-slots **16/16**, Codex **24/24**, primary sources **14/14**, H0 main menu **5/5**, presets **27/27**, help overlay **10/10**; every artifact `ok:true`, 0 failed assertions, 0 pageerrors, all exits 0. `probe-save-slots.json` also contains three unspecific HTTP 404 console lines outside its pageerror contract; Phase B must identify or refute their target before treating them as a defect. No LANE-003 or release-battery command ran. No runtime source or generated deliverable changed.
- **Next:** LANE-004 Phase B runs the standing full-spectrum audit and appends only main-loop-confirmed findings to the queue. Phase C then takes the highest small cleared QoL slices; Phase D returns to LANE-002 5b.

## D366 — PLAYABLE STONES RIVER SHIPS: THE NEAR-PARITY VICTORY IS EXECUTABLE — [CLAUDE Fable 5 main loop, LANE-003 DRIVE] (2026-07-10)

Scenario 16, menu rank `stonesRiver:52` between Shiloh and Vicksburg, implementing the committed D365 spec exactly: `data/stones-river.json` (26 units, two-phase T8, CS attacker / US defender both phases, weights **3+1**, `December 31 - The Dawn Attack and the Round Forest` → `January 2 - Breckinridge at McFadden's Ford`; the Jan-1 lull is ONLY the phase-2 transition card, carrying the Emancipation Proclamation interstitial; fog OFF; weather rain/dawn `Verified`, never snow; role-aware home edges US-low/CS-high; every one of the 26 unit notes carries the exact `Verified identity; Inferred strength` label).

Hazen's brigade fielded as its own Round Forest anchor and a 38-gun pike line) vs CS **32,300** (6 opening units + Breckinridge's two crossing groups at 80s/170s); phase-2 CS **4,500** exactly (Hanson 1,300 / Palmer-under-Pillow 1,100 / Adams-under-Gibson 1,000 / Preston 1,100 — the front/support pairing per Breckinridge's OR report; 0 CS guns fielded, the attached artillery never effectively deployed) vs US **9,300** (Beatty 4,000, Grose 1,500, **Mendenhall 57 guns**/1,200 crew, Negley's Miller+Stanley counterattack 2,600 at 120s).
- **Honest A/B: NO input moved.** The first 8-seed battery passed all five guards **8/8**: phase-1 US holds 8/8 · phase-2 US holds 8/8 · phase-2 CS losses > US (direction only) 8/8 · aggregate US wins 8/8 · **NEAR-PARITY max/min ≤ 1.6 at 8/8 with observed ratios 1.08-1.15** around the historical 1.10 (samples: US 11,256-12,034 vs CS 12,430-13,349 — the engine has CS bleeding somewhat more where history had US more, which the band deliberately tolerates and no tooth anywhere asserts US < CS). Zero A/B iterations; initial inputs logged in the data file are the shipped inputs.
- **Citation-verify workflow before authoring (6 Sonnet/low URL-fetch packets):** every teaching-card URL was checked against its exact claims. Adjudications (Fable): the optional **Wheeler card was DROPPED** (raid details single-sourced to Wikipedia; the ABT page doesn't carry them) — 8 cards ship, all ≥2 URLs; the **Garesché card** cites the two Wikipedia articles for the death/first-battle/ring claims while the *Imitation of Christ* correction rides the D365 refute pass (Wikipedia's own "personal Bible" rendering is precisely the rejected flattening — the card teaches that); **WHN is cited only for the repulse/gun-mass/45-minutes** (its fetched summary renders the two-line pairing opposite Breckinridge's OR report — the primary controls, per the spec's source-precedence law); **quod.lib** returns 403 to bots today but was Verified-fetched in D365 and remains the canonical Collected Works citation, with Wikipedia (which quotes the letter) as the card's second URL; weather cold/rain is ABT ("bitterly cold") + Wikipedia ("freezing rain").

Gen."` on the leader line → plan probe **10/11** (the runtime rank tooth) and the focused probe red on **exactly ONE tooth** (RANK + NAME LOCKS, via the forbidden-rendering regex) → md5-identical restore → final rerun **plan 11/11 + focused 13/13** on the restored bytes.

## D365 — STONES RIVER SPEC + PLAN PROBE: TWO REAL COMBAT DAYS, THE NEAR-PARITY LAW, THE LIEUTENANT-GENERAL FLIP — [CLAUDE Fable 5 main loop, LANE-003 DRIVE] (2026-07-10)

D365 adds `docs/design/stones-river-battle-build-spec.md` and `tools/probe-stones-river-plan.mjs` (**11/11**); Stones River remains unregistered and unplayable until D366. Third LANE-003 battle, from the ratified western-gaps packet.

- **Research before shape:** an 11-agent workflow (5 Sonnet/medium web gathers → 5 Opus/high default-refute verifiers → 1 Opus/high completeness critic; ~581k tokens; 0 errors) resolved the packet §9 unknowns. All 23 rank claims CONFIRMED including the three traps (Polk/Hardee LIEUTENANT generals from Oct 10, 1862 — Maj. Gens. at Perryville two days before the grade existed; Sheridan a BRIGADIER with his MG date-of-rank backdated to Dec 31, 1862 but issued April 10, 1863; Hazen a COLONEL at the Round Forest). The refute pass REFUTED a gathered OR per-brigade strength table (full-text extraction of the cited 115-page NPS HRS contains none of the figures) — the ONLY OR-sourced sub-army CS figure is Breckinridge's 7,053 (Dec 31) / ~4,500 (Jan 2) from his own report; every other division split ships Inferred inside the Verified ABT army anchors (US 41,400 / CS 35,000). Structural corrections landed: S.A.M. Wood is CLEBURNE's reserve brigade (McCown = Ector/Rains/McNair only); Cheatham's fourth brigade is Preston Smith's; the Dec-31 ~4 p.m. assault was Preston + Palmer, never Hanson (the Orphan Brigade charges Jan 2 only); the Jan-2 force locks at ~4,500, not 5,000.
- **The near-parity law (the lane's headline):** the Union WON while losing MORE men (Verified ABT lock US 12,906 / CS 11,739). FORBIDDEN to force US < CS anywhere; the aggregate guard is near-parity (max/min ≤ 1.6, an Inferred tolerance around the historical 1.10). The ONE Verified phase datum — Breckinridge's ~1,700-1,800 of ~4,500 in under an hour — becomes a phase-2-scoped DIRECTION-only guard (CS phase losses > US), because the US Jan-2 figure (~700) is single-source Inferred. The critic's two ship-blockers (no citation-grade phase casualty split; direction-only phase guard scoped never to invert the aggregate) are encoded as label law.
- **Shape:** two-phase T8, CS attacker / US defender both phases — `December 31 - The Dawn Attack and the Round Forest` (w3, DECISIVE, US holds) → `January 2 - Breckinridge at McFadden's Ford` (w1, US holds sharply; Mendenhall's 45-58 massed guns as an OOB input, the RANGE is the tooth). January 1 is a TEACHING INTERSTITIAL (Emancipation Proclamation takes effect mid-battle), never a scored phase. Fog OFF (the dawn surprise is positioning, not a mechanic); weather `rain`/`dawn` (never snow — the Classic layer's `wx:"snow"` is not a source). Menu rank `stonesRiver:52` (Western arc: Shiloh → Stones River → Vicksburg); the Classic `stonesriver` id + rail route are a separate layer (the `malvern`/`malvernHill` precedent), untouched and probe-guarded.
- **Teaching contract (8 cards + codex):** won-by-holding with Lincoln's exact Aug 31, 1863 letter; the dawn breakfast attack; Hell's Half Acre; Breckinridge's protest (Bragg's order attributed to secondary literature, never OR); Mendenhall; Garesché (a pocket *Imitation of Christ*, NOT a "personal Bible"); the generals' revolt (Hardee's "unwise, in a high degree" sourced to his official report; Kirby Smith excluded; "round robin" reserved for Bragg's own circular); the Emancipation interstitial. TWO Confederate generals fell (Rains, Hanson) — "four" is the both-sides total; reject "Robert W. Hanson" (an ABT map error).
- **Negative bind — and a tooth hardened by it:** the first tamper (the Sheridan trap line Brig.→Maj.) did NOT bite because the substring also appears in the spec's probe-teeth section — the HISTORY tooth was anchored to the exact bolded trap lines (`**Brig. Gen. Philip H. Sheridan** — THE TRAP`, `**Lt. Gen. Leonidas Polk** and **Lt. Gen. William J. Hardee** — THE HEADLINE FLIP`), after which the same tamper made exactly the HISTORY step red (**10/11**, exit 1) → byte-identical restore (md5-proven) → **11/11**. Lesson: bind tests must bite the load-bearing LINE, not any occurrence of the substring.
- **Gate:** build **GATE OK** with the generated HTML md5-identical (docs/tools-only slice); schema **45/45**; battle research **15/15**; Stones plan **11/11**; NMH plan **10/10** (adjacent); roster **8/8**; builder **15/15**; Nashville **12/12**; every JSON artifact `ok:true`, 0 failed, 0 pageerrors; `git diff --check` clean.
- **Next:** D366 playable Stones River from this committed boundary; then the LANE-003 release battery.

## D364 — PLAYABLE NEW MARKET HEIGHTS SHIPS: THE GAME'S FIRST USCT-LED BATTLE — [CLAUDE Fable 5 main loop, LANE-003 DRIVE] (2026-07-10)

Fifteen scenarios are live. D364 implements the committed D363 contract: `data/new-market-heights.json` (two-phase T8 over the same ground), registry/menu rank `newMarketHeights:45` (after Gettysburg, before Shiloh), the guarded T13 pre-placed obstacle-belt seam, T10 `E/false/anv` metadata with the Army-of-the-James badge limitation documented, schema 45th row, and every contracted baseline bump. The runtime was built as green WIP in the 2026-07-10 Fable session (Aaron called the stop mid-vet); this entry closes it out with the second bind, the full serialized runtime gate, a teaching-prose refute pass, and the docs/commit.

- **Spec amendment (22nd USCT skirmishers, logged per the spec's own instruction):** the T8 schema convention requires a meaningful reinforcement per phase, and Holman's 22nd USCT skirmishers are SOURCED as opening the engagement while the brigade was delayed in the Four Mile Creek marshes. They join phase 1 on the reinforcement schedule as a detachment of at most 250 (`Verified identity; Inferred strength`); the phase-1 US opening OOB stays 630-770 (Duncan's two regiments) and the phase-1 US total including the arrival is bounded ≤1,000. Spec §Phase-1 and the probe-teeth section were amended in the same diff; the plan probe enforces both bounds separately.
- **Honest A/B log (all inputs inside spec bounds; no result-derived key):** attempt 0 — P2 `timeLimitSec` 240, belts .55/.60, US staged z700, CS residual 890, support arrivals @55/@85 → P1 CS-holds **8/8** but P2 US-carries **2/8** FAIL. Attempt 1 — P2 `timeLimitSec` 320, belts .50/.55, US staged z640 at the marsh line, CS residual 790 (Texas 400 / Gary 300 / Rockbridge 90 with 4 guns), support @40/@60 → P1 CS **8/8** · P2 US **7/8** · aggregate US **7/8** · US-bleeds-more **8/8** PASS. Shipped totals: phase 1 = 900 US (700 opening + 200 arrival) / 1,880 CS with exactly 8 guns; phase 2 = 2,200 US / 790 CS with 4 guns.
- **Bind 1 (T1 registry, proven in the WIP session):** registry line removed → REGISTRY/OBSTACLE/LAUNCH/PASSIVE/DIRECTION teeth red, exit 1 → checksum-identical restore → green.
- **Bind 2 (T13 seam, this closeout):** `fldEngSeedScenarioObstacles` stubbed to return early → rebuild GATE OK → **exactly the OBSTACLE BELTS tooth red** (`phase-1 belts wrong count: 0`), the other 13 teeth green, exit 1, 0 pageerrors → restore (source md5 `aaed2514…` identical) → rebuild (generated-HTML md5 `873f2d7f…` identical) → **14/14** green. Notable negative evidence: the 8-seed direction battery stays green without the belts — the belts are FRICTION, not the outcome carrier, exactly what D74 demands.
- **Teaching-prose default-refute pass (3 Opus/high web verifiers, ~125k tokens, 0 errors)** on the prose claims that went beyond the D363 register: Butler Medal card **4/4 CONFIRMED** (medal at Butler's own expense; `Ferro iis libertas perveniet` — our spelling is correct, the cited afroamcivilwar page itself carries an "Ilis" typo; equal-pay act June 15, 1864; line-officer bar with the Louisiana Native Guards exception) with two stronger sources ADDED to the card (Wikipedia Butler Medal, freedmen.umd.edu); Fort Pillow card **5/5 CONFIRMED** (Forrest MG from Dec 4, 1863; ~58 of ~300 Black prisoners ≈ one-fifth vs ~168 ≈ three-fifths white, per Cimprich/Mainfort via institutional sources); MoH card **one AMEND applied** — "the most for any single Civil War engagement" is true only scoped to Black soldiers (Gettysburg 63, Fort Fisher 54 exceed 14 overall), reworded to "the most awarded to Black soldiers for any single engagement of the war."
- **Stale-pin root cause caught by the gate (the LANE-002 note's predicted interaction):** `tools/probe-gaines-mill.mjs` pinned the whole-registry total at 957; a new battle legitimately moves that pin. Bumped 957 → 990 with the documented-history comment (the D355 idiom); the Gaines-specific 45-row/15-unit teeth are untouched. Not a probe-weakening — the pin's job is to move with a documented reason exactly once per shipped battle.
- **Required D364 Runtime Gate (serialized, one shared :8765 server, `TMPDIR=$PWD/.tmp`, every JSON artifact read):** `node --check` 13/13 touched files · build **GATE OK** · schema **45/45** · NMH plan **10/10** · NMH runtime **14/14** (direction 8/8 · 7/8 · 7/8 · 8/8; belt bite onBelt 0.4 vs offBelt 1.0) · roster **8/8** · builder **15/15** · loot-survival **12 steps** (990 pin + register UI) · flags **44 steps** (15-scenario coverage + the NMH semantic tooth) · weather **30** · Intel UHD-617 **26** (15 opening scenes; Kennesaw 17 units still largest) · media-budget **13** (the known 2.418 MB soft warning, unchanged posture) · `vet --list` **120** · Gaines' Mill **13** · Nashville **12** · field **23** (T0/T8/T13 shared machinery) · `git diff --check` clean · every artifact `ok:true`, 0 failed steps, 0 pageerrors. Full `vet:noreg` stays deferred to the LANE-003 release boundary (D160/D176).
- **Dignity law held in runtime:** no `valorMult`/`heroism`/any forbidden key at any depth (executable tooth); the Fort Pillow absence guard runs against the registry, menu, AND the data directory on disk; the phase-2 carry emerges from Draper's mass + Holman's support against the sourced residual line through partially-breached belts — the universal model owns every seed.

## D363 — NEW MARKET HEIGHTS SPEC + PLAN PROBE: TWO USCT ASSAULT PHASES, FORT HARRISON TEACHING-ONLY, THE WITHDRAWAL AS A PHASE BOUNDARY — [CLAUDE Fable 5 main loop, LANE-003 DRIVE] (2026-07-10)

Fable took the LANE-003 lock at the verified `b1d828b` D362 boundary. D363 adds `docs/design/new-market-heights-battle-build-spec.md` and `tools/probe-new-market-heights-plan.mjs`; New Market Heights remains unregistered and unplayable until D364.

- **Research before shape (the takeover packet's Priority 1):** a 13-agent workflow (6 Sonnet/medium gather → 6 Opus/high default-refute → 1 Opus/high completeness critic; ~697k tokens; 0 errors) resolved every USCT-packet §9 unknown. Pinned: Paine's 3rd Div/XVIII Corps brigade attachments (Holman 1st = 1st/22nd/37th USCT · Draper 2nd = 5th/36th/38th · Duncan 3rd = 4th/6th — the gatherer's "Duncan 1st Bde" was refuted); engaged strengths (Duncan ~700 with the 4th USCT at Fleetwood's first-person 361, Draper ~1,300, ABT's 2,000+ combined, CS line ~1,800-2,000); the refuters REVERSED the "no CS gun counts" claim — Lt. Col. Hardaway's 1st Va Light Artillery Bn (Rockbridge + 3rd Co. Richmond Howitzers, four guns each) is sourced; all battle-date ranks two-source verified (Butler MG · Paine BG · Draper/Duncan/Holman Cols with post-battle brevets flagged · Gregg BG alive until Oct 7 · Gary BG · Ewell LG); all 14 Black MoH recipients verified by name/rank/unit with award-date nuance (12 on Apr 6 1865; Hawkins 1870; Harris 1874) plus the two white officers and the separate Butler Medal; the NPS 961 / ABT "well over 800" casualty figures reconcile (842 = the two assault brigades; 961 = the division).
- **Adjudicated shape (documented deviation from the packet default):** two-phase T8 over the SAME ground — Phase 1 `Duncan's Assault - the Abatis` (scoreWeight 1, direction: CS holds, the documented ~50% repulse) → Phase 2 `Draper Carries the Heights` (scoreWeight 3, direction: US carries against a REDUCED CS OOB labeled `Verified withdrawal order; Inferred residual strength`). Fort Harrison is TEACHING-ONLY: its Union side is OR-pinned (Stannard ~2,800, Stevens/Burnham/Roberts + Cullen succession) but its CS garrison is a ~200-man coarse abstraction with an UNPROVEN commander (Wikipedia's Taylor contradicted by Stannard's OR) — a 2,800-vs-200 phase is below the engine's brigade-scale grain, and in the packet's NMH-then-Harrison order the Fort Harrison event could not reach backward to thin the phase-1 line. The completeness critic's constraints are all honored: NO clock-timed thinning, NO pinned withdrawal causation (the driven-off/ordered-away/both controversy is taught as a controversy), no "stormed-and-settled" framing — the phase boundary itself carries the sourced withdrawal as an OOB input, not a mechanic.
- **Cost direction INVERTS winner-bleeds-less:** aggregate US win majority while total US losses exceed total CS losses (the victor bore the cost — like the Stones River caution, opposite sign).
- **One new engine capability contracted for D364:** a guarded T13 pre-placed obstacle-belt seam (Fleetwood's "two lines of abatis and one line of palisades" as data-declared completed defender obstacles; phase-2 belts partially breached; byte-identical no-op for every scenario without the data key; bind test required).
- **Dignity law travels with the battle:** Black agency not white validation; `valorMult`/`heroism` added to the forbidden D74 wall; an EXECUTABLE Fort Pillow absence guard now runs in the plan probe (no fort-pillow data file/registry/menu/suite row — teaching-only treatment may name the massacre plainly); the CS side-choice card is contracted sober.
- **Integration contract pinned for D364:** menu rank `newMarketHeights:45` (Eastern arc chronology after Gettysburg); T10 `theater:"E", badges:false, csFlag:"anv"` with the Army-of-the-James badge limitation documented and the "Hampton" flag-name-trigger avoided; schema 45th row; loot pin `957 + unique units × 3`; flags/Intel/media 14→15; suite 119→120; weather `fog`/`dawn`; homeEdge US high / CS low; strength bounds phase-1 630-770 US vs 1,700-2,100 CS (0 vs exactly 8 guns), phase-2 1,900-2,400 US vs 600-1,100 CS.
- **Negative bind:** the unique Holman-artifact rank line tampered → exactly the HISTORY step red (**9/10**, exit 1) → byte-identical restore (checksum-proven) → **10/10**.
- **Gate:** build **GATE OK** (generated HTML unchanged); schema **44/44**; battle research **15/15**; Gaines plan **8/8** (its LANE-003 tooth stays green under the DRIVE edit); NMH plan **10/10**; roster **8/8**; custom builder **15/15**; Nashville **12/12**; every JSON artifact `ok:true`, 0 failed steps, 0 pageerrors; `git diff --check` clean. Full `vet:noreg` stays deferred to the LANE-003 release boundary.
- **Next:** D364 playable New Market Heights from this committed planning boundary.

## D361 — GAINES' MILL SPEC + PLAN PROBE LOCK LANE-003; CHATGPT MAY USE INHERITING HELPERS FOR OUTPUT-INSENSITIVE EVIDENCE — [AARON routing clarification · CODEX 5.6 Sol Ultra top loop, LANE-003] (2026-07-10)

LANE-003 moved from CONTRACT to DRIVE before runtime. D361 adds `docs/design/gaines-mill-battle-build-spec.md` and `tools/probe-gaines-mill-plan.mjs`; Gaines' Mill remains unregistered and unplayable until D362.

- **Playable contract:** single-phase CS attacker / US defender, `defaultFog:false`, standard assault doctrine, Watt House plateau behind Boatswain's Creek as the objective, and menu rank `gainesMill:15` between Bull Run and Malvern Hill. The same universal objective-hold engine must produce the opposite Seven Days result from Malvern Hill without a battle-specific output switch.
- **Strength dispute surfaced before runtime:** ABT's 7-8 p.m. map says roughly 32,000 Confederates in 16 brigades against about 34,000 reinforced Federals; Army CMH says Lee amassed 50,000 for the 6:30 p.m. assault; NPS gives roughly 60,000 brought to the attack and Porter's core 27,000 rising toward 34,000. D362's 27,000 US / 31,500-32,500 CS is therefore a disclosed mixed-source abstraction: the core Federal line against ABT's enumerated 16-brigade wave, not an exact same-time OOB. The Confederate aggregate is `Disputed; modeled strength Inferred`; every unit split carries the exact `Verified identity; Inferred strength` label. Meagher/French stay teaching-only unless both sides' paired aggregate is reopened.
- **Rank and terrain locks:** Porter is Brig. Gen. on June 27 (NPS + CMH; major-general promotion July 4); A. P. Hill is Maj. Gen. (ABT + Virginia Humanities); Hood is Brig. Gen. commanding the Texas Brigade (NPS + CMH + ABT map). The separate NPS CWSAC detail corroborates Boatswain's Swamp; Watt House, Adams farm, Chickahominy, and the Cold Harbor roads are cleared. Turkey Hill stays excluded because the packet never verified it.
- **Split historical direction:** CS holds the objective in at least 5/8 seeds. Confederate killed/wounded also meet or exceed Union killed/wounded in at least 5/8, derived after subtracting captured and missing. The two majorities need not share seeds. Hood's break stays teaching text.
- **D362 integration contract:** runtime data, T1 registry/rank, schema 44th row, both historical baselines, loot pin `912 + unique units × 3` at both assertions, explicit T10 flag metadata and 14-scenario tooth, weather, Intel/media opening-scene count 14, suite enrollment 118→119, generated HTML, focused probe, and adjacent guards. The plan probe computes the future loot pin from runtime data and rejects comment-only or partial integrations.
- **Helper routing clarification (Aaron, this session):** keep the highest-quality ChatGPT/Codex model and effort on the top loop. If the surface exposes helper controls, set model and effort explicitly. If it does not, inheriting helpers may handle bounded work whose raw output cannot lower the shipped result: read-only inventories, artifact summaries, gate execution, and independent mechanical audits. State inheritance once. The top loop retains architecture, code ownership, history, balance, UX/accessibility judgment, final synthesis, integration, and the commit. This rule now lives in `AGENTS.md`, `START-HERE.md`, `OPUS-PLAYBOOK.md`, `COORDINATION.md`, and the machine-level `~/.codex/AGENTS.md`.
- **Negative bind:** changing only LANE-003 `State: DRIVE` to `CONTRACT` made exactly the lane-lock step fail (**7/8**, exit 1); restoring the line returned **8/8**, and the COORDINATION checksum returned byte-identical.
- **Focused gate:** build **GATE OK**; schema **43/43**; battle research **15/15**; Gaines plan **8/8**; roster **8/8**; custom builder **15/15**; Bull Run **15/15**; Malvern Hill **27/27**; every inspected JSON `ok:true`, zero failed steps, zero pageerrors; `git diff --check` clean. Full `vet:noreg` remains deferred to the LANE-003 release boundary.
- **Next:** D362 playable Gaines' Mill. Runtime starts from this committed and pushed planning boundary.

## D360 — PHASE I 5c: THE START-ANYWHERE CAREER TRAJECTORY — THE FULL PROMOTION LATTICE + THE TRAJECTORY READ-OUT — [CLAUDE Fable 5 main loop, LANE-002] (2026-07-10)

- **The gap:** D151's promotion hook knew exactly two transitions (Private→Sergeant, Sergeant→Captain), so every other starting rank — Corporal, Bugler, Lieutenant, Captain, Major, Colonel, Brig. Gen., … i.e. most of the D358 batch and every cmd row — had a DEAD career trajectory, the opposite of the play-as-anyone pillar (D91/D93). "Start anywhere" was true of the start and false of the journey.
- **What ships (src/37-loot-survival.js):** `_SS_RANK_LADDER` — an 11-rung promotion lattice from enlisted specialists (Bugler/Musician/Drummer ride the enlisted rung) through General, with escalating win thresholds (2/2/3/2/3/3/3/4/4/5/6) as Inferred game balance; the two legacy D151 transitions keep their EXACT thresholds and the decisive-victory fast track, and the shipped cumulative-wins semantic is deliberately preserved (a long streak ladders one grade per battle at most — the thresholds increase faster than the streak). `next` names are constrained to `data/ratings.json` `rankBase` keys, so `fldPromotePerson`'s unknown-rank guard remains the hard floor. `_ssNextRankReq` is the pure requirement read-out; `_ssTrajectoryHTML` renders a "Career Trajectory" block (current → next rank, victories banked vs needed, decisive shortcut note, arc-so-far from the career log) in the Campaign-Kit journey panel and the full journey report (compact/AAR reports deliberately stay lean); off-ladder ranks are labeled honestly ("outside the standard promotion ladder"), General is the summit.
- **Save/journey/report contracts unbroken (the lane law):** the trajectory is computed ENTIRELY from existing journey state (career[] + the person snapshot) — nothing new rides the save, no `_SAVE_VER` bump, no new accept lanes, no new key classes (E50 posture untouched). D74: journey-inactive paths are untouched; promotion still flows only through the pure `fldPromotePerson`.
- **Teeth (probe-loot-survival, now 12 steps):** new `JOURNEY D360 TRAJECTORY` step — starts a journey on the D358 Sherman row (Col.), asserts the honest 0-of-4 requirement read-out, drives four tactical victories → Brig. Gen. with `promotedFrom` provenance and the promoted career entry, post-promotion requirement points at Maj. Gen. with the streak banked, active-panel + full-report trajectory blocks render (compact stays lean), General caps, off-ladder ranks honest, company-officer + enlisted-specialist rungs present, canonical ratings unmutated. **Negative bind test (surgical):** the colonel rung alone removed → exactly the D360 step red while D151 stayed GREEN (the legacy rungs are independent teeth) → restored, byte-identical by checksum.
- **One in-run defect caught and fixed:** the first edit accidentally REPLACED `_ssRankLabel` instead of inserting above it — three steps + a pageerror went red; restored, all green. Logged as the reminder that Edit anchors are replacements, not insertion points.
- **Gate:** build **GATE OK**; `node --check` clean; probe-loot-survival **12/12**, 0 pageerrors; adjacents probe-ratings **22/22** (one documented `page.goto` load-timeout flake, single-probe rerun per AUTONOMOUS-RUN §5 / D339 precedent) + probe-camp **8/8**; schema **43/43**; `git diff --check` clean.
- **Lane:** LANE-002 5c → SHIPPED. The lane stays OPEN for further 5b batches (840 remaining).

## D358 — PHASE I 5b: THE FIRST PROSOPOGRAPHY BATCH IS EIGHT BULL RUN COMMAND ROWS — [CLAUDE Fable 5 main loop, LANE-002] (2026-07-10)

- **Batch size: EIGHT records, logged here BEFORE the first record per the LANE-002 contract.** Sized from the lane's 848-row inventory + this session's remaining context (~50% at sizing time, with 5c still queued): the lane law says a batch of six finished-and-verified beats twenty half-sourced; eight is the most this session can carry through gather → default-refute → adjudication → importer/probe/docs gates without crowding the 5c slice or the context boundary.
- **Why Bull Run, why command slots:** Bull Run is the ONLY battle with banked in-repo roster research (~560 lines of OOB at HISTORICAL-DATA.md:21-580, provenance-flagged), so per-record cost is lowest there; command slots pair with the banked BRIGADE-level evidence exactly (identity, rank-at-battle-date, sector, fate are all already flagged Verified/contested in the bank and need only independent re-verification, not fresh discovery). Bull Run coverage moves 2/54 → 10/54 replaced, and the game's FIRST battle gets a genuinely named command roster — which the D357 muster-roll UI now surfaces in play.
- **The eight (all `bullrun1`, all `:cmd`, 4US/4CS — side balance 15US/16CS → 19US/20CS):** Col. William T. Sherman (`us_sherman`) · Col. Andrew Porter (`us_porter`) · Col. Oliver O. Howard (`us_howard`) · Capt. Charles Griffin (`us_griffin`, art) · Col. Nathan G. "Shanks" Evans (`cs_evans`) · Brig. Gen. Barnard E. Bee (`cs_bee`) · Col. Francis S. Bartow (`cs_bartow`) · Col. Wade Hampton (`cs_hampton`). Sherman duplicates a strategic-layer `generals.json` card — the D298 Hood precedent already sanctions the coexistence (whole-war strategic card vs rank-at-battle-date battle row); the refute panel is instructed to attack that framing anyway.
- **Pipeline (Contract Relay roles):** MECHANICAL gather = Sonnet/medium, one packet per person (claims + ≥2 independent reputable source candidates each); REASONING default-refute = Opus/high, one skeptic per person attacking rank-at-date, unit/sector, fate, and source independence; TOP LOOP (Fable 5 xhigh) adjudicates every packet and owns all player-facing text. Standard per record: ≥2 independent reputable sources = Verified else Inferred/Disputed; no fabricated people/units/citations; personas stay neutral-64 Inferred per the shipped convention; women/support figures untouched (separate lane).
- **Pins that move (checked against the probe's own teeth, not the kickoff prompt):** the D298-replacements step count moves 31 → 39 with eight new named per-record teeth (the shipped convention); the **912 registry pin does NOT move** — probe-loot-survival:244/:517 pin "replacement should preserve registry length", and a pure replacement batch adds no units. The kickoff's "your batch will move BOTH" expectation is wrong for a replacement-only batch and is corrected here rather than blindly obeyed.
- **What shipped (adjudicated):** all 8 refute verdicts came back AMEND (0 FAIL) and every amendment was applied before authoring: Sherman/Porter ship NO brigade-casualty numbers (the banked ~605/~464 figures failed independent verification — only division-level returns are sourced); Howard's Chinn Ridge attackers are correctly E. Kirby Smith's brigade under Elzey after Smith's wound, plus Early; Griffin's overrun names the chief of artillery's overrule (Griffin identified the enemy correctly) and leaves the attacking regiment un-unit-named (single-institution sourcing); Evans ships the NPS-sourced TEMPORARY-colonel nuance; Bee ships the posthumous-confirmation nuance and treats the stone-wall cry as contested (never verbatim); Bartow logs the Manassas monument's 'Brigadier General' as a documented memorial-rank artifact (he died a colonel); Hampton drops the unfetchable casualty-breakdown/wound-mechanism/enslaver-wealth details (source honesty over color) and scopes to the Legion's engaged infantry. Every replaced generated `:cmd` row previously carried a hardcoded wrong 'Captain' rank — this batch is also a rank-accuracy fix the D357 muster roll now displays.
- **Gates run (all green):** importer `--check`/`--import --write`/canonical = 39/39 Verified · build **GATE OK** · probe-loot-survival **11/11**, 0 pageerrors, registry pin **912 held**, applied=39 · **two negative bind tests** (Hampton record removed → exactly the thirty-nine count tooth red, 1 failed step; Hampton rank tampered → exactly the Hampton deep tooth red) · restore proven byte-identical via checksum, so the 11/11 run stands as the final-bytes run · adjacents probe-ratings **22/22** + probe-camp **8/8**, 0 pageerrors · schema **43/43** · `git diff --check` clean. LANE-002 decremented 848 → **840**, lane stays OPEN.

## D357 — PHASE I 5a: THE MUSTER-ROLL INSPECT SURFACE WIRES THE ARMY REGISTER INTO THE HUD — [CLAUDE Fable 5 main loop, LANE-002] (2026-07-10)

- **What ships:** `fldMusterRollHtml(u)` (T14, probe-vetted since R-5, never player-reachable) now has its inspect-expand surface: new `src/tactical/T29-muster-roll-ui.js` renders a native toggle button in the selected-unit HUD (the first interactive control inside `#fldHud`) that expands the full muster panel — men-mean OVR + materialized person rows. Wired via a typeof-guarded seam line in T0's `fldRenderHud` (the established HUD-seam pattern); manifest gains the module; no overrides (the focus wrapper uses the reassignment idiom, not redeclaration).
- **Byte-identity + D74:** presentation-only — the toggle returns "" exactly when `fldMusterRollHtml` does (no ratings data), the open flag lives on `__FIELD` (dies with the battle, never rides the save), and the probe pins sim-state purity around the render. AI-vs-AI baselines untouched.
- **Accessibility (WCAG 2.2 AA, wcag-auditor run + TOP-LOOP adjudication):** native `<button>` (keyboard for free), `aria-expanded`/`aria-controls`, panel as a named region; text contrast **11.05:1**, border **3.12:1** (flagged razor-thin — recheck on any palette nudge); no outline suppression; **target-size 2.5.8 auto-fixed** (padding 2px→5px vertical, hit box 19-21px→25-27px); the auditor's flagged **4.1.2 dangling `aria-controls`** (panel absent from the DOM when collapsed) resolved by the canonical disclosure pattern — the panel is always in the DOM and toggles `hidden`. Focus survives every HUD innerHTML rebuild via a `fldRenderHud` reassignment wrapper (the S22 lesson); the probe asserts it across both the toggle re-render and an unrelated event re-render.
- **Teeth (probe-ratings, now 22 steps):** button wired/typed/aria-correct · panel exists-hidden when collapsed (4.1.2) · expanded content byte-equals `fldMusterRollHtml(u)` (normalized) · focus stability across two re-render classes · hidden-again on second toggle · sim purity. **Negative bind test:** T0 seam removed → exactly the T29 tooth red ("toggle button missing") → restored green. Probe discovery logged: `renderer:'none'` skips the DOM UI — DOM teeth need `renderer:'2d'`.
- **Gate:** build **GATE OK**; `node --check` clean; probe-ratings **22/22**, adjacents probe-field **23/23** + probe-order-feel **22/22** (the HUD hosts S40's key line; the wrapper had to not disturb it), all 0 pageerrors; `git diff --check` clean.

## D356 — THE CONTRACT RELAY SHIPS: COORDINATION.md CROSS-TOOL LANE LOCKS — [AARON kickoff 2026-07-10 · CLAUDE Fable 5 main loop] (2026-07-10)

- **What ships:** new tracked `COORDINATION.md`, linked from `START-HERE.md` (canonical list), `AGENTS.md`, and `CLAUDE.md`. It is the standing handoff ledger between AI tools: each **lane** carries owner lock, state (`LAW-DRAFT → CONTRACT → DRIVE → VERIFY → SHIPPED`), design-law paths, acceptance criteria IN FULL, probe design (files · what each tooth asserts · how to run), resume pointer, and last-touched SHA.
- **THE HARD RULE (resolves the push-no-red vs hand-off-unfinished conflict):** the lane lock never carries red teeth in git — it carries the CONTRACT, committed prose sufficient for either tool to rewrite the teeth from scratch; **teeth land in the same commit as the fix that greens them.** Working-tree red teeth found by a successor are a candidate rendering of the contract: adopt or deliberately amend (logged), then commit with the fix.
- **Why now (the E50 near-miss as the worked example):** Codex authored E50's five acceptance teeth as uncommitted working-tree edits and died on usage before the fix; one `git checkout -- .` would have destroyed the acceptance criteria. LANE-001 records E50 retroactively as the exemplar; LANE-002 (Phase I "Named Army") is open with the 5a/5b/5c contracts and the 848-row prosopography inventory attached.
- **Role-based routing codified:** lanes and kickoffs bind ROLES (TOP LOOP / REASONING HELPERS / MECHANICAL HELPERS), never model names — subscriptions churn (D223 → D286 → Fable's 2026-07-10 return); each session resolves roles to its tool's best models and states the resolution once.
- **Gate (docs-only):** build **GATE OK** with the generated game byte-unchanged; `git diff --check` clean.

## D355 — FULL RELEASE CHECKPOINT: 118/118 GREEN, 3 FINDINGS ROOT-CAUSED AND FIXED IN-RUN — [CLAUDE Fable 5 main loop, run-2 queue] (2026-07-10)

- **The checkpoint (owed since the run-2 fix stack began, D176):** full `npm run vet:noreg`, **118 commands**, run locally in this full-access non-Seatbelt session (`CODEX_SANDBOX` unset; single dedicated session, serialized runner, `TMPDIR=$PWD/.tmp`, Chrome stderr to /dev/null, shared :8765 server, verdicts read from artifacts). Chosen over a remote runner because this exact harness was proven green here minutes earlier and a remote would add unproven Chrome/repo bring-up.
- **Coverage model (the D192 split-segment precedent, fail-fast runner + `--from=`):** seg 1 commands 1-25 green → red at #26 → fix → seg 2 26-36 green → red at #37 → fix → seg 3 37-93 green → red at #94 → fix → seg 4 94-118 green with the final `VET NO-REGRESSION OK — 25 commands`. **Every command green in its final run; every red root-cause-fixed and re-run, never retried blind.** Each inter-segment delta touched only surfaces re-covered by the fixed probe's own re-run (plus, for the weather data fix, the three battle guards which ran after it in seg 4). Heavy tail verified from artifacts: 13-battle sweep, full campaign, tactical visuals, LLM commander, attacker parity, diag-classic nonBlank **346** — all `ok=true`, 0 pageerrors.
- **Finding 1 — probe defect (stale time-bound premise), probe-western-theater:** the D191 leg asserted chattanooga/atlanta/franklin/nashville battle JSON "must not exist" — true only while battle-build was locked; D324 unlocked and D326/D333/D335 shipped three of them through their own gates. Re-toothed to the durable invariant: **a battle data file may exist only as a fldScenarioRegistry-registered battle** — a stowaway file outside the registry gates is still red (bind-tested with a planted `data/atlanta.json` → exactly that leg red → removed → green).
- **Finding 2 — probe defect (fixture superseded by D353 + stale pin), probe-loot-survival:** (a) the D149-era "valid save" fixture planted an own `hasOwnProperty` in `campaign.loot.equipped`, expecting import-then-sanitize; D353's door rejection — the strictly stronger posture the E50 teeth demanded — now rejects it, so the poison token moved to a NEW rejection tooth (E50 cross-coverage in this probe) while every sanitize-on-init tooth keeps its non-poison tampers (dup stacks, qty overflow, string booleans, ghost journey). No player impact: serializeSave can never write that key. (b) The Army Register pin was stale at **645**; the four post-D176 battles added 89 units × 3 slots = +267 → **912** (arithmetic cross-checked against the independent LANE-002 inventory: 293 units, 879 generated slots + authored = 912 registry people). Pin bumped per the file's documented-history idiom.
- **Finding 3 — PRODUCT DATA defect (citation-contract violation), the three newest battles' weather hints:** `data/chattanooga.json`, `data/franklin.json`, `data/nashville.json` shipped `provenance:"Verified/Inferred"` — an illegal hybrid the ≥2-source law does not admit — and Franklin used the illegal `time:"late afternoon"`. All three notes describe modeling ABSTRACTIONS over multi-condition reality, so all three are now **Inferred** (the Kennesaw convention; Antietam/Shiloh keep Verified for directly documented pairs); Franklin's time is the legal `afternoon` (~4 pm step-off; the note keeps the late-afternoon nuance). Sources retained. Presentation-only: T17 never touches the sim seed (probe-proven), and the three battle guards re-ran green after the fix.
- **M3c disposition: NOTHING deferred.** All three findings were fixed in-run as D355 slices; no new product defect was carried forward. The REVIEW-QUEUE gains a dated checkpoint section recording all three.
- **Gate:** final segment `VET NO-REGRESSION OK`; build **GATE OK** (generated HTML changed only by the weather data fix); schema **43/43**; probe-western-theater **8/8**, probe-loot-survival **13/13**, probe-weather **30/30** standalone green post-fix; `git diff --check` clean.

## D354 — E70 TRANSFER READINESS GETS ITS ONE BOUNDED CONSUMER — [AARON disposition 2026-07-10 · CLAUDE Fable 5 main loop, run-2 queue] (2026-07-10)

- **Aaron's disposition (2026-07-10, supersedes the run-2 "proposal-only" posture):** build the consumer; do NOT suspend/remove the cost. The audit's complaint was that cross-theater Transfer spends political capital and persists readiness that nothing reads — rational gameplay value negative.
- **The consumer — command-friction on the leadership facet (the audit's option b):** new `_cmdTransferReadinessLift(C)` returns 0 or −`unreadyLeadershipMalus` (data/ratings.json transfer cfg, **3**, clamped 0..6 — deliberately below the Q10 corps lift's ±4 per the §27 influence hierarchy) and feeds `commandLeadership` exactly like the Q10/Q12 lifts: an INPUT the bridge already reads (D53/D94 sanctioned channel), never the scoreboard. D74 intact — no per-battle damage/firepower fudge, no output-gate tuning.
- **Exactly-zero default (byte-identical campaigns):** the friction applies ONLY when the player has EXPLICITLY appointed the active general (`cmd.fieldGeneral`) AND he commands outside his home theater with no Transfer order. History-following defaults (fieldGeneral null — the overwhelming default state), natural theater fits, completed Transfers, dead-appointee fallbacks, and missing theater data all read exactly 0. Untouched campaigns are numerically unchanged; only the deliberate unprepared cross-theater appointment — the exact case Transfer exists to fix — is repriced.
- **Source honesty (rationale, not new player-facing claims):** the friction models documented cross-theater command friction — Pope brought east in 1862 over an unfamiliar army and officer corps; Longstreet's Chickamauga detachment arriving without its staff/artillery; contrast Grant's PREPARED March-1864 transfer east. No new player-facing historical claim was added (the UI copy states only the mechanic), so no new citation burden; the calibration is stamped Inferred like the rest of the transfer block.
- **Rational value restored:** transfer-then-appoint or appoint-then-transfer both work (readiness is keyed to the current engagement); paying the capital now buys a real, visible, bounded command benefit relative to not paying it. The Command-desk transfer section now states what capital buys (−3 command-friction until transferred) — the pre-E70 "records readiness only" disclosure became false the moment readiness gained its consumer, so the copy AND its pinned probe tooth were updated together (deliberate tooth change, logged here: the tooth now pins `command-friction` + `never decides the battle`, preserving the no-output wall disclosure).
- **Walls preserved:** cmdTransfer still writes only `cmd.transfer` + the capital debit (existing D323 purity tooth green); no enemy Transfer/shadow state (D323 tooth green); the lift is PURE (new tooth snapshots command state + capital around the call).
- **Negative test (consumer bites):** `+ ready` unwired in commandLeadership → exactly the one E70 tooth red (`readiness must restore command effectiveness: 83 -> 83`), all 89 other steps green → restored → green. Observed values: unready **80**, transferred **83**, malus **3**, inside the [42,88] band.
- **Gate:** build **GATE OK**; ratings.json JSON-parse + schema **43/43** (fail-closed families); `node --check` clean; probe-command **90/90** (89 + the E70 tooth), adjacents probe-ratings **21/21** + probe-bridge **6/6** + probe-oob **17/17**, all 0 pageerrors; `git diff --check` clean. Full battery: the D176 release checkpoint is the immediate next milestone.
- **Queue:** E70 flips to BUILT — the run-2 ledger is fully dispositioned (17 FIX-NOW fixed D338-D353 + E70 built D354). Next: the full `npm run vet:noreg` release checkpoint (118 commands).

## D353 — E50 CAMPAIGN-ENVELOPE DEEP SAVE GUARD — [CLAUDE Fable 5 main loop, run-2 queue] (2026-07-10)

- **The defect (E50, filed D244 panel, re-triaged FIX-NOW by audit run 2):** the D244/E13 guards screened only `sv.settings`; the campaign envelope was accepted wholesale (`G.campaign = sv.campaign || null`), while ~20 downstream sites iterate save-derived campaign sub-objects with the callable form `raw.hasOwnProperty(k)`. The directly verified sink: D323's `raw.ids.hasOwnProperty(k)` in `_cmdTransferClean` (src/35-command.js) — a tampered `gor_save` carrying an own non-callable `hasOwnProperty` deep in `campaign.president.command.transfer.ids` passed every screen, applied at boot, and crashed command initialization on the first Command-tab open (navigation-gated, not the E13 boot brick).
- **The fix — deep guard at every accept lane + safe iteration at the verified sink:** new `_slCampaignPoisoned(root)` (src/91-save-slots.js) deep-scans the whole campaign envelope for an own `hasOwnProperty` at any depth (iterative stack walk, cycle-safe seen-Set, arrays included, fail-closed no-Set fallback). Wired at: `loadLocal` (105-save-guard — a poisoned autosave reads as corrupt/null, so `hasSave()` is false and the boot stays on the menu), `applySave` (105-save-guard — **ATOMIC**: the envelope is scanned before the settings loop, so a rejected payload applies neither settings nor campaign), and `_slValidSave` (91-save-slots — slot-read/import/undo lanes). `_cmdTransferClean` additionally iterates via `Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call` so even directly poisoned live state sanitizes: poison key dropped, legitimate siblings preserved, rebuilt `ids` inherits the callable prototype method.
- **Contract Relay worked example:** the acceptance teeth were authored by the prior ChatGPT/Codex session as uncommitted RED probe edits (probe-save-slots: import-lane + slot-lane rejection teeth, an E50BOOT third page-load with a deep-poisoned autosave, an atomic-applySave step; probe-command: the raw.ids sink sanitization tooth). All teeth adopted unchanged and land in the SAME commit as the fix that greens them (per the no-red-in-git rule; COORDINATION.md formalizes this next).
- **Scanner placement:** the D244 static tooth pins 105-save-guard to exactly the two override declarations, so the scanner lives in 91-save-slots (hoisting makes it live before the eval-time boot lane — the same mechanism the overrides rely on). Deliberate compliance with the existing tooth, not a tooth change.
- **E41 save-shape gate — conscious no-bump decision:** the three pinned validator hashes changed because validation TIGHTENED; the change is purely rejective (a legit save — anything serializeSave ever wrote — never carries an own `hasOwnProperty` at any depth, so every legit save is byte-identical through all lanes). No `_SAVE_VER` bump, no migration; `tools/save-shape.json` updated in the same commit as the E41 law requires.
- **Negative test (teeth bite):** guard stubbed + sink reverted → exactly the 5 E50 teeth went red (import lane, slot lane, boot rejection, atomic apply, and the sink crash reproduced verbatim: `raw.ids.hasOwnProperty is not a function`) with all legacy rows green and 0 pageerrors → restored → all green.
- **Recorded residual (bounded, D160):** the remaining callable-form sites (35-command.js corps/seated/reputation walks, 37-loot-survival, 56-artillery, T14-ratings) stay on `raw.hasOwnProperty(k)` — they are now unreachable from any save lane (every accept lane rejects the poisoned envelope before state applies); only direct console tampering of live state could reach them, which is outside the save-tamper threat model. A future hardening slice may sweep them mechanically.
- **Gate:** build **GATE OK** (save-shape ✓); `node --check` clean on all touched files; probe-save-slots **16/16**, probe-command **89/89** (88 + the new tooth), schema **43/43**, adjacents probe-ratings **21/21** + probe-bridge **6/6**, all 0 pageerrors; schema-report timestamp-only churn discarded (D337 precedent); `git diff --check` clean. Full `npm run vet:noreg` deferred to this queue's release checkpoint (D176), which is now next-but-one.
- **Model note:** this session runs Claude Fable 5 (`claude-fable-5[1m]`, xhigh) per Aaron's role-based Contract Relay kickoff — TOP LOOP binds to the best model on the plan (Fable 5 today), not to a name; D286's Opus-4.8 wording described the plan state of 2026-07-06, not a permanent name-lock.
- **Queue:** E50 flips to FIXED — **the run-2 FIX-NOW queue is EMPTY.** Next: E70 readiness consumer per Aaron's 2026-07-10 disposition (build one bounded, source-honest consumer; do NOT suspend the cost), then the planned full `npm run vet:noreg` release checkpoint.

## D352 — E69 REPORT HTML ENTITY-ESCAPING CONTRACT — [CODEX 5.6 Sol main loop, run-2 queue] (2026-07-10)

- **One text boundary:** the five audited generators (schema validation, orphan assets, probe-log summary, media-budget report, Group 6 dashboard) now import the shared report escaper. It encodes ampersand, angle brackets, double quote, and apostrophe in the required ampersand-first order and normalizes nullish values to blank text.
- **No trusted-number loophole:** the media and Group 6 reports also escape artifact fields previously interpolated raw because they were expected to be numeric. A malformed/tampered artifact can display its bad value but cannot turn that value into markup.
- **Hostile fixture gate:** the new report-escaping probe statically binds all five imports/uses and rejects local identity helpers, then parses seven payloads in both text and quoted-attribute contexts with the HTML5 parser. It requires exact text/attribute round-trip, zero script/image/SVG/iframe/object/embed nodes, and zero event attributes. Parse5 is declared directly; the gate is suite command **5 of 118**.
- **Negative/focused gate:** the in-memory identity diagnostic failed exactly entity output and hostile round-trip (**2/4**, exit 1); final escaping **4/4**. All five real reports regenerated and parsed with 0 errors/forbidden nodes/event attrs; schema **43/43**; orphan assets **199/199**, 0 orphan; probe summary **117/117** artifacts; media budget **13/13**; Group 6 readback **9/9**; build **GATE OK**, generated game unchanged; tool syntax, JSON/report readback, dependency readback, and `git diff --check` clean. ## D351 — E66 LATE-WESTERN BATTLE-FLAG METADATA — [CODEX 5.6 Sol main loop, run-2 queue] (2026-07-10)

- **Four explicit scenarios:** Chattanooga, Kennesaw, Franklin, and Nashville now resolve as Western-theater fields with no Army-of-the-Potomac corps badges and a representative Army-of-Tennessee `hardee` flag-family default. They no longer fall through to the Eastern/ANV sandbox fallback.
- **Historical boundary:** the [Tennessee State Museum's Hardee-pattern record](https://tnmuseum.org/Stories/posts/civil-war-flags-of-tennessee--part-2-flag-construction-and-inscriptions) ties the design to Hardee and especially Cleburne and documents its use into the Atlanta Campaign. NPS identifies the Confederate force as the Army of Tennessee at [Chattanooga](https://home.nps.gov/chch/learn/historyculture/battles-for-chattanooga.htm), [Kennesaw](https://www.nps.gov/kemo/learn/historyculture/confederate-order-of-battle.htm), [Franklin](https://www.nps.gov/articles/000/the-battle-of-franklin-and-beginning-of-the-end-for-the-army-of-tennessee.htm), and [Nashville](https://www.nps.gov/civilwar/search-battles-detail.htm?battleCode=tn038). `hardee` remains a representative family default, not a false claim that every regiment carried an identical design; a documented unit variant still requires its own lookup override.
- **Guard law:** `probe-flags` now compares the complete live 13-scenario registry to `_FLD_BATTLE_META`, so any future registered battle that silently inherits the Eastern/ANV default is red. Separate teeth pin the four new `W / badges=false / hardee` records and representative native-unit pattern resolution.
- **Negative/focused gate:** deleting Nashville metadata in-browser made exactly the three new coverage/semantic/pattern teeth red (**39/42**, exit 1) while all 39 legacy checks stayed green. Final flags **42/42**, 0 pageerrors/texture warnings; schema **43/43**; roster **8/8**, 13 exact scenarios, 0 pageerrors; visual fidelity **27/27**, 0 pageerrors/texture warnings; screenshots read; source/probe syntax clean; build **GATE OK**; artifact readback and `git diff --check` clean. Full `npm run vet:noreg` remains deferred to the D176 queue checkpoint.
- **Queue:** E66 flips to FIXED; **2 run-2 PENDING FIX-NOW items remain: E69 and E50**. Next is E69 report-HTML entity escaping. E70 remains proposal-only.

## D346 — E64 FAIL-CLOSED MEANINGFUL DATA-SCHEMA GATE — [CODEX 5.6 Sol main loop, run-2 queue] (2026-07-10)

- **Closed-world contract:** `tools/validate-data-schemas.mjs` now classifies every current JSON document into one of four explicit families (13 battles, 20 versioned schemas, 9 metadata documents, 1 ratings document). Every registered rule requires a nonempty key set; an unregistered file fails closed instead of falling through to a parse-only pass.
- **Meaningful invariants:** battle validation binds the one-payload root, matching id, roles, field/objective/time shape, brief/sides/teaching, OOB ids, phases, reinforcements, and terrain; the other three families require their real top-level structures and version/metadata types. The report now has an authoritative JSON companion with `ok`, diagnostic state, family totals, results, and pageerrors, and any failed document sets a nonzero exit status.
- **Bind test:** `--diagnostic-invalid=all` removes one required structure in each family in memory only. The tool reported exactly 4 failures (`antietam.objective`, `artillery.guns`, `cabinet.sides`, `ratings.attributes`), wrote `ok=false`, and exited 1. Clean mode then restored **43/43**, all four family counts, zero failed rows, zero pageerrors, and zero zero-key rules.
- **Suite enrollment/gate:** `vet-no-regression` now runs this validator immediately after the two canonical import gates; the visible suite is **117 commands**. Both touched tools pass `node --check`; soldier replacements **31/31** and women in war **9/9** import gates are green; battle-build research **15/15**; build **GATE OK** with generated HTML unchanged; report readback is green; `git diff --check` clean. Full `npm run vet:noreg` remains deferred to the end-of-queue D176 checkpoint.
