## D345 — E63 BATTLE-GUARD ENROLLMENT + HONEST CONSOLIDATED SWEEP — [CODEX 5.6 Sol main loop, run-2 queue] (2026-07-10)

- **Focused direction law is enrolled, not duplicated:** `tools/vet-no-regression.mjs` now runs the Chattanooga, Kennesaw, Franklin, and Nashville focused guards immediately after Chickamauga. The suite remains a serial `for ... await runOne` pipeline, so each Chrome probe finishes and closes before the next begins. The visible suite is now **116 commands**; the four added labels occupy 107-110 before attacker parity and the consolidated sweep.
- **Sweep integrity contract:** `tools/sweep-all-battles.mjs` still runs one browser/page and every scenario/seed serially, but now returns `phase` per run and writes an authoritative artifact with `ok`, diagnostic mode, registry ids, menu order, pageerrors, failures, summaries, and raw rows. Node-side validation requires registry/order set parity with no duplicate order ids, one row per requested seed, matching seed readback, `phase="over"`, a valid winner and nonempty `winBy`, opposite valid attacker/defender sides, finite nonnegative casualties, bounded positive steps, and a phase log for multi-phase battles. Any in-page exception, structural failure, or pageerror makes the artifact red and the process exit 1 after bounded cleanup.
- **Pacing/cleanup:** the sweep uses `domcontentloaded`, flushes asynchronous pageerrors after evaluation, writes even fatal/partial evidence, closes Chrome with a bounded hard-kill fallback, and always stops its local server. The suite timeout is **15 minutes** for the expanded 13-battle × 8-seed run on the 8 GB Mac; the actual clean run remained serialized and finished well inside that budget.
- **Bind test:** `--diagnostic-pageerror` raises one controlled in-page error against the real one-seed/13-battle run. It produced all 13 result rows but wrote `ok=false`, `pageerrors=["E63 diagnostic pageerror"]`, `failures=["pageerrors=1"]`, and exited **1**. The diagnostic was then removed and the full artifact restored green.
- **Gate (final bytes):** both touched tools pass `node --check`; build **GATE OK** with generated HTML byte-identical; suite `--list` shows the four guards in order and **116** total commands; focused Chattanooga **16/16**, Kennesaw **11/11**, Franklin **10/10**, Nashville **12/12**, all `ok=true`, 0 failed steps, 0 pageerrors; clean consolidated sweep covers **13 scenarios / 104 runs**, registry/order parity true, 0 unfinished, 0 missing `winBy`, 0 invalid casualties, 0 failures, 0 pageerrors, exit 0; `git diff --check` clean. Full `npm run vet:noreg` remains deferred to the end-of-queue D176 checkpoint.

## D343 — E65 LLM-CONNECTOR BROWSER-CALLS CONSENT ENFORCEMENT — [CLAUDE Fable main loop, Part 3 M6] (2026-07-09)

- **The E65 defect:** the T28 settings panel presents "Allow direct browser calls" as an explicit Anthropic prerequisite and stores `browserOptIn`, but `fldLlmConnConfigured()` ignored it — so a key+model config could arm at launch and `fldLlmDispatchAsync` could POST to `api.anthropic.com` carrying the `anthropic-dangerous-direct-browser-access: true` header the player never consented to. The probe itself asserted the wrong behavior (anthropic configured without opt-in).
- **The fix — one authoritative consent seam:** `fldLlmConnConfigured()` now returns false for adapter B without `browserOptIn`. Every downstream gate already consults that predicate (T27 `fldLlmConfigured` delegation, `fldLlmEnabledForBattle`, `fldLlmArmOnLaunch`, `fldLlmDispatchAsync`), so no arm and no dispatch can precede consent — one rule, no drift between copies. Adapter A providers (OpenRouter/Groq/Ollama/LM Studio/custom) are untouched. A pre-E65 stored config without the flag is deliberately NOT grandfathered: it reloads unconfigured and silently reverts to the built-in general until the player opts in — consent-conservative by design.
- **Player-facing honesty:** the opt-in note now states the enforced behavior ("While this is off, the game will not call Anthropic at all."), and the Status line names the actual blocker when everything is filled but the toggle is off ("turn on 'Allow direct browser calls'…") instead of the misleading "fill in the fields above."
- **Probe teeth (both directions + the arm seam):** the CONFIG STATE step now requires anthropic+key WITHOUT opt-in to be unconfigured and never battle-enabled, requires it configured WITH opt-in, and pins the requirement as adapter-B-only. A new E65 CONSENT step proves, against a recording mock fetch (never-settling promise; restored in `finally`; the zero-network spy stays authoritative): negative — a complete-but-unconsented config refuses `cb(null)` synchronously with zero fetch; positive — exactly one call to the locked `https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages` with the opt-in header, the player's key, `anthropic-version`, and the model/system/messages body shape; arm seam — an unconsented config never arms at a live launch, a consented one arms, and the recorder still reads exactly 1 call afterward (an arm leg can never dispatch).
- **Adversarial panel (3 lenses: consent-bypass Opus/high, probe-teeth Opus/high, UX-honesty Sonnet/medium):** the bypass hunt was REFUTED at high confidence — no path fires the header without consent, `fldLlmConnSet` normalizes unknown providers to adapter-A custom, and S42's draft/committed summary mismatch is not worsened. Panel LOWs folded in: mock scope extended over the arm legs, request-shape assertions added, the Status-line copy fixed. Accepted residual: the positive tooth's never-settling promise leaves one 12 s timer whose eventual `cb` is a verified no-op.
- **Bind-test (teeth bite):** with the predicate line temporarily removed and rebuilt, the probe went red on exactly the two consent teeth (CONFIG STATE's E65 assertion; the consent step's non-opted-in dispatch reaching the recorder) — and even that red run touched zero real network because the mock caught the unconsented attempt. Restored, rebuilt, green.
- **Gate (final bytes):** `node --check` clean on both files; build **GATE OK** (known 2.42 MB raw-embed soft warning); `probe-llm-commander` **25/25**, 0 pageerrors, **0 network**, exit 0; adjacents on final bytes `probe-field` **23/23** and `probe-accessibility` **25/25**, both exit 0; `git diff --check` clean. Full suite deferred under D176.
- **Queue:** E65 flips to FIXED; **10 run-2 PENDING FIX-NOW items remain (including E50)**. Next mechanical Codex slice: **C70** (Vaughan spelling). Next reasoning-tier slice: **E64** (schema-gate integrity) or **S41** (label declutter).

## D342 — E67 TACTICAL-VISUALS PER-SCENE BROWSER ISOLATION — [CODEX authored · CLAUDE Fable completed, Part 3 M5] (2026-07-09)

- **Provenance:** Codex (5.6 Sol) authored the core `tools/probe-tactical-visuals.mjs` rewrite and ran the full probe green (10/10, 02:47Z artifact), then ran out of usage before the negative test, docs, and commit. This session reviewed the uncommitted diff (3-lens adversarial panel: crash-paths Opus/high, Playwright-API Sonnet/medium, artifact-honesty Opus/high), hardened it, re-ran both gate legs on the final bytes, and shipped. Cross-tool rule held: code-written ≠ milestone done.
- **The E67 defect:** one Playwright page was reused for all ten heavy WebGL scenes, so a single page death (the audited Malvern Hill 3D frame-detach) cascaded and invalidated every later scene. Now every scene owns a fresh browser+context+page (`runSceneIsolated`, deliberately serialized for the 8 GB Mac), teardown is bounded (`closeBounded`, 5 s), a `crash` listener feeds `lifecycleErrors` into the scene verdict, `failureStage` labels every failure path, and per-scene partial JSON (always `ok:false, partial:true`) plus `expectedScenes`/`attemptedScenes` keep interrupted runs honest.
- **Negative-test harness:** `--diagnostic-close-scene=<name>` deliberately closes that scene's page before evaluation and reports `diagnostic.recoveryProven` = target failed AND every later scene passed. Diagnostic mode can never exit 0 and its artifact is never green (panel-confirmed; targeting the last scene honestly yields `recoveryProven=false` on zero later scenes).
- **Panel-driven hardening added this session (all inside the E67 charter — one failure must not erase remaining coverage):** (1) `page.evaluate` had no timeout, so a hung in-page scene script stalled the whole run forever — the scene evaluate is now raced against a generous 240 s hang bound and the `finally` `fldExit` evaluate against 5 s (without the latter the teardown evaluate would re-hang on the same stuck page); (2) a browser whose bounded close times out is now force-`SIGKILL`ed via `browser.process()` so zombie Chrome cannot starve later scene launches on 8 GB; (3) in-page `{ok:false}` assert returns and texture-warning flips now carry `failureStage` (`in-page-assert` / `texture-warning`).
- **Accepted residuals (panel LOW, logged not fixed):** pending 5 s race timers are reclaimed by the final `process.exit`; `cleanupErrors` are recorded per scene but do not flip a rendered-green scene red (safe direction — a leaked browser now gets SIGKILLed anyway); a kill before the first partial write leaves the previous gitignored artifact on disk, which the no-regression wrapper's mtime guard plus exit codes already cover.
- **Gate (final bytes):** `node --check` clean; diagnostic leg exit 1 with `recoveryProven=true later=6/6` and Malvern Hill 3D the deliberate kill; clean leg **10/10, exit 0**, artifact `ok=true`, 0 pageerrors / 0 lifecycleErrors / 0 cleanupErrors across all scenes, `isolation=fresh-browser-context-page`, `diagnostic=null`; Malvern Hill 3D screenshot read and fully painted; build **GATE OK** (parse/hex/collision/no-fudge/citations/women-in-war/save-shape ✓) with byte-identical generated HTML; `git diff --check` clean. Full suite deferred under D176.
- **Queue:** E67 flips to FIXED; **11 run-2 PENDING FIX-NOW items remain (including E50)**. Next this session: **E65** (T28 LLM-connector `browserOptIn` consent enforcement) as the reasoning-tier slice; **C70** (Vaughan spelling) stays queued as the next mechanical Codex slice per D286 routing.

## D340 — S40 KEYBOARD TACTICAL ORDERING EQUIVALENCE — [CODEX main loop, Part 3 M3] (2026-07-09)

- **Keyboard contract:** with one or more player brigades selected, `M` opens a bounded order cursor at their current destination centroid. Arrow keys move the endpoint in 30-yard steps; `[` / `]` turn destination facing in 15-degree steps; Enter commits an immediate movement order; Shift+Enter appends the same endpoint through the existing waypoint queue; Escape cancels the preview without exiting the battle. The live collision scan found `M`, arrows, and brackets unbound in tactical play, so no shipped command moved.
- **One order path:** the cursor is presentation state only. Commit synthesizes a `moveOnly` endpoint/facing gesture through `fldResolveOrderGesture`, which still owns multi-selection spread and delegates to `fldApplyOrder` / `fldEnqueueOrder`. No movement step, AI, combat, casualty, objective, score, winner, or save logic was duplicated or changed.
- **Accessibility/UX:** a static dual-stroke crosshair, destination footprint, and facing arrow remain legible over every 2D terrain palette; the existing 3D ghost receives the same keyboard gesture and a larger endpoint ring. A high-contrast instruction chip plus the HUD and both help references expose the mapping. `fldLive` announces relative endpoint position, facing, commit, queue, and cancel. The cursor has no animation/transition, so `reduceMotion` is honored without a second visual path. Tactical keys are suppressed while play is inactive, focus is editable, or a visible aria-modal dialog owns input.
- **Pointer parity:** the keyboard adapter is dormant unless `M` opens it; a pointer press cancels only a pending keyboard preview before entering the existing pointer flow. All 17 pre-S40 `probe-order-feel` rows are byte-equal to the saved D339 baseline. New real PointerEvent coverage confirms tap, drag+facing, Shift queue, facing-handle re-aim, and drag-to-chosen-foe charge unchanged when the keyboard cursor is unused.
- **Gate:** `node --check` clean on all five touched JS/probe files; build **GATE OK** (known 2.42 MB raw-embed soft warning only); `probe-order-feel` **22/22**, `ok=true`, 0 failed steps, 0 pageerrors; adjacent `probe-field` **23/23**, `probe-accessibility` **25/25**, `probe-h0-tactical-hud` **3/3** viewports, and `probe-help-overlay` **10/10**, all artifacts read back green with 0 pageerrors. The keyboard cursor and the desktop/tablet/phone HUD screenshots were visually inspected; `git diff --check` clean. Full `npm run vet:noreg` remains deferred under D176.
- **Next:** C69, the outcome-neutral Antietam Richardson battle-date rank label plus a focused probe tooth. Do not begin C70 or another MED/LOW item in this milestone.

## D339 — C71 KENNESAW PIGEON HILL INPUT CORRECTION + HONEST A/B — [CODEX main loop, Part 3 M2] (2026-07-09)

- **Source adjudication:** the ~5,500 Pigeon Hill figure includes the complete committed assault, not only an opening wave. The NPS National Register text says 5,500 Federals in three brigades attacked there and names Giles A. Smith, A. J. Lightburn, and Charles C. Walcutt. The NPS Pigeon Hill site bulletin independently describes two brigades from Brig. Gen. Morgan L. Smith's division plus Walcutt's brigade from Harrow's division, then gives about 5,500 as the total Union assault. Neither source supports a separate 1,200-man Morgan L. Smith echelon joining the failed assault.
- **Input correction:** removed `us_morgan_smith_support` from `data/kennesaw.json`, reducing the live Pigeon Hill commitment **6,700→5,500** and total fielded US strength **16,000→14,800**; Confederate fielded strength remains **11,925**. Morgan L. Smith remains correctly taught as a brigadier general and division commander in Logan's note, without adding a combat unit or officer effect. No terrain, timing, doctrine, weapon, experience, gun count, combat formula, score, winner, or output gate changed.
- **Probe correction:** all eight fielded US infantry brigades now carry an explicit `assaultSector`. `probe-kennesaw` gathers OOB plus reinforcements by side, rejects any unclassified US infantry, requires exactly the three sourced Pigeon Hill ids at 5,500, keeps Cheatham Hill at 9,000, and rejects any return of `us_morgan_smith_support`. The seed battery now derives starting strength from all fielded units rather than the old hard-coded 15,700/11,600 casualty bases and records all eight seed rows plus start/loss values in JSON.
- **Honest A/B:** the A artifact was captured at `f67cec5` before edits; the B artifact uses the identical seed battery after the input correction. Historical direction did not flip: **CS holds 8/8 → 8/8** and **US higher loss 8/8 → 8/8**. Normalizing the A sample against its real 16,000/11,925 fielded totals, seed deltas (B minus A, US/CS losses) are: seed 1 **+98/+40**, seed 7 **+71/−36**, seed 21 **−266/+29**, seed 33 **−391/+315**; deterministic seed 909 is **−100/−66**. The non-monotonic casualty response is recorded as simulation output, not tuned away.
- **Gate:** touched probe syntax clean; Kennesaw JSON parse clean; build **GATE OK** (known 2.42 MB raw-embed soft warning only); schema report read back **43/43, 0 failed**; Kennesaw plan **6/6**; Kennesaw runtime **10/10**, `ok=true`, 0 failed steps, 0 pageerrors; adjacent Franklin **10/10**, 0 pageerrors after one initial no-step 30-second readiness timeout and a clean serialized retry; Chattanooga **16/16**, 0 pageerrors; schema timestamp-only churn restored; `git diff --check` clean. Full `npm run vet:noreg` remains deferred under D176.
- **Next:** S40 keyboard order/facing/waypoint equivalence, the last remaining run-2 HIGH. Keep pointer play; add a bounded keyboard endpoint path and probe it under WCAG 2.1.1 without starting C69/C70 or any MED/LOW item.

## D338 — GETTYSBURG TRUTH BUNDLE C66+C67+C68 SHIPPED (TEXT/HISTORY ONLY) — [CLAUDE FABLE 5, Part 3 M1] (2026-07-09)

- **What shipped:** wording corrections in `data/gettysburg.json` for the three run-2 findings, one new data-only truth-teeth step in `tools/probe-gettysburg.mjs`, and the regenerated `civil_war_generals.html`. No OOB, strength, timing, terrain, combat, score, or outcome change — the probe's historical-direction battery is unchanged (Day 1 CS 7/8, Day 2 US 8/8, Day 3 US 8/8, aggregate US 8/8).
- **C66 (Lost Cause framing):** the Day-2 Longstreet leader note/teach, the header `_comment`, the top-level provenance, and the `gb_command` afterword no longer teach the sole-blame "delay and reluctance ... costs the Confederacy Little Round Top" / "foot-dragging" story. The replacement teaches the documented contested context — Longstreet's vigorous objection and preference to move around the Union left, Lee overruling him, the late-afternoon step-off after the approach march and countermarch, real-but-partial delay, Lee's hands-off piecemeal management — and names the Jubal Early Lost Cause scapegoating and its modern rejection, now agreeing with the codex Longstreet entry ("largely overturned", the codex's own hedge). Encyclopedia Virginia ('Gettysburg Campaign'; 'James Longstreet (1821-1904)') was fetched this session and added to the file's Sources manifest and the relevant Verified stamps alongside the standing Wert/Sears/Coddington/Pfanz citations.
- **C67 (unsourced quote):** the "General, I will take command" Meade quote is removed everywhere — the cited NPS 'Union Commanders at Gettysburg' page was fetched and contains no such quote. Hancock's record now teaches July 1 (NPS verbatim: "Meade sent Hancock as his representative to Gettysburg on July 1, where he took command of the chaotic situation"; ABT confirms he was to "decide if this was a good battle position") separately from the July 3 wound. The saddle-pommel/nail mechanism and the refusing-to-be-carried-off detail are attributed inline to Sears because NPS and ABT are silent on those specifics (ABT fetched: "seriously wounded in the thigh").
- **C68 (Kemper):** Pickett's summaries (leader note + teach + `cs_pickett_div` note) now read Garnett killed, Armistead mortally wounded, Kemper seriously wounded and captured — per NPS Fort McHenry 'Gettysburg Prisoners of War' (fetched: Kemper and Trimble "seriously wounded ... prisoners being held at the fort") and Sears; this also matches the file's own Day-3 fate `_note`, which had it right all along.
- **Incidental one-word precision fix inside the edited C66 passage:** the `gb_command` Jackson parenthetical "(wounded at Chancellorsville)" → "(mortally wounded at Chancellorsville)" — Jackson was dead by Gettysburg; flagged by the verify panel, in-passage, outcome-neutral.
- **Verification method (D145 routing honored):** 4 Sonnet source-fetch agents on the four mandated URLs, then a 3-lens Opus adversarial panel (anti-Lost-Cause framing / citation integrity / consistency) on the drafted wording — all three returned PASS_WITH_EDITS and every accepted edit is in the shipped text ("largely overturned"; maneuver-around-the-left instead of "attacking at all"; a leaner teach line; Wert instead of NPS on the Longstreet stamp; ABT added to the Hancock stamp). The probe teeth were negative-tested: the clean file passes and all four reinserted regression classes are caught.
- **Gate:** `node --check` clean on the touched probe; JSON parse clean; build **GATE OK** (known 2.42 MB raw-embed soft warning only); schema report **43/43, 0 failed** (report read, not exit code, per E64); `probe-gettysburg` **19/19** `ok=true` **0** pageerrors (new truth-teeth step green); adjacent `probe-antietam` **16/16** `ok=true` **0** pageerrors; schema-report timestamp-only churn restored; `git diff --check` clean.
- **Next:** C71 (Kennesaw Pigeon Hill overfielding) as its own sim-affecting A/B milestone.

- **[DOCS] Canonical guidance:** `START-HERE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `AUTONOMOUS-RUN.md`, `OPUS-PLAYBOOK.md` (filename retained), `AUDIT-PROMPT.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, and `WAKE-UP.md` now carry the current policy. The live game remains D335/Nashville at `8579829`; this is docs-only.

## D335 — NASHVILLE IS NOW A PLAYABLE TWO-PHASE T8 BATTLE — [CODEX GPT-5.5, Group 7 Franklin/Nashville battle-build implementation] (2026-07-08)

D334's Nashville source/OOB/rank/terrain packet was implemented as runtime data without changing the universal combat model. **Nashville is now playable** and registered after Franklin in the historical battle menu. Franklin remains a separate battle; Spring Hill remains teaching-only.

- **What shipped:** `data/nashville.json`, a registry/menu-rank line in `src/tactical/T1-bull-run.js`, focused guard `tools/probe-nashville.mjs`, both historical registry baselines updated (`tools/probe-tactical-roster.mjs`, `tools/probe-custom-battle-builder.mjs`), and `tools/validate-data-schemas.mjs` now classifies `nashville.json` as a battle file. `civil_war_generals.html` and the schema artifact were regenerated from source/tooling.
- **Playable shape:** two-phase T8 Nashville, December 15-16, 1864. Top-level roles are US attacker / CS defender; `defaultFog:false`; Phase 1 = Redoubts and Montgomery Hill, scoreWeight 1; Phase 2 = Shy's Hill and Peach Orchard Hill, scoreWeight 3.
- **Source honesty:** runtime uses compact phase strengths, not broad 85,000 / 55,000 battle-scale totals. Probe readback: phase 1 **16,300 US / 7,575 CS**, phase 2 **19,550 US / 11,150 CS**, with **20** Inferred-strength labels across units/reinforcements. Broad force figures remain context only.
- **Rank/sector/USCT traps held:** Thomas, A. J. Smith, Schofield, Wilson, and Steedman are Maj. Gens.; McArthur and Wood are Brig. Gens.; Hood is `Gen. John Bell Hood (temporary grade)` with permanent lieutenant-general nuance; S. D. Lee and Stewart are Lt. Gens.; Cheatham and Bate are Maj. Gens.; Shy is not a general; Forrest is absent from the main Nashville field. Sector separation holds: S. D. Lee = Peach Orchard/Overton/right, Cheatham/Bate/Shy = Shy's Hill/left, Stewart = center. USCT participation is pinned to Peach Orchard/Overton: 12th/13th/100th USCT under Thompson/Steedman, with 18th USCT supporting context, not invented Shy's Hill placement.
- **D74 boundary:** no battle-specific damage, firepower, casualty, winner, score-bonus, or force-win keys shipped. The Nashville result comes from OOB, terrain, works, artillery gun counts, phase weights, Wilson pressure, USCT/Steedman pinning pressure, and objective mechanics under the universal model.
- **Direction guard:** focused probe seed battery gives the intended historical pattern without count forcing: Union aggregate win **8/8** deterministic seeds, Redoubts phase **8/8**, Shy's Hill phase **8/8**, and Confederate losses exceed Union losses **8/8**. Sample readback: seed 1 = US/US aggregate US, US loss 3,154 vs CS loss 7,908.
- **Focused gate:** `node --check` clean for touched runtime/probe/tool JS; `node tools/build.mjs` **GATE OK** (known raw-embed soft warning only); `node tools/validate-data-schemas.mjs` **43/43**; `probe-nashville-plan` **7/7**; `probe-nashville` **12/12**; `probe-tactical-roster` **8/8**; `probe-custom-battle-builder` **15/15**; adjacent `probe-franklin` **10/10** and `probe-kennesaw` **10/10**; all six required probe JSON artifacts read back `ok=true`, **0** failed steps, **0** pageerrors; `git diff --check` clean.
- **Next:** stop at the D335 battle-build boundary. Remaining natural Group 7 candidates include USCT/New Market Heights or another researched lane; the D328 Antietam rank-label fix remains a small independent cleanup; full `vet:noreg` stays deferred to a planned batch/release gate unless explicitly requested.

## D330 — KENNESAW MOUNTAIN BATTLE-BUILD SPEC AND PLAN PROBE — [CODEX GPT-5.5, Group 7 Atlanta/March battle-build planning] (2026-07-08)

D327 made Kennesaw Mountain the lead Atlanta/March candidate; D330 turns that research packet into a durable implementation spec and filesystem guard before any runtime/data work. **Planning/spec only — no `data/kennesaw.json`, runtime registry, menu button, generated-HTML behavior, or combat-code change.**

- **What shipped:** `docs/design/kennesaw-battle-build-spec.md` and `tools/probe-kennesaw-plan.mjs`. The spec locks a **single-phase** battle, not T8: US attacker, CS defender, `defaultFog:false`, objective = the Confederate ridge/breastwork line across Pigeon Hill / Little Kennesaw and Cheatham Hill / Dead Angle. The intended historical teaching read is that a frontal US assault loses because of terrain, fieldworks, abatis, and OOB/timing — not because of per-battle output modifiers.
- **Source/OOB re-verification before spec:** D327's Atlanta/March packet was treated as a start, not authority. The load-bearing sector details were rechecked against NPS Kennesaw materials, NPS Union/Confederate OOB pages, the NPS National Register text, and ABT Cheatham Hill. The spec pins the two assault sectors: **~5,500 Federals at Pigeon Hill** via Giles A. Smith, A. J. Lightburn, and Charles C. Walcutt; **~9,000 Federals at Cheatham Hill** via Newton's and Davis's divisions, with Harker/Wagner/Kimball and McCook/Mitchell named for D331 verification.
- **Rank/identity traps locked for D331:** Sherman is Maj. Gen.; Thomas, Schofield, and McPherson are Maj. Gens.; Morgan L. Smith is Brig. Gen.; Johnston is full General CSA and still army commander at Kennesaw; Hood is not army commander yet; Hardee is Lt. Gen.; Cheatham/Cleburne are Maj. Gens.; Maney/Vaughn are Brig. Gens. Exact Confederate brigade-strength inputs are not fully supplied by the OOB pages, so D331 must mark any such strength as **Verified identity; Inferred strength** rather than inventing certainty.
- **Strength-total guard:** D331 must not use the campaign-scale **150,000 / 100,000** Atlanta-campaign totals as battle strengths. The probe/spec explicitly forbid that failure mode.
- **D74 gate:** no per-battle `damage`, `firepower`, `casualty`, `morale`, `retreat`, `winner`, `outcome`, `scoreBonus`, `forceWin`, or similar fudge is allowed. Kennesaw's result must emerge from source-honest inputs and the universal combat model.
- **Plan probe:** `probe-kennesaw-plan` verifies the spec exists, has the required source register and history traps, names the single-phase/fog/role/objective shape, preserves planned-only registry state before `data/kennesaw.json`, and flips to requiring runtime registration + both historical baselines + focused probe once data exists.
- **Focused gate:** `node --check tools/probe-kennesaw-plan.mjs`; `node tools/build.mjs` **GATE OK** (known raw-embed soft warning only); `node tools/probe-kennesaw-plan.mjs` **6/6**, JSON `ok=true`, `failedSteps:0`; `git diff --check` clean.

## D329 — BATTLE-RESEARCH COVERAGE COMPLETION: the last six unbuilt, uncovered lanes — [OPUS 4.8, Aaron directive "finish the battle-research coverage"] (2026-07-08)

D327 researched five remaining lanes; D328 audited the ten shipped battles. D329 closes the gap between them: **every** buildable Civil War battle or campaign that is neither shipped nor already covered now has a durable, citation-grade packet. **Research/docs only — no `data/*.json`, runtime, registry, or generated-HTML change; `civil_war_generals.html` byte-unchanged.**

- **Gap set (confirmed before researching, not assumed):** grepping `data/` proved every candidate appears only as teaching/codex/bio prose, never as a scenario. Six new lanes shipped: **`eastern-1862`** (Second Bull Run + the Seven Days beyond the built Malvern Hill), **`shenandoah-1862`** (Jackson's Valley Campaign), **`shenandoah-1864`** (Sheridan's Valley Campaign), **`western-gaps`** (Perryville + Stones River), **`trans-mississippi`** (Wilson's Creek, Pea Ridge, Glorieta, Red River), **`appomattox-campaign`** (Five Forks, the Apr 2 breakthrough, Sailor's Creek, the surrender). Jackson's Valley was split off the prompt's suggested `eastern-gaps` into its own lane so it carries a distinct verdict.
- **Method:** the D327 workflow, rerun — 6 × [Opus researcher with live WebSearch/WebFetch → Opus adversarial verifier that re-fetched the riskiest claims and edited the packet in place] + an Opus 4.8 main-loop final verify. 12 agents, 0 errors.
- **Verdicts: all six READY_FOR_SPEC**, but with one lane carrying an explicit blocking constraint (below). Lead candidates: **Gaines' Mill** (single-phase defender-hold — the exact inverse of the built Malvern Hill), **Cross Keys + Port Republic** (T8 two-phase, roles flip), **Cedar Creek** (T8 two-phase, same-day role reversal), **Stones River** (T8 two-phase), **Pea Ridge / Elkhorn Tavern** (T8 two-phase), **Five Forks** (single-phase attacker-seize).
- **THE D183 GATE (a live Aaron decision — surfaced, not resolved).** `trans-mississippi` is gated. D183 and `data/under-told-perspectives.json`'s `native-nations` source note say verbatim: *"this is not a license to create a playable Trans-Mississippi battle, OOB, unit roster, or Confederate-diversity claim. **M8 battle-build still needs Aaron go/no-go.**"* The packet reads that as a **two-tier** gate — **Tier 1** gates the *entire* Trans-Mississippi build (even the Native-free Elkhorn Tavern axis); **Tier 2** additionally bars any Leetown-sector Native OOB. Its `READY_FOR_SPEC` therefore means "evidence verified, spec writable **once Aaron clears the gate**," NOT "start now." The packet resolves neither tier and does not self-clear. **Aaron's call is required before any Trans-Mississippi spec.**
- **Real defects the adversarial + final-verify layers caught** (each would have shipped a wrong fact or a probe that falsely fails a correct build):
  - **The run prompt itself was wrong.** It asserted Jackson was "never a lieutenant general" and told the implementer to reject `Lt. Gen. Jackson` for 1862 *or* 1863. **False** — Jackson was promoted **Lt. Gen. Oct 10 1862** (Second Corps, ANV) and held it at Fredericksburg and Chancellorsville. The verifier **refuted the prompt from sources** rather than deferring to it, and re-scoped the guard to Valley-1862 battle ids only. A blanket "any year" guard would have falsely failed a *correct* future Chancellorsville build. This is the intended behavior of an adversarial layer, and it is the second time (cf. D328's refuted Fredericksburg/Owen flag) that the layer protected the repo from a confident wrong "fix."
  - **Main-loop final verify** then caught a residual date defect the adversarial pass let stand: Jackson was **wounded May 2 1863** and **died May 10 1863**; the packet conflated the two. Corrected inline.
  - **ABT Second Manassas** displays Union **125,000 *total*** (a paper/theater figure), not an engaged force — and that same page prints the anachronistic label **"Lt. Gen. Longstreet"** (he was a Maj. Gen. in Aug 1862), which the packet's own rank trap must override. Both corrected.
  - **Cedar Creek casualties:** the 7,682 total is **NPS**'s, not ABT's (8,824) or Wikipedia's (8,575). All three agree on the *direction*, so the guard survives; the mis-attribution did not.
  - **The Oct-1862 lieutenant-general flip, resolved:** **Polk and Hardee were MAJOR generals at Perryville (Oct 8 1862)** and **LIEUTENANT generals at Stones River (Dec 31 1862)** — the grade was created Oct 1862 and they were appointed Oct 10-11, *two days after Perryville*. Cleburne flips Brig.→Maj. Gen. (Dec 13 1862) across the same pair. Sheridan is **Brig. Gen. at both** (his MG date-of-rank is backdated to Dec 31 1862 — a textbook D92 backdating trap).
  - **Stand Watie was a COLONEL at Pea Ridge** (Brig. Gen. only in 1864 — do not backdate); his Cherokee regiment *number* is genuinely muddled in the records and is left unpinned. **Sterling Price** was a Maj. Gen. of the **Missouri State Guard**, a state force, not the Confederate Army.
- **Direction-guard discovery (load-bearing for D74/D92).** Several of these battles **invert** the usual "the winner bleeds less" assumption: at **Stones River**, **Perryville**, **Wilson's Creek**, and **McDowell**, the side that held the field lost *more* men. A Stones River spec must assert **near-parity aggregate casualties, explicitly NOT US < CS**. Casualty figures remain direction guards, never count-forcing gates.
- **Dignity line held.** Teaching-only, never scored: **"The Burning"** of the Shenandoah (hard war on civilians), the **Leetown scalpings** and their weaponization by the Northern press, **Sand Creek** (named wherever Chivington appears), the **Appomattox surrender**, and the mass surrenders at Front Royal and Sailor's Creek. The surrender additionally carries Blight's reconciliationist critique so the "Gentleman's Agreement" mythology is not laundered; the XXV Corps (USCT) is centered at the war's end.
- **Engine-honesty calls (a correct "no" is a valuable answer).** **Glorieta Pass** is a **teaching-only engine mismatch** — Chivington's burning of ~80 supply wagons at Johnson's Ranch decided the campaign, not the fight at Pigeon's Ranch. Jackson's Valley Campaign *as a campaign* is operational maneuver the engine cannot represent (only Cross Keys/Port Republic stand as brigade fights). **Brandy Station** and all pure cavalry raids are teaching-only (no mounted-melee/raid mode). The Red River river-war needs the naval engine the project lacks.
- **Lane boundary (no double-claim):** `1864-65-attrition` owns Overland, Cold Harbor, the Petersburg **siege**, and the Crater; `appomattox-campaign` owns **Apr 1-9 1865**, including the Apr 2 breakthrough that the attrition packet had explicitly deferred as un-researched. Fort Stedman stays a teaching card.
- **Guard:** `tools/probe-battle-build-research.mjs` now lists all **11** forward packets and adds a **HYGIENE** step failing on leaked JSON-escape artifacts (`\"`, literal `\n`) across all 21 packets — a real corruption class for markdown authored through a JSON-carrying pipeline. Probe **14/14 → 15/15**.
- **Exact gates run:** `node --check tools/probe-battle-build-research.mjs`; `node tools/probe-battle-build-research.mjs` **15/15, 0 fail**, JSON readback `ok=true`, hygiene `checked:21 clean:true`; `node tools/build.mjs` **GATE OK** (`no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓`; known raw-embed soft warning only); `git diff --check` clean; `civil_war_generals.html` SHA-256 identical pre/post build and absent from the commit.
- **Next:** the library is **complete** — a future battle-build slice finds its evidence already assembled. ## D328 — BUILT-BATTLE RESEARCH/AUDIT LAYER (all 10 shipped battles), FOR CODEX REVISION — [OPUS 4.8, Aaron mid-run request] (2026-07-08)

Aaron asked (mid-D327) to confirm the ALREADY-BUILT battles are researched/supported to the same standard as the new D327 lanes, and, where not, to do that research so Codex can revise them alongside building new battles. D328 answers it. **Research/docs only — no `data/*.json`, runtime, registry, or generated-HTML change; `civil_war_generals.html` byte-unchanged.**

- **What shipped:** `docs/design/battle-build-research/built-battles/` — a README index + one durable audit packet for each of the 10 shipped battles (Bull Run, Antietam, Fredericksburg, Chancellorsville, Malvern Hill, Gettysburg, Shiloh, Vicksburg, Chickamauga, Chattanooga). Each packet carries a Source Register, a "Confirmed Solid" list, a "Revision Checklist For Codex," OOB/rank re-verification, a terrain+teaching/anti-Lost-Cause check, a D74/D92 no-fudge adherence check, remaining uncertainties, and a SOLID_AS_IS/MINOR_REVISIONS/NEEDS_REVISION verdict. `tools/probe-battle-build-research.mjs` was extended to guard the subfolder (now **14/14**).
- **Method:** for each battle, a compact digest of the *actually-encoded* ranks/units/terrain/teaching (from `data/<id>.json`) was audited by an Opus auditor (web-verified), then by an **adversarial Opus verifier** tasked with protecting the D92-hardened data from bad "fixes," then by the Opus 4.8 main loop against the live file.
- **Finding — the prior hardening held:** **9 of 10 battles are SOLID_AS_IS** (D92 rank/roster audit, D86 Vicksburg, D90 Chickamauga, D325/D326 Chattanooga all re-confirmed). The two-layer design paid off twice: it **caught one missed error** and **refuted one false flag**.
  - **Antietam → MINOR_REVISIONS (1 confirmed fix):** the `us_richardson` unit's `commander` field reads `Brig. Gen. Israel B. Richardson` but he was a **Major General** (of volunteers, Jul 4 1862) at Antietam — and the file's own `ld_richardson` leader entry already says `Maj. Gen.`, so it is an internal self-contradiction (the D92 "file contradicts itself" class, cf. Malvern Hill's Hunt). Outcome-neutral label fix; the antietam probe should stay 16/16. (The separate battery captain "Capts. Squires, Richardson, Brown" is a different man — leave it.)
  - **Fredericksburg → SOLID_AS_IS (1 false flag refuted):** the auditor proposed Owen `Brig. Gen.`→`Col.`; the adversarial pass **refuted** it — Joshua T. Owen genuinely was a brigadier general at Fredericksburg via a Nov 1862 recess appointment (unlike Zook, whose BG was backdated from March 1863). Applying the "fix" would have injected an error into a correct D92-hardened fact. The flag is recorded as DO-NOT-APPLY, not in the actionable checklist.
- **D74/D92 + anti-Lost-Cause:** every audit confirmed no per-battle fudge and sound anti-Lost-Cause teaching text.
- **Codex handoff:** the only data revision to apply is Antietam's `us_richardson.commander` → `Maj. Gen. Israel B. Richardson` (then re-gate: build GATE OK + `probe-antietam` 16/16 + `git diff --check`). Everything else is confirmed source-accurate.
- **Exact gates run:** `node --check tools/probe-battle-build-research.mjs`; `node tools/probe-battle-build-research.mjs` **14/14**; `node tools/build.mjs` **GATE OK** (known raw-embed soft warning only); `git diff --check` clean; built-verdict JSON readback correct. Docs-only; `civil_war_generals.html` unchanged.
- **Next:** the first battle-build spec slice — **Kennesaw Mountain (Atlanta/March)** per D327 — unless Aaron reorders; Codex may apply the single Antietam revision at any time under the antietam gate.

---

## D327 — DURABLE BATTLE-BUILD RESEARCH LIBRARY FOR EVERY REMAINING LANE — [OPUS 4.8, Group 7 battle-build research] (2026-07-08)

Chattanooga (D325/D326) proved that a battle should be spec'd from a durable, source-verified packet before implementation, not authored from memory. D327 generalizes that to the whole remaining battle-build queue: it creates `docs/design/battle-build-research/` with one citation-grade research packet per remaining lane, plus a filesystem guard. **Research/docs only — no `data/*.json`, no registry line, no runtime, no generated-HTML behavior changed.**

- **What shipped:** `docs/design/battle-build-research/README.md` + five packets — `atlanta-march`, `franklin-nashville`, `usct`, `naval-river`, `1864-65-attrition` — and `tools/probe-battle-build-research.mjs`. Each packet carries: candidate battles ranked by buildability, recommended playable shape (single-phase vs T8, roles, phase list, scoreWeight logic), a Source Register with URLs + per-source confidence, OOB/rank traps to re-verify, terrain/objective landmarks, teaching cards + anti-Lost-Cause framing, D74 no-fudge risks, candidate probe teeth, a "safe to build next?" verdict, and the exact next slice.
- **How it was produced:** an Opus research→adversarial-verify workflow (5 lanes × [Opus researcher w/ live WebSearch/WebFetch → Opus adversarial verifier that re-fetched the riskiest claims]) plus an Opus 4.8 main-loop final-verifier pass. Real defects were caught and corrected before landing: **Forrest was Lt. Col. (not Col.) at Fort Donelson**; the **54th Mass lost ~40-45% (not ">50%") at Fort Wagner**; the **Jonesborough casualties were day-by-day mislabeled**; **two-phase scoreWeights sum to 4 (not 5)** — the "total 5" convention is three-phase only; the **Cold Harbor "7,000 in 30 minutes" figure is a disputed myth, not an engine input**. Each packet ends with a Verification Notes section.
- **Verdicts (all lanes READY_FOR_SPEC for their lead candidate, with explicit remaining traps):** Atlanta/March → **Kennesaw Mountain** single-phase (attacker historically loses) + Jonesborough two-phase; Franklin/Nashville → **Franklin** single-phase defender-hold + Nashville two-phase; USCT → **New Market Heights / Chaffin's Farm** two-phase (14 MoH); Naval/river → **Fort Donelson** three-phase land siege *only* (Hampton Roads/Mobile Bay/Fort Henry are DO_NOT_BUILD_NOW — the project has a land engine only); 1864-65 → **Spotsylvania "Bloody Angle"** single-phase → Overland Campaign three-phase.
- **Dignity line held:** **Fort Pillow is DO_NOT_BUILD** as a scenario (a massacre of surrendering Black soldiers = teaching/memory only); the Crater ships as a failed-assault scenario with the massacre as teaching only, never a scored objective.
- **D74/D92 preserved:** every packet forbids per-battle damage/firepower/casualty/winner fudge and frames casualty figures as direction guards, never count-forcing gates; outcomes must emerge from OOB/terrain/timing/doctrine/gun-counts/scoreWeight.
- **Most ready for the next implementation slice:** Atlanta/March → **Kennesaw Mountain** (clean objective-hold "attacker loses" fit; also the standing Priority-1 queue item). Franklin (single-phase) and Fort Donelson (3-phase) are close seconds. **Research-thin / not yet buildable:** all ship-vs-ship naval actions and Fort Pillow.
- **Guard:** `tools/probe-battle-build-research.mjs` verifies the folder, all required packets, that each carries Source Register / OOB-and-rank traps / playable-shape / D74-no-fudge / probe-teeth / verdict, that the README indexes every packet, and that no packet claims READY_FOR_SPEC without ≥2 source rows + explicit remaining traps.
- **Exact gates run:** `node --check tools/probe-battle-build-research.mjs`; `node tools/probe-battle-build-research.mjs` **8/8**; `node tools/build.mjs` **GATE OK** (known raw-embed soft warning only); `git diff --check` clean; probe JSON readback `ok=true`, 0 fail. Build left `civil_war_generals.html` byte-unchanged (docs-only), so it is not in the commit.
- **Next:** a follow-on research/audit pass over the already-built battles (D328) so Codex can revise them alongside building new battles; then the first battle-build spec slice (Kennesaw Mountain) unless Aaron reorders.

## D326 — CHATTANOOGA IS NOW A PLAYABLE THREE-PHASE T8 BATTLE — [CODEX GPT-5.5, Group 7 battle-build implementation] (2026-07-08)

D325's source/OOB/rank substrate re-verified clean enough to implement Chattanooga without inventing units, ranks, terrain, or outcome modifiers. D326 ships the runtime battle.

- **Playable shape:** `data/chattanooga.json` adds a three-phase T8 scenario: Orchard Knob / Indian Hill on Nov. 23, Lookout Mountain at the Cravens-house bench below the palisade on Nov. 24, and Missionary Ridge on Nov. 25. Top-level roles are `attacker:"US"` / `defender:"CS"`; Missionary Ridge is the decisive scoreWeight-3 phase.
- **Historical guardrails:** the battle teaches Lookout as fighting below the summit, Missionary Ridge as base rifle pits plus physical-crest/military-crest confusion, and Cleburne's Tunnel Hill / Ringgold Gap role as teaching and end-note content rather than an unsupported fourth playable phase.
- **D74 boundary:** no per-battle damage, firepower, casualty, or winner fudge shipped. Outcomes emerge from the existing universal systems plus OOB, terrain, timing, doctrine, artillery gun counts, and phase scoreWeight.
- **Registry/probes:** Chattanooga is registered after Chickamauga in the tactical menu. `tools/probe-chattanooga.mjs` gates content teeth, rank traps, no-fudge keys, menu/side-choice/interphase/HUD/end-screen behavior, deterministic simulation, historical pattern, and the Missionary Ridge alt-history hinge. Both historical baselines were updated in `tools/probe-tactical-roster.mjs` and `tools/probe-custom-battle-builder.mjs`.
- **Focused gate:** `node tools/build.mjs` GATE OK with the known raw-embed soft warning; `node --check` clean for touched runtime/probe JS; `probe-chattanooga-plan` **6/6**; `probe-chattanooga` **16/16**; `probe-tactical-roster` **8/8**; `probe-custom-battle-builder` **15/15**; `probe-vicksburg` **18/18**; `probe-chickamauga` **18/18**; all required JSON artifacts read back `ok=true`, **0** failed steps, **0** pageerrors; `git diff --check` clean.
- **Next:** stop at the D326 battle-build boundary. The next queued battle-build item is the remaining unlocked battle queue, starting with an Atlanta/March planning/spec slice unless Aaron reorders to Franklin/Nashville, USCT, Phase H, Soldier's Story, Phase D, or custom-battle phase authoring.

## D325 — CHATTANOOGA BATTLE-BUILD SPEC AND PROBE SCAFFOLD ARE NOW DURABLE BEFORE IMPLEMENTATION — [CODEX GPT-5.5, Group 7 battle-build planning] (2026-07-08)

D324 unlocked the battle-build lane, but Chattanooga should not be authored straight from scratch notes or memory. D325 creates a durable `docs/design/chattanooga-battle-build-spec.md` packet and a focused filesystem gate, `tools/probe-chattanooga-plan.mjs`, so the next implementation slice starts from source/OOB/rank/terrain traps rather than invented data.

- **Scope locked for implementation:** Chattanooga is planned as a three-phase T8 battle: Orchard Knob (US seizes, scoreWeight 1), Lookout Mountain / Cravens-house bench (US seizes, scoreWeight 1, light-casualty/fog teaching), and Missionary Ridge (US seizes, scoreWeight 3 decisive phase). Top-level roles are `attacker:"US"` / `defender:"CS"`, intentionally mirroring Chickamauga's CS-offensive arc.
- **Source honesty:** the spec records current source anchors and marks the old `.tmp/chattanooga-factsheet.json` as scratch-only, not authority. It names rank/sector traps up front: Grant is not lieutenant general yet; Breckinridge is not lieutenant general; Anderson and Bate are not major generals yet; John K. Jackson commands Cheatham's Division in the relevant sector; Indian Hill is not a separate objective; Lookout fighting is on the bench below the palisade; Missionary Ridge needs the physical-crest vs military-crest teaching.
- **D74 gate:** no data/runtime implementation shipped in D325. No `data/chattanooga.json`, registry line, menu button, scenario button, generated HTML, or combat code changed. The probe asserts this planned-only state today and will later require that any future Chattanooga data is accompanied by `tools/probe-chattanooga.mjs`, `tools/probe-tactical-roster.mjs` EXPECTED, and `tools/probe-custom-battle-builder.mjs` EXPECTED updates.
- **Recurring gotcha recorded:** when D326 adds the battle id, it must be added to BOTH `tools/probe-tactical-roster.mjs` and `tools/probe-custom-battle-builder.mjs` historical baselines, or the custom-builder registry guard should go red.
- **Focused gate:** `node --check tools/probe-chattanooga-plan.mjs`; `node tools/build.mjs` GATE OK with known raw-embed soft warning; `node tools/probe-chattanooga-plan.mjs` green **6/6**; adjacents `probe-tactical-roster` **8/8**, `probe-custom-battle-builder` **15/15**, `probe-vicksburg` **18/18**, and `probe-chickamauga` **18/18**, all browser probes with **0** pageerrors; `git diff --check` clean.
- **Next:** D326 Chattanooga playable implementation, beginning with re-verification of the OOB/source substrate before authoring data.

## D323 — GROUP 2 TRANSFER MOVE: CROSS-THEATER READINESS IS NOW EXPLICIT, PLAYER-GATED, AND OUTPUT-WALLED — [CODEX GPT-5.5, Priority-1 Group 2 GM follow-up] (2026-07-08)

D322 made theater fit honest; D323 turns it into the explicit player move without coupling it to battle outcomes.

- **Runtime move:** `src/35-command.js` now adds `cmdTransfer(C,id)` plus bounded `_cmdTransfer*` helpers. A Transfer spends configured political capital, stores only current-battle readiness under `C.president.command.transfer`, and makes `cmdTransferReadiness(C,id)` read `transferred:true` for that engagement.
- **Command desk:** the Command tab renders a `Cross-theater transfer` section showing the next battle theater, same-theater/Multi fits, cross-theater candidates, affordability, and completed transfers. The control is player-visible only; there is no enemy Transfer button or shadow write.
- **Save hardening:** `cmdInit` sanitizes malformed, stale-battle, wrong-theater, native-fit, invalid, dead/off-date, or uncommissioned commission-pool Transfer records. Absent/null means no default transfer state and no default behavior change.
- **Output wall:** Transfer writes only `cmd.transfer` plus the political-capital debit. It does not write scoreboard, battle result, casualties, OOB totals, combat output, hidden commissions, hidden enemy command state, or enemy Transfer.
- **Focused gate:** `node --check src/35-command.js`; `node --check tools/probe-command.mjs`; `node tools/build.mjs` GATE OK with known raw-embed soft warning; `node tools/validate-data-schemas.mjs` green **39/39**; `node tools/probe-command.mjs` green **88/88**, **0** pageerrors; adjacents `node tools/probe-ratings.mjs` green **21/21**, `node tools/probe-oob.mjs` green **17/17**, and `node tools/probe-bridge.mjs` green **6/6**, all **0** pageerrors; `git diff --check` clean.
- **Next phase decision:** Group 2's explicit Transfer slice is closed. Optional Group 6 report polish remains the next eligible non-locked item, but M8/Q5/Q6 battle-build still requires Aaron's explicit go/no-go; Phase H media, H2/HDRI/model media, Tripo, assets, and Soldier's Story rows remain locked/deferred unless Aaron redirects.

## D322 — GROUP 2 GM/TRANSFER SUBSTRATE: THEATER CLASSIFICATION IS NOW HONEST, READABLE, AND PROBE-GATED BEFORE ANY TRANSFER MOVE — [CODEX GPT-5.5, Priority-1 Group 2 GM follow-up] (2026-07-08)

Group 2's D173 AI-GM shadow explicitly left Transfer blocked because `data/generals.json` had no honest theater classification. D322 closes that blocker as input/readout substrate only.

- **Data substrate:** every starting-roster and commission-pool general now carries `theater`, `theaters`, and `theaterProvenance`. The broad buckets are `Eastern`, `Western`, and `Multi`, inferred from each already sourced biography and the campaign's existing Eastern/Western battle taxonomy. These fields are not new biographical claims and do not override each record's existing source trail.
- **Runtime substrate:** `src/35-command.js` now exposes pure helpers for normalizing a general's theater list, resolving the next battle's broad theater from `BATTLES.th` / logistics routes, and reading `cmdTransferReadiness(C,id)`. The helper flags whether a general already fits the next battle's theater or would need a future cross-theater Transfer move.
- **AI-GM readout:** the enemy command shadow now carries commander theater fields and the next battle theater into its pure snapshot/readout. It still writes no enemy command save state, commissions no hidden officers, and performs no Transfer.
- **Focused gate:** `node --check src/35-command.js`; `node --check tools/probe-command.mjs`; `node tools/build.mjs` GATE OK with known raw-embed soft warning; `node tools/validate-data-schemas.mjs` green **39/39**; `node tools/probe-command.mjs` green **81/81**, **0** pageerrors. `git diff --check` pending in the final closeout gate.
- **Next phase decision:** the `theater` blocker is closed. This is now superseded by D323's explicit Transfer move, which stayed gated and input/readout-bounded without combat/output coupling.

## D307 — OPERATING MODEL: SAFE HOMOGENEOUS QUEUES SHOULD RUN AS SAME-CHAT EXECUTION BUNDLES — [AARON directive, docs-only instruction update] (2026-07-08)

Aaron directed that current runs stop too often and should include more tasks. Going forward, the D171 fresh-chat boundary remains a guard against unsafe cross-group/context drift, but it no longer means every tiny safe slice gets its own handoff.

- **New default:** when the active queue is homogeneous, low-risk, and already cleared (for example Group 6 tooling/reporting), run a same-chat execution bundle of **2-4 small slices** before stopping.
- **Per-slice discipline remains:** each slice still gets source/doc readback, focused gates, JSON/stdout readback, docs sync, `git diff --check`, commit, and push before the run continues to the next slice.
- **Stop conditions:** stop and return a paste-ready continuation prompt when the bundle is exhausted, the next item would enter a new group/phase, a design fork/risk/lock appears, a browser-heavy/full-suite gate is next, context is near compaction risk, or the worktree state becomes ambiguous.
- **Locks preserved:** no media assets, asset fetches, credits/Tripo, H2 footage, Phase H reopening, Soldier's Story slice, M8/Q5/Q6, or Phase D work is authorized by this operating change.
- **Reversibility/impact:** docs-only operating update. No runtime/data/gameplay change. It changes future prompt shape and run behavior: fewer one-slice stops inside safe queues, without weakening per-slice gates.

---

## D291 — PHASE I SOLDIER'S STORY SCALE-OUT: THREE NEW CITATION-GRADE REPLACEMENTS (23-25), A SECOND CS-WEIGHTED WESTERN-THEATER SLICE — VAL C. GILES (CS Chickamauga, Hood's Texas Brigade), WILLIAM PITT CHAMBERS (CS Vicksburg — the second Vicksburg-CS record), and SAM HOUSTON JR. (CS Shiloh, the Unionist governor's son) — [CLAUDE Opus 4.8, Priority-1; web-grounded research verified INLINE in the main loop + read-only default-refute Opus fact-check panel `wf_a1ac5e1b-4c9`] (2026-07-07)

The Soldier's Story replacement lane grows from twenty-two to **twenty-five** `Verified` records. All three are Confederate AND Western-theater, lifting side balance from 14US/8CS to **14US/11CS** and deepening the three Western battles (Chickamauga → 3 CS, Vicksburg → 2 CS, Shiloh → 2 CS) per the standing "theater balance favors more Western/CS records" note (Aaron picked this lane over a `vet:noreg` batch/release checkpoint by popup). Data + probe only — no engine/combat change, D74 intact, the standalone/sandbox path byte-identical.

- **The three records (`data/soldier-replacements.json`):**
  - **Val C. Giles** → `ss:chickamauga:CS:cs_law_gap:nco`. Sergeant (4th Sergeant from 1 May 1863), Company B ("Tom Green Rifles"), 4th Texas Infantry, Robertson's Texas Brigade, Hood's Division (Law commanding 20 Sep), Longstreet's Corps on loan to the Army of Tennessee, at Chickamauga 19-20 Sep 1863 (Robertson's brigade broke the Federal line near the Brotherton Farm on the second day). Author of the memoir *Rags and Hope* (ed. Mary Lasswell, 1961); captured at Wauhatchie 29 Oct 1863; recalled Chickamauga as "the meanest, most unsatisfactory place I struck during the whole war." **Rank honesty:** ships at Sergeant, his documented Chickamauga grade — Wikipedia's 4th-Texas article quotes "Sergeant Val Giles" on the field, so the `:nco` slot is neither inflation nor deflation; no portrait.
  - **William Pitt Chambers** → `ss:vicksburg:CS:cs_smith_stockade:nco`. Sergeant (rose from private to first sergeant), Company B, 46th Mississippi Infantry, Baldwin's Brigade, Martin L. Smith's Division, in the Vicksburg garrison. A Covington County schoolteacher (b. 14 Dec 1839, d. 1916); enlisted 26 Mar 1862 with the "Covington Rebels," briefly the 6th Mississippi, then Company B of the 46th Mississippi after Oct 1862; his journal *Blood and Sacrifice* (Blue Acorn Press, 1994) runs from Shiloh through the Vicksburg siege. The **second Vicksburg Confederate** record (after Tunnard). **Honesty:** does NOT claim the 46th personally held the Stockade Redan salient itself — its parent Smith's Division held that northern sector (the salient proper was garrisoned by other troops); ships his first-sergeant grade as "Sergeant" (transparently disclosed in the sourceNote — a within-NCO generalization, not a deflation across the officer/NCO line); no portrait.
  - **Sam Houston Jr.** → `ss:shiloh:CS:cs_bragg_corps:pvt`. Private, Company C ("Bayland Guards"), 2nd Texas Infantry, Jackson's Brigade, Withers' Division, Bragg's Corps, at Shiloh (b. 25 May 1843, d. 3 May 1894). Wounded and left for dead on the second day (7 April 1862); a Bible in his pocket, inscribed by his mother Margaret, stopped a second ball; a Union clergyman who found the Bible and her letter had him carried off; POW ~6 months incl. Camp Douglas, then exchanged and medically discharged. Eldest son of the Unionist governor **Sam Houston**, removed from office for refusing the Confederate loyalty oath. Placed for his documented 2nd Texas service, NOT his famous father; echoes the war's fluid loyalties beside the Stanley (D289) record. **Honesty:** ships his enlisted Private rank (no inflation); the dramatic Bible/left-for-dead details are attributed to the cited sources, not asserted; the wound-day is pinned to 7 April per the regimental account with a bio hedge that some accounts place it on the first day; no portrait.
- **Slot-fit / open-slot enumeration:** a harness over the nine battle OOBs (replicating the T0 `_ssCollectScenarioUnits`/`_ssUnitSpecs` slot derivation `ss:<battle>:<side>:<uid>:<cmd|nco|pvt>`) enumerated the open CS Western slots; all three `replacePid` targets were confirmed OPEN and correctly typed (nco/nco/pvt matching each soldier's documented grade), and the importer independently re-validates that each targets a real generated row. cs_law_gap (Chickamauga, Hood's/Law's Division), cs_smith_stockade (Vicksburg, Smith's Division), and cs_bragg_corps (Shiloh, Bragg's Corps) were chosen to deepen the three Western theaters.
- **Process note (D289/D290 lesson APPLIED):** every load-bearing citation was independently verified IN THE MAIN LOOP via inline `WebSearch`/`WebFetch` (TSHA "Fourth/Second Texas Infantry", Wikipedia 4th/2nd Texas, Emerging Civil War Chickamauga OOB, NPS "46th Mississippi Infantry", USM finding aid M214, TSHA "Houston Sam Jr.", Wikipedia "Sam Houston Jr.") rather than parking the main loop on a background research workflow — the D289 hang (a stalled per-agent `WebFetch`) is avoided by owning the fetches inline. Key facts corrected DURING verification: Giles's rank was raised from a planned `:pvt` to `:nco` once his 4th-Sergeant grade was confirmed (honest, not deflating), and Houston's wounding was pinned to Shiloh's second day by the 2nd Texas regimental account. The adversarial panel ran only as a read-only background cross-check with a bounded wait — this time it completed cleanly (no hang, no args mis-feed, because each lens prompt embedded the full record text rather than passing `args`).
- **Gate (focused, D176):** importer validate 25/25 Verified · `node tools/build.mjs` GATE OK (citations ✓, women-in-war ✓) · `node --check` probe clean · **probe-loot-survival ok=true 11/11, applied=25, hostileRejected=true, 0 pageerrors** (the D290 "twenty-two" step retitled → "D291 twenty-five"; counts bumped 22→25; three new per-record verification blocks added — Giles/Chambers/Houston: alias lookup + generated/replacement/provenance/name + rank/side/branch/team + bio substrings + front-loaded sourceNote caveats + sources.length≥4 + no-portrait) · adjacents probe-tactical-roster 8/8 + probe-women-in-war lane-separation green (replacementRecords:25, lane still separate; the probe reads the count dynamically so no edit was needed) · `git diff --check` clean. Deliverable diff = the roster-data embed only, no engine/combat edit. All three sourceNotes trimmed to ≤360 chars so the runtime's `_lootCleanText(...,360)` display clip cuts nothing (the D289 clip lesson).
- **Adversarial fact-check (read-only default-refute Opus ×3, `wf_a1ac5e1b-4c9` — read-only lenses only, the D281 race lesson): 9/9 SHIP, 0 FIX, 0 BLOCK.** The HONESTY, ACCURACY, and SLOT-CONSISTENCY lenses each returned SHIP for all three records — every rank matches the documented grade with no inflation/deflation across the officer/NCO/private line, every unit genuinely belongs in its slot's brigade/division/corps, no portrait asserted, and the caveats are accurate (notably Chambers's Stockade-Redan division-vs-regiment disclaimer, which the panel confirmed is historically correct — the salient proper was Shoup's Louisianans, not Baldwin's brigade). Two minor advisories, both handled: Chambers ships "first sergeant" as generic "Sergeant" (all three lenses judged this non-inflating and *already disclosed* — no change), and Houston's 7 April wounding is contested in the literature (ACCURACY lens) → **advisory adopted before commit**, a bio hedge added ("some accounts place his wounding on the first day"); re-gated GREEN after the edit (GATE OK · probe-loot-survival 11/11 · 0 pe).
- **Decide-&-log (not surfaced):** each record is bulletproof, narrow, honesty-caveated, and maps to a real unreplaced generated slot; all three are CS Western to serve the standing theater-balance mandate. Haley (US Gettysburg) stays held because a US record worsens the side balance this lane is correcting. Full detail: RUN-LOG D291 · V1-CHECKLIST Phase I · SOLDIER-REPLACEMENT-FORMAT.

## D290 — PHASE I SOLDIER'S STORY SCALE-OUT: THREE NEW CITATION-GRADE REPLACEMENTS (20-22), A CS-WEIGHTED THEATER-BALANCING SLICE — BERRY BENSON (CS Chancellorsville), JOHN W. "JOHNNY" GREEN (CS Chickamauga, the Orphan Brigade), and WILLIAM H. TUNNARD (CS Vicksburg — the FIRST Vicksburg Confederate record) — [CLAUDE Opus 4.8, Priority-1; web-grounded research + read-only default-refute Opus fact-check panel `wf_e9626494-e91`] (2026-07-07)

The Soldier's Story replacement lane grows from nineteen to **twenty-two** `Verified` records. All three are Confederate, lifting side balance from 14US/5CS to **14US/8CS** and spanning three theaters (Eastern + two Western) to answer the standing "theater balance favors more Western/CS records" note. Data + probe only — no engine/combat change, D74 intact, the standalone/sandbox path byte-identical.

- **The three records (`data/soldier-replacements.json`):**
  - **Berry Benson** → `ss:chancellorsville:CS:cs_ap_hill_div:nco`. Corporal, Company H, 1st South Carolina Infantry, McGowan's Brigade (Gregg-McGowan), A. P. Hill's Light Division, Jackson's Second Corps, at Chancellorsville (wounded in the leg, missed Gettysburg). The celebrated Confederate scout/sharpshooter who later tunneled out of Elmira prison. **Rank honesty:** shipped at Corporal, bracketed — the New Georgia Encyclopedia gives corporal at enlistment; American Rifleman dates his promotion to sergeant to *after* his late-1864 capture — so May 1863 = corporal. No formal sharpshooter-battalion claim at Chancellorsville (McGowan's battalion was organized in 1864); no portrait. The prompt's held candidate, now shipped with the 2nd rank source confirmed (American Rifleman + the Light-Division OOB page beyond NGE).
  - **John W. "Johnny" Green** → `ss:chickamauga:CS:cs_breck_rock:nco`. An enlisted man of the 9th Kentucky Infantry, the Kentucky "Orphan Brigade" (Helm's), Breckinridge's Division, D. H. Hill's Corps, Polk's Right Wing, at Chickamauga (20 Sep 1863, the assault where Helm was killed). Entered service 4 Oct 1861 and rose to the regiment's Sergeant-Major; his journal *Johnny Green of the Orphan Brigade* is a valued common-soldier account. **Rank honesty:** his exact grade at Chickamauga is undocumented; he is placed in the NCO (`:nco`) slot at "Sergeant" because he demonstrably rose to regimental Sergeant-Major (a `:pvt` slot would risk DEFLATING a documented NCO, forbidden like inflation — the D92/Dooley principle), with the sergeant-major-at-the-battle claim explicitly NOT asserted; company not identified; no portrait.
  - **William H. Tunnard** → `ss:vicksburg:CS:cs_3rd_louisiana:nco`. Sergeant, Company K (the Pelican Rifles origin), 3rd Louisiana Infantry, Hebert's Brigade, Forney's Division, in the Vicksburg garrison; his regiment held the 3rd Louisiana Redan (the salient the Union twice mined, 25 June and 1 July 1863); captured at Pemberton's 4 July surrender. Author of *A Southern Record* (1866), one of the most valued Confederate regimental histories. The **FIRST Vicksburg Confederate** record (Vicksburg had 1 US, 0 CS). **Honesty:** does not claim Tunnard was personally in a mine crater — he is a sergeant of the regiment that held the redan; his sergeant rank is corroborated (Wikipedia dates it by Iuka, Sept 1862, before Vicksburg); no portrait.
- **Slot-fit / open-slot enumeration:** a harness over the nine battle OOBs enumerated 612 generated slots (593 open before this slice). All three `replacePid` targets were confirmed open and correctly typed (nco/nco/nco); the importer independently re-validates that each targets a real generated row in the live registry. Vicksburg-CS and a second Chickamauga-CS were chosen deliberately to fill the emptiest theaters (Vicksburg had zero CS; the West was under-represented).
- **Process note (D289 lesson APPLIED):** every load-bearing citation was independently re-fetched in the MAIN LOOP (NGE, American Rifleman, Wikipedia Tunnard, UPK, UA Press, Orphan Brigade / A. P. Hill's Light Division / 3rd Louisiana / Chickamauga-Confederate-OOB Wikipedia, Genealogy Trails) rather than parking on a background research workflow — the D289 hang (a stalled per-agent `WebFetch`) is avoided by owning the fetches inline. The adversarial panel ran as a read-only background cross-check with a bounded wait, not as the primary verification.
- **Gate (focused, D176):** import gate `--check` 3/3 then `--import --write` merged to 22/22 Verified · `node tools/build.mjs` GATE OK (citations ✓, women-in-war ✓) · `node --check` probe clean · **probe-loot-survival ok=true 11/11, applied=22, hostileRejected=true, 0 pageerrors** (the D289 "nineteen" step retitled → "D290 twenty-two"; counts bumped 19→22; three new per-record verification blocks added — rank/side/unit/bio/front-loaded-caveats/no-portrait) · adjacents probe-tactical-roster 8/8 + probe-women-in-war lane-separation green (replacementRecords:22, lane still separate) · `git diff --check` clean. Deliverable diff = the roster-data embed only (+233/−2), no engine/combat edit.
- **Adversarial fact-check (read-only default-refute Opus ×3, `wf_e9626494-e91` — read-only lenses only, the D281 race lesson; + a clean single-agent accuracy re-run):** the HONESTY lens returned all 3 SHIP (Benson's rank bracket verified against NGE; Green's NCO-slot placement judged HONEST; Tunnard's Vicksburg facts confirmed). The SLOT-CONSISTENCY lens returned Benson/Tunnard SHIP (Tunnard the strongest — a direct regiment-to-slot match) and Green FIX — the ONE substantive question, that Green's undocumented Chickamauga grade could make `:nco` a mild inflation if he was still a private. **Addressed:** multiple sources consistently record Green "promoted for bravery at Shiloh (April 1862), and rose to Sergeant-Major," placing his NCO status ~17 months before Chickamauga; the caveat was strengthened to state this and to note a `:pvt` slot would DEFLATE a documented NCO. The workflow's ACCURACY lens mis-received args (it fact-checked the already-shipped D289 records 17-19), so it was **re-run cleanly as a single read-only Opus agent on the final text**: Green SHIP, Tunnard SHIP, and Benson FIX — one minor, source-contradicted bio clause ("promoted to sergeant only after his late-1864 capture") — American Rifleman actually dates his capture to Spotsylvania in **May 1864** (mid-year) and the promotion to his **late-1864 return** from escaping Elmira. **FIXED before commit** (bio + sourceNote reworded); no BLOCK-level errors in any record. Panel advisories all adopted before commit: Benson caveat margin tightened (the "no portrait" clip position), Green tier justification strengthened, Tunnard period-source hedge + "sector of the redan" precision added. Re-gated GREEN after every edit (GATE OK · probe-loot-survival 11/11 · 0 pageerrors).
- **Decide-&-log (not surfaced):** each record is bulletproof, narrow, honesty-caveated, and maps to a real unreplaced generated slot; all three are CS to serve the theater-balance mandate. Benson was the prompt's held candidate; Haley (US Gettysburg) stays held because a US record worsens the side balance this slice is correcting. Full detail: RUN-LOG D290 · V1-CHECKLIST Phase I · SOLDIER-REPLACEMENT-FORMAT.
