- **DEFERRED-&-LOGGED (the rest of §12, mirrors Q7→R-5 / Q8→Q8b):** the **symmetric AI-GM** (the opponent drafts/promotes — needs enemy-commander→combat wiring; would break byte-identity / risk fudge), cross-theater **Transfer** (needs authored theaters — `generals.json` has none), **Commission** of generated officers + the full corps/division **depth-chart tree** (needs the strategic↔tactical OOB mapping — the Q8b blocker family). This slice ships the player-side promotion economy now; the loop proceeds once those substrates exist.
- **Adversarial bug-hunt** (7 Opus agents: 4 diverse-lens finders × default-refute verify + a completeness critic; 2 raw → **2 confirmed + 1 actionable critic gap, ALL LOW, ALL latent**; 0 HIGH/MED; critic overall: *"SOUND and may be committed — the no-fudge wall, byte-identity, bounding, determinism, and no-exploit invariants all hold; no HIGH/MED missed."*). ALL 3 fixed: **(1)** `cmdPromote` gated on `Math.round(capital)` but spent from raw `capital` → a fractional balance could pass the rounded gate and be floor-absorbed on the spend → now debits the same rounded `cap` (byte-identical under every current integer-only capital path). **(2)** `_cmdBaseGrade`'s unrecognized-rank fallback was "Maj. Gen." (rankBase 68) → would over-grade a future non-named officer (e.g. a Colonel, 64) two grades onto the General ladder → now defaults to the LOWEST grade "Brig. Gen." (unreachable on the shipped surface; hardens the deferred depth-chart path). **(3, critic)** `cmdInit` re-seeded `seniority` only when invalid → a valid-but-out-of-band tampered/stale save value wasn't re-clamped until the next resolve → now clamps to the cap on LOAD. The critic's other gap (the two `diag-classic` 404s) is the **pre-existing documented tolerated asset-layer 404 noise**, not Q9 (Q9 touches no asset paths). Re-vetted after the fixes.
- **Vetting:** GATE OK (no-fudge ✓); `probe-command` **25/25** (16 prior + **9 new Q9 steps**: seniority seeded + empty promotions [byte-identical default]; the save-tamper LOAD re-clamp; the grade-ladder parse [Lee=General / Grant=Lt. Gen. / Meade & Stuart=Maj. Gen.]; Lee [top grade] not promotable; promotion GATED when either currency is short + SPENDS both + stores the grade; EARNED raises rep / ABOVE-MERIT costs more capital + cuts rep; a promotion lifts effective skill [bounded ≤6] → reaches commandLeadership, un-promoted byte-identical; every general both sides reads the SAME skill with/without C; the render surfaces the section + currencies + a Promote control), 0 pe; **byte-identity harness IDENTICAL for all 10** vs committed HEAD `fc1d251` + the Q9 live sanity (a promotion stores "Lt. Gen.", lifts commandLeadership 79→80 [bounded], renders the section, un-promoted reads identical, 0 pe); `probe-ratings` 19/19 + `probe-desk` 13/13 + `probe-camp` 8/8 + `t1probe` 14/14 (0 pe each); `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`; `bootprobe` ok; `wcag-auditor` 0 AA failures (fixed the new warning text `#c2502e`→`#d66040`, 4.51:1 measured against the real dark `.sheet` ground). **ENV:** the `load` suite stalled intermittently on the THREE-CDN headless load this session (the documented gotcha — cleared by a fresh threaded server + retry); the `domcontentloaded` byte-identity harness remains the authoritative no-regression proof. Next: a follow-up GM increment (the symmetric AI-GM + Transfer/Commission + the depth-chart tree) once the enemy-command + OOB-mapping substrates exist; or **Q8b SCOUTING**; then **R-6** negative-badge sweep + the catalog-wide badge-provenance sweep + live dev-traits. (Reversible; design law RATING-SYSTEM-DESIGN §12.2/§14.3/§15.)

## D104 — RATING SYSTEM **R-6 (the badge ASSIGNMENT to the 9 shipped rosters)**: the rating system finally BITES in the historical battles — [OPUS] (overnight run, 2026-06-22)
Now that the catalog is citation-clean (D103), the documented commander/unit traits are **assigned to the 9 shipped battles' brigade tokens** — the first time the rating system changes combat in the real battles (it was byte-identical-when-unassigned since R-3). **43 assignments** keyed by scenario→unit in a central `data/ratings.json` `rosterBadges` map; the seam stamps them onto units via `fldBrSpec`→`fldMakeUnit`; the existing R-3/R-4 engine (`fldBadgeFactor` static factor + `fldXFactorStep` surge, gated on `__FIELD.badges`, per-lever-capped) does the rest. **Honors D74/D92: ACCURATE INPUTS, never an output gate** — a documented trait nudges the universal levers (rally/fire/speed/melee) and the result EMERGES; build-gate 4d still forbids the rating module writing cas/winner/men/sev.
- **Design (the ultracode citation pattern):** a 10-agent Workflow — one Opus historian per battle proposing **source-cited** badge→unit assignments + a cross-battle **anti-Lost-Cause / flip-risk critic**. The critic's 5 required edits were applied (add `the_slows` at Antietam · move `rock_of_chickamauga` to the right Chickamauga phase · drop `bayonet` from a repulsed Fredericksburg attacker · trim a duplicate Vicksburg `stonewall` · trim a duplicate Chancellorsville `foot_cavalry`). **Anti-Lost-Cause balance (after the D104 bug-hunt trims): CS 12 clear virtues : 6 flaws : 4 double-edged; US 11 virtues : 10 flaws** — the Union's command flaws (McClellan `the_slows`, Burnside `rigid_plan`, McClernand/Sherman `piecemeal`, green/surprised troops) are named as plainly as the Confederacy's virtues; the CS flaws (Bragg/Magruder/D.H. Hill `piecemeal`, green Western corps) too.
- **`the_slows` (decide-&-log):** `data/antietam.json` has NO McClellan army token (only corps/division), so per D92 it is NOT forced onto every division — it sits on `us_sturgis` (the P2 Burnside's-Bridge assault, the battle's iconic delayed/hesitant Union attack). **`grand_charge`** sits on `cs_johnson_gap` (Chickamauga — the breakthrough that SUCCEEDED) and was DECLINED on Pickett's repulsed charge (flip-risk). **SHILOH retune (the A/B sweep):** the headless single-phase Shiloh baseline is a ~tossup (US ~3/5 badges-off); a rally penalty on the green Union defenders flipped it to CS (against history), so the Union's caught-napping flaw is carried by the SCENARIO (the CS surprise attack) + teaching cards, NOT a combat penalty; the decisive Hornet's-Nest STAND (Prentiss `rock_of_chickamauga` + W.H.L. Wallace `stonewall`) is modeled instead → US ~4/5.
- **New: an accessible badge-chip HUD** (`fldRatingBadgesHtml`, wired into `fldRenderHud`) — each trait a triple-encoded chip (rung glyph + ±polarity sign + label + aria-label); `wcag-auditor` PASS (contrast 5.51–12.53:1, CVD-safe, no fixes). **`probe-ratings` +1 R-6 step** (rosterBadges resolves to real defs + every unit-id resolves in ALL 9 scenarios [rot guard] + the seam stamps initial-OOB + reinforcement units + byte-identical-when-off + anti-Lost-Cause balance FLOORS + chips). **Engine seams:** `fldBrSpec`/`fldMakeUnit` copy `badges` (a `.slice()`, null when absent → byte-identical); `fldScenarioRosterBadges` reads the central map (fresh array, never aliases canonical data).
- **A/B sweep (deterministic, sandbox + 9 battles × seeds):** **byte-identity OFF == committed HEAD `610ae6c` 30/30** (the seam is inert when off); badges-ON shifts CASUALTIES but essentially never the VERDICT — every phased battle's per-phase winner byte-identical off→on, and the ONLY single-phase winner flip is TOWARD the historical winner (Chancellorsville seed 1 US-timeout→CS-hold = Jackson's flank march = Lee's victory). **NO flip against history.** Casualty deltas bounded + emergent (e.g. Fredericksburg's flawed Union assault bleeds more — historically apt). **All 9 per-battle probes PASS with badges ON** (the authoritative direction check): Antietam CS/US/CS 8/8, Fredericksburg CS 8/8, Gettysburg day1 CS / day2 US / day3 US 8/8 (Pickett repulsed), Vicksburg assaults-fail / mine-US 8/8, Chickamauga breakthrough + Rock-holds 8/8, Shiloh 29/29, Chancellorsville 24/24, Malvern 24/24, Bull Run ok.
- **Adversarial bug-hunt (16 Opus agents: 5 diverse lenses [no-fudge · byte-identity · accuracy · exploit · probe/doc] × default-refute verify + a completeness critic; 10 raw → 8 confirmed + 3 critic-missed, ALL LOW/MED, NONE blocking; critic: "SAFE TO COMMIT — both hard invariants [no output gate, byte-identical-when-off] re-verified intact").** Fixed: `grand_charge`'s note overclaimed a modeled "doomed double-edge" downside that doesn't exist → corrected to upside-only/deferred (MED); the probe only checked bullrun1 unit-ids → now resolves all 9 scenarios (MED); a latent `_spdMul` reset gap when a non-speed X-Factor activates (LOW code); `cs_colston_div`'s `stonewall` was a role mismatch (Colston was ATTACKING in the flank march) → removed, teaching rides `cs_rodes_div` (LOW); the `_rosterNote` honesty pass (dual-badge tally bucket + the always-on R-3 static-trigger disclosure + the flip framing). **Deferred-&-logged (no live defect):** the bounded morale-cost downside lever for dual X-Factors (grand_charge); per-situation gating of the R-3 static triggers (currently always-on, small+capped, net validated historically faithful) — a future R-7 refinement; the dead Custom-Battle inline-`d.badges` branch (forward-compatible, no UI yet).
- **VETTING (PROPER VETTING gate, all GREEN — fought through a memory-pressure env):** `node tools/build.mjs` **GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓**; the **`domcontentloaded` byte-identity harness IDENTICAL all 30** (sandbox + 9 battles × 3 seeds) badges-OFF vs committed HEAD `610ae6c` + **0 winner flips** (the seam inert when off) + bounded badges-ON casualty deltas; **all 9 per-battle probes PASS with badges ON** (the authoritative direction check above); **`probe-ratings` 21/21** with the new R-6 step (43 assignments · unit-ids resolve in ALL 9 scenarios · balance floors · stamping incl. reinforcements · byte-identical-off · chips); the 3 layer-isolation fixes **`probe-presets` 26 · `probe-arms` 23 · `probe-logistics` 14** (each adds `_badgesOff` to isolate its layer from the now-default-on badges, restoring the pristine CS-5/8 / [4,6] HEAD baseline — re-confirmed green); `wcag-auditor` clean (no fixes); `diag-classic nonBlank:346`; `bootprobe` ok; 0 pageerrors throughout. **ENV NOTE:** the 8 GB Mac was memory-starved this session (~400 MB free; the user's VS Code + Chrome + Codeium + Claude Code held the RAM) so headless-Chrome probes crashed/stalled intermittently (the documented load-stall + the user-CLAUDE.md "memory pressure" constraint) — every probe above was confirmed by retrying until a memory window opened (the `domcontentloaded` A/B harness was the reliable spine). Committed + pushed only after the full empirical gate passed. (Reversible; non-negotiables honored: D74/D92 no-output-gate + the empirical-vet-before-push gate — START-HERE / CLAUDE.md.)

## D103 — RATING SYSTEM **R-6 (citation-provenance portion)**: the CATALOG-WIDE citation hardening + a permanent build-gate — [OPUS] (overnight run, 2026-06-22)
The R-6 increment's **negative-badge ASSIGNMENT to rosters + live dev-traits remain**; this milestone shipped the **citation-grade prerequisite** that gates them: every `Verified`-stamped claim in the data catalog now carries **≥2 independent real sources**, the non-negotiable made un-erodable by the build. **A live violation was found and closed:** an audit revealed the debt was NOT confined to badges (the logged D98–D101 framing) — **47 records across SIX data files** were stamped `Verified` with <2 sources (badges 13 · personas 2 · weapons 9 · artillery 9 · engineering 8 · terrain 1 · cabinet 5). PURE METADATA + a little teaching-card text — **byte-identical sim** (all 10 deterministic battles IDENTICAL to committed HEAD `5b12916`; no numeric combat field touched, bug-hunt-confirmed).
- **The citation workflow (the ultracode pattern):** a 15-agent research+verify Workflow (7 domains: per-domain Opus finders → **default-refute** adversarial verifiers → a completeness critic; sources **web-checked**; discipline = NEVER fabricate, **downgrade-to-Inferred over guess**). Outcome: **0 fabrication** (critic-confirmed), all citations real/on-claim/independent. Two judgment calls logged: **`beloved` held *Inferred*** (its one solid cite is Symonds 1997 on Cleburne; the obvious Lee source — Freeman 1934 — is Lost-Cause-tinged and held out until a non-hagiographic Lee cite is added), and **`grand_charge` upgraded *Verified*** (Stewart 1959 · Hess 2001 · Sears 2003, satisfying its in-data "Inferred until the sweep" note). Anti-Lost-Cause cut both ways: `feared`'s second cite was swapped Wyeth 1899 (Lost-Cause-era hagiography) → **Hurst 1993** (modern critical bio); the NEGATIVE badges on real men carry the firmest pairs (Sears+Rafuse, Marvel+O'Reilly, Cozzens+Daniel, McMurry+Sword).
- **The permanent gate (`tools/build.mjs` 4e, the keystone):** a recursive scan of every `data/*.json` — any record whose `prov`/`provenance` is "Verified" (matched **trim+case-normalized on EITHER key**, so a whitespace/case variant or an `Inferred`-masks-`Verified` pairing cannot erode it) with a `sources`/`src` array of <2 → `die(5)`. **Verified firing on all 47 before the fix; passes after** (`GATE OK · … · citations ✓`). The long descriptive forms ("Verified specs (…)", "Verified (high) — …", ~72 of them) cite inline in prose and trim-lowercase to ≠ "verified", so they stay out of the structured gate **by design**. Mirrors the 4d no-fudge wall: a non-negotiable made structural.
- **Accuracy fixes folded in (critic-surfaced, false-claims-under-Verified):** the Fredericksburg `cover-killing-ground` card's "almost impregnable, and a deathtrap" was **misattributed to the NPS** (the phrase traces to a fan site) → paraphrased, no false attribution; `massed_battery`'s note dropped "Pelham" (Fredericksburg, not covered by its Gettysburg sources); `earned_in_blood` tightened to "14 of the 16 Medals of Honor awarded to Black soldiers (Army)"; the Springfield "~1.5M" and three art-cs-handicap dates softened to match the sourced precision; the Whitworth flavor reconciled.
- **Surface/probe/doc:** `probe-ratings` +1 step (the catalog-wide ≥2-source invariant + the beloved/grand_charge/the_slows assertions + a badgeDefs count pin ≥26) — 20/20; `RATING-SYSTEM-DESIGN.md` §4/§9 synced (sweep done · the gate · the two judgment calls). Player-visible upside: the engineering/cabinet/teaching cards that render their provenance now display the real citations.
- **Adversarial bug-hunt** (34 Opus agents: 4 diverse-lens finders [gate-logic · data-integrity · citation-soundness · probe/doc] × default-refute verify + a completeness critic; **16 confirmed, ALL LOW except 1 MED, all latent**; critic verdict: *"R-6 is SAFE TO COMMIT — every citation web-verified real, no fabrication, gate green, byte-identity proven."*). Fixed now: the **MED** (gate strict-equality evaded by "Verified "/case variants) + the `||`-masking LOW → the normalized both-key matcher above (synthetic test 8/8); the doc "47 records" count corrected; the terrain co-attribution + feared-Wyeth optics. **Deferred-&-logged** (no live offender): adding the singular `source` prose key to the gate; a distinct-author independence check (every record already has ≥2 distinct authors); polishing the other negative-badge second sources (all defensible as-is).
- **Vetting:** `GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓` (hardened); the gate matcher synthetic test **8/8**; the **`domcontentloaded` byte-identity harness IDENTICAL for all 10** vs committed HEAD `5b12916` + an R-6 live citation sanity (catalog clean, 0 offenders; the_slows V/2, beloved I/1, grand_charge V/3, materiel + cabinet sourced; 0 pe); `probe-ratings` **20/20**, `probe-engineering-corps` 22/22, `probe-cabinet` 12/12, `probe-weapons` 8/8, `probe-cannon` 9/9 (retry — the documented `load` stall), `probe-arms` 23/23, `probe-decisions` 14/14 (0 pe each); `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`. **PROCESS NOTE:** a workflow research agent applied the 5 cabinet edits to `data/cabinet.json` DIRECTLY (it should only have returned data); reviewed — the edits exactly match the verified output + cabinet's format, so accepted rather than reverted. Next: the negative-badge ASSIGNMENT to rosters (the rest of R-6, now citation-clean) + live dev-traits; or the follow-up GM increment / Q8b. (Reversible; non-negotiable: citation-grade ≥2-source Verified — START-HERE / CLAUDE.md.)

## D105 — RATING SYSTEM **LIVE DEV-TRAITS (the Madden development arc)**: a general's OVR now develops over the campaign, shaped by a hidden potential ceiling — [OPUS] (overnight run, 2026-06-22)
The last open item on the R-6 rating track (RATING-SYSTEM-DESIGN §9-R-6: "reputation level-up → OVR still TODO") + the D94-fork-#5 lock ("Reputation→OVR dev-traits = LIVE in-campaign now"). `cmdOnResolve` already evolved a general's reputation by battle outcome (decisive win +6 / win +3 / draw −1 / loss −4 / decisive loss −8), and `_cmdGenRating` (0.55·skill + 0.45·reputation) → `commandLeadership` already read it — so winning a campaign already nudged his OVR. D105 adds the **Madden DEV-TRAIT** that SHAPES that arc: a hidden potential **ceiling** (how high he can climb), a **floor** (how far he can fall), a development **rate** (rise vs fall), and an **attritionDrag** (the §15-R3 decline-with-heavy-attrition). **ACCURATE INPUTS, never an output gate (D74/D92)** — a dev-trait only shapes the general's REPUTATION (an INPUT the existing pipe consumes), it NEVER writes a casualty/winner. Design law: RATING-SYSTEM-DESIGN §9-R-6 / §14.6 / §15-R3.
- **The keystone (byte-identical-when-off, the R-6/D104 pattern):** the engine seam in `cmdOnResolve` is **byte-identical for an UNASSIGNED general (or absent devTraits data)** — it takes the LITERAL pre-D105 path `clamp(cur+delta, 5, 98)`, rate 1, no drag (proven across ALL five outcome quadrants + both clamp rails). The deliberate, citation-grade, historically-directed change is the AUTHORED `data/ratings.json` `devTraits.assign` map (20 generals → one of 12 archetypes). The ceiling/floor are RELATIVE to the general's SEEDED start reputation (`devTrack.start`, fixed), every archetype keeps `ceilingAbove≥5 & floorBelow≥5`, so the pre-D105 "a winning general gains reputation / a losing one loses it" invariant holds for all.
- **The arc (in `cmdOnResolve`):** an assigned general's base delta is scaled by his `riseRate`/`fallRate`, plus a bounded extra `attritionDrag` when his side bled the lion's share (casualty SHARE > pivot — the D100 unit-correct measure), then clamped to HIS `[floor,ceiling]` band. New pure fns (`src/35-command.js`): `_cmdDevCfg`/`_cmdDevStart`/`_cmdDevTrait` (resolves the absolute band) / `_cmdAttritionDrag` (bounded ≤ attritionDragMax) / `_cmdDevTrackUpdate` (pure observation) / `_cmdDevTrend` / `_cmdCareerArcHTML`. `cmdInit` seeds + sanitizes `cmd.devTrack[id]={start,peak,low,battles}` (rides the save, no `_SAVE_VER` bump). NO RNG.
- **The 12 archetypes + anti-Lost-Cause balance:** rising_star (Grant/Sherman/Sheridan) · steady_rock (Thomas) · established_legend (Lee/Jackson) · natural_genius (Forrest) · steady_professional (Meade/Scott→administrator/Longstreet/A.S.Johnston) · cautious_fabian (J.E.Johnston) · mercurial_cavalier (Stuart/Beauregard) · administrator (Scott/Halleck) · plateau (McClellan) · overpromoted (Burnside/Hooker) · volatile_aggressor (Hood) · quarrelsome (Bragg). **The thesis is anti-Lost-Cause:** the Union's elite are RISERS (4 high-ceiling developers rated as high as Lee/Jackson — the honest claim that the Union found and elevated its best command) while command failures are named plainly on BOTH sides (US McClellan plateau / Burnside-Hooker overpromoted; CS Bragg quarrelsome / Hood volatile-disaster). All 20 assignments are stamped **Inferred** (a calibrated reading of a documented arc — gate-4e-exempt) with 1-2 real sources each; nothing laundered (Forrest's Klan + Longstreet's Lost-Cause slander named in the notes).
- **The read-out (`_cmdCareerArcHTML`, a new Command-desk "Career Arc" section):** the sitting general's dev-trait tier (label + glyph + polarity sign + desc), his OVR arc (start→now→peak on a floor..ceiling band with start/peak ticks), a trend verdict (Rising/Holding/Fading/Untested) + the net delta, and a soft potential-room note. Triple-encoded (word + sign + number; glyph + colour decorative) → CVD-safe; `wcag-auditor` AA (one nested-opacity spot 3.93→4.57:1 fixed).
- **The no-fudge wall extended (bug-hunt):** build-gate 4d (the no-fudge OUTPUT WALL) now also scans `src/35-command.js` (confirmed it writes ONLY command state — reputation/seniority/capital/devTrack, none in the forbidden set) — closing the coverage gap so the strategic rating code's no-fudge invariant is STRUCTURAL too (mirrors how D103's gate 4e made citations un-erodable).
- **Adversarial bug-hunt** (the ultracode pattern — 5 diverse-lens Opus finders [no-fudge · byte-identity · bounding/determinism · citation/balance · UI/probe] × default-refute Opus verify + a completeness critic; 29 agents; **8 confirmed + 3 critic-missed; 1 HIGH, 3 MED, 7 LOW; critic: "SAFE TO COMMIT — both hard invariants [no output gate, byte-identical-when-off] CONFIRMED HOLD"** via independent grep corroboration that `devTrack`/`reputation` are read by no combat code). ALL 11 fixed: **(HIGH, the only blocker)** a fabricated-by-misattribution Thomas citation ("Conquered into Liberty" is Eliot Cohen's, not Larry Daniel's) → corrected to Daniel's actual *Days of Glory: The Army of the Cumberland* (CLAUDE.md non-negotiable #4); **(MED)** the Burnside note "proved right at Fredericksburg" laundered a Union command failure → rewritten to name the futile Marye's-Heights assaults plainly (anti-Lost-Cause); **(MED, the keystone hardening)** `_cmdDevTrait`/`_cmdDevStart` consumed `devTrack.start` RAW → a tampered/stale save (start NaN / 500) poisoned the reputation clamp → now coerced through `_cmdNum` + clamped [0,100] at the read site, `cmdInit` SANITIZES the whole record on LOAD (the Q9 re-clamp pattern), and `_cmdCareerArcHTML` coerces the whole `tr` record (no "NaN" can render); **(MED→the gate gap above);** **(LOW ×7)** byte-identical-off probe now covers all 5 quadrants + both clamp rails; the off-test asserts `devTraits.assign` is restored (no global-state leak); floor/drag steps assert the locked general's trait; the render step covers all 4 `_cmdDevTrend` branches + the battles=0 edge; the new SAVE-TAMPER probe step (start NaN/500/−99 → finite + in-band + no NaN render); the balance check counts named-failures as (−,~) so it doesn't rest on the single cs-bragg record.
- **Vetting (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK · no-fudge ✓` (4d now scans 35-command.js) `· citations ✓` (the Thomas fix); `probe-command` **34/34** (9 D105 steps: data+balance · byte-identical-off all-quadrants+rails · assign-restored · rising-star-vs-plateau+ceiling · floor cap · attrition drag bounded · no-output-gate+devTrack-purity · save-tamper · render+4 trend branches); `probe-ratings` **21/21** (the tactical rating engine + the 9-battle badge resolution intact — D105 touches NO tactical code, byte-identical by construction, confirmed by `git diff`), `probe-desk` 13/13, `probe-camp` 8/8, `probe-officers` 15/15, `t1probe` 14/14, `bootprobe` ok; `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`; 0 pageerrors throughout; `wcag-auditor` AA. **ENV:** the 8 GB Mac had a healthy memory window this session; probes ran foreground via a threaded server (the documented load-stall gotcha did not bite). Committed + pushed only after the full empirical gate passed. (Reversible; non-negotiables honored: D74/D92 no-output-gate + the empirical-vet-before-push gate.)

## D284 — LLM-OPPONENT SLICE 1 SHIPPED: THE T27 ENGINE SEAM IS LIVE, ZERO NETWORK — per-launch state + the PURE fog-respecting band-quantized digest + the deterministic validation wall + order application behind four independent refusal gates; probe-llm-commander 13/13 ×2 (0 pe, 0 net) enrolled in vet:noreg; the D278-owed T0 badges-comment fix rode along; 3× SAFE_TO_COMMIT read-only Opus panel — [CLAUDE Fable 5, executing the D283 Aaron-locked law §9/§7.4 verbatim] (2026-07-06)

- **What shipped:** `src/tactical/T27-llm-commander.js` (manifest-registered after T26, T26 header discipline) + the one T0 dispatch line (the law §3.2 line VERBATIM, inside the asymmetric block above fldAiDefender; T4-logistics/T5-arms keep their existing priority above it) + `tools/probe-llm-commander.mjs` + its `vet:noreg` enrollment (112→113 entries). NO network code, NO UI, NO adapters (law §7.4 slice-1 scope) — the deliverable's runtime remains fully offline; `fldLlmConfigured()` is honestly `false` until the slice-2 connector exists, so **the shipped game cannot activate the seam at all**; the only plan source is the `__FIELD._t27MockPlan` test hook.
- **The seam (law §3):** writes ONLY `u.order`/`u.formation` + its own `__FIELD._t27` diagnostics bag — order vocabulary exactly `move|hold|charge` + `line|column`, `hold` anchored at the unit's own position (engine idiom), NO fldRng, NEVER `playerCharge`. Constants: `LLM_PLAN_INTERVAL 25` sim-s (+ phase-boundary refresh; attempt-time advances even on a failed cycle, so failures retry next interval and never busy-loop), digest bands `250` men / `25` yd / `5` s / 8-way compass facing. The digest is a PURE read: own-side brigades (id/arm/men-band/state/pos/facing/current-order/formation) + `fldVisible`-filtered enemy contacts ONLY (the D58/D64 fog law binds the LLM — it can be feinted; unseen enemies are simply absent) + objective + coarse terrain/elevation + phase/clock. The wall: malformed whole response → whole-cycle fallback KEEPING the last good plan; per-order legality (alive AI-led commanded-side brigade, on-map finite move/charge targets, enum vocabulary) → an illegal order drops THAT brigade to the real engine (defender/T26/D64), field-for-field; only sanitized primitives enter the plan (`Object.create(null)` map — no prototype pollution).
- **The four refusal gates (law §4):** default-off `llmCommander` (nothing in slice 1 sets it) · sticky `_llmOff` (the `_parityOff` idiom) · the PM3 double lock (`autoBoth` OR `rendererKind === "none"` hard-refuse — the D277 replay-×2 path is structurally unreachable even with a stale arm + stale mock leaking in) · no-config/no-plan-source. Plus the `_gen` self-heal: a stale `llmCommander` from a previous launch DISARMS on first dispatch — consent never carries across launches. **The slice-2 arm contract is documented in the T27 header:** `__FIELD.llmCommander = true; __FIELD._t27 = null;` at launch, only from explicit player opts + live connector config.
- **Probe (13 teeth ×2 green, 0 pageerrors, 0 net; the law's §4.3 a–d):** contract + source hygiene (dispatch above defender asserted; T27 source scanned clean of fldRng/playerCharge/fetch/XHR/WebSocket/EventSource/beacon) · digest purity ×2 + no-write/no-state · band quantization (with a non-vacuity guard) · fog mask (steps deterministically to the first genuinely-hidden state — hidden=1 at t≈83s — then pins digest enemies === the exact visible set; fog-off sees all) · wall-legal EXACT writes (hold anchor, formation, no playerCharge key, men/morale untouched at the write boundary) · wall-illegal 6-class drop (unknown id / off-map / unknown type / unknown formation / player-side / non-finite) falling to the engine FIELD-FOR-FIELD vs baseline · malformed + cadence + continuity (2 pulls in 26.5 sim-s — never per-tick; garbage cycle → last good plan stands and keeps commanding) · four independent inertness legs (`_llmOff` / no-config / `autoBoth` / headless-renderer each → zero `_t27` + disarm + byte-identical vs baseline) · armed determinism ×2 · zero network (in-page fetch/XHR/WS/beacon spy = hard 0 + a playwright request listener tolerating ONLY the pre-existing frozen-base `_m3dLoadScripts` THREE-CDN boot loader — any slice-2 connector call trips both halves).
- **Honest session re-anchors (recorded, panel-verified):** (1) the fog tooth was vacuous at deploy (only 2 US brigades on field, all visible) → re-anchored to step-until-hidden with a hard vacuity throw; (2) the continuity tooth accepts the engine's documented arrived-move→hold conversion (T0 order-queue arrival keeps tx/tz/tface) as the plan standing — pinned at the exact 600/450/1.25 triple an engine-authored order cannot fake; (3) the single observed http request is base.html's boot-triggered THREE-CDN script loader — pre-existing, fired pre-legs, not connector-class traffic.
- **The owed D278 fix (law §7.1):** the T0 badges comment now carries the honest drift chain — 5/8 (D104 era) → 7/8 (pre-D273 under E49b shedding, per the D278 A/B) → 8/8 (the D273 cautious posture) — layered onto D281's provenance framing (the current per-seed vector stays pointed at probe-arms/probe-presets pins).
- **Panel (read-only default-refute Opus ×3, `wf_89fe1c05-993` — read-only lenses only, the D281 race lesson): 3× SAFE_TO_COMMIT at high confidence, zero refutations.** Four advisories, all adopted/recorded: (a) **write-set amendment (this line is the amendment):** T27 additionally writes `__FIELD.llmCommander = false` on its disarm legs — a seam-owned CONTROL FLAG (off-direction only, unreachable in the OFF state, no sim-state/RNG effect); the law §3.1 write-set is read as order/formation/diagnostics + this control flag. (b) the 5/8→7/8→8/8 chain re-confirmed against the D278 record. (c)+(d) **two slice-2 arming traps recorded as slice-2 contract law:** the relaunch/new-seed button `Object.assign`s `__FIELD._launchOpts` into a fresh launch — slice 2 must NEVER persist `llmCommander` into `_launchOpts` (re-arm requires a fresh explicit enable, §6); and slice 2 must stamp `__FIELD.playerSide` BEFORE arming (fldLlmSide falls back to enemy-of-US — arming pre-stamp could aim the LLM at the human's own side).
- **Gates (all green):** `GATE OK` · `node --check` on every touched JS/probe · probe-llm-commander 13/13 ×2 (0 pe, 0 net) · adjacents probe-ai 15/15 / attacker-parity 13/13 / presets 27/27 / field 23/23 · spots bullrun 15/15 / fredericksburg 22/22 (byte-identity by construction — the seam is unreachable in all of them) · the PM3 lock probe-auto-resolve 10/10 + campaign-link 19/19 · deliverable diff = exactly the T27 splice + the T0 dispatch/comment block · `git diff --check` clean. No UI, no WCAG/screenshot surface this slice.
- **Queue: SLICE 2 — the connector (adapters A+B, presets, settings panel + per-battle enable, key handling, zero-network tooth extended to UI paths; manual OpenRouter `:free` smoke, never a gate) → slice 3 voice/persona → E59 → Priority-1 Soldier's Story. ## D283 — LLM-OPPONENT DESIGN LAW LOCKED (Q-D270-5 PLANNING SLICE): THE LIVE LLM FIELD COMMANDER (Aaron's override of the judged hybrid) + THE UNIVERSAL BYOK CONNECTOR ($0 rungs web-verified) + GUARDED LIVE VOICE + OPT-IN/NEUTRAL-PM3 DEFAULTS — [AARON 4 popups + CLAUDE Fable 5 synthesis; docs-only, BUILD NOT STARTED] (2026-07-06)

The Q-D270-5 planning slice ran the D280 pattern: workflow `wf_888c5559-785` (10 agents: 4 Sonnet repo readers → 3 independent Opus design candidates [authoring-time doctrine engine · live LLM commander · advisor/narrator character layer] → 3 adversarial Opus judge lenses [contract/determinism · history/teaching/gravity · product/feasibility/$0]); the Fable main loop re-verified every load-bearing seam first-hand (fldAiUnit dispatch T0:753-778 · the order vocabulary `u.order {move|hold|charge,tx,tz,tface}`+`u.formation` · the T26 idiom · PM3 headless invocation 87:98-100 · the runtime's zero network calls today · T6-presets.js:140 neutralPreset early-return), then loaded the claude-api reference and web-verified the 2026 provider landscape before the popups. Judge scores: doctrine-engine 22/30 · narrator 21/30 · live-commander 14/30, with a judged hybrid recommendation.

- **AARON'S FOUR LOCKS (2 popup rounds):** (1) **identity = the LIVE LLM FIELD COMMANDER** — chosen over the judged hybrid with the kill shots disclosed; a player-configured LLM commands the AI-led army at operational cadence through a new T27 order-writing seam, engine fallback everywhere. (2) **providers = the UNIVERSAL CONNECTOR** — one OpenAI-compatible adapter (OpenRouter `:free` models · Groq free tier · localhost Ollama/LM Studio · custom) + one Anthropic Messages adapter (browser opt-in header; haiku-class default; the UI carries the "Claude Max is NOT an API key" honesty note). The judges' "~0% of players" reach kill shot is REFUTED for this architecture: genuine $0 browser-CORS rungs verified 2026-07-06 (OpenRouter free models; Groq free tier; local models). Gemini-direct and OpenAI-direct are EXCLUDED by law (no reliable browser CORS; a proxy would break $0/no-backend). (3) **voice = GUARDED LIVE GENERATION** — the ≤160-char dispatch string may be live-generated under the containment stack (one-way facts from the fog digest; no schema fields for figures/dates/citations; anti-Lost-Cause + grim-professional system charter; "Dramatization" caption; somber-set suppression — no live text over the dead; failure=silence). (4) **defaults = OPT-IN, OFF-BY-DEFAULT, PM3 STRICTLY NEUTRAL** — zero behavior change unconfigured; T27 hard-refuses on `autoBoth`/headless so the LLM can never enter the D277 replay-×2 path; flip-the-default is a future fork.
- **THE SEAM LAW (highlights):** T27-llm-commander per the T26 idiom — writes ONLY `u.order`/`u.formation`/`_t27`, no fldRng, never `playerCharge`; dispatch line inside T0's asymmetric block above fldAiDefender; fog-respecting band-quantized digest (the D58/D64 fog law binds the LLM too — it can be feinted); a deterministic validation wall (illegal order → that brigade falls to the REAL engine; malformed/timeout/refusal → whole cycle falls back, last good plan stands); operational cadence ~25 sim-s + phase boundaries, latency-masked, never per-tick. OFF-state byte-identity by construction (sticky `_llmOff` + no-config + autoBoth each independently gate) — the entire vet:noreg battery runs with the seam unreachable, zero re-pins. New probe-llm-commander pins digest purity ×2, fog-masking, the wall via the `_t27MockPlan` hook, triple inertness, and a ZERO-NETWORK-when-off fetch-spy tooth; network never enters a probe; live-LLM nondeterminism is disclosed as the feature, not hidden.
- **Keys/network law:** first-ever runtime network class, firing only in an explicitly-enabled live battle; keys/config in device-only localStorage (`cw_llm_*`, the T6 precedent) with a clear-key button; never in saves/shares/exports/repo.
- **Scope:** three slices (S1 engine seam ZERO network → S2 connector+UI → S3 voice/persona texture); the D278-owed T0:351 stale-comment fix RIDES slice 1 (the next T0-touching slice). The rejected candidates are recorded as future milestones with their salvage (the doctrine engine's damage-invariance D74 tripwire; the narrator's canned-dispatch rung on the D173 shadow). Persona prompts are INPUT texture only — an outcome-encoding prompt is a D74 violation in prompt form.
- **LAW: `docs/design/llm-opponent-design.md`** (committed this slice) — §0 provenance/locks · §1 design · §2 connector law · §3 seam law · §4 determinism/probe law · §5 voice/gravity law · §6 settings law · §7 scope/slices · §8 gates · **§9 the paste-ready slice-1 build prompt**. Docs-only commit; the game is untouched. **Queue: the T27 slice-1 build (law §9) → E59 → Priority-1 Soldier's Story. M8/Q5/Q6 locked; Phase H parked; Phase D deferred.**

## D282 — THE D280 MAIN-MENU BUILD SHIPPED: THE AARON-LOCKED HYBRID COMMAND SCREEN IS LIVE — FULL-BLEED SOMBER-STATIC BACKDROP + ≥.90 DARK-GLASS CONTRAST ARCHITECTURE + THE CAMPAIGN-CHAIN TRACKER + THE FIELD OPERATIONS GROUP + THE E61 TAKE-COMMAND NUDGE (the read-only half); PRESENTATION-ONLY (D74) WITH DUAL NO-WRITE TRIPWIRES; 3× SAFE_TO_COMMIT READ-ONLY OPUS PANEL, BOTH ADVISORIES ADOPTED — [CLAUDE Fable 5, executing the D280 Aaron-locked design law §7 verbatim] (2026-07-06)

The D280 law (`docs/design/main-menu-redesign-design.md`) built clause by clause: ONE module rewrite (`src/98-h0-main-menu.js`, the presentation layer only) + the probe re-anchor (`tools/probe-h0-main-menu.mjs`) + docs. The menu graduates from the boxed-hero prototype to a full-bleed historical command screen. **The three Aaron locks, honored and probe-pinned:**

- **LOCK 1 (hybrid synthesis):** the next field's PD scene photo (`__ASSETS["scenes/"+key]`, the existing `h0SceneKey` fallback chain) promoted to a full-bleed `position:absolute;inset:0` layer inside `.h0-menu` (`aria-hidden`, empty alt, cold grade `grayscale(.62) contrast(1.15) brightness(.52)`, heavy vignette + bottom scrim, STATIC — no ken-burns, per the law's WCAG 2.2.2 judgment); ALL copy sits on near-opaque dark-glass panels (**.94/.96 panels · .93 stage/rail · .93 nudge · .92 caption band — worst-case contrast computed against a WHITE photo pixel bleeding through: 19 text/non-text pairs ALL pass AA, weakest 7.53:1 text / 9.08:1 non-text**); a `panelNearOpaque` tooth pins the ≥.90 alphas; `html[data-a11y-contrast="high"]` force-hides the backdrop (probe-toggled tooth); the command-grid texture survives as a faint z-1 layer OVER the backdrop (pre-existing tooth intact). The imageless path keeps the procedural fallback plate, full-bleed.
- **LOCK 2 (campaign-chain tracker in scope):** a full-width procedural rail replaces the "H0 prototype" footer — one segment per `CHAINS[C.side]` battle, name/year from `BATTLES` in the title + SR text, **theater-tinted from the honest `th:` field** (E `#5d86b7` · W `#5f9273` · TM `#d0a047` · N `#7d92a9` — decoration only, never the info carrier), fought = filled / current = **STATIC brass ring** (no pulse at all — the conservative read of "static or one-shot") / ahead = dimmed .26; `aria-hidden` `<ol>` paired with a visually-hidden SR summary ("Battle 2 of 31: First Bull Run, 1861. 1 fought, 29 ahead.") + a visible now-line; the rail scrolls inside its own `overflow-x:auto` container; suppressed in the no-save state. **Placed OUTSIDE `.h0-top` so the notices aside stays `.gn-col:last-child`** — the 95/97/92 classifieds-append contract survives by construction. NO war-effort chip expansion, NO Soldier's Story slot (the lock's scope boundary).
- **LOCK 3 (casualty photos somber-static ONLY):** `H0_SOMBER_SCENES = {antietam, gettysburg, chancellorsville}` — the panel's design lens independently verified this is an EXACT match to the SCENE_IMG dead-in-frame provenance (51-scenes-imagery). On a somber backdrop the one-shot entrance animation is suppressed entirely (`.h0-somber`), and per the panel's advisory (adopted) even the 1px hover lift is stilled (`transform:none` — border/glow hover cues remain). Nothing loops anywhere (`infinite` absent from the CSS — probe-pinned); the entrance budget tooth pins ≤400ms (shipped 340ms one-shot honoring BOTH `G.settings.reduceMotion` and `prefers-reduced-motion`); the provenance plate keeps the informative `img[alt]` + figcaption caption/credit on its own near-opaque band (14.6:1/10.3:1) — never text on the raw photo.
- **The Field Operations group (The Front graft):** `#gnFree` lives inside a labeled `role="group"` panel in the stage column — the T0→T1→T2→T6→T11 sibling-insertion chain clusters under the heading with the contract unchanged (same anchor id, same parentNode/nextSibling mechanics — panel-verified first-hand at T0:2387/T11:812); the list is scroll-capped so 6+ scenario buttons never blow the viewport.
- **The E61 nudge (the honest half, §3):** read-only consumption of `C.recoveryLossCount`/`C.recoveryMode` (base.html:2547-2549/2715-2718/2739-2741 writes them). At `recoveryMode && recoveryLossCount>=2` the stage column renders the take-command guidance card naming the walled battle ("Take command at Fredericksburg… Auto-resolve fights the same field with the same inputs — repeating it will not change the ground. Taking field command yourself, or pressing the political path to victory, is the honest way forward."). Renders per menu open, no dismissal state, no save writes. **The collapse-terminal half stays OPEN in REVIEW-QUEUE** (strategic-layer design fork).
- **Two real defects caught by the MANDATED screenshot readback (neither caught by the geometry teeth — both now probe-pinned as regression teeth):** (1) a scroll-capped GRID froze Chrome's button rows at min-height so 3-line scenario decks painted over the next button → the list is a flex column (content-based sizing) + a `fieldOpsButtonsFitContent` tooth (scrollHeight ≤ box+3 on every button); (2) the Aaron-locked tracker sat below the desktop fold once the async injections grew the list (~700px) → coordinated vertical trims (list cap 160px · plate 128px · notices/rail tightening) + a `chainVisibleFirstViewport` tooth measured against the SHEET's visible box AFTER injection settles (the first fold-tooth draft measured the window and false-greened — rewritten honestly, red-verified, then fixed). Incidental fix: the Season chip now reads the battle row's `year` (a saved-but-not-applied campaign used to show "1861" for every save).
- **D74 dual tripwires (probe):** static — the generated deliverable's module region (marker-sliced) must contain NO `C.<any-field> =` assignment (panel advisory adopted: ANY field, not an enumerated list), no `G.campaign =`, no `saveLocal(`; runtime — opening the menu twice must leave `localStorage.gor_save` byte-identical. Both green; the panel's integrity lens independently reproduced the extraction.
- **PANEL (read-only default-refute Opus ×3, wf_a20efea1-d4c — run per the D281 lesson: zero tree-mutating lenses):** contracts lens **SAFE_TO_COMMIT** (all six §2 items verified first-hand; "the probe re-anchor weakens no pre-existing tooth… strictly additive; the token pin STRENGTHENED"); design-law/locks lens **SAFE_TO_COMMIT** (1 advisory: somber hover lift — adopted above); D74/integrity lens **SAFE_TO_COMMIT** (1 advisory: tripwire field list — adopted above). ## D277 — PARITY-M3 SHIPPED: THE SIM-BACKED AUTO-RESOLVE IS LIVE — ONE BATTLE-TRUTH MODEL IN ALL THREE MODES; THE RATING-MARGIN MODEL, THE YEAR CURVE, THE LOSER FLOORS, THE DRAW BAND, AND THE commandMarginEdge NUDGE ARE DELETED; THE PARITY ARC (E42/E43/E45) IS COMPLETE — [CLAUDE Fable 5, per the D246 Aaron-locked design law; 3× SAFE_TO_COMMIT Opus panel] (2026-07-05)

The E42 resolution ("Gold standard: sim-backed", Aaron popup 2026-07-03) is built. A delegated campaign battle now runs the SAME conditioned battle the "Fight in real time" button would launch — headless, both sides under AI — and feeds back through the SHARED compute/apply path. Substitutability is true BY CONSTRUCTION (PL-1; D74 extended to the third mode). This supersedes D48's outcome model (rider added there); D48's orchestration contract (briefing button, campaignAdvance pipeline, `_arApplyCasualties`, XP parity, flip handling) survives inside the shared apply path.

- **THE BUILD:** `src/87-auto-resolve.js` rewritten — `_arRunHeadlessSim(C)` (the testable core: same flip handling/scenario routing/skirmish params/campaign ctx as `fldLaunchCampaignBattle` → `fldLaunchSandbox({renderer:'none', autoBoth:true, neutralPreset:true, seed})` → `fldStepN` to decision → `fldCampaignComputeOutcome()` → `fldExit(true)` clean teardown) + `_arSimSeed(C,bd,flipped)` (PL-6: FNV-1a over side/battle-id/idx/stats.battles/recovery/flip — war-state-pure; deliberately EXCLUDES war strength, which reaches the field as INPUTS via the T2 conditioning, never as a reroll) + the honest `_arShowResult` card (PL-8: "Your commander fought this battle in the same real-time engine… Your war shaped the inputs — the field decided the outcome. The same war state resolves the same field"; PL-10 neutral-setting disclosure; Continue → `fldCampaignApplyOutcome`). DELETED: `bridgeResolveOutcome`, `_arEnemyRating` (the generic year curve — the OOB carries each battle's year truth), the 0.42/0.72 loser floors, the ±5 draw band, the margin≥18 decisive threshold, and the `commandMarginEdge` output nudge (the general reaches every mode through bridgeArmy leadership + the officers layer; `commandMarginEdge` itself stays defined in 35 — probe-command pins it — its consumer is gone; removal is a future command-slice call). `src/tactical/T6-presets.js` gains the guarded `opts.neutralPreset` early-return (PL-10: the sim runs Veteran × Balanced regardless of the player's preset — the Recruit cushion is a human affordance; the seam also skips the preset's global fog/auto-pause writes, and fog deliberately keeps the fought launch's precedence — a battle-condition input, not a difficulty lever). T2/85/86/33 edits are copy/comment honesty only (the "balanced equivalently"/"SUBSTITUTABLE" header claims die per PL-8; panel-verified zero logic change).
- **DECIDE-&-LOG sub-decisions:** (1) **Bull Run = FAITHFUL** — the sim routes exactly as the fought path (`bullrun1` scenario for a US player, procedural otherwise); the A/B (`.tmp/ab-pm3-core-run1.json`) measured scenario→CS-win at dominant/fresh/weak (fog-on cautious-McDowell per D273 — delegation repeats history) vs forced-procedural→US-win; faithful preserves PL-1 purity, is historically apt, and the recovery flip converges in 1 attempt in every measured chain. Honest footnote (panel history lens): PL-3 is effectively null at THIS one battle — the scenario disposition dominates the ±12% band. (2) **Synchronous resolve** (no deferred "resolving…" shell) — probe-compatible, deterministic, same UX class as the old flow. (3) Re-opening the briefing without resolving re-resolves IDENTICALLY (same war state → same seed) — deterministic replay, nothing to save-scum; resolving increments stats.battles → a recovery re-attempt is a genuinely new field.
- **PL-6 / PURITY (measured):** determinism ×2 identical (winner/type/fractions/seed) at 3 war states; **0 Math.random + 0 Date.now calls** during the sim (thrower-stubs probe tooth); seed stable per state, moved by battles/recovery/flip/side, INVARIANT to war strength (probe-pinned).
- **PL-3 / INVESTMENT VISIBILITY (Aaron's rider — measured):** Spencer loadout @ Gettysburg 1863 (strong war, same seed): eFrac .417→.510, pFrac .236→.160; **Henry loadout @ Wilderness 1864 (fresh war, same seed): FLIPS a delegated CS timeout-win into a US hold-win at 240s.** Bought weapons decide delegated battles through the same era-gated T2 re-arm the fought path uses.
- **THE PACING A/B (`.tmp/ab-pm3-pacing-{old,new-US,new-CS}.json` — old model = the preserved D276 deliverable; 2 sides × dominant/fresh/weak, full delegated chains, cap 90):** **5 of 6 cells strictly improve.** US: old {dominant completed 31/31 · fresh completed · weak STALLED 0W/90L}; new {ALL THREE postures complete at 34 resolutions, 31W/3L, maxConsecLoss 1 — the losses are bullrun1 (history) + sevenpines/fthenry, each recovered in one}. CS: old {dominant completed 28/28 (riding the E42 defect — the margin ignored the authored OOB) · fresh STALLED at idx 0 (1W/84L — the margin's per-battle-id RND made a losing battle lose forever) · weak stalled}; new {ALL postures reach idx 22-23 of 28 (through Antietam/Gettysburg/Chickamauga…), then honestly stall at the terminal odds — Nashville (US 55k atk v CS 30k) / Bentonville (CS 21k atk v US 60k)}. **The judgment (panel-attacked, 3× FAILED_TO_REFUTE): the CS terminal stall is the one-battle-truth model working** — the sim refuses the Lost-Cause fantasy of delegating your way through the battles where the Confederacy was historically destroyed; the outs are TAKE COMMAND (either engine — D265 proved player agency decisive) or the political `victoryReady` path (probed; per the panel's history lens, NOT guaranteed reachable by delegation alone — losses net-stiffen Northern resolve via vicOnResolve — so the honest out at the wall is take-command; copy softened accordingly). **Explicit teaching-pivot ack (panel gates lens):** gross war strength no longer creates completion jeopardy for the delegating US player (weak/fresh/dominant all complete; jeopardy lives at specific battles + in costs) — authorized by PL-2 (outcomes emerge from authored inputs; the old strength→jeopardy coupling was the margin model), PL-3 still proven; acknowledged here, not silently absorbed.
- **PROBES (the old margin pins die honestly):** `probe-auto-resolve` REWRITTEN 7→**10/10** (PL-1 structural absence of the margin model · determinism ×2 · thrower purity · seed purity incl. strength-invariance · PL-3 shift + the Wilderness flip · faithful-Bull-Run repeats history · flip preserved-through-sim/consumed-at-apply · PL-8 card honesty incl. no-"rating"-copy · shared-path apply bookkeeping + XP parity · teardown hygiene). `probe-campaign-link` 18→**19/19** (+the PM3 shared-shape/determinism/teardown tooth; all fought-path teeth untouched). `probe-full-campaign` REWRITTEN **4/4** (US dominant chain COMPLETES with bounded recoveries ≤45 resolutions/mcl≤2 + per-battle stats/idx/completed/recovery integrity; CS chain reaches idx≥20 with wins≥15 and the stall is honest — completedWar is no free pass, panel-hardened; the flipped recovery converges ≤6; victoryReady flow green). Adjacent: `probe-bridge` 6/6. Budgets (ceilings, not bars): full-campaign 360→900s, auto-resolve →600s. All artifacts `ok=true`, 0 pageerrors, JSON read back.
- **ADJACENT-PROBE FINDING → NEW E60:** `probe-presets` reds 2 of 27 teeth — REPRODUCED BYTE-FOR-BYTE against the preserved D276 HEAD deliverable, so PRE-EXISTING D273 E53-v2-arc drift (T26 attackerParity is not in that probe's layers-OFF isolation set; the wave-commit now owns the marginal-commit decision the aiSkill tooth pins; probe-presets was not in D273's focused set). Filed as **E60** for the E53-arc owner's attribution + deliberate re-anchor (the E56/D268 recipe); `vet:noreg` will red on that entry at the next batch checkpoint until it lands — expected and recorded (the D266 posture). Also filed from this slice's evidence: **E59** (the `_fldCampaignSkirmishParams` clamps compress authored odds both ways — Sumter's 6:1 walkover → a coin flip; late-war lopsided fields under-compressed; pre-existing, shared with the fought procedural path, its own future A/B'd slice) and **E61** (panel history lens: no strategic-defeat terminal — the endless recovery loop with relief funds is engine-old; nudge-card/collapse-terminal fork for Aaron).
- **THE PANEL (3-lens default-refute Opus, wf_178fdb5b-883, ~321k tokens): 3× SAFE_TO_COMMIT, ZERO blocking.** No-fudge: all 6 refutations failed first-hand (no output gate/constant — the only clamp is the pre-existing shared 0.92; seed pure + strength-excluded + no save-scum; the T6 seam unreachable by any player/probe launch; T2/85/86/33 confirmed copy-only; flip single-consumed; teardown clean). History/§27: all 4 failed (the stall is honest teaching, not an unplayable-delegation HALT — it CLOSES the old §27 violation of delegate-and-skip-the-centerpiece-and-still-win; faithful Bull Run is accurate inputs, not a thumb; anti-Lost-Cause verified two-directional; the card's numbers arithmetically consistent). Gates: 5 REFUTED + 1 confirmed-and-fixed-in-session (the completedWar shortcut now asserts the final report; the deleted teeth were mandated-dead by the locked design or re-homed; E60 deferral verified structurally sound; budgets legitimate; the A/B drivers comparable).
- **Player-visible:** the Auto-resolve button now delegates the battle to a real headless fight of the same field; the result card names how the day ended (hold/containment/destruction), both sides' losses of men fielded, prisoners when taken, and the true contract — inputs from your war, outcome from the field, deterministic per war state, resolved at the neutral setting.
- **Ships:** `src/87-auto-resolve.js` (rewrite) · `src/tactical/T6-presets.js` (PL-10 seam) · `src/tactical/T2-campaign-link.js` + `src/85-battle-bridge.js` + `src/86-battle-conditioning.js` + `src/33-morale.js` (honest copy/comments) · `tools/probe-auto-resolve.mjs` + `tools/probe-full-campaign.mjs` (rewrites) · `tools/probe-campaign-link.mjs` (+1 tooth) · `tools/vet-no-regression.mjs` (budgets) · docs (this entry + the D48 rider · design-doc PM3 build-status · REVIEW-QUEUE E42→SHIPPED + E59/E60/E61 filings · V1-CHECKLIST M3 ticked + gate state · RUN-LOG · HANDOFF/WAKE-UP tops). Evidence preserved: `.tmp/ab-pm3-core.mjs`+run1 · `.tmp/ab-pm3-pacing.mjs`+3 legs · `.tmp/pm3-head-base.html`. **THE BATTLE-MODE-PARITY ARC (PARITY-M1 D246-248 · PARITY-M2 D266 · PARITY-M3 here) IS COMPLETE.** Queue: the E60 owner re-anchor (small, before the next batch checkpoint) → then the standing ladder (main-menu redesign + LLM-opponent remain Aaron's queued milestones; M8/Q5/Q6 locked; full `vet:noreg` at the arc's batch/release checkpoint per D160/D176 — a natural candidate now the arc is closed, Aaron's call).

## D276 — E46 PIPER GUNS RE-ATTEMPT RED-HALTED AFTER E53-v2+E54: P1 IMPROVES BUT STILL MISSES THE UNCHANGED SUNKEN ROAD GATE; DATA REVERTED; PM3 MAY START FROM THIS PARKED BOUNDARY — [CODEX top-model 5.5, red-halt docs closeout] (2026-07-05)

The documented Piper-farm Confederate gun row was re-applied exactly from the D243/D264 record on top of D275: `cs_piper_guns`, 8 guns / 136 crew, smoothbore, sourced farm seat `x:600,z:212` outside the Sunken Road objective ring. No clocks, hold thresholds, weapons, morale, casualty math, combat constants, or probe thresholds were changed.

Focused A/B (`.tmp/ab-e46-piper.mjs` -> `.tmp/ab-e46-piper-d276-run1.json`, 0 pageerrors) says the row still cannot ship:
- All 8 non-Antietam battles plus sandbox stayed byte-identical base->Piper.
- Base Antietam remains green: P0 CS 8/8, P1 US 7/8, P2 CS 8/8, aggregate CS 8/8 on both seed sets.
- Piper Antietam improves materially from D264's 1/8 default and 2/8 probe, but still reds the unchanged gate: **P1 Sunken Road US 5/8 default and 5/8 probe vs required US >=6/8**. The failing rows are still CS/timeout rows.
- Captured stayed 0 on all Antietam rows, including the Piper rows. E54's pocket-collapse seam does not fire here because the US attacker is not getting the required HOLD win on the red rows.
- The no-shedding guard remains non-shipping evidence, not a license: PiperNoShed P1 is 2/8 default and 5/8 probe.
- Watch numbers moved toward the historical cost ratio but not enough to pass direction: base attacker-pays ratio 0.93/0.92; Piper 1.07/1.05 vs the historical ~1.20.

Decision: **revert-file-halt.** The Piper row is removed again, and `data/antietam.json`'s `_e46Note` now records the D276 evidence. E46 remains complete except for this documented divergence. Because E47 is now shipped and E46 has been honestly re-attempted and parked behind the unchanged gate, **PM3 is no longer waiting on another Piper loop**; the next bounded item is the PARITY-M3 sim-backed auto-resolve build. Any future Piper restore must run the same seeded A/B and pass P1 Sunken Road US >=6/8 without tuning per-battle constants or weakening the gate.

Focused vet (D176 red-halt boundary): `node tools/build.mjs` GATE OK before the A/B; focused A/B ran to completion with 0 pageerrors and exactly two expected red gate failures; the Piper data row was reverted; `node tools/build.mjs` GATE OK again after the revert with only the known raw-embed soft warning; `probe-antietam` 16/16, `probe-gettysburg` 18/18, `probe-shiloh` 31/31, and `probe-field` 23/23 all `ok=true` with 0 pageerrors; JSON readback confirmed those artifacts clean and the A/B failure list limited to the two P1 Sunken Road rows; `git diff --check` clean. Full `npm run vet:noreg` remains deferred under D176.

## D275 — E54 POCKET-COLLAPSE CAPTURE SEMANTICS SHIPPED, AND E47 SHILOH/GETTYSBURG HOME-EDGE FLIPS ARE LIVE; NEXT = E46 PIPER GUNS RE-ATTEMPT, THEN PM3 — [CODEX top-model 5.5, engine/data/probe/docs closeout] (2026-07-05)

D274 proved direction was fixed but captured stayed zero, so D275 defines the missing universal captured/pocket seam without per-battle combat constants. The rule fires only after an attacker HOLD win with explicit role-aware `homeEdge`, attacker field-collapse superiority of at least 2:1 in live men, and attacker local objective control around the objective. It then captures live routing/wavering defenders before the T8 phase/end ledgers freeze. It does not fire in symmetric sandbox/no-homeEdge cases, near-parity defender collapses, or non-attacker/timeout endings.

Implementation:
- `T25-surrender.js` now owns `fldPocketCollapseOnVictory(w, by)` plus the small local helpers and `FLD.POCKET_COLLAPSE_RATIO = 2`.
- `fldCheckVictory` calls that hook before `_fldPhaseResolved()`/`fldOnOver()`, so the captured subset is reflected in the existing after-action and phased ledgers.
- `probe-field` adds an E54 fixture with one positive attacker-HOLD pocket and two inert controls: near-parity and no-homeEdge.
- Shiloh and Gettysburg now carry role-aware `homeEdge` data; Shiloh's Hornets' Nest card names Prentiss's surrender/captured soldiers as a teaching beat.

Acceptance:
- Shiloh flip: CS direction holds; every CS-hold seed in the shipping flip smoke records nonzero captured US, while the one honest US timeout seed stays captured zero. Official `probe-shiloh` is 31/31.
- Gettysburg flip: aggregate remains US; Day 1 CS holds 7/8 in both default and probe sets, and every Day-1 CS-hold row records nonzero captured US. Official `probe-gettysburg` is 18/18.
- E53 false-capture guards remain green: `probe-attacker-parity` 13/13, `probe-chickamauga` 18/18, `probe-chancellorsville` 27/27, `probe-malvern-hill` 27/27, and `probe-antietam` 16/16 all ok with 0 pageerrors.

Focused vet (D176, no full `npm run vet:noreg`): `node tools/build.mjs` GATE OK; `node --check` passed for touched runtime/probe files; `probe-field` 23/23, `probe-shiloh` 31/31, `probe-gettysburg` 18/18, `probe-attacker-parity` 13/13, `probe-chickamauga` 18/18, `probe-chancellorsville` 27/27, `probe-malvern-hill` 27/27, and `probe-antietam` 16/16 all `ok=true` with 0 pageerrors; JSON readback confirmed no failed steps; preserved E47 smokes `smoke-shiloh-flip` and `smoke-gettysburg-flip` both `pe=0`; `git diff --check` clean. ## D274 — E47 FLIP RE-ATTEMPT RED-HALTED: D273 A+B FIXES SHILOH/GETTYSBURG DIRECTION, BUT THE CAPTURED HALF STILL FAILS WITH ZERO CAPTURES; DATA REVERTED; E54 CAPTURED/POCKET SEMANTICS REQUIRED — [CODEX top-model 5.5, red-halt docs closeout] (2026-07-05)

The D262 E47 Shiloh/Gettysburg flip edits were re-applied only as a temporary acceptance-test build on top of the shipped D273 E53-v2 parity seam, then reverted before this commit. This was a direct test of the D273 claim: A+B attacker parity should fix direction, while any remaining captured-ledger failure goes to E54 rather than being tuned or weakened.

Results:
- **Shiloh:** flip direction now passes enough to continue (CS 7/8 default, 8/8 probe; flipNoShed 8/8 both sets), but the captured half is still red: `capUS/capCS` stayed 0 on every seed. The Prentiss/Hornets' Nest mass-capture requirement does not fire in correct-direction geometry.
- **Gettysburg:** aggregate stays green (US 8/8 both sets) and Day 1 direction improves to CS 7/8 default + 7/8 probe, but the captured half is still red: captured stayed 0 on every seed. flipNoShed kept aggregate green but Day 1 stayed marginal (CS 7/8 default, 6/8 probe), still captured 0.
- Both smoke runs had 0 pageerrors. The attempted data/narration edits were reverted, and `node tools/build.mjs` restored the generated deliverable.

Decision: **E47 does not ship.** D273 A+B resolves the direction half but does not supply the captured/pocket semantics needed for Shiloh Prentiss and Gettysburg Day 1. The next legitimate unblocker is a bounded E54 captured/pocket semantics session, then the same E47 acceptance tests. Do not weaken the captured requirement, do not encode per-battle capture constants, and do not treat the old artifact-direction capture ledger as acceptable history.

## D273 — E53-v2 ATTACKER-PARITY BUILD SHIPPED: A+B WING/WAVE DOCTRINE IS LIVE, C/ABANDONMENT IS ABSENT, THE E53-SCOPED SIDESTEP CAPTURE VALVE KILLS THE FALSE MASS-CAPTURE CLASS, BULL RUN GETS UNIVERSAL CAUTIOUS-v2 + ACCURATE McDOWELL CAUTIOUS INPUT, AND THE FULL BATTERY IS GREEN x2 BYTE-IDENTICAL — [CODEX top-model 5.5, sim/data/tool/docs closeout] (2026-07-05)

E53-v2 was re-landed from the preserved D269 materials and amended to the D272 law. The shipping build adds `src/tactical/T26-attacker-parity.js`, enrolls it in `src/00-manifest.json`, dispatches it from `fldAiUnit` before the D64 `fldAiAttacker` branch, and defaults `__FIELD.attackerParity` ON with the sticky `_parityOff` test hook. The T26 seam writes only attacker AI orders/formation plus its own `_e53` diagnostics/wing flags. It never writes men, morale, casualties, captured, victory, score, or RNG state. C/abandonment/recall is absent: no `E53_ABANDON_X` path ships.

Mechanics shipped:
- **A+B only:** the surplus envelopment wing (`E53_WING_DEPTH=180`, `E53_WING_X=120`) and wave-commit order seam. D64 remains fallback whenever the trigger is unarmed, fog is on, the attacker is cautious, the scenario is symmetric, or the attacker-generic isolation hook is active.
- **Mass-capture governor:** T25 keeps the original real-lane SL-2 predicate as the global fallback, but when `fldParityCaptureValveActive()` is true it requires the real lane and both virtual sidestep lanes (`x±FLD.RALLY_R`, clamped to field-edge margin) to be blocked before surrender. The valve is scoped to E53 parity/fog/generic-isolation, not a global T25 rewrite. It intentionally remains available in cautious phases' capture ledger, because Chickamauga P2 otherwise revives the D269 false POW class; cautious affects order selection, not the ledger governor.
- **Cautious-v2 + Bull Run accurate input:** the old `ATK_CAUTIOUS_HOLD` objective-ring band is removed. A cautious AI attacker with fog off now presses until it is within existing `ATK_ASSAULT_R` of the nearest visible defender, then holds in line; fog falls through to the stock fog doctrine. `data/bullrun.json` adds `assaultDoctrine:"cautious"` as an accurate input for McDowell's piecemeal commitment. This changes order selection only; there is no First Bull Run combat constant.

The three requested closeout lenses were run against the live diff:
- **Mechanism:** D272 A+B only, no C; the valve is E53-scoped and no longer a global T25 change; cautious gates are split correctly (T26 orders inert in cautious, capture valve still protects cautious phase ledgers); no per-battle damage/firepower/morale constants were added.
- **History:** Bull Run's cautious row is an honest input, not a tune. Chickamauga P0 movement and the aggregate-cost direction are logged as watch rows in `probe-chickamauga`, while P1/P2/aggregate direction and false-capture guards remain hard gates.
- **Gates:** `.tmp/ab-e53-v2.mjs` ran twice and the artifacts are byte-identical by `cmp`. Run summaries: Bull Run CS/timeout 11/11, Chancellorsville CS 10/11 with CS 8/8 default + 7/8 probe, Shiloh CS/hold 11/11 with capUS 0, Gettysburg aggUS 8/8 with capCS 3,531, Chickamauga CS/phases 11/11 with capUS 0, Antietam aggCS 11/11 with capUS 0, depth legs green, `pe=0`.

Focused vet (D176, no full `npm run vet:noreg`): `node tools/build.mjs` GATE OK; `node --check` passed for `T0-field-sandbox.js`, `T25-surrender.js`, `T26-attacker-parity.js`, `probe-ai.mjs`, `probe-chickamauga.mjs`, `probe-attacker-parity.mjs`, and `vet-no-regression.mjs`; `.tmp/ab-e53-v2.mjs` run1/run2 green and `cmp` byte-identical; `probe-attacker-parity` 13/13, `probe-ai` 15/15, `probe-chickamauga` 18/18, `probe-field` 22/22, and `probe-bullrun` 15/15 all `ok=true` with 0 pageerrors; JSON readback confirmed no failed steps; `git diff --check` clean. Contrary to the D272 expected-red carry-forward, the shipped build's `probe-bullrun` rail-pivot tooth is green: with CS rail pivot = CS timeout win at 480s; without = US hold at 305s. Record this as E58 resolved by live evidence, not silently assumed.

Next queue: re-attempt the E47 Shiloh/Gettysburg flips behind the same unweakened gates, then E46 Piper honestly, then PM3. E54/E57 remain scoped follow-ups for global pocket/capture nuance and phase-clock accurate-input audit; they are not part of this shipped E53-v2 slice.

## D272 — E53-v2 DESIGN SESSION LOCKED: A+B RETAINED, C DROPPED, MASS-CAPTURE GOVERNOR IS E53-ACTIVE ONLY, AND BULL RUN USES UNIVERSAL CAUTIOUS-v2 PLUS AN ACCURATE `bullrun1` CAUTIOUS DATA ROW; NO SIM/CODE/DATA CHANGE SHIPPED; D269 FORK FILE RESOLVED — [CODEX top-model 5.5, docs-only doctrine lock] (2026-07-05)

The D271 option-(a) path was executed from synced clean `main` (`85e5765` at start). Engine facts were re-verified first-hand against `T0-field-sandbox.js` and `T25-surrender.js`: D64 still uses the per-unit `localSup`/hold-at-fire-range attacker branch; `ATK_CAUTIOUS_HOLD` is the old 0.85 objective-ring band; T25's SL-2 surrender predicate is the current x-lane blocker; and no T26/E53 runtime seam is live at HEAD. The preserved D269 T26 build was re-landed only as a temporary measurement build, then removed and rebuilt away before docs edits.

Fresh measurements ran foreground/serialized with `TMPDIR=$PWD/.tmp`, existing port 8765, and direct JSON readback; all artifacts report 0 pageerrors:
- `.tmp/fresh-e53v2-anatomy-d272.json`: the D269 capture reds decompose as open-flank/tollbooth events and wing-suicide events, with Gettysburg's real cornered captures isolated from the false five-figure classes.
- `.tmp/fresh-e53v2-variants-d272.json`: the sidestep valve plus C off (`govNoC`) gives Chancellorsville CS 8/8 default + 7/8 probe with capCS 0, Chickamauga CS 11/11 with capUS 0, Antietam aggCS 11/11 with capUS 0, and Gettysburg aggUS 8/8 both sets with capCS 3,531 preserved. C on (`gov`) harms Chancellorsville (6/8 + 5/8). Wave-only/no-wing is refuted for Shiloh; wing-only flips Bull Run; wave-only is Bull Run-safe but insufficient elsewhere.
- `.tmp/fresh-e53v2-cautious-d272.json`: cautious-v2 (hold only within existing `ATK_ASSAULT_R` of the nearest visible defender; fog falls through to stock fog doctrine) pins the Bull Run tooth CS 8/8 and keeps fog-on shipping Bull Run row-identical to base. Watch rows remain: Malvern Hill at the 6/8 floor, Fredericksburg cost ratio shifts, Chickamauga P0 movement.
- `.tmp/measure-e53v2-final.json`: wave-only+valve is refuted (Chancellorsville 4/8 + 3/8; Gettysburg capCS 0). The valve is inert on the parity-off Bull Run tooth common fields, but the broader `govBase` parity-off read proves a global T25 valve would alter base captures elsewhere.

The 3-lens default-refute review was run locally by the top-model main loop from those measurements:
- **Mechanism lens:** D270's global-valve draft is rejected for this milestone because the contract demands OFF-state row identity. The valve ships only inside the E53-active/parity-gated path; E54 owns future global pocket semantics.
- **History lens:** under-capturing smaller documented capture classes is less harmful than false five-figure POW columns. Gettysburg's Day-1 captured band is preserved; Antietam/Chickamauga capture nuance remains E54-scoped.
- **Gate lens:** C is not salvageable by retuning. `govNoC` is strictly stronger than `gov` at Chancellorsville, and wave-only/no-wing breaks required gates. Bull Run is solved by an accurate-input cautious row plus the universal defender-anchored cautious rule, not by a per-battle combat knob.

Doctrine lock for the next build:
1. **Mass-capture governor:** E53-active sidestep valve only. A router is cut off only if the existing T25 lane test fails at the real x and at both virtual sidesteps `x±FLD.RALLY_R` (clamped to the field edge safety margin). No new constants; no global T25 rewrite in this milestone.
2. **Bull Run-safe attacker behavior:** amend cautious universally to hold at `ATK_ASSAULT_R` from the nearest visible defender, fog-guarded; add `assaultDoctrine:"cautious"` to `bullrun1` as an accurate-input posture for McDowell's piecemeal commitment.
3. **C-v2:** no abandonment/recall ships in E53-v2. A+B remain the measured core; C is dropped. A future assault-abandonment mechanic is a separate item, never a retune of `E53_ABANDON_X`.
4. **Battery:** all D267/D269 §4 gates remain intact; v2 adds hard OFF-state row identity, valve-isolation teeth, the Bull Run cautious-v2 successor tooth, and explicit watch rows. E58 rail-pivot remains known expected red until its owner-slice resolves it.

Docs-only ship: `docs/design/e53-attacker-parity-design.md` now carries the D272 current-law amendment; ledger/handoff/wake-up/checklist/review queue are synchronized; the stale `DECISION-NEEDED-e53-build.md` fork is removed as resolved. No sim/code/data surface, `build/base.html`, or generated deliverable change ships from D272. **Next:** E53-v2 BUILD/re-land from `.tmp/e53-build-d269/`, apply the §0 deltas, run the full battery with ×2 determinism and commit panel, then E47 flips → E46 honestly → PM3.

## D268 — E56 RESOLVED: THE PROBE-ARMS BULLRUN1 BASELINE DRIFT IS 100% E49b STRAGGLER-SHEDDING — E48 AND E49a EACH PROVEN EXACTLY ZERO; DELIBERATE RE-ANCHOR CS 5/8 → 7/8 WITH THE PER-SEED WINNER VECTOR PINNED; 2× RE_ANCHOR_SOUND OPUS PANEL; INCIDENTAL: NEW LEDGER E58 (probe-bullrun's rail-pivot tooth red at HEAD — same E48/E49-arc drift class, drift window pinned first-hand) — [CLAUDE, the E56 owner-slice per D266/D267; tools+docs commit, ZERO sim surface] (2026-07-05)

- **`head`** (HEAD `5d4edbb`): tooth-subset **CS 7/8** (union 9/11) — the E56 drift reproduced live (CS on 1,7,21,42,101,303,909; US-hold on 55).
- **`headNoE48`** (fldCheckVictory wrapped to force `holdLive=false` — suppresses ONLY the D252 buzzer rule; the wrapper demonstrably live, 95,842 calls): **FULL-ROW IDENTICAL to head** on all 11 seeds (winner/by/steps/endT/final strengths/ledgers). **E48 contributes ZERO** — no bullrun1 seed ever reaches the buzzer mid-hold in this config.
- **`headNoSur`** (`__FIELD._e49NoSurrender`, the T25:117 sticky hook): **FULL-ROW IDENTICAL to head**. **E49a contributes ZERO** — `surrenderEverCount` 0 and `captured` 0/0 on every row of every leg; the panel verified first-hand that the SL-2 directional predicate is structurally unreachable at bullrun1 (routers flee AWAY from the enemy — the D262-D267 geometry finding at yet another config).
- **`headNoShed`** (`__FIELD._e49NoShed`, T25:71): **FULL-ROW IDENTICAL to the d251 leg on all 11 seeds** — byte-identical winners, step counts, strengths, ledgers. **E49b is 100% of the drift; nothing else in D252–D267 touches this config at all.**
- **`headNoE49`** and **`headNoAll`**: also FULL-ROW IDENTICAL to d251 — attribution closure (the harness's persisted closure gate upgraded to full-row JSON equality per the panel sharpening).
- **Per-seed decomposition d251→head:** 5 seeds flip US→CS (21, 33, 42, 202, 303 — fast ~4,100–5,300-step US holds become full-clock 9,600-step CS timeouts) and 2 flip CS→US (49, 55) — net +2 on the tooth subset (55 out, 21/42/303 in) and +3 on the union (33/202 in, 49 out; the arithmetic reconciles exactly). On full-clock rows missing reads US ~825–858 (~4.6% of fielded) and CS ~341–520 (~4–5%) — both sides inside E49b's D261-measured 2.8–4.8% historical band.

**THE ADJUDICATION (D74/SL-7 posture): 7/8 is the HONEST NEW BASELINE of a better engine — DELIBERATE RE-ANCHOR, never a silent re-pin.** The sole mover is a shipped, Aaron-locked, panel-vetted UNIVERSAL mechanic (E49b first-break straggler-shedding, law D259, ship D261) that landed after the tooth's 5/8 was committed at D251: Bull Run's US attacker breaks more often than the CS defender, so the side-symmetric 5% first-break shed costs the attacker more men, starving marginal assaults into defender timeouts — and on two seeds cutting the other way. The mechanism story is code-verified (side-agnostic `fldShedStragglers`; the sticky `shedDone` no-double-fire flag; the call site strictly inside the T0 rout-transition branch; live strength drops match the ledger; no missing→strength leak). Historical note logged never load-bearing (D74): CS 7/8 at First Bull Run is directionally MORE historical than 5/8.

**THE PANEL (2 independent default-refute Opus lenses, both RE_ANCHOR_SOUND at high confidence; wf_7af919b5-001 + one standalone re-run after the first mechanism-lens agent died on a StructuredOutput retry cap — an agent failure, not a refutation, honestly re-run):** probe-integrity lens — every gate-weakening refutation FAILED (exact-equality at 7 is as sensitive as at 5; E49b is engine-universal, not a toggleable layer, so the melee-table bug class still perturbs the count; the attribution methodology verified first-hand); mechanism-honesty lens — all four attack lines FAILED (no masked interaction: the E48/E49a suppression legs ran WITH shedding live and still matched head exactly; no side-biased defect; captured=0 structurally correct; rout-count movement is battle-length composition). **Sharpenings ADOPTED:** (1) the tooth now pins the PER-SEED WINNER VECTOR (CS on 1,7,21,42,101,303,909; US on 55), so compensating opposite-direction seed flips can never net back to 7 and false-green — the one surviving pre-existing tooth weakness, closed while the tooth was open; (2) exact-equality kept, never a band; (3) the tooth comment records the full attribution + the dual meaning (a future drop toward 5/8 signals an E49b regression, NOT restoration of the melee-table bug); (4) recorded here: **the pin now depends on E49b's numeric behavior (SHED_FRAC 0.05, first-break-only, attacker-gated) — any future D74-approved E49b retune will legitimately red this tooth and requires a fresh attribution A/B + deliberate re-anchor, never a silent re-pin;** (5) the harness closure gate mechanized at full-row identity.

**INCIDENTAL FILED (NEW E58):** the adjacent-probe gate surfaced **probe-bullrun's HISTORICAL-ASYMMETRY rail-pivot tooth red at HEAD** (`csScore with=255029 < without=274005`, deterministic ×2, deliverable bit-identical to HEAD — this slice is tools-only and cannot reach it). Drift window pinned first-hand (`.tmp/ab-e58-railpivot.mjs`, the tooth's exact logic + exact probe SETUP flags on both deliverables): at **D251** the pivot is dramatically load-bearing (with-rail = CS timeout win at the full 480s; without = US hold at 263s); at **HEAD** both runs are US wins (266s vs 291s — removing the CS rail makes the US SLOWER, inverting the time-score) with shedding ledgers active on the with-rail row. Seed 55 is the same seed that flipped CS→US in this slice's A/B — presumptively the same E49b composition, but the E58 owner runs its own attribution + adjudication (single-seed direction tooth; remedy space includes a multi-seed re-anchor — its owner's call, never weakened silently). `vet:noreg` will red on the probe-bullrun entry at the next batch checkpoint until E58 lands — expected and recorded (the exact D266/E56 posture).

**VET (focused D176 gate, tools+docs only, zero sim surface):** `node --check` on probe-arms + both harnesses · the A/B ok=true exit 0 (7 legs, 0 pe) · **probe-arms 23/23 green ×2** with the re-anchored per-seed tooth (exit 0 under pipefail both runs; artifact readback ok:true, 0 failed steps, 0 pe — live console the anchor per the D232/D233 slow-Mac note) · adjacent **probe-field 22/22** 0 pe · adjacent probe-bullrun = the E58 finding above (pre-existing at HEAD, proven) · `git diff --check` clean · no build needed (no src/data change; deliverable untouched — bit-identical to HEAD throughout, `git status` shows tools/docs only). No Opus commit panel due beyond the two in-session adjudication lenses (tools+docs, the A/B was the arbiter — the D242/D256/D260/D262/D264 precedent).

**Player-visible: nothing** — probe-and-docs only; the game is byte-identical.
