## D267 — THE E53 ATTACKER-PARITY DESIGN SESSION RAN AND LOCKED: A+B+C SHIP AS ONE DOCTRINE (envelopment wing + wave-commit + abandonment/recall); THE D64(a) SUPERSESSION IS RECORDED; SIX AARON POPUP ANSWERS; NEW LEDGER E57 (phase-clock accurate-inputs audit); THE QUEUE IS E56 → THE E53 BUILD → THE FLIPS → PIPER-HONESTLY → PM3 — [AARON 6 popups (5 recommended + Bull-Run-to-the-A/B); CLAUDE measurements + panel + law; docs-only commit] (2026-07-05)

The session Aaron ordered in D263/D265 ran the full D255/D259/D263 pattern: engine facts first-hand → the repo's largest attacker-behavior measurement program → a 3-lens default-refute Opus panel BEFORE Aaron saw candidates (wf_bd1e6180-aaf, ~346k tokens, ZERO refutations — all three candidates PROPOSE_WITH_SHARPENINGS on all three lenses) → a seam-faithful emulation answering the panel's central open → six Aaron popups. **LAW: `docs/design/e53-attacker-parity-design.md`.** Docs-only; the deliverable was cmp-proven byte-identical to HEAD `f2871d7` after every temporary measurement build; tree clean.

- **THE MEASUREMENTS (harnesses `.tmp/measure-attacker-d267{,b,c,d,e}.mjs`, all 0 pe; temp builds = the exact preserved E46/E47 evidence rows, applied → measured → reverted → cmp-proven; harness cross-checks reproduced D262's flip red (CS 3/8) and D264's Piper red (P1 US 1/8) verbatim):** (1) THE GAP IS TWO BEHAVIORS — the aggressive D64 doctrine (cautious posture removed by runtime hook) still loses Fredericksburg 0/8 at 5,770 mean cas and ~90 routs/battle where a naive move-order player wins 8/8 at 2,578 (the per-unit localSup gate + hold-at-fire-range keeps the mass out of the ring; lateral spread was never the gap — the AI is TIGHTER than the player), and nothing is ever tasked around a position (aimZ always the attacker-side face). (2) ENVELOPMENT IS THE LEVER — a scripted ½-deep + ½-press plan wins flipped Shiloh **8/8** (every other plan 0/8; the AI 3/8) at the LOWEST winning cost, converts Piper-P1 **6/8**, and fires E49a captures for the FIRST time in any correct-direction config (Antietam P1: 9,582 CS captured, blockedS 78, defRally 0) — mechanism: deep presence within RALLY_R denies rally by existing (T0:542), and a rout (62) outruns a charge (30) so only PRE-POSITIONED depth ever captures. (3) JOINTLY REQUIRED — wing-alone on the real D64 front reaches 1/8 at flipped Shiloh (below the shipping 3/8), press-alone 0/8: only the composite reaches the ceilings. (4) THE TRIGGER IS LOAD-BEARING — an unconditional wing at Malvern Hill got the attacker's own deep half captured wholesale (19,231 men vs documented CS losses ~5,650 TOTAL, no mass surrender); with the trigger (canCommit + aggregate front bar) the wing stays HOME at MH (0 deploys, US 8/8). (5) THE SEAM-FAITHFUL A+B EMULATION (panel clauses applied): Antietam P0/P1/P2 ALL hold (the front-read wing-exclusion fixed a measured P1 8/8→5/8 double-count regression) · Chancellorsville §6 zero-headroom CS **8/8 on the full seed set** · MH US 8/8 · Shiloh unflipped CS 8/8 · Gettysburg D1 CS 8/8 (toward history) · **flipped Shiloh CS 8/8 vs the ≥4/8 gate** · Piper P1 **4/8 vs ≥6/8 (still red-projected)** · **Bull Run FLIPS US 8/8** (the same craft that fixes the targets overturns First Bull Run) · phantom-prisoner class persists without C (cap US 22,267 at Antietam vs documented ~750 — the wing dies deep at uncarryable phases). (6) THE CLOCK CEILING — Antietam's historically-held P0/P2 resist EVERY measured plan inside their 220s clocks (~48 rows, one P2 crack); phase clocks are a flat 220s = ~49×–90× inconsistent compression vs the single-battle ~30×.
- **THE PANEL (3-lens default-refute Opus): zero refutations, mandatory sharpenings adopted into the law:** trigger must include canCommit; the front read EXCLUDES wing units by flag; surplus-only sticky peel with the flank LATCHED at formation; WING_DEPTH 180 flagged as a harness artifact — the build proves it non-load-bearing (±60 sensitivity leg) or anchors it independently; B must be an order-writing pre-seam (SL-10 clean — never mutates the D64 localSup internal); C excludes cautious battles (the AI must never be wiser than Burnside — his doomed assault is the historical INPUT), threshold FIXED a-priori at 2× the phase-INITIAL brigade count (a red at hard gates REFUTES C, never re-tunes — the anti-gate-chasing clause), own flag never `_atkCautious`; OFF-state byte-identity ships as ASSERTED teeth. **Panel sign correction (all three lenses): the digest's "7,040 CS captured" Fredericksburg row was 7,040 US — the ATTACKER'S OWN cut-off wing surrendering (T25:134 keys the ledger by the surrendering side), at a battle where the wing is posture-gated OFF — player-agency evidence only, never cited for the wing.** History lens: the wing fires where envelopment is historically apt (Sudley, the Barlow wheel, the Hornets' Nest pocket, Jackson) and the archetypal frontal set pieces are already cautious-postured; endorses the (c) clock option.
- **AARON'S SIX LOCKS:** (1) **A+B+C as one doctrine** (the composite is the only measured config that reaches the ceilings; C doubles as the wing recall). (2) **The D64(a) supersession recorded** (what the lock rejected — a global-ratio knife-edge — stays rejected; what it prescribed — per-unit-only commitment — is the measured grinder; the wave keys on locally-ASSEMBLED mass; D64 remains the fallback whenever the trigger is unarmed). (3) **Bull Run: the build's A/B decides** — no pre-approved posture; a red = revert-file-halt + the prepared McDowell-piecemeal `assaultDoctrine` remedy (citation-grade, the Fredericksburg precedent) surfaced as DECISION-NEEDED. (4) **Captured-honesty = decision-rule, not numeric gate** (captured logged everywhere; five-figure captured at a no-documented-mass-surrender battle = refute-and-revert class, Aaron adjudicates; per-battle numeric capture gates rejected as D74 output-tuning). (5) **NEW ledger E57 — the phase-clock accurate-inputs audit** (normalize documented phase durations to a consistent compression ratio; D92/E52 playbook; separate sim-affecting milestone; clocks stand meanwhile; lengthen-to-flip stays rejected). (6) **Queue: E56 owner-slice FIRST** (bullrun1 is asymmetric and wing-active — a drifted baseline makes build attribution impossible) **→ the E53 BUILD → the E47 flip re-attempt** (direction projects GREEN, seam 8/8; if `captured>0` reds, that half goes to an E54-scoped session, never a weakened gate) **→ the E46 Piper re-attempt HONESTLY EXPECTED RED** (seam 4/8 vs ≥6/8; E46 stays open pending E57/E54) **→ PM3** (still hard-gated on E47 fully landing).
- **Supersessions recorded (law §5):** D64 lock (a) as above; D262/D263's "the SL-2 corridor is structurally unreachable" SHARPENED to "unreachable by any attacker that existed then" — deliberate deep presence completes the predicate at correct-direction battles (measured captures), while flipped-Shiloh's shallow ~180yd corridor still never completes (captured 0 in every config incl. the 8/8 ceiling) — that half remains E54's.
- **Incidental watch (logged, not filed):** MH seed 7 reads CS/hold at HEAD where D260/D261 recorded US 8/8 — the standing probe-malvern-hill tooth (US ≥6/8 on its 8-seed set) reads 7/8, still green; E56-class drift watch for the next battery owner.
- **VET (focused, D176 — docs-only, zero sim surface):** GATE OK ×6 across the session (every temp apply + revert) · deliverable cmp-proven byte-identical to HEAD after each of the three temp-build cycles and at session end · all harness runs 0 pe · `git status`/`git diff --check` clean · no commit panel due beyond the in-session design panel (docs-only; the D242/D256/D260/D262/D263 precedent). Ships: docs/design/e53-attacker-parity-design.md NEW (the law) · DECISIONS D267 · RUN-LOG · REVIEW-QUEUE (E53 → DESIGN LOCKED; E54 captured-half note; NEW E57; E56 promoted to next) · V1-CHECKLIST gate-state · WAKE-UP/HANDOFF tops + Prompt A rewritten as the E56 owner-slice kickoff (the E53 BUILD prompt staged behind it).

## D266 — PARITY-M2 SHIPPED: THE D250 CAPPED ENEMY-STRENGTH CONDITIONING CHANNEL IS LIVE — REBUILT PER THE D251 SPEC WITH E48+E49a+E49b UNDERNEATH; THE D249/D251 WIN-CHANNEL INVERSION IS MEASURED DEAD (playerWins 13→17 FOR THE PLAYER); 3× SAFE_TO_COMMIT OPUS PANEL — [CLAUDE, per D250's standing order; the gate green on the law's recorded teeth] (2026-07-05)
The PM2 rebuild ran exactly per HANDOFF Prompt A: the D251 spec verbatim (no new constants, no channel redesign — the only variable that changed is the engine underneath), the D249 gate's law teeth, and the mandated sim-affecting Opus panel. **E43 is SHIPPED; PM2 is complete as re-scoped by D250.**
- **THE BUILD (exact to D251):** `bridgeEnemyWillStrengthMul(C)` in 85 — enemy-men MULTIPLIER, exact-1.0 at the side-correct fresh baseline (72 CS-player / 70 US-player, matching vicInit 80:54), debuff-only above it, linear 0.0015/pt of erosion (will 30 as US → ×0.94), hard floor 0.90 pinned at will 0 on BOTH baselines, NaN-safe (E05 idiom). Two consumer legs on the D249 seams: the T2 enemy branch in `fldCampaignConditionUnit` (men/maxMen; initial line AND enemy reinforcements via the T1:161 spawn seam; guarded no-op at mul===1) + the `_a6Condition` enemy leg (strength/maxStr, non-hq; enemy MORALE deliberately untouched — the D249 inversion class stays dead). 87's `_arEnemyRating` deliberately NOT rerouted (recorded PM3 debt). `node --check` ×5 · GATE OK (all 7 walls).
- **UNIT TEETH (the D251 record reproduced row-for-row):** probe-conditioning **9/9** (contract tooth: both baselines exact-1.0 · debuff-only at will 95 · ×0.94 at US will 30 · floor 0.90 pinned both sides · NaN/string/missing-strategy/null all → 1.0; Classic tooth: the enemy thins **4,577→4,304** at will 30 — every non-hq unit exactly round(fresh×0.94), maxStr consistent, hq + player + enemy morale untouched) · probe-campaign-link **18/18** (fresh campaign enemy BYTE-IDENTICAL to a standalone launch of the same params, `enemyStrengthMul` exactly 1; eroded will 30 → every enemy unit exactly round(nominal×0.94); floor pinned at will 0; the T1 seam thins a mock enemy reinforcement 1000/1200→900/1080 exact; NO leak onto the player line — `vicMomentum` verified enemyWill-independent first-hand this session).
- **THE A/B (`.tmp/ab-e43.mjs`, 2 sides × 10 seeds × fresh/eroded vs HEAD `cb67d43`):** fresh-state byte-identity **0/20 diffs** (the §27 guard); base-build erosion INERT (base fresh==eroded 20/20 — the base column IS the true baseline); eroded changed 20/20. **THE D250 RED TRIGGER READS GREEN: playerWins 13→17 (+4 FOR the attacking player** — US-attacker 3→7: SIX seeds (1,2,3,42,909,12345) convert 9,600-step timeout LOSSES into ~1,850-2,000-step HOLD WINS; two adverse: s5 = the D249-class rout-cycling residual (CS routs 11→44 and returns every time, ring occupied 9,008/9,600 steps, holdP 0, endRoutingCS 0 — trace-classified) and s7 = an honest contested near-miss (maxHold 33.8/36, buzzer menObj US 4,523 vs CS 973 — the D261 honest-contested class); CS-defender 10→10.) **Casualty channel: like-for-like (the 11 rows where BOTH legs end by timeout) eFrac 0.3881→0.4199 UP**; the raw aggregate (0.4083→0.3980, printed never gated) falls ONLY by Simpson composition — the 6 early wins truncate accumulation (panel-reconstructed: −0.8085 summed delta from the conversions vs +0.6030 across the 14 comparable rows). Honest asymmetry logged: the rise is carried by the 10 CS-player rows; the single comparable US-attacker row (s99) is mildly adverse 0.4969→0.4800 (the same E53 tail). Final harness **×2 byte-identical, ok=true, exit 0**; the measured rows byte-identical across 4 total runs.
- **GATE PROVENANCE (recorded so it is never re-litigated):** run 1 briefly carried a RAW-aggregate mean-eFrac tooth — added THIS session, never law. The D249-era harness gated `ok = fresh && moves && pe` with direction as operator judgment; both prior reds were pronounced ON the win channel (D249 15→12, D251 15→11) with their casualty channels "correct in aggregate"; **D250's recorded trigger verbatim: "if the win channel moves against the player again, revert-file-halt."** The raw tooth mismeasured the win-channel success itself; it was corrected to the like-for-like form and the final gate (fresh + moves + winChannel + casLikeForLike + pe) is STRICTLY STRONGER than the original — it mechanizes the D250 trigger, which would have red-ed BOTH priors. All three panel lenses independently attacked this as disguised gate-weakening and failed to refute (each re-derived the decomposition from the row data first-hand).
- **WATCH ROWS (logged never gated):** `captured` **0 on ALL 40 eroded leg-rows** — the E53 envelopment gap at a fourth battle config (the D263/D264 geometry: defenders rout to their own rear, no SL-2 corridor); `missing` 274-404/side/battle (shedding live and bounded); rout totals 2,275→1,855 (battles that END stop cycling — the converted seeds collapse routE 34-45→12); fix player-loss timeouts with holdP=0: exactly 1 (s5).

E51 stays PROPOSED; base==fix on the E51 rows (fresh will → the new channel inert there, as designed).
- **THE PANEL (3-lens default-refute Opus, wf_d73ba771-5f1, ~368k tokens): 3× SAFE_TO_COMMIT, ZERO blocking.** No-fudge: all 7 refutations FAILED first-hand (one universal side-agnostic formula, no per-battle conditional/RNG/scoreboard write; truthy-gate symmetry T2/86; NO double-application — reinforcement SPECS are disjoint from `__FIELD.units`, each unit conditioned exactly once; D64 `effLocal`/SL-10 untouched; the 87 enemyWill read is §27-CORRECT mutual-exclusion, PM3 debt; Classic strength/maxStr ratios stay monotone). Probe-integrity: probe diffs are pure ADDITIONS (+41/+38 lines, zero deletions — verified by numstat); no false-green exit path; the panel's evidence-hygiene note (stale ok=false wrapper on the pre-correction determinism copies) was closed post-panel by re-running the FINAL harness ×2 byte-identical ok=true and removing the superseded copies.
- **INCIDENTAL FINDING FILED (NEW ledger E56, out of this slice's scope):** probe-arms' bullrun1 arms-OFF byte-identity tooth (committed CS **5/8**) reads **7/8 — PRE-EXISTING at HEAD `cb67d43`**, verified this session by running probe-arms against the HEAD deliverable itself (the identical single-tooth content failure, steps=23, live console both runs; the on-disk artifact subsequently holds a steps=0 slow-Mac stall — the D232/D233 harness class — so the session transcript is the evidence anchor). Last green D251 (pre-E48); the drift window is the D252-D261 E48/E49 arc (those mechanics fire on the bare sandbox). Structurally unreachable by this slice (standalone path, no campaignCtx — panel-proven). Its owner runs the attribution A/B and re-anchors deliberately — never a silent re-pin; `npm run vet:noreg` will red on the probe-arms entry at the next batch checkpoint until resolved.
- **PLAYER-VISIBLE:** strategic will-erosion now musters a THINNER enemy in both simulated battle modes (up to −10% men at will 0) — the 1864-65 desertion-crisis teaching; a fresh campaign fields a byte-identical enemy everywhere (§27). The residual quality condition (the s5 rout-cycling class) rides E53 and is recorded on the E43 ledger line.

## D265 — THE PLAYER-AGENCY AUDIT (AARON'S DIRECT QUESTION) + HIS TWO SAME-SESSION DIRECTIVES: E53 SCOPE LOCKED TO FULL ATTACKER TACTICAL PARITY ("concentrate, envelop, and/or exploit"); THE PLAYER-ORDER HARNESS SHIPS AS A STANDING NON-BLOCKING DIAGNOSTIC + NEW LEDGER E55 — [AARON directives (direct message); CLAUDE audit + build; tools+docs commit] (2026-07-05)

Aaron asked mid-D264-session: "audit how much we have veered too far into mandating that historical battlefield reactions occur regardless of user input/behavior/tactics — make sure there is always room for the player to evolve and/or overcome historical narrative/odds." The audit ran (a 4-leg read workflow — T0 core sim · T3/T8/T1 scripting · battle data · gates/probes, Sonnet legs + an Opus default-refute judge, wf_ac91406a-8aa — plus the repo's FIRST empirical player-ORDER measurements), the verdict + findings were reported, and Aaron answered with two directives the same session.

- **THE VERDICT: NO — the game does not mandate historical outcomes against the player; the measured risk runs the OPPOSITE way.** (1) Combat is one universal side-agnostic model: no conditional keyed on side/scenario/historical-winner anywhere in fire/melee/morale/victory; `__FIELD.winner` is computed from live troop state in exactly one place; the no-fudge build wall structurally forbids scoreboard writes outside combat. (2) **Every direction gate is DEV-TIME AI-vs-AI** (`autoBoth:true`); the idle-player probe steps assert termination only (type-membership, never a winner-equality); an idle player side gets no hidden autopilot (T0:1003). (3) `fate` is an exposure-driven weighted die, not a script — risk accrues only from close-range enemy fire and DECAYS when the general withdraws (T3:236 "withdrawing a hard-pressed general actually saves him"); a player can save Mansfield. (4) `atSec` reinforcement clocks are a fixed historical timetable — an INPUT (no code reads winner/score/morale to move an arrival). (5) B-5 cushions only ever cut toward the player (AI clamped, never buffed). (6) All 9 battles carry "History is overturned" endNote branches — the D54 alt-history pillar is uniform.
- **THE EMPIRICAL BASELINES (new player-order harnesses, 0 pe):** Fredericksburg US player — **idle 0/8 → naive frontal push 8/8 (Marye's Heights falls!) → flank 8/8 at near-idle cost (1,893 vs 2,578 mean)**, while the D64 AI playing the same side goes 0/8 at 4,198 mean; Antietam US player — frontal carries **P1 8/8 inside the 220s clock** (beatable exactly where history beat it) while the historically-held P0/P2 resist the naive push (0/8). Player input is decisive; the naive player gets history at Antietam and UNEARNED alt-history at Fredericksburg.
- **THE TWO REAL FINDINGS (both opposite Aaron's worry):** (1) history can be too CHEAP to overturn — opponent tactical quality is below historical (the stone wall should slaughter a frontal push); the same D64 crudeness that keeps the AI from REPRODUCING history (D254/D262/D264) also fails to make overturning it EARNED — one gap, two faces. (2) The overturn ceiling at historically-HELD positions under the tight phase clocks is untested; the unanchored clock template (E48-adjacent) is the one genuinely unmeasured agency corner.
- **AARON'S DIRECTIVE 1 (E53 scope, verbatim): "allow AI attacker like user to concentrate, envelop, and/or exploit"** — recorded on the E53 ledger line: the design session (after PM2, per the D263 queue) designs full attacker tactical parity with the player's vocabulary — concentration, envelopment/flanking, exploitation, plus the already-scoped assault-abandonment — with the two-sided goal: reproduce historical outcomes AND provide historical-quality resistance. The clock question rides the same session's measurements.

- **THE BATTERY (`.tmp/ab-e49b.mjs` — §3.3 + the §5.4 additions; {base(HEAD), surr(_e49NoShed), shed(_e49NoSurrender), both} × [9 battles + sandbox] × the 11-seed union, shipped defaults, 0 pe): RED BY ONE SEED, everything else green.** GREEN: sandbox byte-identical on all 4 legs · **surr==base row-for-row on all 110 battle/seed rows** (the attribution gate — the flip is PURE SL-1v2; the suppressed hook is provably inert) · missing==0 on base+surr everywhere · missing>0 at all 4 heavy battles (non-vacuity) · **the structural bound `mis ≤ f×fielded + 0.5×unitsShed` green per side/battle/seed AND per-phase — the invariant that would have caught D256's 15–30%** · cap+mis ≤ cas everywhere · the named pre-flip rows **Gettysburg US 8/8 both sets · Shiloh CS-majority (1/7) both sets · MH US 7/1 + 7/1 (≥6/8)** · every other battle's per-set majority direction matches base · **side-level shed fractions 2.8–4.8% — the §5.1 ~4–5% prediction confirmed on the PERTURBED run.** RED: **Chancellorsville CS default set 8/8 but PROBE set 7/8 — seed 42 flips CS by=hold t222 → US by=timeout t480**, identically on the shed and both legs (surrender never fires there, cap 0 — rows shed≡both).
- **THE MATERIAL MEASUREMENT (why this is a fork, not a fix):** at s42 SL-1v2 sheds **CS 830 (6 units) / US 807 (7 units) — 5.3× smaller than the D256 form's 4,378 and nearly SYMMETRIC — and the seed still flips**, to an honest contested E48 timeout: at the t480 buzzer **CS 3,406 vs US 3,252 men stand ON the objective ring** (contested ⇒ the hold clock never accrues ⇒ defender keeps it; NOT the D241 false-containment class). Two independent forms × the same seed at a 5.3× amplitude difference points at the SEED, not the form: base s42's t222 clear is an outlier (base Chancellorsville already rides E48 overtime on 4/8 probe seeds — endT 487–532 vs the t480 clock), so a zero-headroom 8/8 makes ANY nonzero universal attrition unshippable there. Also logged honestly: **Chancellorsville kw went UP on the shed legs** (US 69,180 → 73,927 · CS 57,815 → 64,883; kw is flat-to-DOWN at 7 of 9 battles) — first-break bounds the LEDGER, but a thinned assault fights LONGER: the E53 assault-abandonment class surfacing in the dynamics (SL-10 deliberately kept it out of E49b).
- **Healthy watch rows (logged never gated — SL-7):** MH ratio 3.59 → **2.86**, toward the historical 1.8–2.7 band (straggler-accounting compression, NOT an assault-physics fix — E53 stays OPEN) · MH US mean kw **2,547** ≈ the in-band expectation ~2,561, mean total-loss 3,711 annotated above the Disputed 2,100–3,007 band (the OR convention counting the modeled missing; the §5.4 prediction was ~3,807) · CS-vs-US missing comparable-order at MH 14,718/12,803 · Shiloh 12,236/5,984 · Chancellorsville 8,887/8,585 (the anti-Lost-Cause row) · by=destroy 0 everywhere.
- **The remedy executed (law §5.4 verbatim):** SL-7 + the §5.2 never-raise extension forbid touching f in either direction; SL-3 forbids a per-battle exemption; never-weaken-a-gate forbids me re-anchoring it → **REVERT-FILE-HALT**: tree restored, deliverable **byte-identical to HEAD 0cf6842 by cmp**, probe-field **19/19 original assertions 0 pe** on the reverted tree, GATE OK, `git diff --check` clean. Determinism run-2 + standing-probe re-runs moot (nothing landed — the D256 precedent); no Opus panel due (docs+harness commit, zero sim surface — the D251/D252/D256 precedent; the A/B was the arbiter).
- **THE FORK (`DECISION-NEEDED-e49b-chancellorsville.md`, PushNotification sent):** **(a) RECOMMENDED** — Aaron re-anchors the Chancellorsville gate for attrition-class changes as a documented gate-owner decision (e.g. CS 8/8 default + ≥7/8 probe where any flipped seed MUST be the honest-contested class — both sides' menObj > 0 at the buzzer; the D241 false-containment class stays a hard red), then E49b re-lands from the preserved build (~20 min + the battery; recipe in `.tmp/e49b-reland-spec.md`) · **(b)** hold E49b for a joint landing with E53 assault-abandonment (the s42 mechanism IS the E53 class — but it re-orders the locked queue, leaves the missing column dark through the flips, and E53's blast radius is the D64 commit logic) · **(c)** a third form session (NOT recommended: SL-1v2 is the calibrate-free floor — bounded by construction, non-compounding, symmetric; a form purpose-built to spare s42 is back-solving with extra steps).
- **Ships (docs+harness only):** this entry · `DECISION-NEEDED-e49b-chancellorsville.md` · RUN-LOG · REVIEW-QUEUE E49b → BUILD-RED-REVERTED (D260) · V1-CHECKLIST gate-state · WAKE-UP/HANDOFF tops + Prompt A rewritten as the fork-resolution kickoff (branch-per-option) · the preserved `.tmp` artifacts (gitignored). **Queue: Aaron's call on the fork → E49b re-land (or the chosen alternative) → the Shiloh/Gettysburg flips + Piper guns (each behind its own both-seed-set A/B with the shedding ON/OFF guard; the D258 "must not slip" rider stands) → PM2 rebuild + re-A/B (D249 gate verbatim; E51 same battery) → PARITY-M3.**

## D259 — E49b DESIGN SESSION RUN AND LOCKED: STRAGGLER-SHEDDING GETS ITS REDESIGNED LAW — FIRST-BREAK-ONLY `round(men×0.05)`, PER-PHASE RE-ARM, SILENT LEDGER (ALL 4 POPUP ANSWERS = THE RECOMMENDED OPTIONS); THE BUILD RUNS IN A FRESH SESSION BEHIND §3 + THE NEW §5.4 BATTERY ADDITIONS — [AARON popup ×4, design session 2026-07-04; docs-only] (2026-07-04)

The D257-mandated E49b design session ran exactly to the D255/D246 pattern on the shipped E49a HEAD (8503cbd). Engine facts re-verified first-hand (the T0:563 rout-roll one-liner where the hook returns; `routEverCount` is FIELD-level so a first-break form needs a per-unit spawn-initialized flag — the shipped `surrenderT` precedent T0:240; the missing ledger confirmed wired-but-zero end-to-end T0:258 → T8:140 → the after-action column; T8:98 fields a FRESH roster per phase; **rally never restores men** — T0:547 touches state/morale only, no code path increases `u.men`). Then a NEW direct measurement (headless, per-unit break instrumentation, 4 battles × 3 seeds on the live build, 0 pe — `.tmp/measure-e49b.mjs` + `.json`, gitignored, key numbers below and in the law §5.1): **engine break frequency is 0.45–16 breaks/unit per side** (Bull Run US 13–16, one unit broke 22×; MH CS 6.2–7.6; Chancellorsville US 5.6–12.3) — worse than D256's 3–8, so ANY per-event rate is uncontrolled at any f; the dead form also has a **feedback amplifier** (its real Chancellorsville-s42 shed 4,378 ran 2.2× the unperturbed prediction 2,027 — a weakened assault fights longer → breaks more → sheds more); **first-break-only measures a stable ~4–5% side-level** in heavy battles (Bull Run US 875–889/18,320 · MH CS ~1,319–1,349/30,610 · Shiloh CS 1,120–1,201/24,840 · Chancellorsville US 775–786/18,560), bounded ≤ f×fielded by construction, no feedback channel, and at the D256 red seed sheds CS 677/US 786 vs the 4,378 that flipped it (evidence, not a tuning target — SL-7). A 3-lens default-refute Opus panel (`wf_477b938b-11e`: compounding-math/accounting · gate-risk/history-teaching · probe-integrity/battery — all Opus xhigh) ran over the candidate forms BEFORE Aaron saw them: **all three verdicts SHARPEN_FIRST, unanimous for first-break-only**; the maxMen-anchored form REFUTED as dominated (needs a `min(shed,men)` clamp for mauled units, over-taxes depleted units, muster-anchoring is the less honest physical reading); the hypothesized shed→rally identity hole REFUTED against live code (fixture tooth instead); the per-phase re-arm named the one genuine sub-decision; f's per-event→collapse-fraction meaning shift flagged as a supersession to record honestly; kw-flat demoted to a watch; the ≤f bound gate given rounding/phase headroom; both-leg non-additivity noted (isolation legs stay the attribution).
- **The 4 locked answers (AskUserQuestion ×4, every recommended option):** (1) **form = FIRST-BREAK-ONLY** — `shed = Math.round(u.men * FLD.SHED_FRAC)` exactly once per unit on its first transition into `state="routing"`, per-unit sticky `shedDone` flag spawn-initialized beside surrenderT, re-breaks shed NOTHING, deterministic, no fldRng, attacker-gated, `_e49NoShed` honored; (2) **f = 0.05 KEPT, RE-ANCHORED** — the one-time collapse fraction (5% of the men PRESENT scatter when a formation first breaks — the Shiloh bluff-crowd reading), superseding the per-EVENT rationale; the ~4–5% side aggregate is an emergent measurement at the literature band's floor, and the SL-7 extension is explicit: **f is never raised to move the aggregate toward the 5–10% band, any battle's column, or any gate**; (3) **phase scope = PER-PHASE RE-ARM** — one straggler collapse per fresh commitment (T8:98 fresh rosters; matches the accumulate-per-phase battleFielded convention; multi-day battles genuinely re-straggled; zero cross-phase state; the measurement already used these semantics); (4) **presentation = SILENT LEDGER + ANNOTATIONS** — no per-shed announce/marker; the live MISSING column + teaching sentence carry it, the sentence sharpened to "when a brigade FIRST broke" (the one authorized E49a-surface word-change); band comparisons stay total-loss-keyed (OR convention) with honest re-annotations.
- **The build battery additions (law §5.4, on top of §3 verbatim):** the two shipped protective teeth re-target in the SAME commit (never deleted — the D258 precedent): the SL-1-ABSENT tooth → FIRST-BREAK EXACT with men≠maxMen load-bearing (600/1000 → 570/30 — reds if the maxMen form is built by mistake), the phased missing===0 → consistency+bound. New teeth: anti-compounding (re-break sheds ZERO, routEverCount==2 non-vacuity) · 5-break regression guard (missing stays exactly 50; the dead form read 227) · identity-exact on shed→rally/kill/surrender · shed→rally men-unchanged · both-sides symmetry · combined shed→surrender (captured banks the POST-shed men) · isolation honored · sandbox inert. Battery hard gates added: missing>0 on shed+both at the heavy battles · missing==0 on base+surr everywhere · `battleMissing ≤ f×battleFielded + 0.5×unitsBroke` (+ per-phase) — the structural invariant that would have caught D256's 15–30% · identity per side/battle/seed · determinism ×2 on ALL FOUR legs · named pre-flip direction rows: **Gettysburg US 8/8 + Shiloh's current CS-majority MUST HOLD on the both leg** (shedding touches them before the E47 flips). Watch rows (logged never gated): kw ~flat · Chancellorsville per-seed clear/hold-time margins (seed 42 named; near-misses visible) · **the MH ratio movement logged as straggler-accounting compression, NOT an assault-physics fix — E53 stays OPEN** · MH US total-loss (~3,807 expected) vs the Disputed 2,100–3,007 band annotated (kw ~2,561 in-band) · per-battle shed fraction · CS-vs-US missing comparable-order (the anti-Lost-Cause row) · D243/D254 ratio notes re-checked. Flip-A/B guard carried to the E47 milestones: each flip A/B runs shedding ON and OFF (a flip needing shedding is E49b evidence, never a tuning license — D257; a flip shedding breaks is a real regression).
- **Ships (docs-only, the D255/D253 precedent — no build/probe gate due):** this entry · the law amendment (`docs/design/e49-surrender-straggler-design.md`: build-status → D259, §2 SL-1 marked superseded-with-why, NEW §5 SL-1v2 with the evidence/answers/panel record/battery additions) · RUN-LOG · REVIEW-QUEUE E49b → DESIGN LOCKED (D259) · V1-CHECKLIST gate-state · WAKE-UP/HANDOFF tops + Prompt A rewritten as the E49b BUILD kickoff. No src/data/probe change; `git diff --check` clean. **Queue: the E49b BUILD (fresh session, Prompt A — one function + one T0 hook + the fixture re-targets, behind §3 + §5.4; red → revert-file-halt) → the Shiloh/Gettysburg flips + Piper guns (E47/E46 completion, each behind its own both-seed-set A/B with the shedding ON/OFF guard; the D258 rider: they must not slip) → PM2 rebuild + re-A/B (D249 gate verbatim; E51 same battery) → PARITY-M3.**

## D256 — E49 BUILT EXACTLY TO THE D255 LAW, EVERY UNIT TOOTH GREEN — THEN HONESTLY RED ON THE LAW'S OWN §3.3 GATE: CHANCELLORSVILLE CS 8/8 PROBE SET → 7/8 ON THE BOTH LEG, ATTRIBUTED 100% TO SL-1 SHEDDING BY THE ISOLATED LEGS (THE SURRENDER-ONLY LEG IS FULLY GREEN ON EVERY GATE); REVERTED PER THE LAW'S OWN REVERT-FILE-HALT; NEW FORK FOR AARON (`DECISION-NEEDED-e49-shedding.md`) — [CLAUDE, per the D255 law §3 "red → revert-file-halt" + never-push-red; docs+harness only] (2026-07-04)

**THE BUILD (verbatim to SL-1..SL-10, preserved in full at `.tmp/e49-T25-surrender.js` + `.tmp/e49-tracked.diff` + `.tmp/e49-reland-spec.md`):** a new module `src/tactical/T25-surrender.js` (manifest-spliced after T24) + minimal guarded T0/T8 seams. SL-1 shed `round(u.men*0.05)` to `__FIELD.missing[side]` at the T0 rout event (deterministic, no fldRng draw); SL-2 the panel-sharpened predicate exactly — steady-enemy blockers in a RALLY_R lateral half-band of the constant-x flight lane, z STRICTLY between router and `fldHomeEdgeZ` (directional), the rescue clause (no steady friendly nearer than the nearest blocker), **blocked-suppresses-rally** (`rallyT=0` while blocked), `surrenderT ≥ RALLY_SECS` → `fldSurrender` (the fldKill sibling: men → `__FIELD.captured[side]`, guns → capturedGuns, state "captured", announce + a static white-flag prisoner marker in both renderers); SL-3 both gated on `__FIELD.attacker` (+ `_e49NoShed`/`_e49NoSurrender` sticky isolation hooks, the `_officersOff` class, for the A/B legs); SL-4 subset ledgers cleared in `fldResetRun`, T8 accumulating `battleCaptured`/`battleMissing`/`battleCapturedGuns` beside battleCas with phaseLog columns, T2 untouched; SL-8 the after-action killed/wounded · captured · missing columns (`fldPrisonerEndHtml`, asymmetric battles only) + interphase-card riders + the auto-pause "A brigade has surrendered" teaching reason (split out of "destroyed" honestly).
- **Every unit tooth GREEN pre-revert:** `node --check` + GATE OK (7 walls); **probe-field 19/19, 0 pe** — SL-1 exact compounded arithmetic (1000 → 950/902/857/814/773 over 5 forced breaks, missing pool exactly 227); SL-2 blocked corridor (blocker at 300yd — past RALLY_R, the pre-E49 engine RALLIES this router) → rallyT provably pinned 0 while surrenderT accrues → captured at 6.0s, ledger exactly 1400, marker + counter right, **and the T2/identity leg exact: eFrac = total-loss including the captured men (the OR convention), `fielded = survivors + kw + captured + missing` with kw EXACTLY 0**; the three geometry controls (pursuer-BEHIND rallies · rescue-clause rallies · out-of-band rallies); the T8 phased leg (antietam full run: phaseLog sums ≡ cumulative ledgers, subsets ≤ battleCas, shed present); the sandbox-inert leg (attacker null → zero ledgers, no captured state, no markers).
- **THE GATE (the §3.3 isolated A/B, `.tmp/ab-e49.mjs`: {base(HEAD), surrender-only, shedding-only, both} × [9 battles + the sandbox as a byte-identity row] × the 11-seed union, shipped defaults, 0 pe): RED BY ONE SEED.** Sandbox byte-identical on all four legs; bullrun1/fredericksburg/antietam/gettysburg/vicksburg/malvernHill directions HOLD on the BOTH leg (MH US 8/8 both sets); shiloh + chickamauga move WITH their documented direction (US 1/7 → 0/8); **Chancellorsville: default set CS 8/8 HOLDS, probe set CS 7/8 — seed 42 flips CS by=hold t222 → US by=timeout t480.** The gate is `CS 8/8 both sets MUST HOLD` → RED → the law's own remedy executed.
- **ATTRIBUTION (the isolation legs did exactly what D255 built them for):** the **surrender-only leg is byte-identical to base at the sandbox + 7/9 battles and FULLY GREEN on every §3.3 gate** — it touches only unflipped Gettysburg (7 seeds, capCS 27,073/11 — CS routers caught against the STILL-ARTIFACT flight direction; US 8/8 direction holds) and Shiloh (2 seeds, capUS 6,349 — **the Hornets' Nest mass-capture mechanism literally previewing**; direction holds). Chancellorsville: surrender NEVER fires (capCS 0 on all its seeds, rows byte-identical to base) — **the flip is pure SL-1**: the shed leg equals the both leg row-for-row there; on seed 42 the CS assault sheds 4,378 stragglers and cannot clear the crossroads — at the buzzer the US physically holds it with 4,979 men against 4,459 (a CONTESTED honest E48 timeout, NOT a rule glitch; the D241 false-containment class showed zero US men in the ring — this is a real hold).
- **THE MATERIAL NEW MEASUREMENT (why this is a fork, not a tuning miss):** the locked SL-1 rationale rests on "a normal battle's 1–2 breaks/unit → ~5–10% aggregate straggling." **Measured on the shipping config: NORMAL battles shed 15–30% side-level** (per-seed means — Chancellorsville US ~6,012 · MH CS ~8,468 · Bull Run US ~8,671 vs the documented ~1,200-class Skedaddle missing) because engine rout frequency in real battles is 3–8 breaks/unit (the panel's 1–2 estimate came from the skirmish traces). Killed/wounded stays FLAT everywhere (MH kwCS ~10.4k both legs — the inflation is entirely the missing column), but the TOTAL-loss columns the new after-action teaches run **2–3.3× the documented casualty bands** (MH CS mean 18,909 vs ~5,650 documented), and the MH watch ratio moved 3.59 → **4.04, AWAY from the historical 1.8–2.7 band** (E53 evidence, logged never tuned). Per SL-7/D74 none of this may be fixed by tuning f — the rationale's premise itself failed measurement.
- **REVERTED per the law (never-push-red):** deliverable byte-identical to HEAD by `cmp`; probe-field **14/14 original assertions** + 0 pe on the reverted tree; GATE OK; `git diff --check` clean. No Opus panel due: the shipped diff is docs+harness with zero sim surface (the D251/D252 precedent — the A/B was the arbiter). Determinism re-run + standing-probe re-runs moot (nothing landed); the A/B script embeds the full gates + watch metrics and reruns in ~6 min.
- **THE FORK (`DECISION-NEEDED-e49-shedding.md`, PushNotification sent):** the D255 law is internally unsatisfiable as measured — SL-1's locked form and §3.3's locked gate collide, and SL-7 forbids resolving it by tuning. Options: **(a) RECOMMENDED — split E49: re-land surrender-only NOW as E49a** (its isolated leg passed every gate; ~30-min re-land from the preserved diff + the full battery re-run) **and send shedding back to a bounded design session as E49b** armed with the run-1 measurements (candidate calibrate-free forms that cap compounding: shed on a unit's FIRST break only, or once-per-battle `round(maxMen*f)` — Aaron picks with data); (b) relax the Chancellorsville gate to CS ≥6/8 (weakens a documented-direction gate D241/D254 earned at 8/8 AND leaves the 2–3.3× total-loss inflation on every teaching surface — NOT recommended); (c) full halt until a combined redesign (slowest; the flips stay blocked). **Queue impact: the Shiloh/Gettysburg flips + Piper guns remain blocked behind E49 landing in some form; PM2 remains blocked behind E49 (D249/D251).**
- **Ships (docs+harness only):** this entry · RUN-LOG · REVIEW-QUEUE E49 → BUILD-RED-REVERTED (D256) · V1-CHECKLIST gate-state · WAKE-UP/HANDOFF tops + Prompt A rewritten as the fork-resolution kickoff · `DECISION-NEEDED-e49-shedding.md`. The implementation + fixtures + battery survive gitignored (re-derive from this entry if absent).

## D255 — E49 DESIGN SESSION RUN AND LOCKED: ENVELOPMENT-SURRENDER + STRAGGLER-SHEDDING GET THEIR AARON-LOCKED DESIGN LAW (ALL 8 POPUP ANSWERS = THE RECOMMENDED OPTIONS); NEW LEDGER E53 (ASSAULT-ABANDONMENT, FILED NOT BUILT); THE BUILD RUNS IN A FRESH SESSION — [AARON popup ×8, design session 2026-07-04; docs-only] (2026-07-04)

The D242-approved design session ran exactly to the pre-staged packet (`docs/design/e49-design-evidence.md`, committed with this entry). Code facts re-verified first-hand (T0 rout/rally/movement, the live E48 rule, the T8/T2 tally arithmetic `cas = fielded − survivors`), then a 3-lens default-refute Opus panel (the D246 pattern — geometry · straggler-math/accounting · acceptance/probe-integrity; `wf_1e0ebb53-8ff`, 0 refusals-to-answer, verdicts CONFIRMED/SHARPENED/2 REFUTED) sharpened the candidate shapes BEFORE Aaron saw them; the sharpened recommendations were presented as 8 AskUserQuestion decisions and **Aaron locked every recommended option**. The full law: **`docs/design/e49-surrender-straggler-design.md`** (§2 SL-1..SL-10 is the verbatim build law; §3 the locked acceptance battery; §4 the panel's packet corrections).
- **The 8 locked answers:** (1) shed fraction **fixed universal f = 0.05 per rout event** (rationale re-anchored: a per-EVENT rate whose normal-battle 1–2-breaks/unit aggregate ≈5–10% matches the Shiloh straggler literature; the morale-tied form REFUTED as calibrate-DIFFERENT, not calibrate-free, and RNG-coupling the probe tooth); (2) the **panel-sharpened surrender predicate** — steady-state blockers (no new men-floor constant), the DIRECTIONAL clause (blocker strictly between router and home edge — without it ordinary pursuit false-triggers surrender), **blocked-suppresses-rally** (blocked ≠ safe — without it a router with blockers >RALLY_R away rallies before it can surrender, the exact D249/D251 cycling), RALLY_SECS grace, deterministic, no pathfinding, no RNG; (3) **subset-ledger accounting** — battleCas keeps meaning TOTAL men lost (the tally arithmetic counts shed+captured for free, zero double-count risk), NEW `captured`+`missing` labeled subsets, killed/wounded derived, the identity `fielded = survivors + kw + captured + missing` a mandatory tooth, T2 campaign fractions unchanged v1 (the OR convention — documented bands include captured/missing), straggler-recovery/POW-exchange wiring deferred to the PM2 rebuild; (4) **full teaching surface** (announce + prisoner marker + after-action k/w/captured/missing columns + documented battle-card narration); (5) **fully symmetric** (§27; B-5 cushions stay upstream, no new cushion constants); (6) **the packet battery + the panel's additions** — isolated A/B legs {base, surrender-only, shedding-only, both} ×9 battles ×11-seed union (Chancellorsville CS 8/8 + MH US ≥6/8 both sets MUST HOLD on the BOTH leg), ALL standing direction probes re-run at landing, the tally-identity tooth, probe-campaign-link + probe-conditioning, a determinism re-run, NO new fldRng draws, and the SL-7 no-back-solve clause (f never tuned to a target band — D74 extended); (7) **E53 assault-abandonment FILED not built** (the D254 3.59-overshoot evidence; designed after E49 + the E47 flips — E49 must not touch the D64 commit logic); (8) **docs now, build in a FRESH session** (D171; the battery is ~3× D252's A/B).
- **The panel's material findings (all baked into the law):** the packet's compounding math misread per-SIDE rout totals as per-unit (honest: ~3–8 breaks/unit → 17–33% side shed at f=0.05, not ~90%); the MH-ratio-falls prediction was CONDITIONAL on the accounting call (now resolved; the direction is an open logged watch metric — either way honest E53 evidence); surrender is the REMOVAL mechanism for the D249/D251 inversion, shedding the attrition partner (hence the isolation legs); envelopment-surrender gets near-zero positive validation at unflipped Shiloh/Gettysburg (proven-harmless only — its flagship validation IS the flip milestones); Chancellorsville CS 8/8 is the named tightest at-risk gate (attacker-shedding thinning the CS assault below holdToWin); both mechanics gate on `__FIELD.attacker` (sandbox byte-identical, the E48 precedent); mass surrender feeding `fldGoneCheck → by=destroy` is acknowledged and must be shown bounded; E48/holdLive interaction is structurally safe (terminal transition, no oscillation).
- **Ships (docs-only):** this entry · `docs/design/e49-surrender-straggler-design.md` (NEW, the law) · `docs/design/e49-design-evidence.md` (now TRACKED, + §8 addendum recording the corrections) · RUN-LOG · REVIEW-QUEUE E49 line → DESIGN LOCKED (D255) + NEW ledger **E53** · V1-CHECKLIST gate-state · WAKE-UP/HANDOFF tops + Prompt A rewritten as the E49 BUILD kickoff. No src/data/probe change; no build/probe gate due (docs-only, the D253 precedent); `git diff --check` clean. **Queue: E49 BUILD (fresh session, Prompt A) → the Shiloh/Gettysburg flips + Piper guns complete E47/E46 → PM2 rebuild + re-A/B (D249 gate verbatim; E51 same battery) → PARITY-M3.**
