## D252 — E48 BUILT AS THE COMPLETE-THE-HOLD OVERTIME RULE AND PROVEN ON ITS TARGET (CHANCELLORSVILLE CS 8/8 BOTH SETS) — THEN HONESTLY REVERTED: THE SAME A/B EXPOSES MALVERN HILL'S DOCUMENTED REPULSE AS 100% CLOCK-DEPENDENT, AND FIXING THAT IS AN UNAPPROVED ACCURATE-INPUTS MILESTONE (NEW E52, AARON'S CALL) — [CLAUDE, per Aaron's E48 approval + never-push-red + never-weaken-a-gate; fork surfaced] (2026-07-04)
The approved E48 milestone was designed, built, tooth-proven, and full-battery A/B'd this session — and the A/B did exactly what it exists to do: it proved the rule right at its target and exposed that one battle's documented direction gate was resting on the artifact being removed. Since both approved sub-forms red that gate and the honest fix is sim-affecting content work outside the approved queue, the slice was REVERTED (deliverable byte-identical to the D251 HEAD by `cmp`; probe-field 13/13 original assertions) and the fork surfaced (`DECISION-NEEDED-e48-malvern.md` + push). **Nothing sim-affecting ships.** The evidence:
- **The design (decide-&-log, from the code):** of option (b)'s two approved sub-forms, **complete-the-hold** wins — at the asymmetric time limit the defender's containment fires ONLY when the attacker's hold clock is not actively accruing (the exact `fldObjectiveStep` predicate: attacker men on the objective, zero steady defenders); if it is accruing, the battle plays on until the hold completes (attacker wins by hold) or the accrual breaks (the timeout fires THAT tick). Zero new constants (the rejected holdSecs-fraction form would set the win ratio by threshold choice — D241's own objection); no third outcome class (the contested/draw form touches every winBy consumer AND is empirically worse — below); bounded by construction (overtime < holdToWin, the clock accrues 1:1); the symmetric sandbox untouched. Implementation (~12 lines, T0 only): `__FIELD.holdLive` stamped per tick in fldObjectiveStep · the fldCheckVictory asymmetric timeout gains `&& !__FIELD.holdLive` · reset in fldResetRun · an "OVERTIME: the position is falling" suffix on the existing objective HUD chip. Phased battles inherit per-phase automatically (same fldCheckVictory → `_fldPhaseResolved`; T8 resets t per phase).
- **The tooth (probe-field 13→14 pre-revert, all green):** a synthetic asymmetric fixture — (a) mid-hold at the buzzer (hold 10/40) completes the fall in overtime, w=att by=hold PAST the clock, overtime 29.1s ≤ holdToWin; (b) a contested objective (one steady defender in the radius) still times out to the defender AT the clock; (c) an unattended objective times out AT the clock (byte-identical pre-E48 behavior).
- **The A/B (all 9 battles × the 11-seed union of both documented sets × base `084fa40`/fix, shipped defaults, 198 headless battles, 0 pe — `.tmp/ab-e48.mjs`, results `.tmp/ab-e48-result.json`):** **bullrun1 (CS 8/8) · fredericksburg (CS 8/8) · shiloh (CS 7/8) · vicksburg (US 8/8) · chickamauga (CS 7/8) all byte-identical row-for-row.** **Antietam:** winners + all phase directions HOLD (agg CS 8/8 both sets; one seed's per-phase overtime jitter, +5 cas — and the D243 rider is answered: complete-the-hold creates NO phase draws, so the T8 draw band stays structurally unreachable exactly as the D243 header annotation says). **Gettysburg:** agg US 8/8 + all phase directions HOLD (one seed pays +1,748 CS casualties in a longer mid-hold phase). **Chancellorsville — THE TARGET, PROVEN: 4/4 → CS 8/8 on BOTH sets** — every D241 false containment (US `by=timeout` at 480 with the CS mid-hold ON the crossroads) plays out to the historical Confederate hold (`by=hold` at 487-532s), and the casualty ratio stays in the honest D241 band (~0.82 attacker-pays vs historical 0.77).
- **The blocker — Malvern Hill: US 8/8 → 4/4 on BOTH sets (the documented gate `usWins ≥ 6` on probe-malvern-hill:300 would go RED).** The base sweep shows the US NEVER wins on the field — all 8/8 are `by=timeout` — and the buzzer diagnostic (`.tmp/diag-e48-malvern.mjs`, all 9 seeds, fix build) shows the position FALLING at the clock on EVERY seed: CS 5,286-13,814 men mid-hold on the Crew-house objective (hold clocks 1-116s of 135), **US ZERO men in the radius, 4-6 US units routing** — physically identical to the Chancellorsville fall (comparator: 12,462 men, 133/140). Under E48, 6/9 holds complete (endT 519-620); on 3 seeds the overtime honestly re-breaks the assault and the containment stands. **The old US 8/8 was manufactured by the clock, not the defense** — the D240 panel's recorded calibration gap (post-flip atk/def 1.34-1.45 vs the historical 1.8-2.7 band; "possible later pass on the cautious doctrine / universal artillery weighting — deliberately NOT touched") is now load-bearing. Teaching liability (the E47/D235 class, raised on the PUBLIC game): the sim currently says Lee's assaults end the day ON the crest every seed, with only the buzzer narrating the repulse.
- **The contested/draw sub-form was evaluated on the same evidence and is strictly worse:** at the buzzer the CS is accruing on ALL 9 Malvern seeds → all 9 become draws → US 0/8 (gate red, harder), while at Chancellorsville it converts the false containments to draws instead of the historical CS victory (partial credit at the target). Complete-the-hold dominates it on both battles.
- **Why revert instead of fix-in-slice (the laws):** re-pinning Malvern Hill's `usWins ≥ 6` gate would weaken a direction gate rooted in documented events (forbidden); a per-battle E48 exemption or threshold tuned to keep Malvern while flipping Chancellorsville is D74 outcome-engineering; and the honest fix — an E46/D243-class accurate-inputs correction of Malvern Hill's under-modeled defense (candidate gaps recorded in E52: US infantry engaged looks light — Morell 3,400/Couch 3,400 modeled vs ~17,800 engaged on the crest line historically, Sykes 2,500, no Meagher/Sickles late brigades; gun-line rotation depth + siege guns + gunboat fire; CS 20,800 modeled vs ~30,000 assaulting — ALL to be verified citation-grade before any edit, D92) — is **sim-affecting content work Aaron never approved.** Options in `DECISION-NEEDED-e48-malvern.md`: (a) RECOMMENDED — approve the E52 correction (research workflow → corrections → A/B under E48 → E48 re-lands from this spec in the same session, ~30-min rebuild + the surviving `.tmp` battery); (b) run the approved E49 design session first and take E52+E48 after (leaves Chancellorsville's proven false containment live on the public game meanwhile); (c) Aaron's own alternative.
- **What ships in this commit (docs + harness only):** this entry; RUN-LOG; the E48 ledger flip (BUILT+PROVEN-then-reverted, BLOCKED pending E52 or Aaron's alternative) + NEW ledger **E52**; V1-CHECKLIST notes; WAKE-UP/HANDOFF tops + Prompt A (two-branch: Aaron's answer → E52+E48; else → the E49 design session); `DECISION-NEEDED-e48-malvern.md`; the probe-field D232-class screenshot repair (120s budget — the 30s default flaked this session on the HEAD-identical deliverable before any assertion ran; assertions untouched, 13/13 after). Gates on the reverted tree: GATE OK (7 walls) · `cmp` byte-identity vs HEAD · probe-field 13/13 · 0 pe · diff-check clean. (No Opus panel: the shipped diff is docs+harness with zero sim surface — the D249/D251 precedent; the A/B + diagnostic were the slice's arbiters. The exact implementation + fixture + re-land gate battery survive gitignored in **`.tmp/e48-reland-spec.md`** beside `.tmp/ab-e48.mjs` / `.tmp/diag-e48-malvern.mjs` / their result JSONs; re-derive from this entry if absent.)

## D251 — PARITY-M2-STRENGTH (THE D250 REBUILD) ATTEMPTED, HONESTLY RED ON THE SAME DIRECTION A/B, AND REVERTED — THE INVERSION IS NOT CHANNEL-SPECIFIC: PRE-E49, *ANY* DEFENDER-DEBUFF CONVERTS INTO A DEFENSE; PM2 (EITHER CHANNEL) IS BLOCKED BEHIND E49, AND E48 ALONE WOULD NOT RESCUE IT (MEASURED) — [CLAUDE, per the never-push-red law + the D249 gate standing verbatim; evidence for Aaron] (2026-07-04)
The D250-ordered strength-channel rebuild was completed exactly to spec and every unit-level tooth passed — then the mandated direction A/B failed on the SAME win channel with the SAME signature as D249, and the whole slice was REVERTED (deliverable byte-identical to the D250 HEAD by `cmp`; conditioning 7/7 original assertions, campaign-link 17/17, arms 23/23 green on the reverted tree). **Nothing sim-affecting ships.** What was built and proven before the red:
- **The contract (85 `bridgeEnemyWillStrengthMul`, the §6.1-amended design law):** returns a MULTIPLIER for enemy men — exact-**1.0** at the side-correct baseline (72 CS-player / 70 US-player), debuff-only above it, linear **0.0015/pt of erosion** (will 30 as US player → ×0.94), **hard floor 0.90** (pinned at will 0 for BOTH baselines: 1−72·.0015 and 1−70·.0015 both floor), NaN-safe (E05 idiom — NaN/non-number/missing strategy all read as baseline → 1.0). Two consumer legs on the D249 seams: the T2 enemy branch in `fldCampaignConditionUnit` (men/maxMen; covers the initial line AND enemy reinforcements via the T1 spawn seam) + the `_a6Condition` enemy leg (strength/maxStr, non-hq). 87's rating term untouched. Unit teeth ALL green pre-revert: probe-conditioning **9/9** (both baselines exact-1.0 · NaN/type/missing-strategy safety · floor pinned both sides · the Classic leg thins the enemy 4,577→4,304 at will 30, every unit exactly round(fresh×0.94), hq + player untouched) and probe-campaign-link **18/18** (fresh campaign enemy byte-identical to a standalone launch of the same params · eroded will 30 → every enemy unit exactly round(nominal×0.94) · floor pinned · no leak onto the player line).
- **The A/B (the D249 gate verbatim — `.tmp/ab-e43.mjs`, 2 sides × 10 seeds × fresh/eroded vs committed HEAD `4ca8d83`):** fresh state byte-identical **0/20 diffs** (the §27 guard holds); eroded changed 20/20 with the casualty channel CORRECT and stronger than the D249 morale form (mean enemy loss fraction .3559→**.3987**; mean enemy survivors 5,854→**5,132**) — **but the WIN channel moved AGAINST the attacking player again: 15→11** (US-player/attacker rows 5→1; CS-player/defender rows 10→10), all 4 flips the exact D249 fingerprint: base `w=US by=hold` at 1,727-2,322 steps → eroded `w=CS by=timeout` at the full 9,600.
- **The trace (all 4 flipped seeds, fix vs base — `.tmp/trace-pm2s.mjs`):** the 6% thinner defender breaks **3-4× more** (rout events 11-14 → 40-47) yet every routed unit rallies at its rear and returns — the CS occupies the objective radius **7,456-9,175 of 9,600 steps** (vs 827-1,206 at base), with 810-2,028 steady CS men in the ring at the buzzer on 3/4 seeds; AND the attacker's own rout events explode **10-18 → 77-89** — the D64 ratio-gated commit logic grinds the assault against a defense that never dissolves, cycling its own consequence-free rabble. **NOT the E48 buzzer class, by measurement:** at the timeout the US holds NOTHING — holdSecs 0, zero US men in the radius, on 4/4 flips (max mid-battle hold 7-28.5 of the 36 needed) — so the approved buzzer-resolution rule would NOT flip these seeds back. This is the E49 class end to end.
- **The finding (supersedes D250's premise, which was a reasonable pre-measurement bet):** the D249 inversion is **not channel-specific** — it is an ENGINE class. Pre-E49, ANY debuff that weakens the defender (morale brittleness OR fewer men) prolongs the defense via consequence-free rout-rally cycling on BOTH sides of the line, and the E48 timeout class then awards the field to the defender. "Monotone in both engines" is true of the casualty channel (it was correct in BOTH attempts) and false of the win channel in the real-time engine. Corollary recorded for E49's design session: attacker aggression is ratio-gated (D64 `effLocal`), so enemy-strength perturbations reach the attacker AI's commit behavior non-monotonically pre-E49.
- **Decision (the queue the evidence forces — no open fork, so no DECISION-NEEDED file):** PM2 (either channel) is BLOCKED behind **E49**; the already-approved queue is unchanged and E48 is NOT gated on PM2 — so this session proceeds directly to **E48** (approved, D242 popup; the Chancellorsville false-containment evidence stands on its own), then E49 (design session first), then the E47 flips, **then the PM2 rebuild + re-A/B under the completed engine** (channel + rate re-tuned there; E51 rides the same battery per its ledger line). Aaron notified by push; he may override at any boundary. The strength contract/teeth above are a ~1-hour rebuild from this entry (the A/B + trace scripts survive gitignored in `.tmp/`; re-derive from this entry if absent).
- **What ships in this commit (docs + harness only):** this entry; RUN-LOG; the E43 ledger flip (strength attempt RED too — PM2 blocked pending E49); V1-CHECKLIST PARITY-M2 note; WAKE-UP/HANDOFF tops + Prompt A; the probe-arms D232+D233-class harness repairs (`waitUntil:'load'`→domcontentloaded+120s; screenshot→120s — it was harness-red this session on the byte-identical-to-HEAD deliverable, steps=0 before any assertion ran; assertions untouched, 23/23 green after). Gates on the reverted tree: GATE OK (all 7 walls) · rebuilt deliverable byte-identical to HEAD (`cmp`) · probe-conditioning 7/7 · probe-campaign-link 17/17 · probe-arms 23/23 · 0 pe · `git diff --check` clean. (No Opus panel: the shipped diff is docs+harness with zero sim surface — the D249 precedent; the A/B itself was the slice's adversarial arbiter.)

## D250 — AARON RESOLVES THE D249 FORK: PARITY-M2 SWITCHES TO THE CAPPED ENEMY **STRENGTH** CHANNEL, BUILT NOW — [AARON, direct chat message, 2026-07-04] (2026-07-04)
Aaron answered `DECISION-NEEDED-parity-m2.md` the same day: **Option B — "switching the enemy-conditioning channel to a capped strength debuff now."** The locked E43 mechanism (ONE shared contract, exact-zero at the side-correct 72/70 baseline, hard-capped, two consumer legs, PL-4 one-enemy-model) stands unchanged; only the CHANNEL moves from morale/steadiness (D249: provably inverts for an attacking player under the pre-E49 engine) to **strength** — will-erosion as desertion, fewer men at muster, monotone in both engines and structurally immune to the D249 rout-cycling backfire. The design doc's PL-4/D2XX-b "morale/steadiness" wording is AMENDED by this decision (annotated in the doc, §7); the morale/steadiness form may be RE-EXAMINED after E49 lands, together with E51 (same mechanism family, one A/B battery). Queue: **PM2-strength NOW → E48 → E49 (design session first) → the Shiloh/Gettysburg flips + Piper guns (E47/E46 completion) → PARITY-M3 (still hard-gated on E47 completion).** The `DECISION-NEEDED-parity-m2.md` file is deleted with this entry (resolved); the D249 direction-A/B gate stands verbatim for the strength rebuild — if the win channel moves against the player again, revert-file-halt.

## D249 — PARITY-M2 (E43 SHARED ENEMY CONDITIONING) ATTEMPTED, HONESTLY RED ON THE DIRECTION A/B, AND REVERTED — THE DEBUFF'S EFFECT INVERTS FOR AN ATTACKING PLAYER BECAUSE ROUT-RALLY CYCLING IS CONSEQUENCE-FREE (THE E49 GAP); PM2 JOINS PM3 BEHIND E48/E49 — [CLAUDE, per the never-push-red law; evidence for Aaron] (2026-07-04)
The M2 build was completed to spec and its unit-level teeth all passed — then the mandated eroded-will direction A/B failed honestly, and the whole slice was REVERTED (the D242 pattern; working tree back to the D246-D248 HEAD, rebuilt deliverable byte-identical, probe-conditioning 7/7 original assertions green). **Nothing sim-affecting shipped.** What was built and proven before the red:
- **The contract (85 `bridgeEnemyWillDebuff`, built to the design law):** exact-zero at the side-correct baseline (72 CS-player / 70 US-player), debuff-only above baseline, 0.10 morale/point of erosion, hard cap −6.0 (raidSupply −6 / E35 ≤6.0 precedent), NaN-safe. Unit-level teeth ALL green pre-revert: probe-campaign-link 18/18 (fresh enemy byte-identical to standalone; eroded will 30 → every enemy unit exactly −4; cap −6 pinned) and probe-conditioning 9/9 (both baselines exact-zero; cap; NaN; Classic enemy −4).
- **Finding 1 (the washout):** a launch-morale-only debuff has ~ZERO battlefield effect in the real-time engine — T0's safe-recovery (`MOR_RECOVER` 2.2/s toward `maxMor`, T0:515-523) restores it during the approach march. A/B receipt: 10/10 eroded runs outcome-identical to baseline at 1e-6 despite enemy units verifiably launching at 74/78 morale. (Corollary, noted not fixed: `raidSupply`'s −6 enemy-morale half is equally transient in the REAL-TIME engine — its ammo −20% is the persistent half; the Classic hex engine has no equivalent washout. Filed as NEW ledger **E51**.)
- **Finding 2 (the inversion — the blocker):** the persistent form (debuff morale AND the `maxMor` ceiling — "steadiness", less absolute headroom above `ROUT_THRESH`) A/B'd 2 sides × 10 seeds × fresh/eroded vs the committed HEAD: fresh state byte-identical **0/20 diffs** (the §27 guard holds); eroded state changed **20/20** with the casualty channel correct in aggregate (mean enemy loss fraction 0.3559→0.3675; mean enemy survivors 5854→5774) — **but the WIN channel moved AGAINST the attacking player: 15→12, all 3 flips one signature: base `w=US by=hold` at ~1,800-2,300 steps → eroded `w=CS by=timeout` at the full 9,600.** Root-caused on US#3 (trace logged): the debuffed defender routs 4× as often (routEverCount 127 vs 31) and bleeds far faster — but every routed unit rallies at its own rear and streams back into the objective radius (csInObj 2-4 for the entire battle; US holdSecs pinned at 0), the hold never consolidates, and the buzzer awards the field to the broken army. **A brittleness debuff in an engine with no envelopment-surrender / straggler-shedding (E49, APPROVED, not built) plus the timeout-favors-defender class (E48, APPROVED, not built) makes an eroded-will enemy HARDER to beat for an attacking player — strategic will-erosion would teach exactly backwards.** The design doc rated PM2 "MED-LOW risk" independent of the E47/E48/E49 arc; the A/B proves it is NOT independent.
- **Decision (recommendation locked into the queue, Aaron may override):** PM2 is BLOCKED behind the same wall as PM3 — build **E48 → E49 → the Shiloh/Gettysburg flips (completing E47) → then PM2 (re-A/B under the completed engine) → then PM3**. Under E49's straggler-shedding, the 127-rout defender dissolves instead of cycling, and the debuff's direction should hold everywhere; the E43 contract/tooth code from this attempt is preserved verbatim in this entry's git history (this session's reflog) and in the spec above — it is a ~1-hour rebuild when unblocked. The alternative channel (a capped enemy STRENGTH debuff — desertion as fewer men, monotone in both engines, immune to rout-cycling) is noted as the fallback if Aaron prefers not to wait for E49; it deviates from the locked "morale/steadiness" wording, so it is HIS call, not mine (`DECISION-NEEDED-parity-m2.md`).
- **What ships in this commit (docs + harness only):** this entry; the E43 ledger annotation (build attempt RED, blocked pending E48/E49) + NEW E51; V1-CHECKLIST PARITY-M2/M3 gate notes; WAKE-UP/HANDOFF tops; the probe-conditioning D233-class harness repair (`waitUntil:'load'` → domcontentloaded+120s — it was harness-RED at HEAD today, steps=0, before any assertion ran; assertions untouched, 7/7 green). Gates on the reverted tree: GATE OK · rebuilt deliverable byte-identical to HEAD (empty diff) · probe-conditioning 7/7 · diff-check clean.

## D248 — ATTACKER-AI-PROPOSAL RETIRED AS SUPERSEDED BY D64 (THE PARITY ARC'S D2XX-d, DOCS-ONLY) — [AARON popup, design session 2026-07-03; executed with PARITY-M1] (2026-07-04)
`ATTACKER-AI-PROPOSAL.md` → `legacy/` with a "RESOLVED — productionized by D64 (2026-06-15), hardened by D73/D231" header (original text preserved verbatim below it); the `START-HERE.md` canonical-list line that still called it "a STANDING OPEN-DECISION doc … not yet approved" replaced by the parity design law's entry + a retirement note; the legacy file list updated. No code change, no balance change. **Why:** verified in the design session — D64 productionized the prototype *with Aaron's locked answers* (the knife-edge `ATK_ASSAULT_RATIO` binary became the gradual `effLocal` per-unit commit; the fog inversion was tuned out — mass-assault/column gated directly on `!__FIELD.fog`, fog aids the defender per D58), it passed the full gate, and T6 `aiSkill`, D73's `_atkCautious` doctrine, and D231's charge releases all build on it. The doc's open label survived by inertia (last touched in D236's docs pass, which annotated `.tmp/` paths without re-examining the approval status). Any FUTURE attacker-AI ambition is a new proposal grounded in today's engine (post-fortifications, post-E47) — not a revival of this one. **Popup answer:** "Retire as superseded" over relabel-at-root and reopen-as-new-work.

## D247 — E45 RESOLVED + BUILT (PARITY-M1): THE CROSS-PHASE TALLY IS A SAFE FUTURE SEAM — battleFielded ACCUMULATES BESIDE battleCas, AND CAMPAIGN FEEDBACK IS PHASE-AWARE — [AARON popup "Future-seam, made safe", design session 2026-07-03; built + gated this session] (2026-07-04)
`__FIELD.battleCas` stays (NOT dead code — it drives the T8 interphase card, the end-screen, and 5 probes). T8 additionally accumulates a cumulative **fielded** tally per phase (`__FIELD.battleFielded`, sourced from the SAME committed-force arithmetic the casualty tally uses — one new `_fldSidePhaseTally` returns both, `_fldSidePhaseCas` is now a thin wrapper so the two can never drift), and `fldCampaignComputeOutcome` (T2) is **phase-aware defensively**: when `__FIELD.phases` is set AND the tallies have accumulated, the campaign casualty fractions aggregate losses/fielded across ALL phases instead of reading only the final sector's units (guarded fallback to the single-phase read otherwise; NaN-safe `||0` + `Math.max(1,…)` denominators; same 0–0.92 clamp).
- **Why (the latent bug, now measured):** no multi-phase scenario is campaign-launchable today (`_fldCampaignScenarioFor` returns only bullrun1/null), but the final-phase-only read would silently mis-count the day the epics join the campaign — and the PM3 sim-backed resolve routes through this exact function. The new probe tooth measured the distortion on a synthetic phased+campaign Antietam (seed 7, US player): cumulative fielded US 36,282 / CS 20,975 vs final-sector-only 7,907 / 5,270 — the old read would have reported a ~0.43 US loss fraction where the honest all-phase figure is **0.094** (eFrac 0.238). `__FIELD` is never serialized (serializeSave emits only settings+campaign), so the extension is save-compat-free; no `_SAVE_VER` bump needed.
- **Byte-identity (PL-9), proven not asserted:** probe-phased-ab ran the full A/B vs the committed D245 HEAD build — **20/20 comparisons identical number-for-number** (all 4 multi-phase battles × 5 seeds: winner/winBy/battleCas/phaseScore/per-phase log/steps). All multi-phase MENU battles carry no campaignCtx and the compute branch additionally requires accumulated tallies → unreachable in every shipped path.
- **Probe tooth (probe-campaign-link 16→17 steps):** a synthetic `fldLaunchSandbox({scenario:'antietam', campaign:{…}})` launch — the exact seam the epics + PM3 will hit — asserts: tallies zeroed at phased init, all 3 phases resolve, fielded>0 both sides, cas≤fielded, cumulative fielded STRICTLY exceeds the final phase alone, and the computed fractions equal the all-phase aggregate formula to 1e-12.
- **Known latent, recorded for the epics day (NOT this slice's scope):** on a phased campaign launch, `fldCampaignCondition` conditions only phase 0's initial units (`ctx._conditioned` short-circuits the per-phase `fldResetRun` re-entry; reinforcement spawns ARE conditioned via the T1 seam in any phase). Harmless today (nothing reachable), but wiring the epics into the campaign (deferred D74 #4) must decide whether later phases' fresh casts re-condition. Filed here so the future slice inherits the fact, per PL-9/PM3 gate discipline.
- **Gates (all green, artifacts read):** `node --check` ×5 (T8, T2, 3 probes) · build **GATE OK** (all 7 walls) · **probe-campaign-link 17/17** 0 pe (E45 tooth values above) · adjacents **probe-phased-ab 20/20 identical** · **probe-antietam 16/16** (battleCas teeth + CS 8/8 aggregate unchanged) · **probe-gettysburg 18/18** (C64 teeth unchanged) · `git diff --check` clean. **Five documented slow-Mac harness repairs rode along, assertions untouched:** probe-campaign-link/antietam/gettysburg off the stalling `waitUntil:'load'` (D233 class — campaign-link and antietam were harness-RED at HEAD today before the repair, steps=0) + antietam/gettysburg screenshots to the 120s budget (D232 class). LOW risk per the design doc; review scaled accordingly (no Opus panel — zero reachable behavior change, proven by the A/B; M2/M3 get panels).

## D246 — BATTLE-MODE PARITY DESIGN SESSION: E42/E43/E45 + THE ATTACKER-AI FORK RESOLVED — [AARON popup, design session 2026-07-03; queue-registered 2026-07-04; doc committed with PARITY-M1] (2026-07-04)
A dedicated read-only design session (authorized in the D237 popup; run parallel to the D237/D238 batch gate, no git) resolved the last four open design debts behind the tactical/strategic contract. Code reality verified first-hand + by a 4-agent Opus default-refute workflow (9 claims: 7 CONFIRMED, 2 sharpened, 0 refuted). The full law + build plan: `docs/design/battle-mode-parity-design.md` (committed with this entry; its §2 PL-1..PL-10 is the verbatim law).
- **E42 (popup: "Gold standard: sim-backed"):** auto-resolve becomes a seeded headless sim of the same conditioned battle, outcome via the shared `fldCampaignComputeOutcome`/`ApplyOutcome` path; the rating-margin model, its 0.42/0.72 loser floors, ±5 draw band, and margin≥18 decisive threshold are superseded. **Why:** the "balanced equivalently / SUBSTITUTABLE" header claim was refuted — the margin ignored the authored OOB and paid a delegated win ~2-3× a fought win's inflicted-blood rewards (measured eFrac 0.245 fought vs the 0.42 floor), teaching players to skip the tactical layer (§27 violation). Sim-backed makes substitutability true by construction (D74 extended). Aaron asked for the cost-no-object gold standard and locked it knowing the full pacing-A/B cost. *(Builds as PARITY-M3, D### at ship time; hard-gated on E47 being fully landed.)*
- **E42 rider (Aaron free-text, verbatim):** "make sure ability to have better weapons upgrade does impact battlefield results though, only makes sense that way" → the PL-3 INVESTMENT-VISIBILITY invariant with a mandatory loadout-vs-empty-armory A/B probe tooth. (Fixes a live gap: `bridgeArmy` never read `C.armory`, so weapon purchases had ~zero effect on delegated outcomes under the old model.)
- **E43 (popup: "Shared enemy conditioning"):** ONE enemy model — authored OOB + era arms in every mode; the generic year curve dies with the margin model; enemyWill couples to battle through ONE shared, capped conditioning function (exact-zero at the 72/70 fresh baseline), consumed by the T2 enemy leg and the `_a6Condition` enemy leg. **Why:** enemyWill previously reached battle outcomes ONLY via the dead rating term — strategic will-erosion paid off only when you did NOT fight; the conditioning form is the accurate-inputs, §27-clean replacement (1864-65 desertion crisis grounding). *(Builds as PARITY-M2, D### at ship time.)*
- **E45 (popup: "Future-seam, made safe"):** built THIS session as PARITY-M1 → **D247** above.
- **Attacker-AI fork (popup: "Retire as superseded"):** executed THIS session → **D248** above.
- **Queue registration (2026-07-04, Aaron-locked): NO reorder** — the arc entered after D239 → E47 → E46 → E13+E41 → S25 (all shipped through D245; E47 HALF-landed by design per D242); **PM3 is hard-gated on E47**, so this session builds M1+M2 and HALTS at the M3 boundary; Aaron alone may pull work forward.

## D245 — S25 PALETTE UNIFICATION: THE THREE PRE-BATTLE CONFIG SURFACES JOIN THE SHARED H0 --h0d-* TOKEN SET (PRESENTATION-ONLY, BYTE-IDENTICAL SIM) — [CLAUDE, per Aaron's D237 approval] (2026-07-04)

Aaron's approved S25: the T2 skirmish menu, the T6 Command & Realism preset picker, and the T11 Custom Battle Builder each invented a private brass/parchment accent palette (T6 `#9f845c/#e8c84a/#f0d98a`, T11 `#9c7a3c/#e4c677/#c9b58e`, T2 `var(--rule)/#e8c84a`) — none matched each other or the H0 shells. All three now consume the shared **`--h0d-*` token set**, defined per-surface on each wrapper (inline on the T2/T6 wrapper divs; on `.fld-cb` in T11's injected style) — the D232 six-shell idiom, with the values **pinned to the `src/99-h0-president-desk.js` canon by a node-side probe tooth** (any drift or unknown token fails probe-custom-battle-builder before the browser even launches).
- **Mapping:** labels/headers → `--h0d-brass` (#d8b458) · selected outlines + selected-card titles → `--h0d-focus` (#ffe27a) · card/input inks → `--h0d-ink` · card/input/status backgrounds → `--h0d-panel`/`--h0d-panel2` · muted/th/small → `--h0d-muted` · decorative dividers/table borders → `--h0d-line` · builder status states → `--h0d-green`/`--h0d-red`/`--h0d-warn`. **Contrast (all computed, worst pairs):** brass labels 8.17:1 on the sheet, warn text 7.48:1 on panel, ink 15.5:1, red status border 3.82:1 non-text — every text pair ≥4.5:1, every non-text UI pair ≥3:1. Side effect fixed honestly: the skirmish labels still carried the latent `var(--rule)` 3.59:1 AA failure the preset picker had once patched locally — brass clears it.
- **Byte-identity, proven not asserted:** probe-presets' standing guards ran green on the new skin — the layers-off Bull Run still lands the committed CS 5/8 and the no-preset stacked run is seed-for-seed identical to an explicit Veteran×Balanced (nothing but colors moved). No aria/role/data wiring, RNG, save field, or sim-parsed string changed.
- **Teeth:** probe-custom-battle-builder **15/15** (NEW: the node-side token-canon check across T2/T6/T11 vs the H0 definitions + an in-page step asserting token use, the retired hexes' absence, and a live `getComputedStyle` resolve of the input border to rgb(216,180,88)); probe-presets **27/27** (NEW: preset cards / lever rows / skirmish rows consume the tokens, retired hexes absent). Two more probes picked up the documented slow-Mac harness repairs, assertions untouched (probe-presets + probe-field off the stalling `waitUntil:'load'` → domcontentloaded+120s, D233 class; probe-presets screenshot → 120s budget, D232 class — both were harness-red at HEAD today, steps=0, before any assertion ran).
- **Review (scaled to presentation-only risk): 1 Opus lens, default-refute — SAFE_TO_COMMIT, 4 LOW informational.** The real find: **the 95-playstyle picker is a FOURTH pre-battle config surface** on the same invented idiom — outside the approved three-surface scope, filed as **NEW ledger S39** (with the deliberately-untouched in-battle drawer + T2 campaign-briefing accents) instead of scope-crept. Also noted: `--h0d-line` composites ~1.79:1 as a divider (decorative-only, parity with the old borders, 1.4.11-exempt); the T2/T6 probe teeth are string-level (the live-resolve tooth lives in the builder probe); a comment's 9.9:1 figure is sheet-ground-coupled.
- **Vetting (D160/D176 focused):** `node --check` ×6 · build **GATE OK** (deliverable rebuilt) · probe-custom-battle-builder 15/15 · probe-presets 27/27 · adjacents probe-field ok + probe-h0-main-menu 4/4 · 0 pageerrors everywhere · screenshot readback of all three surfaces (builder + picker + skirmish — one visual system now) · `git diff --check` clean. (Reversible: revert the three src files' color values + the wrapper token defs; non-negotiables honored — no combat knob, no RNG, no save shape, base untouched.)

## D244 — E13+E41 SAVE HARDENING: THE TAMPERED-AUTOSAVE BOOT BRICK CLOSED (HOISTED OVERRIDE PAIR) + THE SAVE-SHAPE LAW GETS STRUCTURAL TEETH (BUILD GATE 4h + TRACKED MANIFEST) — [CLAUDE, per Aaron's D237 approval] (2026-07-04)

Aaron's approved pair, one gated code-health milestone. **E13 (the boot brick):** base `applySave` iterated settings with `src.hasOwnProperty(k)` (base.html:3159) and base `loadLocal` accepted a settings object carrying an OWN enumerable `hasOwnProperty` — so a tampered `gor_save` made the call throw at base:3983, which executes at SCRIPT-EVALUATION time mid-file: the uncaught TypeError aborted the whole script (every later declaration dead, game bricked until localStorage cleared). The D234 slot/import lanes were already guarded by `_slValidSave`; the autosave boot lane was the hole.
- **The fix — NEW `src/105-save-guard.js`, a D44-class frozen-engine override by REDECLARATION (manifest `overrides` += loadLocal, applySave):** the key insight is that the 91-save-slots reassignment-wrapper idiom CANNOT protect this lane (a wrapper assigned at module eval, ~base:14400, runs after the boot lane at 3983) — but duplicate top-level function declarations are hoisted at script instantiation with last-in-source winning, so the spliced module's declarations are the live bindings BEFORE the first statement executes. Verified empirically in-session and by the Opus panel (both base declarations at global top level; `init()` declared 3882, called top-level 4092; no assignment-form rebinding of either name anywhere in base+src).
- **Behavior:** `loadLocal` = base validation byte-for-byte PLUS reject own-`hasOwnProperty` settings (returns null — the tampered autosave reads as absent, exactly the D234 `_slValidSave` posture); `applySave` = same merge semantics with `Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call` iteration and the pollution skip-list extended by `"hasOwnProperty"` (sanitize: the shadow key can never land on `G.settings`, where base's own iteration would later call it). Byte-identical on every legitimate save (a JSON.parse of serializeSave output can never carry the key; no engine code writes it).
- **Negative control (default-refute):** the pre-fix HEAD deliverable served with the tampered autosave died at boot — `src.hasOwnProperty is not a function` + 2 cascade pageerrors (`TILE_SRC` TDZ, undefined `.failed`), whole module layer dead (no Save&Load button). The fixed build with the SAME tamper: 0 pageerrors, menu up, save rejected, `hasSave()` false.

**E41 (the structural gate):** the architecture law "save changes need a `_SAVE_VER` bump WITH idempotent lazy migration, or be purely additive" had ZERO build enforcement. NEW **gate 4h** in `tools/build.mjs` + tracked **`tools/save-shape.json`**: whitespace-collapsed sha256/16 pins on the 7 save-envelope-owning functions (base serializeSave/loadLocal/applySave/importSave · `_slValidSave` · the E13 override pair), a `_SAVE_VER`↔manifest `saveVer` mirror, a required-core floor (the gate cannot be disarmed by dropping its base entries), a same-file duplicate-declaration die (the gate refuses to hash a dead copy), and an assignment-form rebind scan over every src module for serializeSave/loadLocal/applySave/saveLocal (importSave exempt: the shipped D234 wrapper legitimately uses that idiom and its accept shape is pinned via `_slValidSave`). Any drift fails the build with the computed hashes + the law text; updating the tracked manifest in the same commit IS the conscious bump-or-additive decision (the diff is the audit trail). Purely-additive fields riding inside settings/campaign never touch these functions — the allowed path passes untouched. **Honest coverage limits documented in-gate:** a brand-new persist path (different localStorage key) and the migration-actually-shipped half of the law remain review territory; the extractor has no regex-literal state (enrolled fns contain none; the realistic violation shapes fail closed on the unbalanced-brace die).
- **Fixtures proven in-session (all → BUILD FAIL [5], then live tree green):** (A) shape-owning fn edited without manifest update — fails naming the fn + recorded/computed hashes; (B) manifest saveVer bumped without source bump — fails on the mirror; (C) core manifest entry dropped — fails on the floor; (D) `applySave = <expr>` appended to a module — fails on the rebind scan; (E) duplicate-declaration detection replicated standalone (second exec() finds the dup → die).
- **Probe teeth (`tools/probe-save-slots.mjs`, 10/10 → 14/14, no assertion touched):** a second navigation boots the page WITH the tampered autosave seeded (the pageerror listener persists across reload — a boot crash fails the artifact) + rejection asserts; a direct poisoned-applySave sanitize step; a legit-save round-trip identity step; a module source-hygiene static scan (exactly 2 declarations; the safe-iteration form present; no `_SAVE_VER`/`G.battle` writes). Two harness repairs rode along, assertions untouched: the probe screenshot got the 120s slow-Mac budget (D232 class — the 30s default flaked today under WebGL ReadPixels stalls) and `probe-full-campaign` moved off the stalling `waitUntil:'load'` to `domcontentloaded`+120s (the exact D233 class; it was red at HEAD today for harness reasons — goto timeout before any step — and is 4-steps green after).
- **3-lens Opus panel (default-refute): 3× SAFE_TO_COMMIT.** The override lens confirmed hoisting/load-order, byte-identity, and every consumer; the gate lens' coverage-floor MED was already fixed pre-verdict, its duplicate-declaration + rebind-escape MEDs were hardened post-verdict in-slice (fixtures C/D/E above), and its "cannot be violated silently" doc-overclaim was rewritten to the honest coverage statement; the probe lens confirmed the teeth are non-vacuous (triple coverage: reload pageerror + loadLocal reject + direct-apply) and that both harness edits weaken nothing. **One NEW pre-existing residual filed as ledger E50** (not a D244 blocker, outside the approved scope): `sv.campaign` applies wholesale and ~20 sites iterate save-derived campaign objects with the callable `raw.hasOwnProperty(k)` form (35-command.js:836 et al.) — a tampered `campaign.president.command.corps` still crashes navigation-gated command surfaces (never the boot); candidate deep-campaign sanitize for a future save-hardening slice.
- **Vetting (D160/D176 focused):** `node --check` ×4 (module, build.mjs, both probes) · build **GATE OK · … · save-shape ✓** (new token) with the deliverable rebuilt · **probe-save-slots 14/14 ×2, 0 pe** (JSON readback) · adjacents **probe-full-campaign 4 ok** + **probe-loot-survival 11 ok**, 0 pe · `git diff --check` clean. (Reversible: delete the module + gate block + manifest, restore two probe lines; non-negotiables honored — base.html untouched, no combat knob, no RNG, no `_SAVE_VER` change.)

## D243 — E46 ANTIETAM ACCURATE-INPUTS CORRECTION: THE ERASED ~2:1 UNION WEIGHT RESTORED + THE TOOMBS PROXY REMOVED + TWO DOCUMENTED CS BATTERIES ADDED + ONE HONEST DIVERGENCE (THE PIPER GUNS, E48/E49 COMPLETION TARGET) — [CLAUDE, per Aaron's D237 E46 approval] (2026-07-04)

The approved E46 milestone (the C28/D235 class: verify seating/OOB against sources, fix documented input errors, A/B everything, never gate an outcome). Data-only (`data/antietam.json` + a 2-line probe registry re-pin + a T8 comment-honesty annotation); the pre-staged adversarially-verified dossier (`docs/design/e46-antietam-research.md`) commits with this milestone. Two research workflows ran in-session (a 3-leg citation sign-off on every edited number, Sonnet gather ×2 + Opus default-refute; a 2-leg CS-artillery verify) and a 3-lens Opus panel (default-refute) reviewed pre-commit.

- **The core finding confirmed (dossier read #1):** the model had ERASED McClellan's numerical advantage — Meade's division missing entirely, the XII Corps at ~4,900 vs ~7,100-7,200 engaged, French at 4,400 vs ~5,700 — while CS strengths sat at-or-above sources (the Sunken Road lane double-counted at 3,700 vs NPS's "over 2,500"; Toombs at 1,100 vs his real ~450 as a self-admitted bottleneck headcount proxy; A.P. Hill's attack at 3,700 vs his own OR's "not over 2,000"). **Fielded ratio: 1.32:1 → 1.67:1 US** (historical engaged ~1.6-2:1). Dossier read #2's fate-trim lever was engine-verified FALSE: `fate` is the T3 leader-survival threshold multiplier (history-anchored per leader — Mansfield 0.32 fell, Jackson 2.8 survived), not a unit-durability knob; no fate value touched.
- **The corrections (citation-workflow verdicts applied where they ADJUSTed the dossier):** Cornfield — ADD `us_meade` 3,100 (AOTW 3,131/12 guns; the largest single erasure), Doubleday 3,200→2,900, Ricketts 3,200→3,100, Williams 2,500→**4,600** + Greene 2,400→**2,500** (the verify leg REFUTED the dossier's equal 3,600/3,600 split — Greene's division is consistently ~2,500 and Williams was materially larger; labeled Disputed/Inferred), Hood 2,000→2,300; ADD `cs_stuart_nicodemus` (15 guns/255 crew, Maj. Pelham — the terrain carried this Verified position with no unit; count Inferred within the 13-19 band). Sunken Road — `cs_dh_hill` 2,400→1,150 RELABELED "Rodes's Brigade" (id stable; the old row carried the division ALONGSIDE the separately-modeled G.B. Anderson brigade — the double-count), lane total 2,450 vs NPS "over 2,500"; French 4,400→5,700 (Inferred, contested band); R.H. Anderson 2,100→3,400 (single delayed arrival kept per the dossier; a mid-session 3-echelon split experiment was A/B'd and REVERTED — it over-read "keep staggered arrival", amplified the D242 bottomless-defense artifact, and drove the CS reserve to a 72% loss rate). Bridge — Toombs 1,100→**450** (the proxy REMOVED; the A/B proves the bluff cover band + hold timer carry the bottleneck: the bridge still holds CS 8/8, NOT trivially), D.R. Jones 2,000→2,650 (division-conserving ~3,100), A.P. Hill 3,700→**2,000** split Branch+Gregg 1,650 / Archer 350 ("only 350 strong" — the verify leg REFUTED the dossier's 2,300 as overshooting Hill's own report); ADD `cs_ridge_guns` (10 guns/170 crew — Jones's organic ridge artillery, deliberately EXCLUDING A.P. Hill's battalion guns already narrated with Archer, and Garden's battery as unverified). Weapons — six CS units flipped smooth→rifled = **75.8% of CS infantrymen rifled** (the ANV was majority-rifled by Sept 1862; the smooth minority = the lane garrison (.69 buck-and-ball best-documented), Toombs, and A.P. Hill's units (dossier-barred from upgrades); a scenario-level `_weaponsNote` stamps every weapon tag Inferred, single-estimate basis). Cox's Kanawha (#5) NOT added — the dossier made it conditional and its own research legs could not source a strength figure.
- **A/B (headless sweep-all-battles, BOTH seed sets — default [1,7,21,33,49,101,202,303] / probe [1,7,21,42,55,101,303,909] — LOGGED never gated).** BASELINE: agg CS 8/8 both sets, atk/def **0.57/0.58** (US 4,552/CS 8,055 · 4,510/7,838); P0 CS 8/8 (1,839/1,774) · P1 US 8/8 (1,527/3,717) · P2 CS 8/8 (1,186/2,565). FINAL: agg CS 8/8 both sets, atk/def **0.81/0.82** (4,853/5,999 · 4,756/5,822); P0 CS 8/8 (**3,205/2,299**) · P1 US 8/8 (406/2,455) · P2 CS 8/8 (**1,243/1,245**). Historical: 12,401/10,316 = **1.20 attacker-pays**. Intermediate steps (default set): strengths alone drove the ratio the WRONG way (0.57→0.46→0.39→0.30 — accurate CS trims mean fewer CS men to lose) and **the weapons step was the single biggest honest mover, 0.30→0.86 — the dossier's "rifled-vs-smooth is empirically a WEAK lever" claim is REFUTED for casualty ratios** (its evidence concerned win direction only). Every phase direction stayed historical at every accepted step (Cornfield held / lane carried / bridge-advance repulsed).
- **The honest divergence (the D242 pattern, third instance):** the documented Piper-plateau batteries (Miller's Washington Artillery + Hardaway + Boyce + Carter — Verified presence, ~8-gun documented floor, Longstreet's own staff manning Miller's guns) are deliberately NOT modeled. Built and A/B'd twice: at a plateau seat inside the objective ring the lane inverts to CS 8/8; reseated at the sourced farm position (z 212, outside the ring) it still inverts (US 1/8). The end-state diagnostic (all 8 default seeds) shows P1 ending `CS by=timeout` with 2,300-3,300 steady CS men in the ring and the US NOT mid-hold (7/8 seeds zero US men in the ring) — **NOT the E48 buzzer class; the E49 class exactly**: the historical lane-fall ran on enfilade-driven MASS CAPTURE (~300 prisoners as Barlow wheeled) + the misheard-order command collapse, mechanisms the universal model lacks (Aaron's approved E49). With the guns present the battle ratio hit **1.06** (nearly the historical 1.20); without them the lane is carried too cheaply (US ~270-406 mean vs the historical ~3,000). Both facts recorded in the phase's `_e46Note`; **COMPLETION TARGET: restore `cs_piper_guns` behind the same A/B gates when E48/E49 land.** The law lens's refutation test: the omission COSTS accuracy rather than buying an outcome (both ADDED batteries flip no direction — the Cornfield and bridge held CS 8/8 at baseline with zero CS artillery), the honest-divergence signature, not the fudge signature.
- **The draw-emergence finding (the E46 ledger question answered structurally):** T8's stated design goal — Antietam's tactical draw EMERGES via the aggregate draw band — is structurally unreachable at this battle's shape: a scenario phase resolves "draw" only on mutual annihilation (T0 `fldCheckVictory`), so three decisive weight-1 phases can only produce |diff| of 1 or 3, never <0.5. The historical CS/US/CS phase pattern IS the correct emergent result, and the data's CS-aggregate endNote already narrates it as the historical tactical draw ("History holds — the bloodiest day was a tactical draw"). The T8 header now carries an honesty annotation (comment-only); **the approved E48 buzzer-resolution rule is the mechanism that could make phase-level draws reachable — re-A/B Antietam when E48 lands.** The probe's aggregate expectation (CS ≥6/8) is UNCHANGED — correct before and after.
- **Adversarial pre-commit review (3-lens Opus panel, default-refute): law lens SAFE_TO_COMMIT; history + probe-data lenses FIX_FIRST, both findings fixed pre-commit** — (MED) Pelham was a MAJOR at Antietam, not "Capt." (promoted Aug 1862; the D92 wrong-rank-under-Verified class) → corrected + rebuilt; (MED) D243 cited by the `_e46Note`/probe comments before existing in this log → this entry. Non-blocking panel findings actioned: the `_weaponsNote` provenance stamp; "staggered arrival" wording → "delayed mid-phase arrival"; the T8 header annotation. Panel-verified positives recorded: anti-Lost-Cause NET-POSITIVE (the restored Union weight recovers the wasted-advantage teaching; every CS edit framed by terrain/contingency/command-failure), all teaching/transition text consistent with the new numbers ("Four hundred Georgians" now MATCHES Toombs's 450 — it contradicted the old 1,100), and the removal of an actual shipped per-battle fudge (the Toombs proxy).
- **Registry:** 630→639 (three added formations × 3 prosopography slots — the D239/D237 class), re-pinned in `tools/probe-loot-survival.mjs` with provenance; no Soldier's Story replacement maps to any touched slot; all unit ids stable.
- **VET (focused, D176):** JSON parse ×8 · build **GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓ · women-in-war ✓** ×9 rebuilds · A/B sweeps ×12 (baseline ×2, per-group, final ×2, sim-inert re-proof, plus the rejected Piper/echelon configs) · P1 end-state diagnostic (8 seeds) · **the 8 non-Antietam battles raw-JSON byte-identical to the HEAD baselines on BOTH seed sets** · focused **probe-antietam 16/16 ×3** (all four historical direction gates pass UNCHANGED — no assertion touched) · **probe-loot-survival green ×2** (639 pin) · adjacent **probe-gettysburg ok · probe-shiloh ok** · `node --check` T8 + loot probe · 0 pageerrors via JSON readback throughout · `git diff --check` clean · re-vet after panel fixes (GATE OK, probe-antietam 16/16, sweep byte-identical to the pre-fix final). Full `npm run vet:noreg` not due (D238 banked the batch checkpoint; this is a post-checkpoint focused slice).

## D242 — E47 SLICES 3-4: THE SHILOH + GETTYSBURG FLIPS ATTEMPTED, HONESTLY RED, REVERTED — NEW PROPOSAL E49 (MASS-CAPTURE / STRAGGLER-COLLAPSE GAP); THE ARC PAUSES FOR AARON — [CLAUDE, per Aaron's D237 E47 approval + the never-push-red law] (2026-07-04)

The E47 arc's second half hit a genuine engine-design fork and stops cleanly instead of shipping red or weakening gates. Both flips were built, A/B'd, diagnosed, and REVERTED (the tree ends byte-identical to the committed D241 state — proven by a full re-sweep).

- **Shiloh (attempted):** `homeEdge {US:"low",CS:"high"}` A/B — default seeds BEFORE CS 7/8, meanCas US 10618 / CS 1131, atk/def 0.11 → AFTER **CS 2/8 (probe direction gate ≥4/8 RED)**, US 4708 / CS 1464, 0.31; probe seeds CS 7/8 → 2/8, 0.34. End-state diagnostic of all six US-timeout seeds: at the 480s buzzer the **US holds the Hornets' Nest corridor** — 3,108-15,755 US men ON the objective, CS ZERO in the radius, US hold-clock at 192-387s, nearest steady CS formation 153-445 yd out, CS ~23,000 alive of ~25k fielded (~6% loss). NOT the E48 buzzer class (Chancellorsville's CS stood ON the crossroads at the clock; Shiloh's CS genuinely stalls).
- **Gettysburg (attempted):** top-level `homeEdge` (T8 per-phase fallback) A/B, default seeds — **Day 1 McPherson Ridge inverts to US 8/8 (probe direction gate CS ≥6/8 RED)** (BEFORE: CS 6/8, meanCas 3001/972; AFTER: US 8/8, 2653/1993); Days 2-3 stay US 8/8; aggregate US 8/8 at 1.06. Reverted on the Day-1 red without burning the second seed set (the inversion is decisive).
- **The root cause (the D92 lesson, third time this audit cycle):** the E47 artifact was ACCIDENTALLY SIMULATING two loss mechanisms the universal model does not have — **mass capture by encirclement** (Prentiss's ~2,200 surrendered when the Nest was enveloped; ~3,600 Union prisoners in Gettysburg's Day-1 retreat through town) and **straggler collapse** (thousands under the bluff at Pittsburg Landing who never returned). With the artifact removed, routed defenders always rally at their own rear and RETURN, so a defense that historically dissolved becomes bottomless, and the two battles whose documented outcome depended on those mechanisms invert. Malvern Hill and Chancellorsville shipped clean precisely because their historical outcomes do not depend on mass surrender. Pre-flip Shiloh's US mean casualties (10.5k) approximated the real Day-1 loss by the wrong mechanism; post-flip (3-3.5k) under-counts it — neither is honest, and the honest fix is a mechanism, not a number.
- **Why revert instead of adapt (the laws):** updating the Shiloh/Gettysburg-Day-1 probe expectations would WEAKEN direction gates rooted in documented events (forbidden); shipping red is forbidden; keeping the flip only on Gettysburg Days 2-3 via per-phase homeEdge (the seam supports it) was considered and rejected for now — deliberately retaining a known-wrong physical behavior on Day 1 because it yields the right outcome is a per-battle fudge in spirit (D74), and it is Aaron's call whether that interim state is acceptable. **Filed as proposal E49** (candidate universal mechanics: envelopment-surrender + straggler-shedding — one rule each, no per-battle constants; recommendation: design session, then complete the E47 arc behind the same gates). E48 (the unanchored 480s clock, filed in D241) is adjacent but distinct — Chancellorsville's timeouts are mid-fall truncations; Shiloh's are genuine stalls.
- **State at close:** Shiloh and Gettysburg remain byte-identical to their long-shipped state (the E47 ledger line records the split outcome); probes green as of the D238 battery + D239/D240/D241 focused gates; the final full re-sweep on the default seed set confirms all 9 battles byte-identical to the committed D241 state.
- **VET (focused, D176):** JSON parse ×2 · build **GATE OK** ×4 (flip, flip, revert, revert) · A/B sweeps ×3 (Shiloh both sets, Gettysburg default set) · end-state diagnostics ×2 (6 Shiloh seeds; the 5 Chancellorsville seeds ran in D241) · final byte-identity re-sweep vs the D241 state · `git status` clean (the two expected untracked docs/design files only) · `git diff --check` clean. This commit is docs-only (DECISIONS/RUN-LOG/REVIEW-QUEUE/WAKE-UP/HANDOFF): no src, no data, no probe change.

## D241 — E47 SLICE 2: THE CHANCELLORSVILLE FLIP + THE ENDNOTE HONESTY FIX + NEW PROPOSAL E48 (THE UNANCHORED SINGLE-PHASE CLOCK) — [CLAUDE, per Aaron's D237 E47 approval] (2026-07-04)

The second E47 flip (data + probe tooth + one panel-mandated text-honesty fix; the seam shipped in D240). Aaron's live fair-gameplay concern this session ("doesn't this flipping home team thing undermine the alt history aspect? feels like we're tipping the scale") was answered with the panel's verification and shaped this slice's key call: anything that WOULD move outcomes beyond the approved rout-direction fix goes to Aaron as a proposal, not into the code.

- **The flip:** `data/chancellorsville.json` gains `homeEdge {US:"low", CS:"high"}` + `_homeEdgeNote` (the file seats Jackson's echelons at z 780-840 / CS reinf z 860, the US line toward low z / US reinf z 140-280; panel-verified the declaration matches the seating, incl. the XI Corps correctly seated forward at the exposed z 780 flank and now routing toward its OWN rear instead of through Rodes's echelons).
- **A/B (both seed sets, LOGGED never gated):** default seeds BEFORE CS 7/8, meanCas US 8280 / CS 2740, atk/def 0.33 → AFTER **US 4 / CS 4, US 6244 / CS 5396, 0.86**; probe seeds BEFORE CS 7/8, 8009/2581, 0.32 → AFTER **US 4 / CS 4, 6361/5350, 0.84**. The historical ratio is 17,287 US / 13,303 CS = **0.77 defender-pays — the post-flip ratio lands nearly on it** (the pre-flip 0.32 was the artifact: US routers annihilated inside the CS echelons). The 8 non-Chancellorsville battles: byte-identical to the D240 state on both seed sets.
- **The win-split finding (both panel lenses HIGH, all evidence logged):** every new US win is `by=timeout` at the 480s clock, and an end-state diagnostic of ALL five timeout seeds (7/21/33/303/909) shows the CS standing ON the crossroads at the buzzer (12,039-13,116 men inside the objective radius, US zero, CS holdSecs 88-133/140 — 10-52s short). The pre-flip CS 7/8 was artifact-assisted (CS holds completed at ~170-183s; honest tempo is ~237-405s). So the 4/4 split is a clock-determined knife-edge, not emergent balance — but the clock is a shared unanchored template (480/140 verbatim across bullrun/fredericksburg/shiloh/chancellorsville) and any per-battle edit would be the exact outcome-engineering D74 forbids. **Filed as NEW proposal E48** (with the diagnostic verbatim + a recommendation: a universal position-falling-at-the-buzzer resolution rule over any clock guess) — Aaron's call, not a silent patch. The probe's CS≥4/8 balance step passes at its exact floor — a deterministic canary, direction-correct (a future tempo slowdown reds it; the panel notes a red there means "re-examine assault tempo," and the sim's real CS advantage is closer to dominant than 4/4 suggests).
- **The endNote honesty fix (panel HIGH, text-only, D74-inert, fixed IN-slice):** the US-win endNote claimed the assault "broke against the rallied Union line and never reached the crossroads" — factually contradicted by the sim's own end-state on 100% of the US wins (a clock expiry mid-fall). Both US-win strings now say the honest thing: the Union **denied a consolidated hold** on the crossroads until the attack's momentum was spent. Pre-existing defect (the branch existed at 1/8 pre-flip); the flip made it 4× more frequent, so it could no longer ride as a rare edge.
- **Probe tooth (probe-chancellorsville 27/27, no assertion weakened — panel-verified pure insertion):** the E47 step asserts the data declaration, the role-aware runtime resolution, and the behavioral rout direction both sides (CS 830→892, US 780→718 live; panel-verified deterministic — flat SPD_ROUT 62, no RNG on the routing branch, 130+yd of clamp room). C44/C65/C64-adjacent teeth all still bind.
- **Adversarial pre-commit review (2-lens Opus panel — history+sim · probe+data integrity, default-refute): BOTH verdicts SAFE_TO_COMMIT, BOTH independently recommending SHIP_AS_IS_PLUS_PROPOSAL** over an in-slice clock change (the D235-Ruggles precedent distinguished: that was a wrong FACT with a documented anchor; the clock is an unanchored pacing knob whose every value sets the win ratio). The panel's two HIGHs (endNote text; E48 framing) both actioned pre-commit as above.
- **VET (focused, D176):** JSON parse ×3 · build **GATE OK** ×2 · A/B sweeps ×2 + end-state diagnostic run · byte-identity of the 8 unflipped battles ×2 · `node --check` · focused **probe-chancellorsville 27/27** ×2 (before + after the endNote fix) — `ok=true`, 0 failed steps, 0 pageerrors via JSON readback · `git diff --check` clean. (Adjacent probes not re-run: this slice touches one data file + one probe only; the seam code is unchanged from D240, whose gate ran arms/order-feel/antietam.)

## D240 — E47 ROUT-DIRECTION FIX, SLICE 1: THE ROLE-AWARE homeEdge SEAM + THE MALVERN HILL FLIP — [CLAUDE, per Aaron's D237 E47 approval] (2026-07-04)

The first slice of the approved E47 arc (per-scenario role-aware home edges; Aaron-approved flip order Malvern Hill → Chancellorsville → Shiloh → Gettysburg, ONE AT A TIME, each behind its own both-seed-set A/B + battle-probe re-vet). WHY: all four CS-attacker battles seat the US defender at LOW z while `fldHomeEdgeZ` was side-keyed (US=high z, CS=low z) — so a routing unit fled THROUGH the enemy line, inflating defender casualties (Shiloh 0.11 vs the historical ~1.2 attacker-pays) and reading as a near-costless Confederate assault: an anti-Lost-Cause teaching liability on the public game (D235 panel, ledger E47).

- **The seam (guarded, byte-identical when inactive):** `fldHomeEdgeZ(side)` (T0) now consults `__FIELD.homeEdgeZ` — set from a new scenario-data field `homeEdge: {US:"low"|"high", CS:...}` by `fldScenarioInit` (T1, single-phase) and `_fldBuildPhase` (T8, per-phase `p.homeEdge || top.homeEdge`, the attacker/defender pattern) via the validating `fldHomeEdgeSpec`; lazily resolved ("low"=-60, "high"=FLD.FIELD_H+60) so it always tracks the live FIELD_H; cleared to null on every launch (the T0 reset line — no leak between back-to-back launches, which the sweep depends on). Every consumer keys off the one function relationally (T0 rout/wavering-fallback/objective-press/defender-frame · T3 officer command-post/ride-behind · T4 supply-train rear · T5 battery displace/cav flank-guard/flank-point), so the flip is coherent. Also role-aware: the two T5 no-visible-enemy fallback facings (battery, cav guard) via new `fldArmsFallbackFace` — face the ENEMY's home edge; the default path returns the exact original constants (US 0, CS PI) by construction, and in a flipped battle an idle battery no longer faces its own rear (facing feeds the flank-fire multiplier + the render).
- **NO-FLIP BYTE-IDENTITY PROOF:** with the seam built and NO data flipped, both seed sets' full 9-battle sweeps came back byte-identical to the HEAD baselines (raw per-seed results compared as JSON) — the seam is a true no-op absent the field.
- **The Malvern Hill flip** (`homeEdge {US:"low", CS:"high"}` + `_homeEdgeNote`; the file seats US on the Crew house ridge at z 335-392 / reinf z 145-300, CS in the high-z woods at z 705-810 / reinf z 735-860). **A/B (headless sweep-all-battles, BOTH seed sets, LOGGED never gated):** default seeds [1,7,21,33,49,101,202,303] BEFORE US 7/8, meanCas US 2541 / CS 7363, atk/def 2.90 → AFTER **US 8/8, US 4745 / CS 6891, atk/def 1.45**; probe seeds [1,7,21,42,55,101,303,909] BEFORE US 7/8, 2161/7303, 3.38 → AFTER **US 8/8, 5151/6899, 1.34**. Historically ALL the assaults were repulsed (8/8 beats 7/8) at CS ~5,650 vs US ~3,000 ≈ ratio 1.8-2.7; the pre-flip 2.90-3.38 sat above that band INFLATED BY THE ARTIFACT (CS routers annihilated fleeing through the Union gun line); the honest emergent post-flip ratio is 1.34-1.45 — panel calibration observation: it lands slightly BELOW the band (possible later pass on the cautious doctrine / universal artillery weighting — deliberately NOT touched in this slice, D74). Known tail (recorded so it is never mistaken for a regression): probe seeds 42/909 run defender-pays 0.95-0.97 while the US still holds; the default set's minimum is 1.18. **The 8 unflipped battles: byte-identical to HEAD on both seed sets.**
- **Probe teeth (probe-malvern-hill 27/27, 3 new steps, no assertion weakened — verified by the panel as pure insertion):** the data declaration (US low / CS high); the behavioral rout-direction proof (a forced-routing CS unit moves toward HIGH z — live 780→842 — and a US one toward LOW z — 392→330 — the exact inverse of the pre-fix flight; panel-traced deterministic, rally/melee/death cannot interfere in the 1-sim-second window); the sandbox no-leak guard (homeEdgeZ null + default edges on a following sandbox launch). The probe also CAUGHT a real in-session slip: the first data edit accidentally dropped `defaultFog:false` — restored and re-verified (undefined is falsy-identical here, but the contract assertion is the point).
- **Adversarial pre-commit review (3-lens Opus panel — code/byte-identity · history+sim · probe-integrity, default-refute): ALL THREE verdicts SAFE_TO_COMMIT.** 1 actionable LOW, FIXED pre-commit: `fldHomeEdgeSpec` now requires BOTH sides valid (a one-sided declaration silently collapsed both armies onto the SAME edge — an authoring-error trap for the next three flips and for custom packs); re-gated after the fix (node --check · GATE OK · probe 27/27). Non-actionable panel findings recorded above (ratio-band observation; the 42/909 tail; positive confirmation the T5 fallback-face change is load-bearing for flipped battles and byte-identical otherwise).

## D239 — PHASE I SOLDIER'S STORY JOHN O. CASLER (SIXTEENTH) + LEDGER C64 STANNARD SPLIT (+ THE TWO CORRECTIONS IT EXPOSED) + NEW C65 BERRY ATTACH — [CLAUDE, per Aaron's D237 "Soldier's Story + C64" assignment] (2026-07-04)

The audit ledger's last open FIX-NOW line (C64) closes inside the resumed Priority-1 Soldier's Story lane, per Aaron's D237 popup pairing. Sim-affecting battle-data changes rode full both-seed-set A/Bs; a 3-lens Opus panel reviewed pre-commit.

- **The sixteenth citation-grade replacement — John O. Casler** (`ss:chancellorsville:CS:cs_colston_div:pvt` → `person_chancellorsville_cs_33va_casler`): Private, Company A, 33rd Virginia Infantry, Paxton's (Stonewall) Brigade, Trimble's Division (Colston commanding), Jackson's Second Corps — the FIRST Chancellorsville record and only the third Confederate voice (13 US / 3 CS). Sources (all page-verified this session): his memoir (Internet Archive, 1906 ed., Ch. XV Chancellorsville), the USC Press Krick-edition page (identity, Co. A / 33rd VA, June 1861, Feb 1865 capture / Fort McHenry, 1893/1906), and the NPS FRSP Chancellorsville OOB (the 33rd VA in Paxton's Brigade / Colston / Second Corps). Honesty caveats front-loaded IN the record: he did NOT witness Paxton's death; Jackson's wounding is secondhand; the pioneer-corps road-clearing detail is his own account alone; Private-at-Chancellorsville is inferred from the absence of any attested promotion; no portrait; neutral Inferred ratings. Not asserted: exact birth date, "Potomac Guards" nickname, diary-base provenance. The record's memoir-standing superlative is attributed to the USC Press edition (panel fix). Zero sim effect — proven by 7-battle byte-identity below.
- **C64 — the Stannard Day-3 double-count (verify-first, split option).** Research (Sonnet gather → Opus default-refute) refined the panel's ~1,900-2,500 band to a sourced ~1,790-1,950 engaged: only the 13th/14th/16th Vermont fought July 3 (12th/15th detached guarding the corps trains — brigade monument tablet); tablet strengths 13th 480 engaged / 14th 647 / 16th 661 brought-to-field (Stone Sentinels transcriptions); Stannard's OR ~350 July-3 casualties. Fix: `us_stannard` 2,200 → **650** ("— 14th Vermont (line)") and `us_stannard_flank` 1,800 → **1,140** ("13th & 16th Vermont (flank attack)" — the two-phase wheel vs Kemper then Wilcox/Lang, ~243 prisoners, joined by four companies of the 14th), total 1,790 vs the sourced 1,788. Maneuver Verified; strengths Inferred (mixed engaged/brought-to-field bases; Busey & Martin may raise the 13th toward ~630 — noted in-file).
- **The D92 lesson, again (the heart of the milestone):** the Stannard-only trim honestly FLIPPED Day 3 in the A/B — US 7/8 with the DEFENDER paying more (0.83 ratio) — proving the phantom 2,210 Vermonters had been compensating for two other input errors, both fixed per the D235 precedent with verify-first sourcing: **cs_trimble_div 3,500 → 1,900** (Trimble led only TWO of Pender's four brigades — Lane + Lowrance/Scales, the latter wrecked July 1: ~1,916 present / ~1,724 rank-and-file entered, ~514 casualties 30%) and **NEW `us_osborn_hill`** (Maj. Thomas Osborn's XI Corps Cemetery Hill line, 18 guns bearing on the charge — the documented NORTHERN enfilade that shattered Brockenbrough and raked Pettigrew's left, the mirror of D235's McGilvery southern line; Inferred serviceable-gun count and crew). The corrected CS assault (~12,400) now also matches the transition text's "12,500 men" better than the old 14,000.
- **C65 (NEW ledger line, found by a D239 all-files dangling-attach sweep — the only dangler repo-wide):** chancellorsville `ld_berry` attached to nonexistent `us_berry` (unit id is `us_berry_div`) — the C33 silent-linker class; fldById exact-match made Berry's aura a silent no-op. Fixed; probe-chancellorsville gains the probe-shiloh every-attach-resolves invariant + the explicit Berry tooth.
- **A/B (headless sweep-all-battles, BOTH seed sets — default [1,7,21,33,49,101,202,303] / probe [1,7,21,42,55,101,303,909] — LOGGED never gated):**
  - **Gettysburg** BEFORE: agg US 8/8 both sets, atk/def 1.22-1.23, D3 US 8/8 with the charge paying 2.65-2.69:1. INTERMEDIATE (Stannard trim only): D3 US 7/8, defender paying (0.83) — rejected, root-caused as above. AFTER (all three corrections): **agg US 8/8 both sets, atk/def 1.09 (default) / 1.11 (probe), D3 US 8/8 with the charge paying 2.32:1 / 2.55:1** (D3 means 1,749/4,055 and 1,659/4,234); **Days 1-2 mean-identical to BEFORE** (untouched). The aggregate ratio moved 1.22→1.09-1.11 because the CS Day-3 force honestly shrank ~1,600 (fewer men to lose) — inputs are documented-accurate, the ratio is emergent, and D74 forbids chasing 1.22 as a target.
  - **Chancellorsville** BEFORE: CS 7/8 both sets, means 8,770/2,263 (probe set). AFTER: **CS 7/8 both sets**, means 8,009/2,581 — Berry's counterattack aura now actually anchors his division (marginally less defender-bleed; the E47 home-edge artifact remains and is the next milestone).
  - **The seven untouched battles: byte-identical raw results across both seed sets** (Casler = zero sim footprint; no leakage).
- **Probe teeth (no assertion weakened):** probe-gettysburg — new C64 step locking line ≤800 / flank ≤1,300 / total 1,700-2,000 / Trimble 1,500-2,200 / Osborn 15-22 guns (18 steps green); probe-chancellorsville — every-leader-attach-resolves + Berry binding (26 steps green); probe-loot-survival — 16-record locks, Casler canonical + cleaned-row caveat + UI card/detail assertions, registry re-pin 627→630 with provenance (us_osborn_hill generates 3 slots — the D237 603→627 class), sourceNote fitted to the runtime's 360-char clamp with caveats front-loaded (green).

## D235 — FABLE AUDIT M10: SIM-AFFECTING ACCURATE-INPUTS CORRECTIONS (C28 HIGH + C05-scale/C11/C30/C33/C43/C44/C47) — [CLAUDE, per D224 ledger] (2026-07-03)

The first audit milestone that CHANGES battle inputs. Law: D92 accurate-inputs / D74 no-output-gate — every change is a documented input (identity Verified, strength/count Inferred, sources named); outcomes were re-verified empirically on seeded headless A/B sweeps and the battle probes' historical gates, never forced.

- **C28 (HIGH) — the Gettysburg attacker/defender seating inversion.** All three phases had reused Antietam's US-attacker coordinate template without swapping roles: the CS attacker spawned ON the defended objective terrain (Hood at z410 vs the LRT objective z358; Pickett at z410 vs the Angle objective z295) and the US defender sat ~450 yd away at high z — the inverse of history, of the file's own COORDINATE NOTE, and of the three shipped CS-attacker battles (Malvern Hill / Chancellorsville / Shiloh). Fixed data-side: every phase's units/leaders/supply re-seated (US defender on the objective ridges at low z facing PI, CS attacker at high z facing 0, supplies swapped), Day-3's CS-anchored terrain (Seminary Ridge "CS start", Spangler Woods) moved to the CS side, Ziegler's Grove/Bliss Farm repositioned to their described roles, and the Big Round Top note's now-false "Confederate approach route" clause dropped. The file `_comment` documents the correction.
- **The D92 lesson the flip exposed (the heart of the milestone):** at HEAD the probe's historical gates passed for the WRONG GEOMETRIC REASON — the attacker started on the objective, so "Day 1 CS-held" was literal spawn-and-hold, and Day-1 mean casualties were absurdly light (US 1312 / CS 293). Seated correctly, the UNDER-MODELED Union defense collapsed (first post-flip A/B: Day 2 split 4/4, Day 3 CS 8/8 with the defender paying 2:1 — the charge walked in against the only 6 Union guns on the board). The remedy was accurate inputs, not tuning: the documented formations that historically repulsed the assaults were missing entirely — **Day 3** gained Hazard's II Corps artillery brigade (24 guns) + McGilvery's Reserve Artillery line (39 guns — the documented enfilade of Pickett's right) + Hays's division (Ziegler's Grove — the front Pettigrew/Trimble actually struck) + Hall's & Harrow's brigades (Gibbon's division, who sealed the Angle); **Day 2** gained Birney's III Corps division (the salient — Sickles was previously a leader with NO unit while his corps' shattering is the phase's own teaching) + Bigelow's 9th Massachusetts battery, and the CS side's missing Benning + G.T. Anderson brigades so both sides' southern-front weight is modeled at the same altitude. cs_pickett_arty's note now states the shipped effective-support abstraction honestly (~150-170 cannonade guns in history; overshooting/defective fuzes/ammunition shortage documented via Alexander/Hess; the 20-gun D75 input = effective sustained close support, vs the ~69 modeled Union guns actually bearing at the repulse).
- **C30** cs_heth_division men 5500 → 3400 (Archer ~1,100 + Davis ~2,300 — Pfanz; Busey & Martin; the note said "both brigades" while carrying near-full-division strength). **C43** all Day-2/Day-3 CS infantry smooth → rifled, matching the file's own Day-1 Verified rifled-muskets note (the weapon tag = +25% pow and 130→320 range in the D74 model — Pickett's men no longer charge as smoothbore troops in the same battle where Heth's fight as rifles).
- **C05-scale — Ruggles's grand battery (Shiloh).** New timed CS reinforcement `cs_ruggles_battery`: 53 guns (the conservative bound of the documented 53-62), ~1,200 crew, **atSec 100 — BEFORE the file's dusk Chalmers assault at atSec 120**, restoring the real event order (~4:00-4:30 p.m. massing → Prentiss's 5:30 surrender → dusk assaults) on the file's own compressed clock. The first attempt sat it at atSec 200, which inverted the event order and left probe-seed CS at 3/8 against the ≥4/8 gate — the root cause was the pacing mapping, not the input scale; corrected honestly rather than by touching any strength. The 12-gun `cs_artillery_pool` note now scopes it to the distributed MORNING close support.
- **C47** BOTH armies' Shiloh infantry → smooth (April 1862 near-parity: US mostly M1842s/conversions, CS mostly obsolete smoothbores + ~4,000 blockade Enfields — U.S. Army Staff Ride Handbook; Cunningham; sourced WEAPONS NOTE added to the file `_comment`). The Hornets' Nest collapse is now driven by the accurate Ruggles input instead of a false Union range advantage. **C33** ld_wallace attach `us_wallace` → `us_wallace_div` (fldById is exact-match, so the binding was a SILENT no-op falling back to nearest-friendly anchoring). **C11** display-only `badgeLabelAliases[scenarioId][badgeKey]` in data/ratings.json + `fldBadgeLabel(def)` in T14 wired at the two render sites (roster chip + X-factor banner): Shiloh shows "Hold the Line"; the def label and the D104 mechanical lever are untouched, and the only chip render site is the in-battle HUD (no stale-scenario surface). **C37** turned out to be already fixed in D226 — only its ledger line was stale; flipped with provenance.
- **A/B (headless `sweep-all-battles`, LOGGED never gated; default seeds [1,7,21,33,49,101,202,303] / probe seeds [1,7,21,42,55,101,303,909]):**
  - **Gettysburg** BEFORE: agg US 8/8 · D1 CS 8/8 (meanCas US 1312/CS 293) · D2 US 8/8 (1323/2264) · D3 US 8/8 (1245/1472); battle mean US 3880/CS 4028 (atk/def 1.04). AFTER (final, post-panel): default seeds agg US 8/8 · D1 CS 6/8 (3001/972) · D2 US 8/8 (3042/3453) · D3 US 8/8 (2114/5597 — the charge pays ~2.6:1); battle mean US 8157/CS 10022 → atk/def **1.23**; probe seeds agg US 8/8 · D1 CS 7/8 · D2 US 8/8 · D3 US 8/8, mean US 8159/CS 9931 → **atk/def 1.22 — exactly the historical 28,063/23,049 = 1.22, emergent, not gated**. (The intermediate pre-panel state read 1.06-1.10 with D1 at 8/8; the panel's Day-1 terrain re-seat + Hazard serviceability trim tightened D1 to an honest 6-7/8 — the meeting engagement nearly held, as it nearly did.)
  - **Shiloh** BEFORE: US 5/CS 3 default · CS 5/8 probe seeds. AFTER: **CS 7/8 on both sets** (probe outcomes: seven CS/hold @241-265s, one US/timeout) — for the modeled day-1 slice the objective (the Hornets' Nest corridor) historically FELL at ~5:30 p.m., and the file's own endNote frames the CS field win as "history holds"; the probe gate is CS ≥4/8. US meanCas ~10.5k / CS ~1.1k: the defender-bleeds ratio is the PRE-EXISTING side-keyed home-edge rout artifact (Shiloh was already 0.39-0.62 before), amplified here — filed as NEW ledger proposal **E47**, not silently patched inside M10.
  - **Chancellorsville** BEFORE: CS 8/8. AFTER: CS 6/8 default · 7/8 probe seeds (gate ≥4/8) — direction preserved with honest variance; both sides bleed more at rifle range.
  - The six untouched battles: **byte-identical results across all sweeps** (no leakage).
- **Probe teeth added (no existing assertion touched):** probe-gettysburg — per-phase defender-nearer-objective invariant + all-CS-infantry-rifled + Heth 3000-3800 band; probe-shiloh — every-leader-attach-resolves + Wallace binding + both-sides-smooth + Ruggles ≥50 guns massing before Chalmers + the C11 contract (def label unmutated AND the Shiloh chip aliased); probe-chancellorsville — ≥4 CS infantry formations rifled.
- **Adversarial pre-commit review (full Opus panel — 5 lenses × Opus-high, default-refute, per the M10-risk mandate):** history / sim-data-consistency / code / probe-integrity / D74-fudge-detection+balance. **Code and probe lenses: 0 findings** (fldBadgeLabel collision-free, null-safe, in-battle-only render sites, no sim writes; no probe assertion weakened, all teeth non-vacuous). **6 findings, ALL fixed or filed pre-commit:** (MED, simdata) Day-1's CS-anchored terrain — Oak Hill and the Willoughby Run woods — had been left at pre-flip low z while Rodes's units moved to high z → re-seated to the CS approach (Oak Hill z520, Willoughby z500), which honestly tightened Day 1 to CS 6-7/8; (HIGH, balance) the D104 `_rosterNote` Shiloh paragraph still documented the pre-audit "US ~4/5" calibration snapshot, contradicting the shipped probe gate ("CS-favored ≥4/8") and endNote — reconciled with a D235 SUPERSEDING ADDENDUM in ratings.json (the no-rally-penalty decision itself unchanged); (MED, balance) the Shiloh defender-bleed artifact is also an anti-Lost-Cause teaching liability, not just a ratio bug — E47's ledger text now carries the pedagogy dimension and elevated priority; (LOW ×3, history) Ruggles causal-primacy prose softened to name the encirclement scholarship (T. B. Smith) alongside the NPS narrative; the "~4,000 Enfields" figure hedged and attributed; Hazard's brigade trimmed 24→20 guns for climax serviceability (Rorty/Woodruff attrition), keeping Cushing's 6 separate. The panel also surfaced a PRE-EXISTING Stannard double-count (us_stannard + us_stannard_flank ≈ 4,000 vs the brigade's real ~1,900-2,500) — filed as NEW ledger FIX-NOW **C64** (verify-first, own A/B) rather than rushed in. All battle probes re-ran green after the fixes.
- **VET (focused, D176):** JSON parse ×4 data files; `node --check` ×4 (T14 + 3 probes); build **GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓** ×4 rebuilds; focused probes **probe-gettysburg 17/17 · probe-shiloh 31/31 · probe-chancellorsville 25/25** (re-run green after the panel fixes); adjacent **probe-antietam 16/16** (shared T8 phased engine) + **probe-ratings 21/21** (badge system, re-run after the _rosterNote addendum) — all `ok=true`, 0 failed steps, 0 pageerrors, 0 realErrors via JSON readback; `git diff --check` clean.
