## D234 — FABLE AUDIT M9: SAVE-MANAGER GUARDS (S31/S32/S33/S34) — [CLAUDE, per D224 ledger] (2026-07-03)

The Save & Load slot manager could destroy player data on a single mis-click; four guards close it, UI-only (no `_SAVE_VER` change, no sim writes). **S31** Save onto a FILLED slot confirms ('Overwrite "Union - Turn 4" in slot 1? The existing save will be lost.') — previously it wrote instantly and no undo lane covered it; saving to an empty slot stays frictionless. **S32** Load and every import lane confirm before replacing a LIVE campaign (they also immediately overwrite the base autosave via saveLocal, so the loss was silent and total): the slot-manager Load/Import/Paste buttons via `_slConfirmReplaceLive`, and — a review-workflow catch — the base menu's "Load from File" buttons too, which route through the hardened global `importSave` wrapper and had bypassed the guard on the most prominent entry point; a decline leaves the campaign and menu untouched. **S33** Delete confirms with the save's own label ("This cannot be undone.") — the button sits directly beside Load in the same flex row. **S34** a slot whose RAW localStorage data exists but fails `_slValidSave` (older `_SAVE_VER` / damaged JSON) no longer masquerades as "Empty": it renders a distinct "Incompatible save" state that explains itself, DISABLES Save (the enabled Save invited clobbering the still-recoverable raw string), keeps Load/Rename disabled, and keeps Delete enabled for a deliberate, confirmed clear — on any future `_SAVE_VER` bump every old slot now says what it is instead of silently vanishing.
- **Adversarial pre-commit review (D145 routing, scaled to the one-file diff):** a single Opus-high reviewer over the full module + probe — 1 MED confirmed (the base-menu `importSave` bypass above, fixed at the wrapper so all lanes share one guard), everything else checked clean (no other unguarded `applySave`/`_slWrite`/`_slDelete` paths; boot/Continue/undo restores correctly unguarded; stale-state edge cases; probe-stub hygiene).
- **VET (focused, D176):** `node --check` on both touched files; build **GATE OK**; **probe-save-slots 10/10** (existing flows accept the new dialogs via a stubbed confirm; a new S31-S34 step drives BOTH paths — declines keep the save/campaign, accepts proceed — plus the incompatible-slot state and the menu-import guard) · adjacent **probe-full-campaign ok** (the campaign save/load loop) — all `ok=true`, 0 pageerrors, JSON readback; `git diff --check` clean. Full `npm run vet:noreg` remains deferred to the run's batch checkpoint (D176).

## D233 — FABLE AUDIT M8: A11Y + DISCOVERABILITY + TEACHING-UI STALENESS (18 ledger items) — [CLAUDE, per D224 ledger] (2026-07-03)

Accessibility, gesture discoverability, and stale-teaching fixes across nine files; presentation/teaching only, no sim writes. **Discoverability:** **S07** both controls references (the '?' tactical overlay + How-to-Play) now document the two marquee UG:G gestures — drag-ONTO-an-enemy = charge THAT brigade, Shift-drag = queue a waypoint route — previously taught only by a transient no-selection HUD hint; **S08** the R elevation-display hotkey joins both lists; **C19** the first-launch quick-start names the SHIPPED H0 buttons ("Muster the Union" / "Command the Confederacy" / "Choose a Battle") instead of the retired pre-H0 layout. **H2 cutaway a11y:** **S12** the aria-modal dialog gets a real Tab/Shift+Tab focus trap (the markup asserted a contract it didn't implement); **S14** the pan/zoom honors the in-game reduceMotion toggle via an `.h2-reduced-motion` class (was OS-media-query only); **S15** an explicit dyslexia-mode override reaches the body-mounted overlay (the a11y mode's `.pad`-scoped selectors never did), mirroring 97-accessibility's exact values. **Custom Battle Builder a11y (S21 HIGH + 4):** every editor-table input/select/remove-button now carries an accessible name (row identity + column) via the `_fldCbCell` helper — a unit row was 13 fields announced as "edit text, blank"; **S26** numeric cells get `inputmode` (integer fields "numeric"; FRACTIONAL fields — leader quality, facing, hill height — "decimal", since a numeric keypad has no decimal key: a review-workflow catch); **S22** `_fldCbRefresh(focusSel)` restores keyboard focus to the acted-on control after every rebuild (the T6 convention; the delete-slot path focuses the slot's always-enabled Save button — a second review catch, since the emptied slot's own buttons re-render disabled); **S23** the status block is `role="status"/"alert"` + `tabindex="-1"` and RECEIVES FOCUS on Validate, a blocked Launch, and Import — an invalid Launch is no longer a silent no-op for a non-sighted user; **S24** New Draft / Import / Import Pack / Load slot confirm() before discarding an unsaved draft (baseline snapshot `_fldCbCleanJson`, canonical primitive-stringifying compare `_fldCbCanon` taken post-render so the form round-trip — number→string coercion + empty sub-object materialization — never false-dirties an untouched draft); **S27** a Hold-to-win above the real ceiling (timeLimit−10) pushes a validation warning instead of silently launching a different win condition. **Small a11y:** **S13** the briefing side-card's invalid `<h2>`-inside-`<button>` becomes a styled span (the aria-label already carries the name); **S28** the preset picker's "Selected:" summary is an `aria-live="polite"` region (the Custom-bundle flip is now announced); **S30** the desk tabs group no longer repeats the nav's aria-label (double announcement). **Teaching-UI truth:** **C18** the inline glossary the tutorial advertises for "the teaching panels" now decorates 16 teaching-prose desk tabs, not 2 of ~20 — safe because `glDecorate` skips interactive zones (`_GL_SKIP_TAGS`) by construction; Codex itself excluded; **C20** the Codex systems axis gains the two shipped-but-undocumented campaign systems as Verified entries with 2 real scholarly sources each — Rail Logistics (Gabel's *Railroad Generalship*; Clark's *Railroads in the Civil War*; figures match the repo's rail dossier: ~22,000 vs ~9,000 miles, the Chattanooga relief vs Longstreet-to-Chickamauga contrast) and Medicine & Disease (Hacker 2011; Faust's *This Republic of Suffering*; keeps the D226-honest traditional-basis framing of the 2:1 disease share); **C21** the tour's Desk step routes manpower to "the War Effort" instead of naming a nonexistent Manpower tab.
- **Probe-harness hardening (root-cause, same class as D232):** `probe-codex`, `probe-tutorial`, and `probe-help-overlay` still navigated with `waitUntil:'load'`, which stalls while the multi-MB embed streams (the documented gotcha) — switched to `domcontentloaded` + 120s; `probe-custom-battle-builder`'s screenshot got the 120s slow-Mac budget (its two "step failures" were a STALE artifact from a screenshot-timeout crash — the steps themselves were green). `probe-glossary`'s hook step asserted the OLD 2-tab scoping — updated to the corrected C18 intent (economy decorated; Codex excluded; the discriminating re-decorate test kept).
- **Adversarial pre-commit review (D145 routing):** a 3-lens Opus-high review workflow (builder logic · a11y semantics · content honesty + probe-teeth quality) + Opus default-refute verification — 3 raw, 1 refuted, 2 confirmed LOW (the delete-slot disabled-focus target; `inputmode="numeric"` on fractional fields), both FIXED and re-vetted before commit.
- **VET (focused, D176):** `node --check` on all 17 touched files; `data/codex.json` parses; build **GATE OK** (the two new Verified entries pass the 4e citation gate); probes **custom-battle-builder 14/14** (4 new steps: S27 clamp-warning · S21/S26 names+keypads · S22/S23 focus+announce · S24 confirm-guard) · **help-overlay 10/10** (+C19 quick-start + S07/S08 both surfaces) · **h2-cutaways 10/10** (+S12 trap / S14 app-toggle / S15 CSS) · **codex 24/24** (+C20 entries) · **tutorial 15/15** (+C21) · **presets 26/26** (+S28 live-region) · **h0-president-desk 3/3** (+S30 single label, +C18 press-tab decoration) · **h0-battle-briefing 3/3** (S13 side cards) · **glossary 12/12** (C18-corrected hook step) — all artifacts `ok=true`, 0 pageerrors, JSON readback verified; `git diff --check` clean. ## D232 — FABLE AUDIT M7: H0/UI VISUAL POLISH (9 ledger items) — [CLAUDE, per D224 ledger] (2026-07-03)

Presentation-only fixes across the six H0 shells + the shared 2D field view; no sim writes. **S00** the briefing prep list no longer paints a literal "&amp;" — the "Feint & flank" label is stored PLAIN in `_brgPREP` (85-battle-bridge) and escaped at each sink; the H0 row now escapes the hint too (label/hint were inconsistently handled). **S01** the after-action headline grade letter was rendering tiny/top-pinned in a nearly-empty 56px box because the bare `.h0a-overall span` rule out-specified `.h0a-grade-letter` (display:block + font-size:10px) — scoped to `.h0a-overall div>span`; the probe asserts the letter keeps a centered ≥20px flex treatment. **S02** the interstitial's army panel now spans the grid row (`.h0i-army{grid-column:1/-1}`) — its `.h0i-decisions`/`.h0i-offer` siblings already spanned, so the default no-decision state stranded it beside an empty half-frame. **S03+S11** the main menu joins the six-shell accent system: `--h0-green` #4f8064→#5f9273, `--h0-red` #aa5148→#b35a50, `--h0-amber` #c7863a→#d0a047 (the 99/100 warn tone), `--h0-muted` #b8c3bc→#c5cdc3, plus the three rgba literals of the retired hexes (the `::after` wash, `.h0-alert`, and the `.h0-stage .h0-field` tint — that last straggler was caught by the pre-commit review workflow, fixed not documented); the other shells' `--h0d/h0b/h0i-muted` normalized to the same #c5cdc3. The menu probe locks the shared tokens by computed value. **S04** on portrait ≤760px `fld2dView` (T0) now centers the field band in the strip between the top bar and the highest visible bottom-docked chrome instead of the full window — the phone screenshot previously showed ~a third of the screen as black void above a map stranded behind the unit HUD; `fld2dPick` shares the view so pointer picking stays consistent, and desktop/landscape keep the original formula. **S05** `.h0-actions` absorbs the command column's leftover height (flex:1 + space-evenly) so the no-save state (2 cards) no longer strands a ~200px void against the 514px stage. **S09** a phone-only "▼ Unit" minimize toggle (102, injected once as a #fldRoot sibling — #fldHud's innerHTML is rebuilt every render) collapses the selected-unit panel via a root class so the drag-to-order surface can reclaim the vertical space; it mirrors the terrain legend's collapse affordance, carries aria-pressed, and triggers the S04 recenter on toggle; the probe drives hide/restore. **S29** the President's Desk header shows the year once — the redundant "Year" chip is dropped (the Date chip already reads "July 1861"); the probe asserts the chip labels.
- **Probe-harness hardening (root-cause, not weakening):** `probe-h0-main-menu` was RED at D231 HEAD — a pre-existing resource flake (75s/8s budgets; it timed out on DIFFERENT scenes run-to-run on the 8 GB Mac: `viewport desktop` pre-M7, `saved campaign` post-M7). Ported the `SLOW_MAC` budget profile `probe-h0-president-desk` already uses (nav 150s, scene bound 420s, screenshot 300s); assertions unchanged; the probe then ran green 4/4 with its new S03/S05 teeth. Also caught in-session: piping probe output to `tail` masks the real exit code (`$?` was tail's) — the repo's own PIPESTATUS convention; gate runs now capture exit codes without pipes.
- **Adversarial pre-commit review (D145 routing):** a 2-lens Opus-high review workflow (CSS/markup · runtime behavior of the S04/S09 changes) + Opus default-refute verification — 3 raw findings, 2 refuted, 1 confirmed (the S03 old-amber rgba straggler on the Field-card tint), fixed and re-vetted before commit.
- **VET (focused, D176):** `node --check` on all 14 touched files; build **GATE OK**; probes **h0-main-menu 4/4** (new shared-token + actions-flex teeth) · **h0-tactical-hud 3/3** (new phone S04 band-position + S09 toggle teeth) · **h0-battle-briefing 3/3** (new no-double-escape tooth) · **h0-after-action 3/3** (new grade-letter tooth) · **h0-between-battle 3/3** (new army-span tooth) · **h0-president-desk 3/3** (new single-date tooth) · adjacent **field** + **order-feel 17/17** (the T0 fld2dView change) — all artifacts `ok=true`, `fatal=null`, 0 pageerrors, verified by JSON readback with unpiped exit codes; phone screenshot visually confirmed (band under the top bar, no void, Unit toggle docked above Terrain); `git diff --check` clean. ## D231 — FABLE AUDIT M6: TACTICAL CONTROL / PERF / SMALL-CODE FIXES (14 ledger items) — [CLAUDE, per D224 ledger] (2026-07-03)

Player-control, performance, and hygiene defects across six runtime files; every sim-adjacent change is gated on player-only state (`order.playerCharge`, set ONLY by `fldOrderCharge(u,t,{player:true})`), so headless AI-vs-AI baselines stay byte-identical — proven live by probe-presets' committed-CS-5/8 byte-identity guard + probe-phased-ab + probe-order-feel's no-AI-queue/no-AI-dig invariant. **The H5-i4 charge lock gains its three missing releases:** **E03** a charge stalled at impassable water (the T13 river clamp yielding ~zero progress) now settles to HOLD at the bank exactly like a stalled move — previously the lock never released (live target across the river = no contact/loss/rout) and the brigade was soft-locked forever; **E23** melee contact with ANY enemy releases the lock, not only the charged target — the melee loop resolves combat against every enemy in CONTACT_R, so an interposing brigade used to pin the player's unit in a fight it could not manage; **E34** a rout now PERMANENTLY releases a committed charge — the release was transient (playerCharge survived the rout, so the rallied brigade re-locked into the same broken charge); the rally transition in fldMoraleStep converts it to hold at the rally point. **S10** a HOLDING line (including a fresh spawn) now has a grabbable facing handle and pivots IN PLACE via the re-aim gesture (the UG:G feel gap); the pre-commit review workflow confirmed the naive version created a ~70–116yd forward-nudge dead-zone ahead of a standing line, so the hold handle pivots only on a real DRAG — a TAP on it falls through to the normal move gesture (pre-S10 nudge behavior preserved; the probe asserts both). **Conditioning honesty:** **E05** `(a.overall || 74)` / `(a.morale || 60)` treated a legitimately-computed 0 (a fully collapsed war) as the neutral anchor — now typeof+isFinite guards keep the full 0.88-floor penalty (and absorb NaN, which the bare typeof idiom in 87-auto-resolve would not); probe-conditioning's new E05 step measures ratio 0.880 exactly. **E06** the empty catch around `_a6Condition()` now console.warns (the 87-auto-resolve pattern) — still fail-safe to the unconditioned baseline, no longer silent. **Perf (Intel UHD-617 floor):** **E19** the shared formation-figure InstancedMesh layer is disposed + rebuilt on every base fld3dBuildUnits rebuild (reinforcements/phase advances) — the slot allocator only ever incremented, so after the first rebuild EVERY brigade lost its figures while dead slots re-uploaded each frame; the probe proves figures re-seat post-rebuild with nextSlot ≤ cap; **E20** the whole-layer instanceColor GPU re-upload now happens only when side/routing/active-count changes (was: a THREE.Color string-parse per figure per frame + a forced re-upload every frame); the probe asserts version-stable steady-state + a rout-transition bump; **E21** the woods sway recompute (a full per-clump instanceMatrix re-upload) is throttled to ~24 Hz; **E18** the casualty-fade traverse callback is hoisted (no per-call closure alloc). **UI/hygiene:** **E44** the global '?' keydown bails on INPUT/TEXTAREA/SELECT/contentEditable targets (typing '?' in the slot-rename or any codex/register/loot search box used to open How-to-Play and swallow the character; the probe asserts the guard + that document-level '?' still works); **S06** the duplicate stacked "PAUSED" badge is gone — `#hpPauseInd` is now a visually-hidden (sr-only clip) aria-live lane while the visible pause state lives in the top-bar #fldPhase chip alone (which already shows "Paused: <reason>"); **E04** dead `_fldAiIdx` removed + the false "step a slice of units per tick" AI-throttle comment corrected (full pass at AI_HZ); **E32** the stale T0 badge byte-identity comment corrected to D104/R-6 reality (rosterBadges populates ALL nine shipped scenarios and shifts their baselines; byte-identity holds only against the `_badgesOff` isolation path probes use).
- **Adversarial pre-commit review (D145 routing honored):** a 3-lens Opus-high review workflow (byte-identity/D74 · logic-correctness · THREE.js render correctness) + Opus default-refute verification over the diff — the byte-identity and render lenses returned clean; the one confirmed finding (the S10 dead-zone above) was fixed with the tap-fallthrough and re-vetted, not merely documented.
- **VET (focused, D176):** `node --check` on all 10 touched files; build **GATE OK** (known raw-embed soft warning); probes **order-feel 17/17** (4 new steps: E23 interposer release · E03 stall release · E34 rout→rally permanent release · S10 pivot-in-place + tap-nudge fallthrough) · **conditioning 7/7** (new E05 collapsed-war step, ratio 0.880) · **formation-figures 19/19** (new E19 rebuild-reclaim + E20 color-version steps) · **render-richness all-ok** · **help-overlay 8/8** (new E44 input-guard step + S06 sr-only assertion) · **field ok** · **presets ok (byte-identity guards green)** · **h0-tactical-hud 3/3** · **phased-ab ok** — all ok=true, 0 pageerrors; `git diff --check` clean. ## D230 — FABLE AUDIT M5: PROBE TEETH (E37 + 5) — [CLAUDE, per D224 ledger] (2026-07-03)

The vet suite could report green while probes silently could-not-fail; six audit items closed so a regression can no longer ship green: **E37** — 43 enrolled probes that lacked any `process.exit` (probe-cover, probe-morale, probe-vicksburg, probe-shiloh, every H0 probe, etc.) now carry a `beforeExit` teeth epilogue that exits nonzero unless the artifact they wrote THIS run reports `ok` with no failed steps and no pageerrors (closes the bare `node tools/probe-X.mjs; echo $?` false-green); **E15** — `vet-no-regression.mjs` now requires each expected artifact to EXIST, be parseable, and be newer than the run start (a missing/stale/unparseable JSON is a hard fail, not a silent pass on the exit code) via `statSync` freshness; **E08** — `probe-cab-exploit` converts its recorded exploit metrics into thrown assertions (capital cap <=100, once-per-turn block, no-NaN on undefined fields, intervention sign, turn-0 idempotency, the 100-clamp) + a nonzero exit — the conversion immediately caught a stale probe claim (the navy-heed step asserted "no scalar effect" but navy legitimately gives supply +1 per D49.6; corrected to assert the true bounded lift); **E09** — `probe-conditioning` (which guards the §27 no-silent-buff + Free-Battle-no-leak invariants over the `startBattleRuntime` conditioning seam) gains an exit code and is now enrolled in the SUITE; **E10** — `probe-motion` writes a `shots/probe-motion.json` artifact and exits nonzero on `PASS=false` (kept `headless:false`, so not suite-enrolled — it needs a display; the teeth make a manual run honest); **E24** — a new `tools/probe-help-overlay.mjs` gives the previously-untested help/onboarding subsystem (welcome card, How-to-Play sheet, the global "?" handler in menu + battle modes, the aria-modal tactical overlay with Escape+focus-return, the aria-live pause indicator) real teeth + a no-combat-leak static scan, enrolled in the SUITE.
- **VET (focused, D176):** `node --check` on all touched probes + vet-no-regression; build **GATE OK**; new/changed probes **probe-cab-exploit 8/8 · probe-conditioning ok · probe-help-overlay ok · probe-tutorial ok** (a teethed probe, proving the epilogue is benign on a passing probe), all ok=true, 0 fails, 0 pageerrors; `git diff --check` clean. The full `npm run vet:noreg` (now 2 probes larger + the freshness gate) runs at the run's batch checkpoint.

## D229 — FABLE AUDIT M4: BUILD-GATE HARDENING (10 ledger items in tools/build.mjs + manifest) — [CLAUDE, per D224 ledger] (2026-07-03)

The build gate is the only wall between source and the deliverable; ten audit-confirmed blind spots closed: **E00** the 4d no-fudge wall now also forbids `.strength`/`.maxStr` (the CLASSIC engine's strength fields), `.morale`/`.maxMor` (a ~40% melee multiplier), and `.ammo` — the documented benign evasion in T15 (`co.strength` display aggregate) was renamed `menTotal` so the guard could land without exception; **E30** a receiver-agnostic lever guard closes the one-line alias bypass (`var s=__FIELD.sev; s.attrition=…`) by forbidding direct writes to the six lever names on ANY receiver inside rating modules; **E14** gate 4e counts only DISTINCT non-empty sources (a Verified stamp on `["McPherson","McPherson"]` was passing as 2 citations — now matches 4f's dedup rigor; current catalog passes clean); **E29** a manifest-completeness gate fails the build if any `src/**/*.js` on disk is not enrolled (previously an unlisted file silently shipped nowhere and was scanned by no gate); **E40** a script-terminator gate fails the build if injected data/module content contains `</script`/`<script`/`<!--` (which would truncate the parse gate's own view and ship a broken page); **E28** the collision/collector regexes now match `async function` and generator declarations (a base fn shadowed by an async redeclare previously escaped both the collision error and the manifest-overrides requirement); **E16** the hex-bomb class is case-complete (`0x1G` now trips it); **E31** the per-data-file hex scan masks `data:` URL string content like the assembled-body scan (a future base64 blob in data/*.json would have false-FAILED the build); **E17** the parse-gate temp file is per-process unique (no fixed-path race/symlink hazard in the shared tmpdir); **E07** the manifest `overrides` comment now states the real gate semantics (exactly-two occurrences wherever they live, covering src-internal redeclares). Wall patterns verified to FIRE on six violation forms and pass five legitimate forms before commit.
- **VET (focused, D176):** `node --check` build.mjs + T15; the hardened gate itself run green on the live tree (**GATE OK** — also proving no orphan src files and no duplicate-source Verified records exist today); wall-pattern fire test 6/6 + false-trip test 5/5; focused `probe-oob` ok=true 0 pe (the T15 rename's render path); `git diff --check` clean.

## D228 — FABLE AUDIT M3: TAMPER/INJECTION HARDENING (E38 HIGH + 8) — [CLAUDE, per D224 ledger] (2026-07-03)

**E38 (HIGH, stored XSS):** a shared custom-battle pack could carry HTML in free-text fields (`<img onerror=...>` in a unit name) that reached the tactical HUD's `innerHTML` sink unescaped (T0:1487) — cross-user script execution on import+select. Fixed at BOTH layers: `fldCustomValidate` now strips `[<>]` from unit name/commander, leader name, and teaching head/body/provenance (mirroring the soldier/women importers' rejection rule), and the T0 HUD sink now escapes `u.name` via `htmlEsc` (typeof-guarded; authored names render identically). **Also:** **E12** T11's walk/scrub forbidden-key set gains `hasOwnProperty` (matches the node importers' BAD_KEYS); **E25** imported scenarios are capped (40 units / 16 leaders / 48 per terrain array — error + bounded loops, so a hostile 100k-element pack can't stall validation); **E26** array-form marker paths are now clamped to field bounds pair-by-pair exactly like the string form (and capped at 64 points); **E22** the D69 cushion law is enforced on the APPLY/clamp path too — `_fldClampCfg` caps `aiCushion` to [0,1] and zeroes it unless the tier is a genuine Recruit, so a hand-edited `cw_tactical_preset` or restored save can no longer ride an unbounded player cushion at Hardee (UI path already enforced it; Balanced/neutral paths byte-identical — cushion is 0 there); **E11** `_slEsc`'s fallback now escapes quotes so a tampered slot name can't break out of the `value="..."` attribute even if `_cabEsc` is absent; **E27** the loot Use/Equip `data-*` attributes use the quote-escaping `_lootAttr`; **E36** `campTrainingBonus` clamps fatigue to [0,100] so a tampered negative fatigue can't push the drag term above 1 and amplify the capped lifts; **E39** `import-tripo-unit-assets.mjs` gains the same recursive BAD_KEYS scan as its two sibling importers — all three JSON import lanes now share one hostile-key surface.
- **VET (focused, D176):** `node --check` on all 7 touched files; build **GATE OK**; tripo importer clean (expected disabled-slot warnings only); probes **custom-battle-builder · save-slots · presets (byte-identity) · loot-survival · camp · field · tripo-unit-assets** all ok=true, 0 fails, 0 pageerrors; `git diff --check` clean.

## D227 — FABLE AUDIT M2b: SRC-SIDE CAPTION/TEACHING-STRING CORRECTIONS (10 ledger items) — [CLAUDE, per D224 ledger] (2026-07-03)

Presentation-only copy fixes in the imagery/teaching modules (D74-inert; no data/sim change): **52-leaders-imagery** — Jackson photographed **April 26, 1863, a week** before his wounding (was "weeks"); Sherman's Georgia/Carolinas campaigns dated **1864–65**; the header's "every item is wartime-era" claim corrected (Dix c.1850s, Walker 1872). **53-usct-imagery** — the Tubman/Combahee caption now uses the project-standard "more than 700" (was "some 750", contradicting its own codex card). **51-scenes-imagery** — the Chancellorsville briefing plate now states plainly that no wartime photograph of the crossroads survives and the Marye's-Heights plate is the campaign's eastern front; the Bull Run alt names **Sudley Church**; the Antietam credit now names the actual 1911 book-scan source instead of a blanket LoC credit. **assets/scenes-imagery-provenance.json** — the Fredericksburg pontoon record's photographer/date reconciled to its own O'Sullivan/Gardner attribution, noting the contested December 1862 crossing went unphotographed. **T1-bull-run** — Early's brigade no longer described as rail-borne (menu deck + both end notes teach the rail brigades AND Early's cross-country flank attack). **83-endings** — the 1864 peace platform attributed to the **Democratic Party, with McClellan's repudiation of the plank** stated (was "McClellan ran on a peace platform").
- **VET (focused, D176):** `node --check` on all 5 touched JS files (caught and fixed an unescaped apostrophe in the T1 menu string before commit); build **GATE OK**; probes **bullrun · endings · scenes-imagery · leaders-imagery · usct-imagery** all ok=true, 0 fails, 0 pageerrors; `git diff --check` clean.

## D226 — FABLE AUDIT M2a: CONTENT TEXT CORRECTIONS ACROSS 15 DATA FILES (37 ledger items) — [CLAUDE, per D224 ledger] (2026-07-03)

Batch of D74-inert text/provenance corrections (no men/x/z/weapon/guns changed; one leader id normalized): **codex** (Sheridan≠Brandy Station · Lincoln died Apr 15 · Carney "first to earn, 1900 award" · Bragg's Chickamauga a victory unexploited · Longstreet wing-then-First-Corps · 2 truncated glossary shorts completed); **battles** — antietam (Mansfield 58, "one of the oldest"), fredericksburg (Kimball 520≈25% of real ~2,100), gettysburg (`ld_reyolds`→`ld_reynolds`; Pettigrew mortally wounded at Falling Waters Jul 14, not in the charge), chancellorsville (date Apr 30–May 6), shiloh (Hornets' Nest fell to **Ruggles's massed 53–62 guns** ~4 p.m. — card no longer claims artillery "never massed"; Bragg directed the **center**, correcting the prior wing-correction), vicksburg (Milliken's Bend USCT combat added to the Black-agency card; May 22 sector honestly spans to S.D. Lee's Railroad Redoubt; Cockrell restored to **Bowen's** reserve, Baldwin in Smith's line); **strategic data** — cabinet (+`cs-benjamin-ag` FIRST Attorney General Feb–Nov 1861, +`cs-keyes-ag` ad interim Oct 1863–Jan 1864, Watts tenure ends **1863/10** — the justice seat timeline is now continuous and no longer shows Watts as AG while Governor of Alabama), generals (Atlanta fell Sept 2 in Hood's bio too; Longstreet First-Corps anachronism), artillery (Whitworth **1860** Southport Sands; canister ball **~7 oz**), weapons (Colt/Sharps premium reframed as capture-scarcity, not blockade import — csCostMult gameplay unchanged), human-cost (66% disease share annotated as traditional-basis, not exact fraction of 750k), decisions (Bread Riot = **Holy Week**), economy (rejected 0.11 export figure → **0.035** + numbersAudit framing; Produce-Loan + USMRR provenance honestly downgraded to Inferred; pig-iron marquee ~13:1 matches its own indices), logistics-rail (CS equipment share 4% national + explicit `locomotiveSharePct` 20; CS railMiles 9000 aligned with economy.json), engineering (Cold Harbor "died in minutes" myth removed per the file's own Rhea card; James bridge 2,100 ft; Fredericksburg closest-approach ~25–40 yds ×2, harmonized with terrain-cover), under-told-perspectives (gitignored `.tmp` dossier no longer a Verified source anchor), soldier-replacements (Howe sourceNote completed to the standard caveat formula within the 360 cap; the focused probe caught my first paraphrase dropping the locked lowercase caveat phrases — kept the probe, fixed the data).
- **Deferred to M10 (sim-affecting):** C30 Heth's two-brigade strength 5500→~3,400 · C05's Ruggles-battery OOB scaling · C11 Prentiss badge · C33 Wallace attach id · C43/C44/C47 weapon tags · C28 Gettysburg geometry.
- **VET (focused, D176):** build **GATE OK**; importer `records=15 verified=15 disputed=0`; probes **codex · cabinet · shiloh · gettysburg · vicksburg · loot-survival · economy · weapons** all `ok=true`, 0 fails, 0 pageerrors; `git diff --check` clean. Full vet:noreg deferred to the run batch checkpoint.

## D225 — FABLE AUDIT M1: HIGH HISTORICAL CORRECTIONS UNDER VERIFIED STAMPS (C32/C41/C42/C46/C48) — [CLAUDE, per D224 ledger] (2026-07-03)

Five text-level HIGH ledger items fixed; all are D74-inert (notes/teach/commander-label text only — no men/x/z/xp/weapon/guns changed; unit ids stable). **C41** `chancellorsville.json`: dropped the false "only Stonewall Brigade commander killed in the war" claim (Baylor fell leading the brigade at Second Manassas, Winder at Cedar Mountain); replaced with the sourced correction. **C42** `gettysburg.json`: Pegram's battalion commander corrected Col.→**Maj.** William J. Pegram (his Gettysburg rank; Lt. Col. Feb 1864, Col. Feb 1865). **C46** `chickamauga.json`: removed the unverifiable "Grant later called it one of the finest tactical performances of the war" sentence from the Thomas leader note (no source; cuts against Grant's documented coolness toward Thomas); the army-conferred "Rock of Chickamauga" name and five-hour stand carry the point. **C48** `cabinet.json` (2 sites): the Welles teaching no longer repeats the ~"tenth of peacetime" cotton figure the project's own diplomacy numbersAudit rejected; both surfaces now teach the audit-approved ~3-4%-reaches-Europe / ~95%-collapse framing. **C32** `vicksburg.json`: the May 22 phase is retitled **"The Grand Assault"** and the named 150-volunteer **forlorn hope is relocated (textually) to its real front — Sherman's Graveyard Road assault on the Stockade Redan**; the Jackson Road field's lead unit is renamed to the documented **Logan's Assault Column** (McPherson's XVII Corps; id `us_forlorn_hope` kept stable for sim/probe continuity), with markers/walls/reinforcement/aria/_comment text aligned; `tools/probe-vicksburg.mjs` assertions track the corrected names at identical strength (same steps, same thresholds). **C28 (Gettysburg geometry) verified positional** during this milestone — CS attacker OOB (z 300–410) sits ON the objective/LRT/Cemetery Ridge while the US defender spawns at z 770–850, the Antietam template with roles flipped but coordinates not flipped — reassigned to the M10 sim-affecting milestone (A/B + baseline re-vet required).
- **VET (focused, D176):** build **GATE OK** (all content gates, known raw-embed soft warning); `node --check` probe-vicksburg; focused `probe-vicksburg` **18/18 ok=true 0 pe**; adjacent `probe-chancellorsville` **24/24**, `probe-gettysburg` **ok=true 0 fails**, `probe-chickamauga` **ok=true 0 fails**, `probe-cabinet` **12 steps ok=true**, all 0 pageerrors; `git diff --check` clean. Full `npm run vet:noreg` deferred to the run's batch checkpoint (D176).

## D224 — FABLE AUDIT RUN 1: FULL-SPECTRUM AUDIT LEDGER (findings + triage; docs-only commit) — [CLAUDE Fable 5 audit] (2026-07-03)

First execution of `FABLE-AUDIT-PROMPT.md` at HEAD `6d8b098` (D223). Three read-only audit Workflows — content (historical accuracy/citations + teaching quality), engine (D74/balance + code-health/gate integrity + performance), surface (UX/UI + accessibility + docs coherence + completeness-vs-checklist) — fanned out **304 agents** (Opus high/xhigh finders/verifiers/critics, Sonnet medium mechanical legs; the D145/D223 routing law held — no helper ran on Fable), with loop-until-dry on dimensions 1/2/7 (3 rounds each), adversarial default-refute verification (HIGH findings = 3-skeptic majority kill), and a completeness critic + supplemental sweep per workflow. **Result: 150 findings confirmed (10 HIGH / 71 MED / 69 LOW), 43 refuted.** Checked clean en route: the committed `civil_war_generals.html` is byte-identical to a fresh source rebuild (GATE OK, all content gates); no secrets or backup junk is git-tracked; media-budget + Intel-UHD-617-profile artifacts match the D213 baselines.
- **The full ledger** (stable IDs C##/E##/S##/X##, one line per finding, FIX-NOW vs PROPOSAL split, the refuted list, and the ranked FIX-NOW milestone plan M1–M11) lives in `REVIEW-QUEUE.md` §"FABLE AUDIT — 2026-07-03 (run 1)" — the standing findings memory between audit runs.
- **Notable HIGHs:** a stored XSS through shared custom-battle pack unit names reaching an unescaped `innerHTML` HUD sink (E38); four wrong-fact-under-Verified-stamp battle-data items of the D92 class that survived or recurred (C41 Paxton, C42 Pegram, C46 unverifiable Grant quote, C48 the rejected ~11% cotton figure in cabinet teaching); a Gettysburg defender-seating geometry question (C28, verify-first); a systemic probe-teeth gap — 43 enrolled probes have no failing exit path (E37) and the suite trusts stale artifacts (E15); build-gate blind spots — gate 4d does not guard `.strength`/`.morale`/`.ammo` (E00) and gate 4e accepts duplicate/empty source entries (E14).
- **Proposals for Aaron** (not built): base-engine save hardening (E13), always-on situational badges (E33), a structural `_SAVE_VER` gate (E41), auto-resolve-vs-real-time reward equivalence (E42/E43), multi-phase casualty carry + Antietam draw-band reachability (E45/E46), config-surface art direction (S25), MJI residue deletion (X02).
- Fix milestones ship as **D225+**, each updating its ledger lines from PENDING → fixed-in-D###. This D224 commit is docs-only (ledger + RUN-LOG backfill + live-head refresh); no src/data change.

## D223 — CLAUDE FABLE 5 MIGRATION (docs + settings, no gameplay change) — [AARON directive + CLAUDE implementation] (2026-07-03)

> **→ superseded by D286 (2026-07-06):** Fable left the subscription; the main loop is Claude Opus 4.8 again. This entry stands as history (a Fable session shipped D223–D285); the forward-operating docs it names have been repointed to Opus 4.8.

Aaron switched the Claude Code main loop to **Claude Fable 5** (`/model claude-fable-5[1m]`, persisted in `~/.claude/settings.json`) while Fable is included in his subscription, and directed: (1) snapshot everything being changed so the old setup can be restored when Fable leaves the subscription, (2) reform the operating docs/settings around Anthropic's "Prompting Claude Fable 5" guidance, (3) create a standing Fable kickoff prompt to audit + improve the whole game.
- **Decision:** the main reasoning/integration loop in Claude Code is now **Fable 5 + xhigh** (supersedes "Opus" in the D63/D145 wording; the routing LAW itself is unchanged). Helper routing is unchanged and now stricter in one respect: **no helper is ever Fable** — explicit Sonnet/Haiku on mechanical legs and Opus high/xhigh on reasoning legs, set on every `Agent`/Workflow `agent()` call, because an unlabeled helper now inherits xhigh+Fable (the most expensive possible inheritance). "5.5" in older doc text remains the Codex top model; docs now say "top model" = Fable 5 in Claude Code, 5.5 in Codex.
- **Rollback:** `backups/pre-fable-2026-07-03/` (LOCAL-ONLY, gitignored — contains user-level `~/.claude` snapshots that must not ship) holds pre-change copies of every touched file + `RESTORE.md` with exact revert steps; fastest model-only revert is `/model claude-opus-4-8`. Repo-doc rollback = `git revert` of this commit. Pre-change HEAD: `f27cac9` (D222).
- **Implementation:** new **`FABLE-5-PLAYBOOK.md`** (canonical) distills the Fable guidance to project law — effort/turn-length expectations, the five standard run-prompt snippets (act-don't-overplan · scope discipline · grounded progress claims · checkpoint rule · autonomous reminder), subagent routing under Fable, the D171 context-boundary interplay (boundary stands; no mid-milestone scope-trimming), refusal-classifier handling (offensive-cyber/bio/reasoning-extraction; false-positive fallback = run the leg on Opus 4.8; never instruct "show your reasoning" — skills library audited 2026-07-03, clean), and prompt/skill de-prescription guidance. New **`FABLE-AUDIT-PROMPT.md`** (canonical) is the re-runnable paste-ready kickoff for a full-spectrum audit+improve run (9-dimension adversarial audit → REVIEW-QUEUE.md ledger triage → charter-gated fix milestones; V1-CHECKLIST/locks remain law). Routing sections refreshed in `START-HERE.md` (+ canonical-reference entries + a model-naming note in the priority picker), `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `AUTONOMOUS-RUN.md` (§1 routing + §3 loop line), `OVERNIGHT-RUN-PROMPT.md` §5, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, and user-level `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md`; `.gitignore` gained `backups/`; memory files (`civilwar-subagent-model-routing`, new `civilwar-fable-migration`) updated.
- **Boundary:** docs/settings/tooling only — no `src/`, `data/`, or `build/` change; the playable file is untouched (build gate run anyway as a sanity check). No roadmap change: V1-CHECKLIST order, Phase H park (D214), M8/Q5/Q6 locks all stand.
- **Gate:** `node tools/build.mjs` GATE OK (no source change → byte-stable output), `git diff --check` clean; full battery not spent per D176 (docs-only slice).

## D222 — PHASE I SOLDIER'S STORY ALEXANDER S. WEBB SLICE — [CODEX implementation + source audit] (2026-07-03)

D222 continues Priority 1 Phase I Soldier's Story scale-out after D214 parked Group 5 Phase H as complete-enough. It does not reopen Phase H media work or grant any battle-build approval.
- **Decision:** add Alexander S. Webb as the fifteenth citation-grade Soldier's Story replacement, mapping him to the generated Gettysburg / Philadelphia Brigade command slot `ss:gettysburg:US:us_phila_bde:cmd`. This is a Brig. Gen. / Philadelphia Brigade / Second Division / II Corps / Army of the Potomac command story at the Angle during Pickett's Charge, not a company-command claim, major-general-at-Gettysburg claim, or portrait upgrade: NPS identifies Webb as newly appointed brigadier general placed in command of the Philadelphia Brigade and ties his troops to the Angle during Pickett's Charge; NPS OOB places the Philadelphia Brigade in Second Division, II Corps; CMOHS supports Webb's July 3, 1863 Gettysburg action. The record asserts no company command, no major-general-at-Gettysburg rank, no portrait asset, and neutral inferred ratings.
- **Boundary:** this is a data/probe/doc overlay slice only. No tactical combat rules, battle OOB files, bridge math, save schema, generated-person machinery, women-in-war data, media asset, portrait asset, H2 footage, Tripo action, or runtime web dependency changed. Phase H media additions, M8, and Q5/Q6 remain locked behind explicit Aaron decisions.
- **Implementation:** `data/soldier-replacements.json` now has fifteen Verified records. `tools/probe-loot-survival.mjs` locks the fifteen-record overlay, Webb alias lookup, rank/unit/team/source-note caveats, no unsupported portrait, rendered UI detail/source evidence, and hostile-pack rejection. Startup/routing docs now point the next run to another bounded Phase I Soldier's Story named-person/story slice while preserving the D214 Phase H park.

## D213 — GROUP 5 · PHASE H LOW-TIER MARKER BODY LAYER PERF POLISH — [CODEX implementation + focused gate] (2026-07-02)

D213 continues Group 5 Phase H polish/media/perf without granting the M8 battle-build go/no-go. The slice uses the D212 Intel UHD-617 profile to choose a zero-new-media low-tier render/resource step: low-quality markers still needed the slab/front body read for side and facing readability, but they were paying one slab mesh and one front-strip mesh per unit.
- **Decision:** preserve the low-tier slab/front fallback, but render it through two scene-level shared layers, `markerBodySlabLayer` and `markerBodyFrontLayer`, instead of per-unit child slab/front meshes. High-tier formation-figure infantry still keeps no hidden marker body, `renderRich="off"` / high fallback markers still use the normal per-unit slab/front body, and T18 casualty fade still materializes a temporary fallback body only when opacity needs the cue.
- **Implementation:** `src/tactical/T0-field-sandbox.js` adds the shared low-tier body layer, assigns stable body slots, parks inactive instances, and clears the layer on rebuild/dispose. `src/tactical/T23-tripo-unit-assets.js` clears the shared body slot when a local GLB hides the base marker and avoids restoring stale child slab/front on low tier. `src/tactical/T24-formation-figures.js` makes its low/off fallback restore path aware of the shared low-tier body layer. The profile/formation/Tripo/visual probes now record/assert active shared body slots instead of assuming low-tier child slab/front residency.
- **Verification:** `node --check` passed for touched source/probe files; `node tools/build.mjs` passed **GATE OK** with the existing raw-embed soft warning. Focused `probe-intel-uhd617-profile` passed **19/19** with high 6.30ms / 75 render calls / 116 objects and low 1.62ms / 63 render calls / 97 objects. Adjacent `probe-formation-figures` passed **17/17**, `probe-tripo-unit-assets` **15/15** after it caught the stale child/body-layer duplication, `probe-visual-fidelity` **27/27** after updating the low-tier shared-body assertion, `probe-render-richness` **31/31**, and `probe-media-budget` **6/6** with only the known soft warning. Key-aware JSON readback across counted artifacts found no `ok=false`, failed steps, `FATAL`, pageerrors, realErrors, or texture warnings; `git diff --check` passed.
- **Next:** continue Group 5 Phase H with another bounded zero-byte render/readability/perf slice while the raw embed tier remains above the soft warning, or surface the explicit media-budget/profile decision before adding real PD/surviving-colours/H2/HDRI/model media. M8 battle-build remains blocked pending Aaron go/no-go; Q5 Chattanooga, Atlanta/March, Franklin/Nashville, and Q6 USCT playable battles remain last.

## D209 — GROUP 5 · PHASE H IDLE SELECTION-RING RESOURCE POLISH — [CODEX implementation + focused gate] (2026-07-02)

D209 continues Group 5 Phase H polish/media/perf without granting the M8 battle-build go/no-go. The slice uses the D208 Intel UHD-617 profile to choose a zero-new-media resource step: D201 already stopped drawing opacity-zero idle selection rings, so T0 now stops allocating the idle ring mesh/material in the default high/low marker path until a unit is actually selected.
- **Decision:** lazy-create selection rings only when the selection/fade cue needs them, while preserving the selected-unit ring, casualty-fade ring behavior, and the local-GLB hidden-marker cue. T18 reacquires a ring that T0 creates after the first sync, and T23 explicitly requests a ring when a local GLB hides the base slab/front marker.

## D200 — GROUP 5 · PHASE H FORMATION-FIGURES PERF POLISH — [CODEX implementation + focused gate] (2026-07-02)

The slice uses the D199 Intel UHD-617 profile to choose a zero-new-media T24 render-cost polish step: preserve the high-tier procedural infantry formations, but stop paying five instanced meshes per brigade for them.

- **No-scope creep:** no embedded media bytes, asset fetch, H2 video, surviving-colours ingestion, playable battle, tactical scenario, OOB rewrite, combat model, save schema, bridge/output write, runtime web dependency, external account/trial/download, Tripo action/credit/API call, or `build/base.html` edit. D199 continues Group 5 Phase H polish/media/perf without granting the M8 battle-build go/no-go. The slice uses the D198 Intel UHD-617 profile to choose a zero-new-media render-cost polish step: keep the T21 visual-fidelity contact-shadow readability cue, but stop paying one scene object / render call per brigade for it.
- **Decision:** instance the existing contact-shadow decal instead of removing it. The low-tier profile was green but still showed per-unit shadow meshes resident in both high and low tiers. Removing shadows would save cost at the expense of unit grounding/readability; a shared `InstancedMesh` preserves the visual cue while reducing object and draw-call pressure.
- **What shipped:** `src/tactical/T21-visual-fidelity.js` now owns one scene-level `vfShadowLayer` instanced contact-shadow mesh. Unit groups store only a shadow instance index; `fldVfSetShadow()` updates position/rotation/scale per live visible unit, clears dead/fog-hidden/off instances to a zero-scale parked matrix, and `fldVfClearShadowLayer()` prevents stale phase/reinforcement instances. `tools/probe-visual-fidelity.mjs` now locks the instanced shadow contract, ground gap, positive instance scale, low-tier peg gating, renderRich off-switch, byte-identity burst, and screenshot health. `tools/probe-intel-uhd617-profile.mjs` records and asserts the instanced shadow mode in high and low profile scenes.
- **No-scope creep:** no embedded media bytes, asset fetch, H2 video, surviving-colours ingestion, playable battle, tactical scenario, OOB rewrite, combat model, save schema, bridge/output write, runtime web dependency, external account/trial/download, Tripo action, or `build/base.html` edit. This is presentation/performance-only T21 visual-fidelity polish.
- **Vetting:** `node --check` passed for `src/tactical/T21-visual-fidelity.js`, `tools/probe-visual-fidelity.mjs`, and `tools/probe-intel-uhd617-profile.mjs`; `node tools/build.mjs` printed **GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓ · women-in-war ✓** with the existing raw-embed soft warning and rebuilt generated HTML from source; focused `probe-visual-fidelity` passed **26/26, 0 pageerrors, 0 texture warnings** and visually confirmed the 3D screenshot; adjacent `probe-media-budget` passed **6/6** with the known soft warning only; `probe-intel-uhd617-profile` passed **12/12** with high 9.57ms / 108 render calls / 178 objects and low 2.38ms / 100 calls / 134 objects; `probe-h0-tactical-hud` passed **3/3, 0 pageerrors**. JSON readback across counted artifacts found no `ok=false`, failed steps, `FATAL`, pageerrors, realErrors, or texture warnings. `probe-formation-figures` and `probe-render-richness` were attempted as extra adjacent checks but both hung before writing fresh artifacts and were stopped with exit 130, so neither is counted. M8 remains locked behind Aaron go/no-go.)

## D194 — GROUP 5 · PHASE H INTEL UHD-617 PROFILE READOUT — [CODEX implementation + focused gate] (2026-07-01)

D194 continues Group 5 Phase H polish/media/perf without granting the M8 battle-build go/no-go. The slice turns the Intel UHD Graphics 617 / 8 GB RAM floor into an executable profile/readout gate before adding further media polish.

Timing warnings are nonfatal because headless Chrome may vary, but pageerrors, texture warnings, nonblank rendering, and low-tier gating are hard.
- **What shipped:** new `tools/probe-intel-uhd617-profile.mjs` launches the built single-file game in Chrome, records embedded asset footprint, WebGL renderer info, high/low 3D launch and render-burst timings, render calls, scene object counts, pixel nonblank checks, low-tier formation/peg gating, pageerrors, and Three.js undefined-texture warnings. `tools/vet-no-regression.mjs` now includes `intel uhd617 profile`; `civil_war_generals.html` rebuilt from source because `GAME_DATA["media-budget"]` changed.
- **Profile evidence:** local Chrome used the actual `ANGLE (Intel, ANGLE Metal Renderer: Intel(R) UHD Graphics 617, Unspecified Version)` path, not SwiftShader. Chickamauga high tier measured 3407.9ms launch, 9.92ms average proxy frame burst, 115 render calls, and 195 scene objects. Low tier measured 343ms launch, 2.48ms average proxy frame burst, 107 calls, 144 objects, `fldLow:true`, and formation figures gated out. Embedded core remains 199 files / 2.418 MB raw; generated HTML is about 7.023 MB.
- **No-scope creep:** no playable battle, tactical scenario, battle roster/OOB rewrite, combat model, save schema, H2 video enablement, runtime web dependency, external asset fetch, account/trial/download, or Tripo action. H2 footage and heavier HDRI/model work remain optional-pack or explicit-budget only.

The Erlanger path is one-shot/reduced on repeat.
- **Bridge/save/resolve decision:** inactive default returns exact zero; US side is inert; active CS priorities are capped/costly and can trade morale/fatigue/civilian pressure for limited supply/funds/state-capacity effects. Strategic resolve writes only CS finance ledgers, funds/debt/cotton/impressment/civilian pressure, economy inflation, and capital pressure.
- **Erlanger correction decision:** keep the D180 corrected `data/economy.json` value (`netProceedsPct:0.45`, Erlanger buy price 77, public offer 90) and update `HISTORICAL-DATA-ECONOMY.md` so it no longer repeats the stale ~72% net-proceeds shortcut.
- **Wiring/probe decision:** extend manifest, War Effort/H0 render, economy-tab wiring, lifecycle, and battle bridge through guarded seams; enroll `tools/probe-cs-finance.mjs` in `tools/vet-no-regression.mjs` for the later batch/release gate.

## D184 — GRADUATE-HISTORY PASS · M5 KEYSTONE CARDS — [CODEX implementation + source audit] (2026-07-01)

D184 ships the M5 graduate-history keystone-card slice from the authorized build-out. It remains a pure teaching/readout milestone; no tactical, bridge, save, resolve, victory, OOB, or battle-output path changed.
- **Codex decision:** add two new `Verified` codex keystone entries rather than creating a parallel graduate-themes deck. `second-founding` ties McPherson's Second American Revolution frame and Foner's Second Founding frame to greenbacks, conscription, contraband policy, emancipation, Reconstruction, and Douglass's July 6, 1863 Philadelphia enlistment speech. The card explicitly does not attribute the "brass letters U.S." phrase to the March 1863 "Men of Color, To Arms!" broadside. `age-of-emancipation` compares Britain 1830s compensated emancipation, Russia's 1861 emancipation of roughly 23 million privately owned serfs, and U.S. wartime, uncompensated, Black-agency-driven emancipation.
- **War Effort teaching decision:** extend existing `disease-medical` and `irregular-war` debate arrays instead of adding new systems. The disease card pairs Hacker's roughly 750,000 death-toll revision with Drew Gilpin Faust's death/mourning framework, Good Death, embalming, reburial, and national cemeteries. The irregular-war card names Fellman and Sutherland to move irregular violence out of romance and into civilian danger, coercion, and political violence.
- **Renderer decision:** `src/63-disease-medical.js` and `src/65-irregular-war.js` now render all authored debate cards in the existing details sections. This is display-only: default inactive bridge effects, active caps, resolve hooks, and save shape are unchanged.
- **Gate/probe decision:** extend `probe-codex`, `probe-disease-medical`, and `probe-irregular-war` with M5 locks, including the corrected Douglass attribution, the Russia ~23 million guardrail, Faust/Hacker death-and-mourning terms, and Fellman/Sutherland historiography rendering.
- **Vetting:** JSON parse for codex/disease-medical/irregular-war/manifest; `node --check` for touched source/probe files; `node tools/build.mjs` **GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓ · women-in-war ✓**; focused `probe-codex` **21 steps, 0 pageerrors**, `probe-disease-medical` **8 steps, 0 pageerrors**, and `probe-irregular-war` **8 steps, 0 pageerrors**; adjacent `probe-primary-sources` **12 steps, 0 pageerrors**, `probe-bridge` **6 steps, 0 pageerrors**, and `probe-h0-president-desk` **3/3, 0 pageerrors**; JSON artifact readback clean; `git diff --check` clean. ## D183 — GRADUATE-HISTORY PASS · M4 SOURCE-CRITICISM / UNDER-TOLD VOICES / NATIVE REWRITE — [CODEX implementation + source audit] (2026-07-01)

D183 ships the M4 source-criticism + under-told voices + Native rewrite slice from the authorized graduate-history build-out. It remains a pure teaching/readout milestone, not a simulation or balance milestone.
- **Source-criticism decision:** extend the existing `data/primary-sources.json` document reader with a new `source-criticism-voices` category rather than creating a parallel historiography deck. Rationale: the D181 reader already enforces excerpt word caps, repository/locator attribution, source critique, `Verified` source counts, XSS escaping, and purity scans. M4 belongs in that same source-apparatus contract.
- **Source set decision:** the M4 primary-source records are Gettysburg Address Bliss copy, Susie King Taylor's 1902 postwar critique, Dolly Lunt Burge's hard-war eyewitness/enslaver-language record, William McCarter's Irish Brigade memoir, and William Dwyer's Irish Brigade letter. Each is `Verified` with ≥2 sources and visible source limits; McCarter is treated as retrospective veteran memoir, while Dwyer is treated as a contemporary soldier-family letter preserved through a pension-file transcript with punctuation-normalization caveats.
- **Codex decision:** add Source Criticism, East Tennessee Unionism, and West Virginia Statehood to `data/codex.json`. The East Tennessee entry explicitly states that Unionism and abolitionism cannot be collapsed: Brownlow was anti-secession and pro-Union, but his 1858 Brownlow-Pryne debate defended slavery. West Virginia is framed as Unionist statehood conditioned by the Willey Amendment and gradual emancipation, not simple abolitionist purity.
- **Women-lane decision:** Mary Boykin Chesnut and Susie King Taylor belong in the existing D153 women-in-war presentation lane, not in Soldier's Story. Both records are `canMap:false`; Chesnut teaches elite Confederate diarist/source-editing problems, and Taylor teaches Black woman support labor plus postwar betrayal. The importer/probe now lock the lane at 9 records, 8 Verified, 1 Disputed.
- **Native rewrite decision:** upgrade `native-nations` in `data/under-told-perspectives.json` from Inferred to Verified using the M4 dossier, but keep the scope narrow. The source note is load-bearing: this is not a license to create a playable Trans-Mississippi battle, OOB, unit roster, or Confederate-diversity claim. M8 battle-build still needs Aaron go/no-go.
- **Runtime decision:** preserve D74 by keeping M4 a readout/data/probe milestone. `src/66-under-told-perspectives.js` only updates teaching copy and the women-lane count; the pre-existing perspective-liaison bridge remains exact-zero when inactive, capped/costly when active, and adds no direct overall bonus.
- **Vetting:** JSON parse for primary sources/codex/women/under-told; women importer **records=9 verified=8 disputed=1**; `node --check` for touched source/probe files; `node tools/build.mjs` **GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓ · women-in-war ✓**; focused `probe-primary-sources` **12 steps, 0 pageerrors** and `probe-under-told-perspectives` **8 steps, 0 pageerrors**; adjacent `probe-codex` **20 steps, 0 pageerrors**, `probe-women-in-war` **8 steps, 0 pageerrors**, `probe-h0-president-desk` **3/3, 0 pageerrors**; JSON readback clean; `git diff --check` clean. Full `npm run vet:noreg` remains deferred under D176. (Impact: M4 complete; next M5 = keystone cards.)

## D182 — GRADUATE-HISTORY PASS · M3 RECONSTRUCTION/MEMORY — [CODEX implementation + source audit] (2026-07-01)

D182 ships the M3 Reconstruction/memory slice from the authorized graduate-history build-out. It remains a pure teaching/readout milestone, not a simulation or balance milestone.
- **Primary-source decision:** extend `data/primary-sources.json` with a new `reconstruction-memory` category instead of creating a separate Reconstruction deck. Rationale: the document-card contract created in D181 already carries the necessary `verbatimExcerpt`, `attribution`, `sourceCritique`, `indictment`, `sources`, and purity checks. Keeping M3 in the same reader avoids a parallel source apparatus and keeps quote discipline enforceable in one focused probe.
- **History-source decision:** the M3 seed set is deliberately narrow and load-bearing: Mississippi's 1865 Black Code apprenticeship law, Elias Hill's 1871 KKK testimony, and Frederick Douglass's 1865 "What the Black Man Wants." Each is `Verified` with ≥2 sources, a ≤60-word excerpt, and visible repository/locator notes. The Black Code record teaches coercive labor/restoration; Hill teaches Reconstruction terror as political violence; Douglass teaches Black suffrage before Appomattox as a condition of real freedom.
- **Amendment-date decision:** surface the Thirteenth as ratified Dec. 6, 1865, the Fourteenth as July 9, 1868, and the Fifteenth as certified/adopted March 30, 1870 while also naming the Feb. 3, 1870 state-ratification threshold. Rationale: Aaron's brief required March 30, but the codex must not falsely imply that March 30 was the threshold date.
- **Memory-manufacture decision:** update the Lost Cause codex to pair Edward A. Pollard's 1866 naming/Resistance project with Alexander Stephens's 1868-1870 Constitutional View recast after the 1861 Cornerstone confession. The card now explicitly teaches memory manufacture rather than treating Lost Cause as a vague later myth.
- **After-action decision:** the Reconstruction coda in `src/82-after-action.js` now names amendment dates, Black Codes, Klan terror, Redemption, Jim Crow, and memory manufacture. This is still a pure readout: no grade formula, ending predicate, combat path, save version, bridge facet, OOB, or battle-result output changed.
- **Gate/probe decision:** extend `tools/probe-primary-sources.mjs`, `tools/probe-codex.mjs`, and `tools/probe-afteraction.mjs` to lock the M3 claims. `tools/probe-codex.mjs` navigation timeout is raised from 60s to 90s after a local-Mac load-timeout false red; assertions are unchanged/strengthened, and the change matches the existing slow-browser probe policy.
- **Vetting:** JSON parse for primary sources/codex/manifest; `node --check` for touched source/probe files; `node tools/build.mjs` **GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓**; focused `probe-primary-sources` **11 steps, 0 pageerrors**; adjacent `probe-codex` **19 steps, 0 pageerrors**, `probe-afteraction` **15 steps, 0 pageerrors**, `probe-h0-after-action` **3/3, 0 pageerrors**; JSON readback clean; `git diff --check` clean. Full `npm run vet:noreg` remains deferred under D176. (Impact: M3 complete; next M4 = source-criticism + under-told voices + Native rewrite.)

## D181 — GRADUATE-HISTORY PASS · M2 READ THE DOCUMENT APPARATUS — [CODEX implementation + source audit] (2026-07-01)

D181 ships the M2 primary-source apparatus from the authorized graduate-history build-out: a new President's Desk **Documents / Read the Document** surface plus visible manpower debate-card scholarship. It is a pure teaching/readout milestone, not a simulation or balance milestone.
- **Source-apparatus decision:** the first deck lives in new `data/primary-sources.json` (`cw_primary_sources_v1`) rather than being folded into `data/codex.json`. Rationale: document cards have a different contract than glossary entries: `verbatimExcerpt` (≤60 words), `attribution`, `sourceCritique`, `indictment`, and `catalystFrame` on Confederate self-justifications. Keeping them separate makes the quote discipline and static purity scan easier to enforce. (Reversible, additive.)
- **Rendering decision:** new `src/68-primary-sources.js` renders an expandable, searchable, category-filtered Documents tab. Confederate self-justification cards render **verbatim excerpt → catalyst frame → attribution** before critique/sources; Union and Black soldier agency documents use the same reader without a catalyst frame. The module reads only `GAME_DATA["primary-sources"]` and writes only DOM show/hide/expand state. (Reversible, additive.)
- **History-source decision:** the seed deck starts with 5 Confederate self-justifications (Stephens Cornerstone, Mississippi declaration, Confederate Constitution, Hale to Magoffin, Cobb to Seddon) and 3 counter-documents (Emancipation Proclamation service clause, Douglass "Men of Color, To Arms!", Gooding equal-pay letter). Source notes explicitly preserve transcription/normalization caveats instead of laundering them. Each `Verified` record carries ≥2 sources, and the build citation gate independently enforces the ≥2-source invariant.
- **Manpower readout decision:** the already-authored `data/manpower-teaching.json` cards are now visible in `src/70-manpower.js` as compact consensus / scholarly dissent / Lost Cause claim named-and-countered / primary documents / takeaway blocks. The claims are clipped in the War Effort overview for readability; the full card text remains in data. No manpower math, replacement ratios, pools, or bridge inputs changed.
- **Gate/probe decision:** new `tools/probe-primary-sources.mjs` is the focused M2 gate and is enrolled in `tools/vet-no-regression.mjs` for future batches. It locks schema, excerpt word cap, `Verified` source count, Confederate catalyst frames, desk dispatch, filtering/search/expand, DOM purity, XSS escaping, and a static scan that tactical/bridge/save/output paths do not read `primary-sources` / `_ps*`. `probe-manpower` now locks the surfaced debate lanes and received only local-Mac navigation/screenshot budget hardening after harness timeouts; assertions were strengthened, not weakened.
- **Vetting:** JSON parse for primary sources + manifest; `node --check` for touched source/probe/suite files; `node tools/build.mjs` **GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓**; focused `probe-primary-sources` **10 steps, 0 pageerrors**; adjacent `probe-manpower` **9 steps, 0 pageerrors**, `probe-codex` **18 steps, 0 pageerrors**, `probe-h0-president-desk` **3/3, 0 pageerrors**; JSON readback clean; `git diff --check` clean. Full `npm run vet:noreg` remains deferred under D176. (Impact: graduate-history source apparatus live; next M3 = Reconstruction/memory.)

## D180 — GRADUATE-HISTORY PASS · M1 CORRECTIONS & FABRICATION FIX — [OPUS/CLAUDE] (2026-06-30)

M1 of the authorized graduate-seminar history build-out (brief: `.tmp/civil-war-phd-execution-brief.md`; audit source-of-truth: `.tmp/civil-war-history-audit-and-phd-plan.md`). Ships the audit's Phase-1 corrections on a clean base before the primary-source apparatus (M2+). Pure teaching read-out — **D74 byte-identical** (no OOB/bridge/scoreboard/save writes); anti-Lost-Cause.

- **The one integrity defect (HIGH) — twenty-slave-law composite voices in `data/decisions.json`.** The teaching card's `perspectives[]` shipped dramatized composite voices in verbatim-quote style under `provenance:"Verified"`, untagged — worst cases a padded Sam Watkins line (a real *Co. Aytch* clause inflated with invented words) and an **invented first-person "The enslaved" voice**. Fix: every composite now carries `[composite illustration — not a verbatim quotation]`; the Watkins voice is trimmed to the exact verified 1882 clause ("It gave us the blues; we wanted twenty negroes"); the invented enslaved voice is **replaced** with a real, verbatim, ≥2-source-verified excerpt — Cpl. James Henry Gooding (born enslaved, 1838), 54th Massachusetts, to President Lincoln, Sept. 28 1863: "Now Your Excellency, We have done a Soldiers Duty. Why cant we have a Soldiers pay?" (original spelling preserved). This is the first real Black voice in verified words on a player-facing card; the "Modern scholarship" voice was extended to bridge Gooding to the theme (men born enslaved, now soldiers — the living refutation of the "natural subordination" the Confederacy was founded on). Card `sources[]` gained the Watkins + Gooding citations.
- **Five metadata/internal-doc fixes (audit §3 rows 2–7):** `data/economy.json` timeline string "net ~72%" → "net ~45% of face" (matches the file's own corrected `netProceedsPct:0.45`); `data/vicksburg.json` top-level date standardized to "May 18-July 4, 1863 (invested May 18; first assault May 19; surrender July 4)" (ends the l.17-vs-codex-title mismatch); `src/70-manpower.js` CS replacement-ratio FALLBACK string reframed from fact to a designed model parameter; `data/codex.json` id `confederate-greenbacks-inflation` → `greenbacks-and-wartime-inflation` with all 4 refs (l.993/1003/1066/1185) renamed in lockstep (display term was already correct); `HISTORICAL-DATA.md` Mississippi Declaration date "Jan. 26, 1861" → "Jan. 9, 1861" in both the prose bullet and the fact-table row, per the file's own adversarial ledger.
- **Optional corner-stone spelling:** `data/codex.json` Stephens quote "its cornerstone rests" → "its corner-stone rests" (hyphenated, matching Teaching American History + American Battlefield Trust); commas around "subordination to the superior race" KEPT (the audit rejected the "drop the commas" premise — both reputable transcriptions retain them).
- **Load-bearing verbatim discipline:** the new/changed quotations were confirmed character-for-character against ≥2 independent reputable transcriptions before shipping (Watkins clause = the audit's designated source-of-truth; Gooding = Freedmen and Southern Society Project + the LOC/Lincoln-Papers transcription reproduced by TIME; Cornerstone spelling = TAH + ABT). No paraphrase-into-quotes, no memory reconstruction, no padded real lines; each ≤~60 words with a repository+locator.
- **Concurrency note:** M1 ran while a separate Codex session concurrently built + committed/pushed **D179** (flagship named units, `52fb716`) in the same working tree. Surfaced to Aaron; confirmed Codex done. D179's commit did not touch M1's files; M1 rebased cleanly on `52fb716`. The prior defensive `git stash` of the flagship WIP is now redundant (superseded by `52fb716`) and pending drop.
- **Gate (focused, per D176):** JSON parse passed for decisions/economy/vicksburg/codex; `node --check` passed for `src/70-manpower.js`; `node tools/build.mjs` printed **GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓** and rebuilt `civil_war_generals.html` from source. Focused probes passed: `probe-decisions` **19 steps**, `probe-economy` **8 steps**, `probe-codex` **18 steps** (greenbacks id cross-links resolve). Adjacent: `probe-manpower` **9 steps**, `probe-vicksburg` **18 steps**. All **0 pageerrors**. `git diff --check` clean. Full `npm run vet:noreg` deferred to the end-of-queue batch/release gate under D176.

## D179 — PHASE F/G · FLAGSHIP NAMED UNITS WAR EFFORT SYSTEM — [CODEX implementation + source audit] (2026-06-30)

D179 ships the next Group 4 non-battle content system: flagship named units as a War Effort teaching/system surface. It adds no playable battle, no tactical scenario, no OOB rewrite, no per-battle combat tuning, no battle-result gate, no `_SAVE_VER` bump, and no Soldier's Story replacement or women-lane collapse.

- **Surface decision:** flagship named units live as named regiments/brigades/batteries with identity, readiness, colors/support, and casualty-memory readouts. The system names the 54th Massachusetts, Iron Brigade, Irish Brigade, Stonewall Brigade, the 20th Maine inside Vincent's Brigade, and Battery A, 4th U.S. Artillery / Cushing, but does not make any of them a hidden super-unit or force outcome path. The 54th Massachusetts is visible now as a flagship unit; Battery Wagner/USCT playable battle work remains in the later Q6 lane.
- **History/source decision:** existing repo source trails were sufficient, so no new web fetch was needed. Verified unit entries cite `data/codex.json`, current battle OOB notes, and the already verified Soldier's Story replacement trail for Chamberlain/Vincent/Cushing. The data preserves D153 women-lane separation (`women-in-war` stays its own presentation lane), and does not upgrade Native-nations claims.
- **Bridge/save decision:** `flagshipUnitsBridgeBonus(C)` is exact zero unless the player explicitly enables unit stewardship. Active stewardship is capped and costly: morale +1 max, supply cost 1, fatigue cost 1, and no direct overall bonus. The bridge records a readout, not a battle-output gate. `C.flagshipUnits` is additive/sanitized with no save-version bump.
- **Implementation decision:** add `data/flagship-units.json`, `src/67-flagship-units.js`, manifest registration after under-told perspectives, War Effort/H0 President's Desk rendering and wiring, bridge/lifecycle hooks, focused `tools/probe-flagship-units.mjs`, and future-batch suite enrollment. `civil_war_generals.html` is rebuilt only by `node tools/build.mjs`.
- **Harness/bug decision:** focused probing caught a real recursive readout bug: `flagshipUnitsSnapshot()` must not call `moraleCompute()` because morale computes through `bridgeArmy()`, which reads the flagship bridge and re-enters the snapshot. D179 now reads stored `C.morale.troop` only. The focused probe also gets an explicit success exit so a green focused result cannot hang the later suite.
- **Gate (focused, per D176):** JSON smoke passed for `data/flagship-units.json`; `node --check` passed for touched source/probe/suite JS; `node tools/build.mjs` printed **GATE OK** and rebuilt generated HTML from source. Focused `probe-flagship-units` passed **8 steps, 0 pageerrors** with clean JSON readback and screenshot. Adjacent probes passed: `probe-under-told-perspectives` **8 steps, 0 pageerrors**, `probe-bridge` **6 steps, 0 pageerrors**, and `probe-h0-president-desk` **3/3, 0 pageerrors**. JSON readback found no `ok=false`, no `FATAL`, and no pageerrors in the focused/adjacent artifacts. `git diff --check` clean. Full `npm run vet:noreg` remains deferred under D176.

## D178 — PHASE F/G · UNDER-TOLD PERSPECTIVES WAR EFFORT SYSTEM — [CODEX implementation, CLAUDE focused-gate + docs] (2026-06-30)

D178 ships the next Group 4 non-battle content system: the remaining D34.1 under-told perspective threads (enslaved agency, immigrant/ethnic units, Native nations) as a contained War Effort teaching/system surface, cross-referencing the separate D153 women lane instead of collapsing it. It adds no playable battle, no tactical scenario, no per-battle combat tuning, no battle-result gate, no `_SAVE_VER` bump, and no Soldier's Story replacement.
- **Scope:** new `data/under-told-perspectives.json` (`cw_under_told_perspectives_v1`) plus `src/66-under-told-perspectives.js` track a coverage index, omission pressure, enslaved-agency/immigrant/Native visibility, and a liaison-contact ledger. Threads are provenance-stamped: enslaved agency, immigrant/ethnic units, and the D153 women cross-reference are **Verified**; Native nations is deliberately **Inferred/narrow** with a `sourceNote` deferring a full Trans-Mississippi/Indian Territory pass (D40/D54). Sources consolidate existing manpower-teaching, codex, decisions, chancellorsville (XI Corps), and women-in-war trails; no fabricated citations, units, or portraits. Framing is agency-not-tokenization and anti-Lost-Cause (names coerced labor and divided loyalties without exoneration).
- **Bridge contract:** default inactive under-told bridge input returns exact zero and does not move `bridgeArmy` facets. The explicit **perspective-liaison** priority is capped at morale +1, supply cost 1, fatigue cost 1, and no direct overall bonus (`bridgeCaps.overall = 0`). It reduces omission pressure and records coverage/liaison contacts but costs morale-tempo/supply/capital and cannot write battle outputs or launder omission away. Honors D74: outcomes stay emergent; this is a capped input, never an identity buff or output gate.
- **Wiring:** manifest registration follows irregular war (`66-` after `65-`); the War Effort overview and H0 President's Desk shell render `presUnderToldBlock`; `_wdRefresh` wires `underToldWireOverview`; `bridgeArmy` consumes only the capped explicit bonus; `_t1InitAll`/`_t1Resolve` sanitize/update the new `C.underTold` bag; `tools/vet-no-regression.mjs` includes the new focused probe for the future end-of-queue batch.
- **Gate (focused, per D176):** JSON parse passed for `data/under-told-perspectives.json`; `node --check` passed for all touched source/probe/suite JS; `node tools/build.mjs` printed **GATE OK** and rebuilt `civil_war_generals.html` idempotently from source (no hand-editing). Focused `probe-under-told-perspectives` passed **8 steps, 0 pageerrors** with clean JSON readback. Adjacent probes passed: `probe-irregular-war` **8 steps, 0 pageerrors**, `probe-women-in-war` **8 steps, 0 pageerrors**, `probe-bridge` **6 steps, 0 pageerrors**, and `probe-h0-president-desk` **3/3, 0 pageerrors**. The local 8 GB Mac screenshot/browser-close grace was extended for these probes without weakening assertions; a raw-text `ss:` false positive became a structural JSON scan for actual replacement namespace leaks. `git diff --check` clean. Full `npm run vet:noreg` remains deferred under D176. (This slice was authored in a prior session and left uncommitted; this entry closes the focused vetting + doc trail and commits/pushes it as the D178 backup.)

## D177 — PHASE F/G · IRREGULAR WAR AND CIVILIAN SECURITY WAR EFFORT SYSTEM — [CODEX implementation + source audit] (2026-06-30)

D177 ships the next Group 4 non-battle content system: irregular war and civilian security as a contained War Effort teaching/system surface. It adds no playable battle, no tactical scenario, no per-battle combat tuning, no battle-result gate, no `_SAVE_VER` bump, and no battle roster changes.
- **Scope:** new `data/irregular-war.json` (`cw_irregular_war_v1`) plus `src/65-irregular-war.js` track partisan pressure, reprisals, civilian danger, protected civilians, and local intelligence. The source trail names Mosby's commissioned partisan rangers, Quantrill/Lawrence, General Order No. 11, the Lieber Code, and the rejected 1865 guerrilla-last-stand idea without turning irregular violence into Lost Cause romance.
- **Bridge contract:** default inactive irregular-war bridge input returns exact zero and does not move `bridgeArmy` facets. The explicit civilian-security priority is capped at morale +1, supply cost 2, fatigue cost 2, and no direct overall bonus. It can mitigate incidents/reprisals/civilian harm and record protected civilians, but it costs tempo/supply/capital and cannot erase the ledger.
- **Wiring:** manifest registration follows hard war; the War Effort overview and H0 President's Desk shell render `presIrregularWarBlock`; `_wdRefresh` wires `irregularWarWireOverview`; `bridgeArmy` consumes only the capped explicit bonus; `_t1InitAll` and `_t1Resolve` sanitize/update the new `C.irregularWar` bag; `tools/vet-no-regression.mjs` includes the new focused probe for the future end-of-queue batch.
- **Gate:** JSON smoke passed for `data/irregular-war.json`; `node --check` passed for touched source/probe/suite JS; `node tools/build.mjs` printed **GATE OK** and rebuilt generated HTML from source. Focused `probe-irregular-war` passed **8 steps, 0 pageerrors** with clean JSON readback. Adjacent probes passed: `probe-hard-war` **8 steps, 0 pageerrors**, `probe-bridge` **6 steps, 0 pageerrors**, and `probe-h0-president-desk` **3/3, 0 pageerrors**. Full `npm run vet:noreg` remains deferred under D176.

## D176 — OPERATING POLICY · QUEUE-LOOP GATES DEFER THE LONG AUDIT — [AARON clarification + CODEX docs] (2026-06-30)

Aaron clarified during the D175 queue loop that the repo must not run the long `npm run vet:noreg` audit after every queued item. The intended loop is to build the outstanding queue with focused per-item gates, then run the long audit once at the end of the queue/batch so failures can be fixed in one release pass.

- **Supersedes the ambiguous D160 shared-surface trigger during explicit queue-loop work.** Even if an item touches manifest, bridge, render, lifecycle, or suite enrollment, the per-item loop gate is: `node tools/build.mjs` to **GATE OK**, relevant importer/schema checks, `node --check` on touched JS/probe files, the focused probe, 1-3 adjacent probes based on touched surfaces, JSON/stdout/pageerror readback, and `git diff --check`.
- **Long audit timing:** reserve full `npm run vet:noreg` for the end-of-queue planned-work batch/release checkpoint, or for an explicit Aaron request. Do not treat it as a per-slice tax.
- **No weakening:** D176 changes only when the full battery runs. It does not weaken probes, browser guards, source-only editing, no-fudge/no-output-gate contracts, citation rules, accessibility expectations, or the ban on pushing red focused gates.
- **D175 note:** a full `vet:noreg` was started because older D160/HANDOFF/V1 wording still said shared manifest/bridge/render/suite edits triggered the full suite. Aaron stopped it; the run had passed through render-richness with no red conditions before interruption, but D175 ships on focused/adjacent gates under this clarified rule.

## D175 — PHASE F/G · HARD WAR AND CIVILIAN PROTECTION WAR EFFORT SYSTEM — [CODEX implementation + source audit] (2026-06-30)

Only the explicit **Protect civilians and freedpeople** priority can affect bridge facets, capped at morale +1, supply cost 2, fatigue cost 1, and no direct overall bonus. Resolve tracking records property destruction, displacement, and freedpeople/refugee protection pressure without erasing harm or laundering Confederate cause/framing.
