## D174 — PHASE H0 · BATCH/RELEASE GATE AND SUITE COVERAGE — [CODEX verification + harness hardening] (2026-06-30)

D174 closes the D171 Group 3 H0 batch/release gate. The completed H0 surface set now runs inside the no-regression battery, and the release checkpoint passed under long-grace local-Mac browser settings. This is a verification/harness milestone only: no game source, battle data, combat model, save schema, H0 shell contract, or generated output path was changed.

- **Scope decision:** add the six completed H0 browser probes to `tools/vet-no-regression.mjs` immediately after accessibility (`h0 main menu`, `h0 president desk`, `h0 battle briefing`, `h0 between battle`, `h0 tactical hud`, `h0 after action`). This turns the completed-H0 checkpoint from separate focused probes into durable batch-suite coverage.
- **Harness hardening:** `tools/vet-no-regression.mjs` now passes `PW_TEST_SCREENSHOT_NO_FONTS_READY=1` by default to child browser probes, matching Playwright's supported no-font-wait path for slow local screenshot captures. `tools/probe-h0-president-desk.mjs` uses longer screenshot/viewport grace. `tools/probe-production.mjs` writes its JSON artifact before bounded page/browser cleanup so a green production probe exits instead of hanging after successful assertions. Assertions were not weakened.
- **Release-gate evidence:** `node tools/build.mjs` printed **GATE OK**. Import/schema checks passed for soldier replacements (`records=6 verified=6 disputed=0`), women in war (`records=7 verified=6 disputed=1`), and Tripo asset slots (`records=8 enabled=0 detailedSlots=8 filesPresent=0 pendingLicense=8`). `node --check` passed for touched JS/probe files. Focused H0 probes passed: main menu **4/4**, President's Desk **3/3**, battle briefing **3/3**, between-battle **3/3**, tactical HUD **3/3**, and after-action **3/3**, all with 0 pageerrors. Adjacent probes passed: `probe-desk`, `probe-accessibility`, `bootprobe`, and `diag-classic`.
- **Full battery:** D160/D161 required `npm run vet:noreg` because this was the H0 batch/release checkpoint and the suite harness changed. The first full run passed build/import/boot/t1/menu/desk/bridge/auto-resolve/campaign/economy, then `probe-production` wrote `ok=true` / 0 pageerrors but timed out during cleanup. After the cleanup fix, standalone `probe-production` passed, and the resumed `npm run vet:noreg -- --from=production` completed the tail with **VET NO-REGRESSION OK - 84 commands**.
- **Artifact inspection:** a scan of 95 JSON artifacts found `bad=0`: no `ok=false`, no `FATAL`, no pageerrors, and no realErrors. Filtered console-error lists were limited to the already-known asset-layer 404 noise (`bootprobe` 8 filtered 404 lines; `diag-classic` 2 filtered 404 lines). `git diff --check` passed.
- **Queue effect:** Group 3 is complete. The next live queue is Group 4: remaining Phase F/G non-battle content systems. Q5 Chattanooga, Atlanta/March, Franklin/Nashville, and USCT playable battles remain locked last unless Aaron explicitly reorders.

## D173 — RATING SYSTEM / GM FOLLOW-UP · SYMMETRIC AI-GM SHADOW READOUT — [CODEX implementation, focused-gated] (2026-06-30)

D173 ships the first bounded Group 2 GM leftover: a deterministic opponent command shadow. The opponent now evaluates its current same-side starting roster for the next battle's attack/defend role, chooses an enemy army commander, seats a small AI-GM corps/division staff shadow by difficulty tier, and exposes bounded enemy leadership/margin helper values plus Command/OOB readouts. This is input/readout substrate only: it writes no save state, commissions no hidden officers, invents no theater fields, and never writes battle winners, casualties, OOB totals, or scoreboard outputs.

- **Scope decision:** implement the AI-GM shadow in `src/35-command.js` (`cmdEnemyShadow`, `cmdEnemyLeadership`, `cmdEnemyMarginEdge`, `cmdEnemyCorpsCommanderFor`, and the Command-tab readout), add an `aiGm` config in `data/ratings.json`, and let `src/tactical/T15-oob.js` use the shadow for enemy army/corps commander labels on the existing OOB board. The predicted force edge remains the same force-power readout and is not double-counted with command leadership.
- **Leftover triage:** cross-theater Transfer remains blocked because `data/generals.json` still has no honest `theater` fields. The political-general/election relief-bind is not open: D113 already shipped the Union-only election-support surcharge and readout around commissioned political generals. D173 therefore does not add another political-general system.
- **No-fudge guard:** D173 deliberately does not wire the enemy shadow into `87-auto-resolve.js` or tactical outcome code. Later work may consume the bounded helper values if it can preserve D74/D92, but this slice only creates the symmetric roster/readout substrate and probe locks.
- **Probe/gate:** JSON parse passed for `data/ratings.json` and `data/generals.json`; `node --check` passed for `src/35-command.js`, `src/tactical/T15-oob.js`, `tools/probe-command.mjs`, and `tools/probe-oob.mjs`; `node tools/build.mjs` printed **GATE OK** with `no-fudge ✓` and rebuilt `civil_war_generals.html` from source. Focused probes passed: `probe-command` **79 steps, 0 pageerrors** and `probe-oob` **17 steps, 0 pageerrors**; the first `probe-oob` run timed out during cold page load before evaluation on the 8 GB Mac, then the warm rerun passed cleanly. Adjacent probes passed: `probe-ratings` **21 steps, 0 pageerrors**, `probe-bridge` **6 steps, 0 pageerrors**, and `probe-desk` **13 steps, 0 pageerrors**. JSON artifact inspection found `ok=true`, `failed=0`, `fatal=0`, and `pageerrors=0` for all five probes. `git diff --check` passed. Full `npm run vet:noreg` remains deferred to the planned H0 batch/release gate under D160/D161/D171.

## D172 — PHASE I · SIXTH CITATION-GRADE SOLDIER REPLACEMENT SLICE — STRONG VINCENT — [CODEX implementation + source audit] (2026-06-30)

D172 adds the sixth narrow sourced named-person replacement to Soldier's Story. Strong Vincent replaces the generated Gettysburg brigade-command slot `ss:gettysburg:US:us_vincent_bde:cmd` with a `Verified` sourced record. It changes `data/soldier-replacements.json`, `tools/probe-loot-survival.mjs`, `tools/probe-women-in-war.mjs` cleanup hardening, docs, and the generated `civil_war_generals.html`. It does not change canonical battle data, tactical combat rules, `build/base.html`, save schema version, item balance data, or Women in the War mapping.

- **Source decision:** NPS order-of-battle data places Col. Strong Vincent as commander of the Third Brigade, First Division, V Corps at Gettysburg. NPS Gettysburg material identifies his Little Round Top action, mortal wound on July 2, 1863, and death on July 7, 1863. American Battlefield Trust independently corroborates Vincent's brigade arrival on Little Round Top and frames Chamberlain's 20th Maine within the broader brigade defense. The record asserts no company, no regiment command, no brigadier-rank-at-battle claim, and no portrait.
- **Replacement behavior:** the canonical replacement pack now has six records, all `Verified`. The overlay preserves the 603-person registry, moves raw counts from 22 authored / 581 generated to 28 authored / 575 generated, keeps old-id alias lookup through `ssFindPerson`, and marks Vincent `Sourced` / `Verified`. Persona ratings remain neutral and explicitly Inferred game calibration rather than source claims.
- **UI/schema:** Soldier detail cards render Vincent's bio, source note, source disclosure, and Army of the Potomac / V Corps / First Division / Vincent's Brigade / Little Round Top / 20th Maine context. The no-portrait path remains intentionally tested so absence of an image is honest omission, not a broken asset.
- **Probe/gate hardening:** `tools/probe-loot-survival.mjs` now verifies all six canonical replacement records, Vincent's old-id alias lookup, Col. rank / V Corps / First Division / brigade honesty, no-regiment/no-company/no-portrait/no-brigadier-rank-at-battle caveats, hostile-pack rejection excluding canonical targets, and UI rendering for the Vincent card/detail. `tools/probe-loot-survival.mjs` and `tools/probe-women-in-war.mjs` also use 90s screenshot timeouts and bounded browser-close cleanup after local Chrome hit cleanup/capture false-reds on this 8 GB Mac; assertions were not weakened.
- **Vetting:** `node tools/import-soldier-replacements.mjs --check data/soldier-replacements.json` passed with `records=6 verified=6 disputed=0`; `node tools/import-women-in-war.mjs --check data/women-in-war.json` passed with `records=7 verified=6 disputed=1`. `node tools/build.mjs` printed **GATE OK** with `citations ✓` and `women-in-war ✓` and rebuilt `civil_war_generals.html` from source. `node --check` passed for touched probes. Focused `probe-loot-survival` passed **11/11, 0 pageerrors** with JSON readback clean. Adjacent probes passed: `probe-women-in-war` **8/8, 0 pageerrors**, static scan `0 leaks / 14 files`; `probe-photo-embed` **24/24 OK**; `probe-save-slots` **9/9, 0 pageerrors**. Full `npm run vet:noreg` was not run under the D160/D161 focused-slice batching rule. Q5 Chattanooga and Q6 USCT playable battles remain LAST.

## D171 — OPERATING POLICY · FRESH-CHAT EXECUTION GROUPS AND AUTO-CONDENSE BOUNDARY — [AARON directive + CODEX docs] (2026-06-30)

D171 supersedes the older "loop across the whole arc until context pressure" habit for current Codex usage. Auto-condense is treated as a safety net for finishing one already-bounded milestone, not the default project-memory mechanism across groups.

- **Boundary decision:** after every clean committed+pushed milestone, and before starting a new execution group/phase or browser-heavy batch/release gate, the agent should stop and return a paste-ready next-chat prompt unless Aaron explicitly orders same-chat continuation. If context is near ~70-80% consumed or the run starts depending on stale summarized context, finish the current gate, update handoff docs, and stop clean.
- **Grouping decision:** outstanding planned work is grouped in `V1-CHECKLIST.md` §"Fresh-Chat Execution Groups": Phase I Soldier's Story scale-out first; GM follow-up leftovers; H0 batch/release gate; remaining Phase F/G non-battle systems; Phase H polish/media/perf; meta/deferred tooling; and the last battle-build queue. Chattanooga and USCT playable battles remain last unless Aaron explicitly reorders.
- **Prompt decision:** every handoff prompt must include `cd`, `git fetch origin && git status --short --branch && git pull --ff-only origin main`, current HEAD, exact next group/item, read order, queue locks, and the gate sequence. This makes fresh chats cheap and reliable while preserving the repo as the source of truth.
- **Docs decision:** the rule is written into `AGENTS.md`, `START-HERE.md`, `AUTONOMOUS-RUN.md`, `V1-CHECKLIST.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, and `WAKE-UP.md`, then logged here and in `RUN-LOG.md`. The change is process-only: it does not alter game systems, probes, battle balance, saves, assets, or source/data contracts.

## D170 — PHASE H2 · SKIPPABLE FIELD CUTAWAY FALLBACK SHELL — [CODEX implementation, focused-gated] (2026-06-30)

D170 continues the non-battle queue after D169 with Phase H polish that does not create a new scenario or fetch new assets. The decision is to build the H2 cutaway UX and provenance-gated data contract now, while keeping all actual moving-image/reenactment footage disabled until public-domain provenance and anachronism review are explicit.

- **Scope decision:** add `data/footage-cutaways.json` (`cw_h2_cutaways_v1`) and `src/104-h2-cutaways.js` as a skippable pre-battle briefing overlay. Existing PD-vetted scene stills are reused for Bull Run, Antietam, Fredericksburg, Chancellorsville, Vicksburg, and Gettysburg; Shiloh, Malvern Hill, and Chickamauga use a local procedural map-art fallback because no vetted embedded scene still exists.
- **Asset/provenance decision:** all `video` slots are `enabled:false`, with `sourcePath:null`, `externalUrl:null`, and `licenseStatus:"pending-pd-provenance"`. D170 performs no external fetch, account/trial/download action, runtime web request, upload, or unclear-license asset import. Verified still rows carry at least two source refs; procedural rows are explicitly Inferred.
- **Runtime/contract decision:** the cutaway is optional and skippable, opens from the existing H0 battle briefing actions, and never launches `brgRealTime`/`brgToField`, mutates `battlePrep`, writes battle winners/casualties/OOB, changes save version, or touches the combat model. It uses bare-name globals only (`h2CutawayRecordForBattle`, `h2ShowCutaway`) and no `window.*` export.
- **Probe/tooling decision:** add `tools/probe-h2-cutaways.mjs` and include it in `tools/vet-no-regression.mjs` for the later batch battery. The focused probe locks disabled video slots, offline fallback policy, no fetch/external URL/video/iframe tokens, no combat/output/save contamination, visible overlay layout metrics, skippable close/return behavior, and helper lookup. A local Chrome screenshot timeout led to DOM layout metrics in the JSON artifact instead of a screenshot side effect; UI assertions were strengthened and kept fatal.
- **Gate:** syntax checks passed for `src/104-h2-cutaways.js`, `tools/probe-h2-cutaways.mjs`, and `tools/vet-no-regression.mjs`. Schema smoke printed `cw_h2_cutaways_v1 9`. `node tools/build.mjs` printed **GATE OK** and rebuilt `civil_war_generals.html` from source. Focused `probe-h2-cutaways` passed **5 steps, 0 pageerrors** with clean JSON readback and artifact metrics (`overlay:true`, `hasStill:true`, `hasVideo:false`). Adjacent probes passed: `probe-scenes-imagery` **16/16 OK**, `probe-bridge` **6 steps, 0 pageerrors**, `probe-h0-battle-briefing` **3/3, 0 pageerrors**, and `probe-accessibility` **25 steps, 0 pageerrors**. Full `npm run vet:noreg` was not run under the D160/D161 focused-slice batching rule; the D170 probe is now part of that future batch.

## D169 — PHASE F · DISEASE / ARMY MEDICINE WAR EFFORT SYSTEM — [CODEX implementation, focused-gated] (2026-06-30)

D169 starts the next non-battle Phase F content-system run after D168. The decision was to build disease/army medicine before the remaining hard-war, irregular-war, finance, diplomacy, named-unit, and human-cost systems because disease and medical capacity touch survival, manpower pressure, War Effort teaching, and human-cost gravity without creating a new battle or changing the combat model.

- **System decision:** add a War Effort disease/medical pressure ledger, not a hospital minigame. The system records sickness, wounds, treated men, disease deaths, and recent medical pressure from resolved battles; it teaches that disease killed more soldiers than battle while keeping medical organization as mitigation, not sanitization.
- **Implementation decision:** add `data/disease-medical.json` (`cw_disease_medical_v1`) and `src/63-disease-medical.js`, registered after POW exchange and before manpower. The War Effort overview renders **Disease & Army Medicine** in the existing overview/deep-block chain, wires a **Prioritize medical relief** toggle, registers `medicalInit(C)` and `medicalOnResolve(...)`, and adds a capped `medicalBridgeBonus(C)` to the existing bridge.
- **History/source decision:** existing repo source trails were sufficient, so no web fetch was needed. The data uses Verified side profiles and teaching beats for disease deaths, the Letterman evacuation system, anesthesia/amputation practice, the Sanitary Commission, Barton/Dix/Walker relief and nursing work, and the limits of nineteenth-century medicine. The framing rejects "clean war" and Lost Cause scarcity romance: capable bedside practice could not overcome missing sanitation, food, quinine, ambulances, hospital stores, or state capacity.
- **Bridge/save decision:** default inactive medical relief returns exact zero and leaves existing army facets unchanged. Only the explicit medical-relief priority can affect the bridge, capped at morale +2, fatigue relief 3, supply cost 2, and overall +1. Active relief can treat more men, reduce disease deaths, and mitigate Campaign Kit disease/fatigue only when that survival layer is already active; it never writes battle winners, casualty outputs, OOB, scenario data, or a scoreboard path. State rides additive `C.medical` with sanitizer/defaults and no `_SAVE_VER` bump.
- **Probe/tooling decision:** add `tools/probe-disease-medical.mjs` and include it in `tools/vet-no-regression.mjs` so the future batch battery covers this D169 gate. `tools/probe-prisoner-exchange.mjs` also gets a screenshot timeout increase to 90s after a local 8 GB Mac false-red in screenshot capture; assertions were unchanged.
- **Gate:** syntax checks passed for touched source/probe files, including `src/63-disease-medical.js`, `src/20-president-render.js`, `src/30-president-shell.js`, `src/85-battle-bridge.js`, `src/90-president-register.js`, `src/99-h0-president-desk.js`, `tools/probe-disease-medical.mjs`, and the timeout-hardened `tools/probe-prisoner-exchange.mjs`. JSON schema smoke passed for `cw_disease_medical_v1`. `node tools/build.mjs` printed **GATE OK** and rebuilt `civil_war_generals.html` from source. Focused `probe-disease-medical` passed **8 steps, 0 pageerrors** with clean JSON readback. Adjacent probes passed: `probe-prisoner-exchange` **8 steps, 0 pageerrors**, `probe-logistics-rail` **8 steps, 0 pageerrors**, `probe-manpower` **9 steps, 0 pageerrors**, `probe-bridge` **6 steps, 0 pageerrors**, `probe-h0-president-desk` **3/3, 0 pageerrors**, and `probe-desk` **13 steps, 0 pageerrors**. Full `npm run vet:noreg` was not run under the D160/D161 focused-slice batching rule; the D169 probe is now part of that future batch.

## D168 — PHASE H0 · AFTER-ACTION / FINAL REPORT COMMAND-SHELL — [CODEX implementation, focused-gated] (2026-06-30)

D168 completes the approved H0 surface order after D167 by modernizing the live After-Action tab and war-end final report shell while preserving the pure read-out report, alternate endings, Soldier's Story, codex/glossary, and campaign-end contracts.

- **Surface decision:** the sixth H0 surface is the after-action/final-report layer. With D163-D168, the H0 command-shell pass now covers main menu, President's Desk overview, battle briefing / side choice, between-battle interstitial, tactical HUD/settings, and after-action/final report. The next gate is the H0 batch/release checkpoint or the next roadmap slice Aaron selects.

It wraps `aarRenderReport(C, opts)` rather than redeclaring `warWonScreen`, leaving the D112/D119 final-screen behavior, `#wwReport`, `#wwMainMenu`, and `G.campaign=null` contract intact. It adds a dark command report shell, top summary chips, grade badge, casualty/history metrics, responsive desktop/tablet/phone layout, visible focus, and high-contrast compatibility around the existing report body.
- **Preservation decision:** the existing `src/82-after-action.js` and `src/83-endings.js` logic remains the source of truth for grade formulas, strategic-end framing, Reconstruction copy, compact alternate-ending embeds, and pure read-out behavior. The D151 `ssJourneyReportHTML` panel remains embedded through `aarRenderReport`, and the D120 inline glossary remains active because the new wrapper does not mark the report body as a glossary skip zone.
- **Probe decision:** add `tools/probe-h0-after-action.mjs` as the focused H0 gate. It checks live After-Action and final report shells across desktop/tablet/phone, screenshots both surfaces, asserts no broadsheet shell font/class, command texture, dark shell, summary metrics/chips, preserved report-card/divergence/endings/Soldier's Story content, glossary decoration and visible focus, final `#wwMainMenu`, final campaign nullification, no horizontal overflow, and pageerrors.

## D162 — PHASE H · MODERN UG:G UNIT-ASSET SLOTS + FORMATION FIGURES — [CODEX implementation, focused-gated] (2026-06-29)

D162 adds a safe graphics/playability slice for the Modern UG:G battlefield: optional local GLB/GLTF unit asset slots plus procedural formation figures. It changes `data/tripo-unit-assets.json`, adds `src/tactical/T23-tripo-unit-assets.js` and `src/tactical/T24-formation-figures.js`, registers both modules in `src/00-manifest.json`, adds `tools/import-tripo-unit-assets.mjs`, `tools/probe-tripo-unit-assets.mjs`, and `tools/probe-formation-figures.mjs`, updates `assets/3d/README.txt`, adds the focused checks to `tools/vet-no-regression.mjs` for the later batch battery, and rebuilds `civil_war_generals.html` from source. It also adds a timeout guard to `tools/probe-render-richness.mjs` so a stuck scene returns a clean failure instead of hanging.

- **External-tool decision:** the runtime remains local-files-only. The game never calls Tripo, never needs a key/account/credit balance, and never uploads or generates assets. Tripo site/browser actions, credit use, uploads, Generate clicks, or prompt entry require explicit Aaron approval. Canonical slots remain disabled until optimized local runtime files and clear license/provenance proof exist.
- **Source-quality decision:** canonical Tripo source slots require detailed/Ultra metadata: `geometryQuality=detailed`, `textureQuality=detailed`, `pbr=true`, `smartLowPoly=true`, and `ultraSourcePath`. Runtime files are optimized low-poly GLB/GLTF under `assets/3d/models/units/`; detailed source meshes are source art only.
- **Presentation decision:** when no enabled local model exists, T23 preserves the marker/fallback path. T24 adds procedural infantry formation figures on rich/high tiers only; `renderRich="off"` and low tier restore the slab/front marker. The layer reads render/unit state and does not write sim fields, call `fldRng`, or bump `_SAVE_VER`.
- **Probe/gate:** syntax checks passed for T23, T24, importer, focused probes, and the render-richness support edit. Importer passed with `records=8 enabled=0 detailedSlots=8 filesPresent=0 pendingLicense=8`. Build printed **GATE OK**. Focused probes passed: `probe-formation-figures` **16/16, 0 pageerrors** and `probe-tripo-unit-assets` **15/15, 0 pageerrors**, with JSON readback clean and screenshots captured. Full no-regression is deferred to the planned-work batch/release gate under the D161 batching addendum.

## D161 — PHASE F · POW EXCHANGE CARTEL COLLAPSE — [CODEX implementation + source audit, focused-gated] (2026-06-29)

D161 adds the second bounded Phase-F War Effort system after rail logistics: prisoner exchange collapse, camp pressure, and relief prioritization. It changes `data/prisoner-exchange.json`, adds `src/62-prisoner-exchange.js`, registers the module in `src/00-manifest.json`, wires the War Effort UI through `src/20-president-render.js` and `src/30-president-shell.js`, adds a bounded bridge read in `src/85-battle-bridge.js`, registers init/resolve in `src/90-president-register.js`, adds `tools/probe-prisoner-exchange.mjs`, includes the probe in `tools/vet-no-regression.mjs`, updates docs, and rebuilds `civil_war_generals.html` from source. It does not reopen the exchange cartel, create a parallel campaign map, start Chattanooga, start USCT playable battles, edit `build/base.html`, bump `_SAVE_VER`, or add per-battle combat tuning.

- **Design decision:** POW exchange lives as a War Effort prisoner/camp pressure readout plus one explicit relief priority toggle, not as a second campaign subsystem. The module records detained, returned, and died-in-captivity estimates from resolved battle casualties and current year; the cartel stage moves from open to strained to collapsed through the 1863 USCT fault line and later camp-overcrowding pressure.
- **History decision:** existing repo source trails were enough; no web fetch was needed. The data ties the Dix-Hill cartel, Confederate threats and unequal treatment of captured Black soldiers, Lincoln's retaliation order, Andersonville, Elmira, and cartel collapse into one sourced teaching surface. The framing is not false equivalence: both sides' prisoner suffering is named, but the cartel-break cause is tied to slavery, Black soldier status, and Confederate policy rather than a neutral administrative mishap.
- **Bridge/save decision:** `prisonerExchangeBridgeBonus(C)` is exact zero unless the player explicitly enables relief priority. Active effects are capped (`morale <= 2`, `fatigue relief <= 2`, `supply cost <= 2`, `overall <= 1`) and cannot reopen the cartel; relief only mitigates deaths/returns and adds a small transparent returned-prisoner manpower boost when active. `C.prisoners` is additive and sanitized; no save-version bump.
- **Probe/gate:** the focused probe verifies data schema/provenance, default no-op, bounded active effects, resolve accounting, relief mitigation without cartel reopening, save-load sanitation, UI rendering/toggle behavior, and no tactical/classic contamination. Gate run: `node --check` on touched JS/probe files; soldier/women importer/schema checks; `node tools/build.mjs` GATE OK; focused `probe-prisoner-exchange` 8/8, 0 pageerrors; adjacent `probe-logistics-rail` 8/8, `probe-manpower` 9/9, `probe-bridge` 6/6, and `probe-desk` 13/13, all 0 pageerrors with JSON readback. A full no-regression run was started and passed every completed command through `probe-divergence` with 0 pageerrors, then Aaron stopped it and directed full-suite batching until planned work is built out.
- **Close-out rule:** D161 is resolved under the focused gate. D162 is not assigned here; it is reserved only for a Modern UG:G Tripo/formation slice after that slice survives its focused gate under the new batching rule. Tripo site/browser actions, credit use, uploads, Generate clicks, or prompt entry require explicit Aaron approval; runtime Tripo integration remains local-files-only and requires Ultra/detailed source quality.

## D160 — OPERATING POLICY · TIERED VETTING GATES, NOT FULL SUITE AFTER EVERY NARROW SLICE — [AARON directive + CODEX docs] (2026-06-29)

Aaron directed this policy correction after the D154-D158 sourced-person replacement run made the full 80+ probe suite feel universal even for narrow data/content slices. D160 supersedes the D30/D61/D145 wording only on **gate sizing**. It does not weaken the hard invariants, source/doc discipline, or red-gate rule.

- **Always-required base gate:** start from live disk and `git status`; follow the source/doc read order; edit source/data, never `build/base.html` and never hand-edit `civil_war_generals.html`; run `node tools/build.mjs` to **GATE OK**; run the relevant importer/schema gate; run the focused probe(s); read JSON/output for `ok=false`, pageerrors, warnings, or fatal errors; run `git diff --check`; then commit and push only if green.
- **JS/runtime tier:** any JS/runtime change also needs `node --check` on touched JS/probe files and 1-3 directly adjacent probes. If the narrow slice touches shared seams, it moves to the higher tier.
- **Full-suite trigger:** run full `npm run vet:noreg` when touching shared lifecycle/manifest/build/save/bridge/tactical/render/accessibility surfaces, adding or materially changing a probe in the suite, or before a large milestone/release-batch push.
- **2026-06-29 Aaron batching addendum:** after D161's focused/adjacent gates were green, Aaron stopped the full battery and directed that the big suite stay disabled as a per-step requirement until the planned work is built out. The probes remain in the suite and must not be weakened; focused green slices may commit with honest documentation, and `npm run vet:noreg` becomes the batch/release gate or an explicit-request gate.
- **Repeated content-slice rule:** for repeated sourced-person/data slices like D156-D158, focused green + importer/schema green is enough unless UI changed, in which case add one smoke/Classic check. Run the full suite every 3-5 such slices or before the release batch.
- **Hard invariants preserved:** no generated/base hand edits, no per-battle combat fudge, no scoreboard/output-gate hacks, no save-compatibility break, no citation laundering or Lost Cause framing, no fabricated ranks/units/sources, no weakening probes to pass red gates, and no pushing red. `npm run vet:noreg` remains the full-suite command; D160 decides when it is required.

## D159 — PHASE F · STRATEGIC LOGISTICS / RAIL NETWORK — [CODEX implementation + source audit] (2026-06-29)

D159 adds the first bounded Phase-F logistics/rail network milestone. It changes `data/logistics-rail.json`, adds `src/61-logistics-rail.js`, registers the module in `src/00-manifest.json`, wires the War Effort UI through `src/20-president-render.js` and `src/30-president-shell.js`, adds a bounded bridge read in `src/85-battle-bridge.js`, registers init/resolve in `src/90-president-register.js`, adds `tools/probe-logistics-rail.mjs`, includes the probe in `tools/vet-no-regression.mjs`, updates docs, and rebuilds `civil_war_generals.html` from source. It does not create a parallel campaign map, start Chattanooga, start USCT playable battles, edit `build/base.html`, bump `_SAVE_VER`, or add per-battle combat tuning.

- **Design decision:** logistics lives as a War Effort rail/supply readout plus one explicit railhead priority toggle, not as a new map or campaign subsystem. The module reads existing seams: production rail/food, blockade import factor, engineering construction/pontoons, War Room supply/nodes, and next-battle route/theater notes.
- **History decision:** existing repo sources were enough; no web fetch was needed. The data models the U.S. advantage in track mileage, equipment, repair capacity, and military railroad practice against Confederate company fragmentation, gauge breaks, iron/equipment bottlenecks, and service decay. The framing rejects Lost Cause romantic scarcity and instead teaches political economy, slavery-capital conversion limits, and material logistics.
- **Bridge/save decision:** `logisticsBridgeBonus(C)` is exact zero unless the player explicitly enables railhead priority. Active effects are capped (`supply <= 7`, `fatigue relief <= 5`, `overall <= 2`) and shown in the bridge as rail logistics inputs. `C.logistics` is additive and sanitized; no save-version bump.
- **Probe/gate:** the focused probe verifies data schema/provenance, default no-op, bounded active effects, production/engineering/blockade/War Room input movement, resolve/save-load safety, UI rendering/toggle behavior, and no tactical/classic contamination. Adjacent probes covered production, engineering, bridge, blockade, manpower, desk, and Classic. ## D158 — PHASE I · FIFTH CITATION-GRADE SOLDIER REPLACEMENT SLICE — ALONZO H. CUSHING — [CODEX implementation + source audit] (2026-06-29)

Cushing replaces the generated Gettysburg Battery A command slot `ss:gettysburg:US:us_cushing_battery:cmd` with a `Verified` sourced record. ## D154 — PHASE I · FIRST CITATION-GRADE SOLDIER REPLACEMENT SLICE — ELISHA HUNT RHODES — [CODEX implementation + source audit] (2026-06-28)

D154 adds the first narrow sourced named-person replacement to Soldier's Story. The accepted RIHS source is `Mss1089`, which verifies Rhodes enlisted as a private in the 2nd Rhode Island Infantry and served at First Bull Run. Rhode Island Heritage verifies Company D, 2nd Rhode Island, private, and Bull Run. NPS places the 2nd Rhode Island in Burnside's Brigade, Hunter's Division, McDowell's army at Bull Run. Soldier detail cards can render a portrait, bio, source note, and source disclosure for sourced replacements. Journey snapshots preserve replacement/bio/source/portrait fields so save/load does not strip the sourced context.
- **Probe/gate hardening:** `tools/probe-loot-survival.mjs` no longer assumes the canonical replacement pack is empty; it compares against a forced empty-pack raw registry, verifies the Rhodes overlay, and still rejects hostile packs. `tools/probe-women-in-war.mjs` now checks cross-lane separation without requiring `soldier-replacements.records:0`. `tools/probe-photo-embed.mjs` locks the new 156-portrait embed baseline and the `portraits/elisharhodes` key. `tools/probe-desk.mjs` now uses the same 60s load budget as the other early full-page probes after reproducing a cold-load false-red at 45s.

D153 completes the Phase-F "Women in the war — soldier & relief threads" queue item as a citation-grade, presentation-only Campaign Kit lane beside Soldier's Story and the Army Register. It integrates the locked `.tmp/D153-CLAUDE-RESEARCH-OUTPUT.md` dossier without rewriting the player copy. It changes `data/women-in-war.json`, adds `src/38-women-in-war.js`, `tools/import-women-in-war.mjs`, and `tools/probe-women-in-war.mjs`, wires the section after `src/37-loot-survival.js`, registers the module in `src/00-manifest.json`, extends `tools/build.mjs` and `tools/vet-no-regression.mjs`, updates docs, and rebuilds `civil_war_generals.html` from source. It does not add soldier replacement records, change canonical battle data, alter tactical combat rules, edit `build/base.html`, bump saves, or change item balance.

- **Canonical women pack:** `data/women-in-war.json` declares schema `cw_women_in_war_v1` with 7 records: Sarah Rosetta Wakeman, Albert D. J. Cashier, Clara Barton, Dr. Mary Edwards Walker, Harriet Tubman, and Dorothea Lynde Dix are `Verified`; Loreta Janeta Velazquez remains the sole `Disputed` record with the dossier's dispute note. Every provenance stamp, source entry, player-copy string, integrity note, and warning flag from the dossier is preserved.
- **Separation law:** this lane is not a Soldier's Story replacement importer. Barton, Walker, Tubman, and Dix reuse existing codex portrait helpers through `codexRef`; Wakeman and the other non-codex records show "No verified portrait used" rather than asserting an unverified image.
- **Validation gates:** `tools/import-women-in-war.mjs` validates schema, forbidden keys, safe ids, no `ss:`/`replacePid`, allowed roles/sides/provenance, at least two independent sources, required disputed notes, resolved non-`ss:` `codexRef` links, warning flags, integrity notes, and 80-150 word player copy. `tools/build.mjs` delegates to the importer before writing the playable HTML, and `tools/vet-no-regression.mjs` runs both the importer and focused browser probe.
- **Audit/probe:** `tools/probe-women-in-war.mjs` asserts canonical data separation, 603-person Soldier's Story registry non-leakage, 7 visible cards, Velazquez `Disputed` status and dispute note, codex portrait reuse for Barton/Walker/Tubman/Dix, no Wakeman portrait claim, filter behavior, empty-data no-op, scoped focus CSS, XSS escaping, screenshot output, 0 pageerrors, and no women-in-war reads from bridge/tactical/after-action files. The direct audit also scanned D74 purity, registry leakage, `ss:`/`replacePid`, no combat/bridge reads, codexRef reuse, contrast, keyboard/focus, and data separation.
- **Vetting:** `node --check` passed for `src/38-women-in-war.js`, `tools/import-women-in-war.mjs`, and `tools/probe-women-in-war.mjs`. `node tools/import-women-in-war.mjs` passed with `records=7 verified=6 disputed=1`; the write-path gate using `.tmp/d153-empty-women-in-war-pack.json --write` preserved 7 women records and `soldier-replacements.records:0`. `node tools/import-soldier-replacements.mjs` still passed with `records=0`. `node tools/build.mjs` printed **GATE OK** with `women-in-war ✓` and rebuilt `civil_war_generals.html` from source. ## D152 — PHASE I · CITATION-GRADE SOLDIER REPLACEMENT TOOLING — [CODEX implementation + direct main-loop audit] (2026-06-28)

The fourth Phase-I continuation milestone builds the import/validation lane for replacing generated Soldier's Story representative rows with sourced named people later. It changes `src/37-loot-survival.js`, `tools/build.mjs`, `tools/probe-loot-survival.mjs`, `tools/vet-no-regression.mjs`, adds `tools/import-soldier-replacements.mjs`, `data/soldier-replacements.json`, `SOLDIER-REPLACEMENT-FORMAT.md`, docs, and the generated `civil_war_generals.html`. It does not add any sourced historical person, change canonical battle data, tactical combat rules, `build/base.html`, save schema version, or item balance data.

- **Canonical empty pack and format:** `data/soldier-replacements.json` now declares schema `cw_soldier_replacements_v1` with an empty `records` array plus a documentation-only template. `SOLDIER-REPLACEMENT-FORMAT.md` documents the stable replacement shape, the CLI workflow, and the rule that the canonical array stays empty until a real sourced candidate pack passes the importer, build gate, focused browser probe, and no-regression gate.
- **Runtime overlay seam:** `ssPersonRegistry` validates the replacement pack against the current generated registry before applying anything. A valid record can replace exactly one current generated `ss:` row, preserve the registry length, move the row from Generated/Inferred to sourced Verified/Disputed, keep `ssFindPerson` alias lookup by the old generated id, and display a `Sourced` source label. Invalid or empty packs leave the current 603-person registry unchanged.
- **Validation gates:** `ssValidateSoldierReplacementPack`, `tools/import-soldier-replacements.mjs`, and build-gate 4f reject malformed schema, prototype/meta keys, unsafe ids, `ss:` ids for new sourced people, replacement targets that are not generated `ss:` rows, duplicate `pid`, duplicate `replacePid`, generated-mislabelled records, `Inferred` replacements, under-cited records, missing `Disputed` notes, bad teams, bad branches/years, and incomplete/out-of-range persona attributes. `tools/vet-no-regression.mjs` now runs the importer as a normal no-regression command.
- **Probe expansion:** `tools/probe-loot-survival.mjs` now has an 11th D152 step. It asserts the canonical pack is empty, the empty pack validates, hostile packs reject, a valid two-source fixture overlays only its generated slot, registry length is preserved, generated/authored counts change by exactly one in the temporary overlay, alias lookup works, the replacement row is sourced/Verified with two sources, and restoring the canonical pack returns the registry to the D151 baseline.
- **Audit:** direct main-loop adversarial audit exercised the CLI with process-substituted packs: valid fixture accepted; under-cited, `Inferred`, generated-mislabelled, duplicate-target, and prototype-key packs rejected. A follow-up scan confirmed the canonical data still has `records:0`; only the documentation template and probe fixture contain placeholder names.
- **Vetting:** `node --check` passed for `src/37-loot-survival.js`, `src/tactical/T14-ratings.js`, `tools/probe-loot-survival.mjs`, `tools/import-soldier-replacements.mjs`, `tools/build.mjs`, and `tools/vet-no-regression.mjs`. `node tools/import-soldier-replacements.mjs` passed with `records=0`. `node tools/build.mjs` printed **GATE OK** and rebuilt `civil_war_generals.html` from source. Focused `probe-loot-survival` passed **11/11, 0 pageerrors** and wrote `tools/shots/probe-loot-survival.png` (279,520 bytes); the D152 step used generated target `ss:gettysburg:US:us_stannard:pvt`. ## D151 — PHASE I · SOLDIER'S STORY JOURNEY PERSISTENCE — [CODEX implementation + direct main-loop audit] (2026-06-28)

The third Phase-I continuation milestone makes a selected Soldier's Story journey persist as an honest career record instead of only a start flag. It changes only `src/37-loot-survival.js`, `src/82-after-action.js`, `tools/probe-loot-survival.mjs`, docs, and the generated `civil_war_generals.html`; no canonical battle data, tactical combat rules, `build/base.html`, save schema version, or item balance data changed.

- **Career log and battle association:** `ssStartJourney` now creates a bounded start entry for the selected person, and each resolved battle appends a sanitized career entry with battle id/name, outcome, turn, status, rank-before/rank-after, casualties, and a plain-language note. The active journey also keeps `startBattleId`, `currentBattleId`, `lastBattleId`, `lastBattleName`, `lastOutcome`, `battles`, and `promotionCount`, plus a bounded `L.people[personId]` summary for save/load and UI readback.
- **Status, survival, and promotion hooks:** journey outcomes can mark the selected person `alive`, `wounded`, or `captured` under deterministic low-risk rules keyed to battle outcome/casualty pressure. Wounded/captured states create bounded survival-meter consequences and a tiny active-only bridge penalty; inactive Campaign Kit saves still produce exact zero. When a generated low-rank person earns a decisive result, the journey uses `fldPromotePerson` to update the saved person snapshot/rank without mutating canonical `GAME_DATA.ratings`.
- **UI/report tie-in:** the Campaign Kit active-journey panel now shows status, last battle, battle count, promotions, and the recent career log. `ssJourneyReportHTML` provides a small display-only Soldier's Story panel that `src/82-after-action.js` includes in after-action/final reports when a journey is active.
- **Save/load safety:** `lootInit` now sanitizes restored journey career arrays, bad statuses, bad keys, person snapshots, dual OVR fields, promotion counts, and tampered people-state summaries into bounded plain data. Generated rows remain `Generated`/`Inferred`; D151 does not replace any generated row with an unsourced named person.
- **Probe expansion:** `tools/probe-loot-survival.mjs` now asserts D151 career-log persistence, battle association, status/survival effects, `fldPromotePerson` promotion through a generated Private-to-Sergeant path, no canonical ratings mutation, active-only bridge effects, after-action/end-report tie-in, save/load round-trip safety, tamper sanitation, and preservation of the D149/D150 locked restart behavior.
- **Vetting:** `node --check src/37-loot-survival.js`, `node --check src/tactical/T14-ratings.js`, `node --check src/82-after-action.js`, and `node --check tools/probe-loot-survival.mjs` passed. `node tools/build.mjs` printed **GATE OK** and rebuilt `civil_war_generals.html` from source. ## D150 — PHASE I · ARMY REGISTER / PERSON-UNIT AXIS — [CODEX implementation + direct main-loop audit] (2026-06-28)

The second Phase-I continuation milestone turns the D148/D149 Soldier's Story selector into a real Army Register over the existing 603-person current registry. It changes only `src/37-loot-survival.js`, `src/tactical/T14-ratings.js`, `tools/probe-loot-survival.mjs`, docs, and the generated `civil_war_generals.html`; no canonical battle data, tactical combat rules, `build/base.html`, save schema version, or item balance data changed.

- **Army Register UI:** the Campaign Kit now renders all 603 current registry people as searchable/filterable cards, with live search plus side, rank, provenance, and unit filters. The register keeps the full legacy person selector for broad selection, but detail-card selection and card-level journey start are now first-class.
- **Person detail cards:** selecting a card renders OVR, grade, attack/defend split, Authored-vs-Generated source, Verified/Inferred provenance, latent command, source count, and the team hierarchy. Generated representative rows remain visibly `Generated` / `Inferred`; authored sourced rows show `Authored` / `Verified`. This is display over current data, not a claim that generated representative rows are named historical people.
- **Team hierarchy:** `fldPersonTeam` now preserves authored `army -> corps -> division -> brigade -> regiment -> company` fields instead of truncating to army/corps. The current generated representative rows use the modeled battle/brigade plus a representative company/battery anchor, so the D93 person-unit axis has a usable hierarchy while D152 remains responsible for future citation-grade replacement tooling.
- **Journey start and restart lock:** Begin Journey works from the detail card or any register card. Once a journey is active, selected/person-card/detail-card starts render disabled as `Journey Active`, preserving D149's no-overwrite rule and leaving any future retire/restart path for D151+.
- **Probe expansion:** `tools/probe-loot-survival.mjs` now has a D150 UI assertion covering exact 603-card/full-selector count, search narrowing, side/rank/provenance/unit filters, detail-card selection, generated-vs-authored/provenance display, team hierarchy, card journey start, survival activation, and locked restart UI. The older save-shape, exploit, bridge, journey, and restored-save checks remain in the same focused gate.

## D149 — PHASE I · LOOT/SURVIVAL HARDENING + GATE EXPANSION — [CODEX implementation + adversarial main-loop audit] (2026-06-28)

The first Phase-I continuation milestone hardens the D148 Campaign Kit instead of expanding its scope. It changes only `src/37-loot-survival.js`, `tools/probe-loot-survival.mjs`, and the generated `civil_war_generals.html`; no canonical battle data, tactical combat rules, `build/base.html`, save schema version, or item balance data changed.

- **State-machine hardening:** `lootInit` now uses safe own-property checks, rejects prototype-pollution/meta keys in `equipped`, sanitizes equipped slots before validation, caps saved `found` notes, normalizes survival turn sentinels, and cleans saved journey snapshots/logs into bounded plain JSON. `lootSurvivalActive` now requires literal boolean `true`, closing the restored-save/string-tamper path where `"true"` could pass the active gate before init normalized it. Stack additions now report the actual accepted quantity, not the requested over-cap amount.
- **Journey restart guard:** `ssStartJourney` refuses to overwrite an active Soldier's Story journey unless an explicit future caller passes `{ force:true }`. The visible Campaign Kit UI mirrors that rule by disabling Begin Journey controls after a journey starts. This prevents restart abuse while keeping the D151 persistence lane free to add an intentional restart/retire control later.
- **Survival tick hardening:** future-tampered `lastTurn` / `forageTurn` values no longer freeze survival updates forever; the tick/forage guards normalize future sentinels back to the current turn window before applying the once-per-turn rule.
- **Probe expansion:** `tools/probe-loot-survival.mjs` grew from the D148 MVP probe to a D149 adversarial gate. It now covers corrupted inventory/equipped/survival/journey save shapes, overflow and stack clamps, unique duplicates, actual accepted stack quantity, inactive and tampered bridge leakage, future-turn tick/forage tampering, save-slot import rejection for hostile settings, restored-save sanitation before use, full 603-person UI selector coverage, restart-blocking UI behavior, and a real Campaign Kit screenshot artifact.
- **Bug-hunt findings fixed:** direct main-loop audit confirmed and fixed the truthy-string active-gate leak, `equipped.hasOwnProperty` shadow/crash risk, raw stacked-item `found` notes, active-journey overwrite, and future-turn survival freeze. Follow-up static checks verified no direct `L.equipped.hasOwnProperty` remains and that the new probe locks the restored-save and screenshot paths.
- **Vetting:** `node --check src/37-loot-survival.js` and `node --check tools/probe-loot-survival.mjs` passed. `node tools/build.mjs` printed **GATE OK** and rebuilt `civil_war_generals.html` from source. Focused `node tools/probe-loot-survival.mjs` passed **9/9, 0 pageerrors** and wrote `tools/shots/probe-loot-survival.png` (279,504 bytes). D149 slice gates passed: `probe-save-slots` **9/9**, `probe-bridge` **6/6**, `probe-full-campaign` **4/4**, and `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`, `m3dActive:false`. Full `npm run vet:noreg` finished **VET NO-REGRESSION OK — 79 commands**; `probe-full-campaign` was **4/4, 0 pageerrors** and Classic remained `nonBlank:346`. `git diff --check` clean. Existing known noise only: the embed soft-budget warning and Classic's two tolerated 404 console lines. Q5 Chattanooga and Q6 USCT playable battles remain LAST. **Next: D150 Army Register / person-unit axis.**

## D148 — PHASE I · LOOT/SURVIVAL MVP + SOLDIER'S STORY PLAY-AS-ANYONE SPINE — [CODEX implementation + adversarial main-loop audit] (2026-06-27)

Phase I now has an additive Campaign Kit instead of a parallel economy or combat model. It ships rarity-tiered loot, a bounded inventory, default-off survival pressure, and the first playable Soldier's Story journey path. It reuses the existing R-5 prosopography/rating substrate and the Armory/Cannon rarity vocabulary; it does not change canonical battle data, tactical combat rules, `build/base.html`, or hand-edit generated HTML.

- **Campaign Kit:** new `data/loot-survival.json` + `src/37-loot-survival.js` + a `Campaign Kit` desk tab. `C.loot` rides existing saves as plain JSON: `inventory`, `equipped`, `survival`, `journey`, and `people`. Battle/campaign outcomes call `lootOnResolve` for deterministic rewards; the player can inspect inventory, use consumables, equip kit/weapon/keepsake items, toggle survival, forage, and begin a Soldier's Story journey.
- **Survival gate:** rations, exposure, disease, fatigue, forage, morale. Survival is **off by default**. `lootSurvivalBridgeBonus(C)` returns exact zero unless survival or a Soldier journey is active; active effects are bounded facet deltas only, never scoreboard writes or per-battle combat tuning.
- **Soldier's Story / prosopography:** `ssPersonRegistry` enumerates the current modeled game roster: authored rating personas, strategic generals and commission-pool figures, plus representative company officer/NCO/private rows for every current tactical brigade token across single-phase, multi-phase, and reinforcement OOBs. The Campaign Kit selector exposes the full registry (603 selectable people in the current build), not just the visible sample cards, so the player can start a journey from any current modeled person. Generated rows remain visibly `Inferred`; future work can replace them with citation-grade named bios/portraits/unit detail without changing the play-as-anyone API.
- **Reuse / no mutation:** person rows materialize through `fldMaterializePerson`, `fldPersonTeam`, and `fldPromotePerson`; D148 does not fork rating math. The adversarial audit fixed canonical-data aliasing so registry/journey creation does not annotate or mutate `GAME_DATA.ratings`.
- **Bug-hunt fixes:** stack-full inventory additions now return `stack-full` instead of false success; survival meter init preserves explicit zero values; the Campaign Kit UI now exposes all current registry people for play-as-anyone selection.
- **Vetting:** `node --check` passed for every new/edited JS file. `node tools/build.mjs` printed **GATE OK** and rebuilt `civil_war_generals.html` from source. Focused `node tools/probe-loot-survival.mjs` passed **8/8, 0 pageerrors**, including save-shape sanitation, bounded inventory, unique/stack safety, default-off bridge identity, active survival tick/forage bounds, deterministic battle rewards, canonical-data nonmutation, 603-person play-as-anyone registry coverage, and full-registry UI journey start. Full no-regression completed green in resumed segments after an Aaron interruption; after the final full-registry UI fix, `npm run vet:noreg -- --from="loot survival"` finished **VET NO-REGRESSION OK — 60 commands**. `probe-full-campaign` was **4/4, 0 pageerrors** and Classic still painted `nonBlank:346`, `m3dActive:false`. Probe JSON readback was clean; `git diff --check` clean. Q5 Chattanooga and Q6 USCT playable battles remain LAST.

## D147 — PHASE J · CUSTOM SCENARIO PACKS AND MOD-FRIENDLY SHARE FORMAT — [CODEX implementation + adversarial main-loop audit] (2026-06-27)

Phase J's broader share/mod pass extends the already-shipped C4 Custom Battle Builder instead of creating a parallel mod system. Custom scenarios remain local/shareable data, not canonical historical battles, and continue to launch only through the explicit `custom_...` path. Hosting remains deferred; GitHub Pages can come later on request.

- **Share format:** added `CUSTOM-SCENARIO-FORMAT.md` as the versioned reference for single-scenario `cw_custom_battle_v1` JSON and multi-scenario `cw_custom_battle_pack_v1` packs. The doc records the deliberate V1 scope: single-phase custom scenarios only, no custom `phases[]`, no per-battle damage tuning keys, and no custom ids in the historical registry.
- **Builder exports/imports:** `src/tactical/T11-custom-battle.js` now exposes a template exporter, scenario-pack exporter, pack importer, and pack installer. The UI adds Template, Export Pack, and Import Pack to Slots actions beside the existing scenario import/export flow. Packs validate every scenario first, install only into empty local slots, refuse duplicates, and write nothing if validation or capacity fails.
- **Hardening decision:** the installer itself rejects duplicate scenario ids inside one batch, not just the higher-level import path. This closes the adversarial finding that a public helper could otherwise be called directly with two same-id scenarios and fill multiple slots with ambiguous local data.
- **Probe coverage:** `tools/probe-custom-battle-builder.mjs` now covers the pack/template helpers, UI buttons, import-to-empty-slots behavior, duplicate rejection in both the import path and direct installer, forbidden combat key rejection, pack lookup through `fldCustomScenarioData`, registry non-leakage, no Classic contamination, and the unchanged historical roster.
- **Vetting:** `node --check src/tactical/T11-custom-battle.js` and `node --check tools/probe-custom-battle-builder.mjs` passed. `node tools/build.mjs` printed **GATE OK** and rebuilt `civil_war_generals.html` from source. Focused `node tools/probe-custom-battle-builder.mjs` passed **10/10, 0 pageerrors**. Full `npm run vet:noreg` passed **78 commands** before the bug-hunt fix; after the direct-installer hardening, `node tools/vet-no-regression.mjs --from="custom battle builder"` passed **12 commands**, including `probe-full-campaign` **4/4, 0 pageerrors** and `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`, `m3dActive:false`. `git diff --check` clean; `build/base.html` and `src/00-manifest.json` untouched. Q5 Chattanooga and Q6 USCT playable battles remain LAST.

## D146 — PHASE J · SAVE / LOAD / SHARE HARDENING — [CODEX implementation + adversarial main-loop audit] (2026-06-27)

Phase J's save/share pass is now a hardening/probe milestone over the existing save system, not a new persistence architecture. It changes `src/91-save-slots.js`, adds `tools/probe-save-slots.mjs`, wires that probe into `tools/vet-no-regression.mjs`, and rebuilds `civil_war_generals.html` from source. It does **not** touch tactical/combat state, battle data, `build/base.html`, or hand-edit the generated deliverable.

- **Save validation and slot behavior:** saves are cloned and validated before import/load. The validator rejects wrong versions, arrays/primitive payloads where objects are required, `settings` objects that shadow `hasOwnProperty`, primitive/array `campaign` payloads, and stale/corrupt named slots. Named slot save/load/rename/delete remains localStorage-based and bounded to the existing save namespace.
- **Import/export hardening:** pasted JSON and file import now route through the same hardened validator. The visible file-import path no longer delegates to the base `importSave`; D146 adds `_slImportFile` and assigns a hardened `importSave` wrapper with an `_slHardened` marker so the base main-menu "Load from File" path is covered too. This closes the bug-hunt finding where file imports could bypass the hardened text path and reach base `applySave`, which calls `sv.settings.hasOwnProperty(k)`.
- **Menu and undo:** the Save Manager menu injection dedupes itself and reinjects after menu rebuilds instead of relying on a brittle one-shot latch. The one-turn undo path is deliberately narrow: it captures before `campaignAdvance` only for the learner-friendly accessible difficulty path, not Ironman/harder campaign modes.
- **Probe coverage:** new `tools/probe-save-slots.mjs` verifies manager API presence, UI labels, menu dedupe/reinjection, named slot save/load/rename/delete, export/import text round-trip, invalid/stale/corrupt/tampered rejection, `settings.hasOwnProperty` shadow rejection, hardened import marker, one-turn undo capture/load, and no tactical/combat state writes or redeclaration hazards. `tools/vet-no-regression.mjs` includes the save-slot probe in the normal suite.
- **Vetting:** `node --check src/91-save-slots.js`, `node --check tools/probe-save-slots.mjs`, and `node --check tools/vet-no-regression.mjs` passed. `node tools/build.mjs` printed **GATE OK**. Focused `node tools/probe-save-slots.mjs` passed **9/9, 0 pageerrors**. Post-fix `node tools/vet-no-regression.mjs --from="save slots"` passed **51 commands**, including `probe-full-campaign` **4/4, 0 pageerrors** and `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`, `m3dActive:false`; probe JSON readback showed `ok=true` and `pageerrors=0`; `git diff --check` clean. Q5 Chattanooga and Q6 USCT playable battles remain LAST.

## D145 — MODEL ROUTING + UI/UX REDESIGN LAW + NORMAL NO-REGRESSION GATE — [AARON directive + CODEX docs/tooling] (2026-06-27)

Aaron explicitly directed this blocker-clearing pass before new gameplay work: update model-routing instructions, consolidate stale roadmap language, add a real frontend redesign milestone because the broadsheet interface is not the desired global shell, research a better game-like UI/UX direction, and harden the full-campaign probe into the normal no-regression gate.

- **Model-routing law:** the main reasoning/integration loop stays on the highest-quality model, specifically **5.5** for complex architecture, important code, user-facing text, historical judgment, UI/UX design decisions, accessibility judgment, bug hunts, final review, and irreversible/quality-critical work. Token-efficient helpers are allowed only for bounded packets where quality cannot be harmed: `rg`/search summaries, file inventories, probe-log summaries, mechanical doc syncs, exact-schema data cleanup, first-pass source gathering, and repetitive fixture/probe scaffolding. Every helper/subagent/workflow call must explicitly set model **and** effort. Smaller models never own architecture, combat balance, historical claims, UX direction, accessibility judgment, or final integration; they produce packets that 5.5 verifies.
- **UI/UX design law:** D145 supersedes "broadsheet everywhere." The app shell should become a more video-game-like historical command interface: brighter, rounder, more dramatic, icon-led, and centered on public-domain photos/illustrations where they clarify people/place/stakes. Broadsheet remains the in-world language for press/editorials/dispatches/provenance, not the whole interface. The redesign must preserve Civil War gravity, anti-Lost-Cause framing, WCAG 2.2 AA, keyboard support, `reduceMotion`, CVD-safe cues, no unclear-license assets, and the Intel UHD-617 performance floor. Research packet saved at `docs/design/ui-redesign-research.md`.
- **Roadmap/gate cleanup:** `V1-CHECKLIST.md` now has a real H0 UI/UX redesign milestone with target screens, visual-system requirements, probes/screenshots, and acceptance criteria. The Phase J saves item is corrected to hardening/probing over existing `src/91-save-slots.js` named slots/export/import, with undo-last-turn still open. D143's full-campaign probe is now part of the normal no-regression command via `tools/vet-no-regression.mjs` and package scripts `vet:noreg`, `vet:noreg:list`, and `vet:full-campaign`.
- **Docs synced:** `START-HERE.md`, `AUTONOMOUS-RUN.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `.clinerules`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, `V1-CHECKLIST.md`, `RUN-LOG.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, and `WAKE-UP.md` were refreshed so the next run starts from the same routing, UI, and gate contract. Q5 Chattanooga and Q6 USCT playable battles remain LAST.
- **Probe policy decision:** do **not** remove probes from the universal scheme. The right correction is smarter harness discipline: focused probe + relevant no-regression/full milestone gate, JSON/pageerror enforcement, process-group timeout cleanup, and root-cause fixes for probe hangs. This audit found useful failures: accessibility's static scan saw comment text, `bootprobe` could hang after writing green JSON, `probe-phased-3d` could hang without a bounded failure, and heavy visual probes needed explicit budgets.
- **Vetting:** `node tools/build.mjs` **GATE OK**; focused accessibility **25/25**, phased-3D **64/64**, render-richness **30/30**, weather **30/30**; all 0 pageerrors. `tools/vet-no-regression.mjs` completed the full normal gate in resumed segments on the 8 GB Mac: build through visual-fidelity, render-richness, then weather through `probe-full-campaign` and `diag-classic`. Final segment ended with `probe-full-campaign` **4/4, 0 pageerrors** and `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`, `m3dActive:false`. `git diff --check` clean.

## D144 — CLOUD/FULL-ACCESS OPERATING RULES + LOCAL-MAC FAST-FORWARD SYNC — [CODEX ops/docs] (2026-06-27)

Aaron explicitly granted dangerous/full-access permissions and directed future work to use Codex Cloud for most implementation/probe-heavy sessions while keeping the local 8 GB Mac copy current after cloud work. This is an operating-policy milestone only: no gameplay, source, data, combat, save, or generated-deliverable behavior changed.

- **Full-access default:** `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `START-HERE.md`, and `AUTONOMOUS-RUN.md` now say to prefer Codex Cloud or another non-Seatbelt/full-access environment for real implementation and browser-probe vetting. Browser probes remain mandatory; the change is where they should run, not a weakening of the gate.
- **Seatbelt handling:** if `CODEX_SANDBOX=seatbelt`, agents must restart/reconfigure into full access instead of bypassing or weakening `tools/guard-probe-browser.mjs`.
- **Local Mac sync:** after a cloud milestone is committed and pushed, local work must not continue until the Mac repo has been fast-forwarded with `git fetch origin && git status --short --branch && git pull --ff-only origin main`; if local dirty edits exist, stop and inspect/stash/commit rather than overwriting them.
- **Permissions JSON:** `.claude/settings.json` now records broad Bash/read/write/edit/web permissions and the relevant additional directories. `package.json` adds `sync:local-after-cloud` as a one-command local fast-forward helper plus `vet:build` for the build gate.
- **Vet:** docs/JSON-only; `node -e` JSON parse for `.claude/settings.json` + `package.json`; `git diff --check`; `node tools/build.mjs` GATE OK.

## D143 — PHASE J · **FULL-CAMPAIGN PLAYTHROUGH PROBE** — the owner-mode campaign now has an end-to-end guard from first battle to final graded report — [CODEX probe-only] (2026-06-26)

This milestone adds the missing Phase-J no-regression guard without changing gameplay. New `tools/probe-full-campaign.mjs` drives the live single-file game in Chrome and verifies the cross-layer contract that individual probes only checked in slices: campaign chain -> bridge auto-resolve -> `campaignAdvance` -> President's interstitial -> final D112/D119 graded war report.

- **Coverage:** the probe runs a dominant Union auto-resolve campaign through the full 31-battle US chain (`sumter` -> `appomattox`) and a dominant Confederate auto-resolve campaign through the full 28-battle CS chain (`sumter` -> `appomattox`). Each resolved battle must win, increment `stats.battles`, advance `idx`, add the battle id to `completed[]`, clear recovery flags, and surface the President's interstitial before the next fight. The final chain victory must null `G.campaign` through `warWonScreen` and render `#wwReport`, `Final Reckoning`, and `#wwMainMenu`.
- **Recovery branch:** a controlled Bull Run loss keeps `idx` fixed, enters recovery, sets `flipAtk` because the player lost while attacking, then a recovery auto-resolve consumes the flipped attacker (`bd.atk === "CS"`), advances to the next battle, and clears recovery flags.
- **Strategic-ending branch:** a mid-campaign `victoryReady="will"` state must surface the between-battles "The war can be concluded" offer and `pdConcludeWar`; accepting it renders the negotiated-peace framing plus the graded final report and ends the campaign.
- **Architecture:** probe-only. No `src/`, `data/`, build, combat, or save logic changed. The probe deliberately uses the existing `bridgeAutoResolve`, `campaignAdvance`, `openUpgrade`, `aarConcludeWar`, and `warWonScreen` seams; it does not invent a parallel campaign runner.
- **Vet:** `node tools/build.mjs` baseline **GATE OK** before work; `node tools/probe-full-campaign.mjs` **4/4, 0 pageerrors** (`tools/shots/probe-full-campaign.json` confirms US 31/31, CS 28/28, recovery, and strategic ending); `node --check tools/probe-full-campaign.mjs` clean; `git diff --check` clean. This completes the V1-CHECKLIST Phase-J "Full-campaign playthrough probe" item.

## D142 — PHASE H · H5-i4 **FORTIFICATIONS DEPTH** — staged works, facing-aware cover, prepared abatis, charge impetus, and terrain sim hooks (the 4th step closing the locked D138 arc) — [CLAUDE impl, D74 gameplay carve-out] (2026-06-26)

The fourth H5 increment closes the UG:G-fidelity modern-engine push. Unlike H5-i2/i3, this is **not** presentation-only: it is a deliberate **D74 gameplay carve-out** because fortifications, terrain friction, and committed charges change player-facing tactics. The discipline is the D74 universal-model law: one shared combat/movement model, no per-battle outcome fudge, no scoreboard writes, and no AI-only hidden exception.

- **FORTIFICATIONS (`src/tactical/T13-engineering.js`):** entrenchment now stages from rifle pits / hasty cover to a full parapet to a redoubt/earthwork while preserving the existing `u.entrench` progress meter and B-5 realism scaling. `fldEngCover(u, fromX, fromZ)` is stage + facing aware: front fire gets full earned cover, flank fire gets reduced cover, rear fire bypasses the works. A unit that leaves its own works no longer receives cover while the visible works fade. The HUD names the stage and front-cover multiplier; 2D/3D draw hasty/parapet/redoubt shapes; the end card teaches the Hess/Mahan spade-to-fieldworks lesson.
- **PREPARED ABATIS:** abatis built by a brigade already inside its own parapet/redoubt is stamped `prepared`/`worksStage`, starts stronger, slows attackers more, inflicts stronger morale/disorder/fatigue shock, clears slower, and renders with a heavier backing + more stakes. A standalone abatis still works as before; prepared bite is tied to real works, not a free obstacle buff.
- **CHARGE IMPETUS (`src/tactical/T20-order-feel.js` + T0 seams):** player-issued charges get a 1.2s correction grace. After commit, casual move/hold/queue/re-aim orders are blocked until contact, target loss, fog loss, own rout, or own death. AI inline charge behavior remains unchanged. The HUD reports correcting/committed/contact status.
- **TERRAIN SIM WIRING (`src/tactical/T0-field-sandbox.js`):** H5-i3's swamp/town/fort arrays now feed universal `fldCoverAt`/`fldMoveFactor` hooks. Fort = strongest cover + attacker ditch/parapet friction; town = hard cover + street/fence friction; swamp = soft concealment + major movement slow. Existing wall/woods/hill behavior is retained. `src/tactical/T22-terrain-readability.js` remains presentation-only; the sim reads live in T0 only.
- **Static guard updates:** probes and comments no longer describe swamp/town/fort as combat-inert in the live model. The D141 record remains historical ("visual-only in that increment"), but D142 is the point where those terrain arrays become active.
- **Adversarial bug-hunt:** direct audit found one confirmed bug: the charge-lock comment promised release on own rout/death, but `fldChargeLocked` did not test the charging unit's own state. Fixed `fldChargeLocked` and `fldChargeHudSelected`; extended `probe-order-feel` to assert own rout/death release and restored lock behavior before contact. Other audited risks held: AI charges unchanged, target loss releases, contact releases, no terrain read leaks outside T0/T22, flank/rear bypass retained, abandoned works give no cover, prepared-abatis scaling is bounded.
- **VET (PROPER VETTING, GREEN after the final fix):** `node tools/build.mjs` **GATE OK · parse ✓ · hex ✓ · collision ✓ · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓**; focused probes `probe-engineering-corps` **23/23**, `probe-terrain-readability` **29/29**, `probe-order-feel` **13/13**, `probe-bullrun` **15/15**, all 0 pageerrors; `probe-presets` **26/26**; `probe-phased-ab` **20 comparisons / 0 diffs**; full no-regression battery rerun after the last charge-lock patch **PASSED** (`bootprobe`, `t1probe`, core tactical probes, visual/3D probes, all current battle probes, and strategy probes); `diag-classic` reports **`nonBlank:346`**; `git diff --check` clean. Known tolerated harness noise: `bootprobe ok=true errors=10` with no real failure bucket and two `diag-classic` 404 console lines while the diagnostic still passes.

## D141 — PHASE H · H5-i3 **TERRAIN READABILITY** — the modern engine now makes high/low ground (and every topography type) EXPLICIT (the 3rd step of the locked D138 arc) — [AARON 3 forks via AskUserQuestion + CLAUDE impl] (2026-06-26)

The third H5 increment: the modern (non-hex) engine's terrain is now EXPLICIT and READABLE — high/low ground at a glance, a player-chosen elevation render, an on-hover readout, and every topography type visually distinct. **Same discipline as i2 (NOT i1's carve-out): PRESENTATION-ONLY → the FULL D74 byte-identity law** (proven via `probe-presets` 26/26 + `probe-phased-ab` 20/0-diff). Hex = Classic, untouched.

- **Aaron's 3 forks (AskUserQuestion, 2026-06-26):** (1) elevation palette = **Viridis** (the reference CVD-safe perceptual ramp); (2) legend = **always-on compact panel** (docked bottom-right, collapsible); (3) topography scope = **add swamp/town/fort too** (not just name the existing types) — Aaron accepted these as VISUAL-now / sim-cover-wiring-later, which is what keeps i3 byte-identical.
- **WHAT SHIPPED:** NEW `src/tactical/T22-terrain-readability.js` — wraps the render seams BY ASSIGNMENT (fld3dInit / fld3dBuildTerrain / fld2dInit / fldPointerMove / fldExit; the T16/T18/T21 pattern, carrying ._wx/._atmo/._rr/._vf markers forward). **(a) Three elevation modes** on a new `G.settings.fldElevMode` ("hillshade" DEFAULT / "contours" / "hypsometric"), cycled from the T6 settings drawer + an `R` hotkey + the legend's mode chip. **HILLSHADE in 3D = T21's existing relief** (T22 adds NOTHING to the 3D ground in hillshade → the relief is never double-counted); the 3D hypsometric tint (a viridis overlay mesh) + contour LineSegments+labels are PREBUILT once and `.visible`-toggled per mode (snappy, no rebuild, no camera jerk). **2D top-down had NO relief at all** → T22 paints its own cached hillshade / hypsometric / contour layers (offscreen-canvas-cached, one blit/frame). **(b) Legend + on-hover readout** — a `#fldElevLegend` panel (mode chip, viridis gradient bar, a live elevation+type readout from `__FIELD.hover`+`fldTerrainH`, and a topography key of the types present this battle). **(c) Swamp / town / fort** — new render types read from `__FIELD.terrain.swamps[]/.towns[]/.forts[]` (each `{x,z,r}`), each visually distinct in 2D + 3D (swamp = wet teal disc + reeds; town = clustered buildings; fort = angular earthwork berm + bastions); a demo set placed in the sandbox showcase.
- **BYTE-IDENTITY (the i2 discipline, PROVEN):** T22 NEVER writes a sim field, NEVER calls `fldRng`, NEVER bumps `_SAVE_VER`. The new swamp/town/fort arrays are **combat-INERT — read by NO sim/combat function** (a static-scan assertion in the probe enforces it: only T0's `fldBuildTerrain` writes them, only T22 renders them) — so combat stays byte-identical despite ADDING terrain. The elevation mode is a new `G.settings` key the sim never reads (additive, the renderRich pattern — kept in `G.settings` only, NOT localStorage, so the documented "hillshade" DEFAULT holds every fresh session while the choice still sticks within a session + rides the campaign save). Proven: `probe-presets` **26/26** + `probe-phased-ab` **20/0-diff**; the sandbox COMBAT terrain (hill/woods/wall) stays byte-identical even with the new decoration — `probe-bullrun`'s golden snapshot was deliberately updated to the new intended terrain AND gained a `GOLD_COMBAT_TERRAIN` guard asserting the combat-relevant keys are unchanged (strengthened, not weakened).
- **VISUAL VETTING:** NEW `tools/probe-terrain-readability.mjs` (**26/26**) — 3 scenes (3D / 2D / low): the 5 wrappers + carried markers; all 3 modes render (hillshade hides both overlays; contours show a >0-vertex LineSegments + label sprites; hypsometric shows a viridis overlay whose LOW vertex reads darker than its HIGH); the toggle REVERTS; the legend + hover readout (hill crest `+yd`, swamp/town/fort named correctly); the decor group built + the key lists all present types; viridis MONOTONIC in luminance (CVD-safe); byte-identity (seed/sim invariant across a render burst WITH mode toggles, `errN===0`); 2D parity (all 3 modes run, `errN===0`); low-tier trims the 3D labels; a clean teardown→relaunch (legend removed + overlays nulled + exactly one legend); focus-restore on the legend mode chip; the legend key stays LIVE on a topography change; 0 pe; screenshot VISUALLY CONFIRMED (hypsometric reads high=yellow crest → low=purple, legend + town + fort + woods all distinct).
- **DESIGN + BUG-HUNT (ultracode):** an AskUserQuestion design-note resolved the 3 forks before building. A **43-agent bug-hunt** (6 diverse lenses — D74-purity · render-math · lifecycle/leak · perf/UHD-617 · a11y/CVD · edge-robust — × 3-skeptic default-refute + a completeness critic) returned **4 confirmed of 12 raw** + 2 critic-new; **ALL FIXED before commit:** **(perf, the keystone)** 2D contour mode re-stroked ~600–1600 segments individually EVERY frame → now rendered ONCE into an offscreen canvas (batched to 2 strokes) + blitted, the shade/hyp cache pattern; **(a11y MED)** the legend mode/collapse buttons dropped keyboard focus to `<body>` on every activation (innerHTML rebuild) → focus restored to the new control in the click handlers; **(MED) the legend topography key + height-range went STALE on a phase advance** (new sector, same-or-different hills) → a topography-aware legend signature now refreshes the key on any terrain change in BOTH renderers; **(LOW)** dropped the localStorage round-trip (default-restoration + global-state concern); **(LOW)** removed a redundant double-dispose of the cloned hypsometric mesh; **(LOW)** the per-pointer hover classify now early-outs on a coarse cell; **(robustness)** the 2D ground hook (called from the base `fld2dDraw`, not a try/catch seam) gained a full-body guard so a render throw can never break the 2D loop. The critic independently confirmed combat-inertness, the fldExit dispose ORDER (T22 removes its meshes BEFORE the base scene-traverse → no double-dispose), and the 3D contour orientation are all correct.
- **VET (PROPER VETTING, GREEN):** `GATE OK`; `probe-terrain-readability` 26/26; `probe-presets` 26/26 + `probe-phased-ab` 20/0-diff (byte-identity) + `probe-bullrun` 15/15 (golden + combat-inert guard); `probe-visual-fidelity` 26/26 (T21 coexists) + `probe-flags` 36/36 + `probe-render-richness` 30/30 + `probe-weather` 30/30 + `probe-atmospherics` 18/18 (3D health); `bootprobe` realErrors:[]; `t1probe` ok; `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346` + `m3dActive:false` (Classic untouched); 0 pageerrors; `wcag-auditor` **AA** (15/15; one gradient-bar border lifted #4a3c28→#725e40 for 1.4.11 3:1; the `aria-live="off"` on the per-move hover readout confirmed correct). **ENV NOTE:** the 8-scene `probe-tactical-visuals` stalled partway on the 8 GB Mac (the documented heaviest-swiftshader memory stall, NOT a regression — its completed scenes incl. a clean 3D one, pe:0); full 3D health is proven by the 5 full-3D probes above. **Committed + pushed.** (Reversible; presentation-only under the full D74 byte-identity law.)
- **NEXT in H5:** i4 FORTIFICATIONS DEPTH (deepen entrench/abatis/redoubt earthworks + the charge-impetus commit-lock deferred from i1; this is also where swamp/town/fort could gain their sim cover/move effects). Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT battles remain LAST (Aaron's locked queue).
