## D140 — PHASE H · H5-i2 **VISUAL FIDELITY** — the modern engine now LOOKS better than Ultimate General: Gettysburg (the polished HYBRID; the 2nd step of the locked D138 arc) — [AARON 3 forks via AskUserQuestion + CLAUDE impl] (2026-06-25)

The second H5 increment: the modern (non-hex) real-time engine's LOOK upgraded to surpass UG:G — richer terrain relief, grounded troops, atmospheric depth — while honoring the period broadsheet palette. **This is the OPPOSITE discipline from i1's gameplay carve-out: it is PRESENTATION-ONLY, held to the FULL D74 byte-identity law** (proven via `probe-presets` 26/26 + `probe-phased-ab` 20/0-diff). The hex engine IS Classic — untouched.

- **Aaron's 3 forks (AskUserQuestion, 2026-06-25):** (1) scope = **Max** — the full additive stack PLUS tier-gated 3D peg-ranks on capable hardware; (2) colour pipeline = **stay LINEAR + additive** (NO global ACES/sRGB flip); (3) ground relief = **Moderate**.
- **THE r128 DECISION (why ADDITIVE, not a tone-map flip):** THREE is r128 (0.128.0), PREDATING the r152 colour-management overhaul — no `outputColorSpace`/`SRGBColorSpace`. A grounded 5-agent recon proved a global `toneMapping=ACESFilmic` + `outputEncoding=sRGB` flip would SPLIT the pipeline (the T16 smoke + T17 precip ShaderMaterials write raw `gl_FragColor` and would NOT tone-map → a visible seam: smoke darkens, rain warms) AND force re-tuning every hardcoded colour across T0/T10/T17/T18 for a palette that already reads period-correct → EXCLUDED. Real shadow maps + MeshStandard/PBR terrain also excluded (r128 InstancedMesh depth-pass bug → woods cast one blob; MeshStandard renders DARKER without an envMap under the linear pipeline; both UHD-617 perf sinks). The win is purely ADDITIVE, palette-preserving.
- **WHAT SHIPPED:** NEW `src/tactical/T21-visual-fidelity.js` — wraps the render seams BY ASSIGNMENT (the T16/T17/T18 pattern, OUTERMOST, carrying every prior `._wx/._atmo/._rr` marker forward) with five layers: (1) **ground RELIEF** — a slope-AO recolour of the terrain VERTEX COLOURS after T18 (height-relief darkens low ground / lifts crests, normalized to the battle's own height range, + a steep-slope exposed-soil tint + a small concavity term; latch `colA._vfAO`; positions NEVER touched → units seat on analytic `fldTerrainH`); (2) soft round CONTACT SHADOWS — a per-brigade MeshBasic plane decal glued to the ground (a soft pool slightly wider than the block so the marker sits IN the field); (3) gradient SKY DOME — a BackSide MeshBasic sphere whose horizon tracks the live weather fog colour each frame (seamless ground-fog→sky) deepening to a richer zenith, + a free CSS VIGNETTE div (below the HUD, `pointer-events:none`, `aria-hidden`); (4) stylized rank MAP — a white-based CanvasTexture multiplied on each slab so a brigade reads as MASSED INFANTRY (CVD-safe luminance pattern); (5) Max-tier PEG RANKS — a per-brigade InstancedMesh of stylized pegs atop the slab (`!fldLow()` only), laid out once + x-scaled per frame (no per-peg matrix churn), with a bounding sphere so r128 frustum-culls it. Master gate = `fldVfOff()` (rides T18's `G.settings.renderRich==='off'` → one toggle reverts the whole stack to the byte-identical default look). Manifest: T21 loads last among the tactical modules.
- **BYTE-IDENTITY (the i2 discipline — the INVERSE of i1's carve-out, PROVEN):** the module reads ONLY read-only render+sim fields, writes vertex COLOUR (never position), never a sim field, never `fldRng`, never `_SAVE_VER`. Per-brigade decorations are CHILDREN of unit groups (the base's rebuild/dispose loop frees them); T21 owns only the shared shadow/rank textures + dome + vignette (freed in `fldVfDispose` via the `fldExit` wrap). Casualty fade is correctly delegated to T18 (its `_rrSetGroupOpacity` traverses all children, fading the transparent pegs+shadow; `fldVfSyncUnit` early-returns on `!u.alive`). Proven: **`probe-presets` 26/26 + `probe-phased-ab` 20/0-diff** (final build).
- **VISUAL VETTING (the presentation analog):** NEW `tools/probe-visual-fidelity.mjs` (**26/26**) — 3 scenes (high/off/low): the 7 wrappers + carried markers; the BackSide dome (renderOrder -1) + its fog-matched horizon + the retained sky-background far-clip fallback; the `pointer-events:none` vignette; `colA._vfAO` + high-reads-brighter-than-low relief + positions untouched; the per-brigade glued shadow (gap ~0.5yd) + rank map + Max-tier peg IM (count 22, bounding sphere); the `fldLow()` peg tier-gate; seed/sim invariance across a synchronous render burst; `FLDVF_S.errN===0`; the `renderRich="off"` full revert; 0 pe / 0 texWarn; + a captured screenshot **VISUALLY CONFIRMED** (grounded units in soft shadow pools, the lighter-crest / darker-low-ground relief, rank+peg detail, the dome sky + vignette — a clear palette-preserving uplift over the byte-identical default).
- **DESIGN + BUG-HUNT (ultracode workflows):** a 5-agent recon+design panel grounded the r128 verdicts + the scope ladder before the AskUserQuestion. A 19-agent bug-hunt (6 diverse lenses — D74 purity · render-math · lifecycle/leak · perf/UHD-617 · a11y/CVD · edge-robust — × 3-skeptic default-refute + a completeness critic) returned **SAFE TO COMMIT, 0 confirmed findings** (high confidence; D74 confirmed STRUCTURALLY — the headless stepper never enters a 3D seam). The 4 raw findings: 2 refuted (mid-battle `renderRich`/`gfxQuality` toggles aren't reachable through the fld3d UI — grep-confirmed no `renderRich` setter; `gfxQuality` drives the SEPARATE legacy `__M3D` engine), 2 non-blocking perf nits → APPLIED anyway as forward-looking polish (the peg IM gained a bounding sphere so r128 frustum-culls it off-camera — removing the one genuine overdraw, scaling to the larger Q5/Q6 OOBs; the redundant per-frame shadow-opacity re-pin dropped).
- **VET (PROPER VETTING, GREEN):** `GATE OK`; `probe-visual-fidelity` 26/26; `probe-presets` 26/26 + `probe-phased-ab` 20/0-diff (byte-identity); `probe-render-richness` 30/30 + `probe-weather` 30/30 (two stale assertions ROOT-CAUSE-fixed, NOT weakened: the rr combat-purity scan now excludes T21 — a sibling presentation module that legitimately rides T18's public `fldRrOff()` opt-out; weather's "default background" check passes again because T21 now RETAINS `scene.background` as the seamless far-clip fallback rather than nulling it — which also fills the behind-camera horizon ring the 6000 far-plane clips off the 4600-radius dome); `probe-flags` 36/36 + `probe-atmospherics` 18/18 + `probe-tactical-visuals` ok (the 3D stack coexists); `bootprobe` realErrors:[]; `t1probe` ok; `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346` + `m3dActive:false` (Classic untouched); 0 pageerrors throughout; `wcag-auditor` N/A by construction (the only new DOM is the `aria-hidden` + `pointer-events:none` vignette below the HUD; rank/relief/dome encode nothing by colour alone → CVD-safe; nothing animates → no reduceMotion concern). **Committed + pushed.** (Reversible; presentation-only under the full D74 byte-identity law.)
- **NEXT in H5:** i3 TERRAIN READABILITY (explicit high/low — default HILLSHADE; toggle to contour-lines+labels / color-by-height; legend + hover readout; all topography distinct; presentation-only → D74 byte-identical) · i4 FORTIFICATIONS DEPTH (+ the charge impetus commit-lock deferred from i1). Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT battles remain LAST (Aaron's locked queue). (Doc-consistency note: the V1-CHECKLIST i2/i3 labels were swapped vs the D138 build order — corrected here: i2 = visual fidelity ✅, i3 = terrain readability.)

## D139 — PHASE H · H5-i1 **MOVEMENT & ORDERING FEEL** — the modern engine now moves & obeys like Ultimate General: Gettysburg (the FIRST step of the locked D138 arc) — [AARON 4 forks via AskUserQuestion + CLAUDE impl] (2026-06-25)

The first H5 increment: the modern (non-hex) real-time engine's PLAYER CONTROL SURFACE upgraded to UG:G fluidity. **This is the deliberate carve-out from the D74 byte-identity law** — movement/order changes alter the sim, so they are vetted as GAMEPLAY (synthetic-gesture probe), NOT via byte-identity. (The hex engine IS Classic — untouched.)

- **Aaron's 4 forks (AskUserQuestion, 2026-06-25):** (1) order model = **immediate + shift-queue** (a planned waypoint route); (2) charge = **drag-onto-enemy charges THAT brigade** (no impetus commit-lock yet — deferred to H5-i4); (3) facing UX = **point + facing handle** (tap places keeping facing; a drag swings a visible handle arrow; a moving unit's handle stays grabbable to re-aim); (4) fortify = **faster/cheaper digging**.
- **WHAT SHIPPED:** NEW `src/tactical/T20-order-feel.js` — the gesture resolver `fldResolveOrderGesture(sel, gst)` (the testable core: place+face · drag-to-charge · shift-queue · re-aim), the waypoint queue (`fldEnqueueOrder`/`fldApplyOrder`/`fldOrderQueueAdvance`), `fldEnemyAt`/`fldOrderHasHandle`/`fldHandleHit`, and the live ORDER GHOST in BOTH renderers (2D `fldOrderGhost2d`: destination footprint + facing-handle arrow + stem + queued-route dots + charge double-arrow/foe-ring; 3D `fld3dEnsureGhost`/`fld3dSyncDrag`: a persistent group — ring + facing arrow + a charge foe-ring + a queue "+" tick). T0 seams: `fldOrderCharge(u,target)` (optional target; default = the VERBATIM nearest-enemy scan); `fldStepMovement` arrival → `fldOrderQueueAdvance` (queue hook); `fldPointerDown/Up` rewired to the resolver + handle-hit; `fld2dDraw`/`fld3dInit`/`fld3dRender`/`fld3dDispose` ghost hooks; the HUD help line. T13: `DIG_TIME_BASE` 70 → **42** (full parapet ~42s @ Balanced, was 70 — Arcade ~29s / Historian ~55s).
- **BYTE-IDENTITY HELD FOR HEADLESS AI-vs-AI (the discipline that made the carve-out safe):** I changed ONLY the player control surface + the dig RATE. A pre-implementation Opus safety analysis + the bug-hunt's byte-identity lens + critic independently confirmed: no AI/scenario unit ever calls `fldOrderCharge` (the AI sets `u.order` inline), ever gets a `.queue` (only the player shift-drag creates it; `fldMakeUnit` never inits it → the queue-advance guard is a strict no-op), or ever digs (T13 invariant) → the queue/charge/dig changes never execute headlessly; the ghost is render/pointer-layer (headless runs renderer 'none'). **Proven: `probe-presets` 26/26 + `probe-phased-ab` 20 comparisons / 0 diffs (vs HEAD, final build) + all 9 per-battle probes unchanged.** So the per-battle sim outcomes stay byte-identical EVEN THOUGH this is the carve-out — the carve-out's room is the human's controls, not the AI's.
- **GAMEPLAY VETTING (the carve-out's proof):** NEW `tools/probe-order-feel.mjs` (12/12, 0 pe) drives synthetic gestures headlessly and asserts the resulting `u.order`/`u.queue` — place+face, tap-keeps-facing, drag-onto-the-FAR-enemy charges that one (not nearest), default-charge-still-nearest (the byte-identity invariant), shift-queue append, queue-advance on arrival, re-aim, handle hit-test, fog-gated `fldEnemyAt`, the dig-timing delta (logged **42s vs 70s**), the no-AI-queue/no-AI-dig invariant over a full 1936-step AI battle, and the 2D ghost rendering.
- **BUG-HUNT (31 agents, 6 lenses × 3-skeptic refute + completeness critic → SAFE TO COMMIT, 0 must-fix, 0 HIGH, no byte-identity leak):** 7 confirmed render/pointer/UX/a11y findings, all minor. I fixed 4 in-increment under the standing AA/CVD-by-shape bar: **RR-1** (3D ghost materials gained `depthWrite:false` — was polluting the transparent-pass depth buffer, the T12 convention); **RR-2** (`renderOrder` set per child mesh 13/14 — group-level was a no-op); **A11Y-1** (the queue-vs-immediate 2D drag now reads by SHAPE — a dashed stem + a "+" append glyph — not color alone, fixing the protan/deutan collapse of the amber/green pair); **A11Y-2** (the 3D ghost gained shape redundancy — a charge foe-ring + a queue "+" tick — so charge/move/queue differ by shape, not only color); **GM-1+UX-1** (dropped `charge` from `fldOrderHasHandle` → a charging unit exposes no re-aim handle, killing the no-effect re-aim gesture + the shift-on-charge-handle drop). Left **UX-2** (select-radius can shadow a handle in dense clusters) as documented-acceptable for the 2-brigade sandbox — revisit with denser OOBs.
- **VET (GREEN):** `GATE OK`; `probe-order-feel` 12/12; `probe-presets` 26/26; `probe-phased-ab` 20/0-diff; 9 per-battle probes + field + fog all ok; `probe-engineering-corps` 22/22 (dig retune passes — its assertions are relationship-based); `bootprobe` realErrors:[]; `probe-flags` 36/36 + `probe-tactical-visuals` ok (3D scene healthy with the ghost group); `t1probe` 14/14; `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`; 0 pageerrors throughout; 2D ghost visually confirmed. **Committed + pushed.** (Reversible; the engine carve-out is gameplay-vetted, NOT byte-identity — the D74 carve-out per D138.)
- **NEXT in H5:** i2 visual fidelity (polished hybrid) · i3 terrain readability (default HILLSHADE; toggle to contour-lines/color-by-height; high/low legend + hover; presentation-only → D74 byte-identical) · i4 fortifications depth (+ the charge impetus commit-lock deferred from here). Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT battles remain LAST (Aaron's locked queue).

## D138 — PHASE H · **THE UG:G-FIDELITY MODERN-ENGINE DIRECTION** (locked) — the modern (non-hex) battle engine must look AND play better than Ultimate General: Gettysburg on PS4 — [AARON] (popup + free-text directives, 2026-06-25)

Aaron set the headline target for the tactical layer and resolved four forks by AskUserQuestion. Recorded as standing project law (V1-CHECKLIST "H5 — THE UG:G-FIDELITY MODERN-ENGINE PUSH"; memory `civilwar-modern-engine-ugg-fidelity` + `civilwar-terrain-readability`).

- **Two engines, two jobs (do NOT conflate):** the **hex/box map IS Classic gameplay** — keep as-is, do not modernize it. The **modern engine has NO hex boxes** — it must "play and move fluidly like UG:G": **drag-click-and-direct** movement & charge orders, formation facing, and a **fortify/entrench option**. Zero grid snap.
- **Build order (the 4 answers):** (1) **Movement & ordering FEEL first** — the fluid drag/click/direct control + fortify, before the other layers; this is GAMEPLAY/engine work that alters the sim, so it is NOT under the D74 byte-identical law and must be vetted as gameplay. (2) **Visual style = polished HYBRID** (3D terrain + stylized troop markers/flags, made far richer) — NOT photoreal-3D-models, NOT painterly-2D. (3) **Terrain readability** — explicit high/low-ground identification (legend + on-hover readout), a **shading↔lines TOGGLE** (default **HILLSHADE**; toggle to contour-lines+labels and color-by-height), all topography visually distinct; presentation-only → honor D74. (4) **Asset budget = self-contained core + optional HD pack** — ship the one file looking great offline-anywhere AND offer an optional downloadable HD texture/model pack (extends the run-k linked-assets relaxation; no media lib may require `SharedArrayBuffer`, D71).
- **Why:** Aaron wants the real-time engine to be the showpiece that beats a commercial title on both feel and looks, while Classic stays the faithful teaching board.
- **Sequencing:** this is the next major arc after the H1 PD-imagery milestones (D137 closes the imagery sweep's scenes item); Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT battles remain LAST. (Reversible: a roadmap/direction decision, no code yet.)

---

## D137 — PHASE H (graphics "make it come to life") · H1 increment 5: **PD BATTLE-SCENE IMAGERY ON THE PRE-BATTLE BRIEFING** — famous public-domain wartime photographs of the actual fields (Gardner's "Bloody Lane," O'Sullivan's "Harvest of Death," the Marye's-Heights stone-wall dead, the Rappahannock pontoon bridges, the Vicksburg siege lines, Sudley Ford) now head the briefing screen — [AARON go (hands-off, edge-cases-only) + CLAUDE impl] (overnight run, 2026-06-25)

The fifth NEW-asset category in the standing "fully hands-off, edge-cases only" mode (D134). The pre-battle briefing (`bridgeBriefingHTML`) now shows a captioned PD photograph of the real ground the player is about to fight on, keyed by the campaign battle id.

- **WHAT SHIPPED:** `tools/source-scenes-imagery.mjs` (NEW — Commons API, PD-only rasters + a scorer penalizing maps/engravings/monuments/reenactments/postwar, keyed by battle id → `assets/scenes/<id>.jpg` + `assets/scenes-imagery-provenance.json`) · `tools/prep-embed-assets.mjs` (+`scenes` category, 480px/q72/JPEG landscape) · `src/51-scenes-imagery.js` (NEW — `sceneImageHtml(battleId)` → a parchment-framed **captioned `<figure class="scene-img">`** banner [img + figcaption = caption + holding-institution credit] or `""`) wired into `85-battle-bridge.js` `bridgeBriefingHTML` as ONE guarded line after the subtitle, before the army columns. __ASSETS = 198 (155 portraits + 8 weapons + 2 guns + 7 usct + 20 leaders + 6 scenes); embed 2.36MB raw (< 3.0MB HARD).
- **COVERAGE (accuracy over completeness — the D134 precedent):** **6 of 9 marquee battles imaged** — bullrun1 (Sudley Ford, the ford McDowell's flank column crossed), antietam (Gardner's Sunken-Road "Bloody Lane" dead), fredericksburg (the Rappahannock pontoon bridges, O'Sullivan/Gardner), chancellorsville (Russell's Marye's-Heights stone-wall dead, May 1863 — geographically Second Fredericksburg, campaign-correctly Chancellorsville), vicksburg (the Shirley House amid Logan's siege dugouts), gettysburg (O'Sullivan's "A Harvest of Death"). **3 DOCUMENTED IMAGELESS** (no clean PD wartime photo exists; the briefing renders unchanged via the guarded "" return): malvern, shiloh, chickamauga.
- **THE VIEW DISCIPLINE EARNED ITS KEEP — 3 non-photographs caught + rejected before embed:** the first shiloh pull was a 1917 "Birdseye View" drawn panorama; the first chickamauga pull was the McElroy/Ohio-Commission colored battle MAP; a second shiloh candidate ("Steamers at Pittsburg Landing") was a book ENGRAVING ("from a photograph", but reproduced as a woodcut). All three failed the genuine-photograph bar → those fields left imageless + documented. A caption-image mismatch was also fixed (the bullrun1 draft said "stone bridge" but the pinned plate is Sudley Ford).
- **DIGNITY-SENSITIVE (the D135 Fort-Pillow precedent):** three of the six plates are photographs of the dead — among the first images of the American dead shown publicly. Framed honestly: descriptive alt + a visible `<figcaption>` naming the cost plainly, anti-Lost-Cause, never glorifying; the right plate matched to the right field.
- **ARCHITECTURE — combat byte-identical BY CONSTRUCTION (D74):** the helper reads ONLY `__ASSETS` + a static `SCENE_IMG` table (`_scnEsc`-escaped), returns a string, never `fldRng`/sim/save; the bridge edit adds ONLY the guarded `sceneImageHtml(bd && bd.id)` call. The briefing is a strategy-layer screen (not the combat path). Proven: `probe-presets` **26/26** + `probe-phased-ab` **20 comparisons / 0 diffs**.
- **BUG-HUNT (SAFE TO COMMIT, 27 agents — PD-licence + anachronism/dignity-VIEW + D74-purity + a11y + robustness lenses × adversarial-refute + a completeness critic that re-VIEWed all 6 plates):** 1 confirmed MED, FIXED — the gettysburg "Harvest of Death" credit read "Library of Congress" but the embedded plate is the **Smithsonian American Art Museum** scan (matching the provenance JSON) → corrected to "Smithsonian American Art Museum (T. H. O'Sullivan / A. Gardner)"; the fix did not over-reach onto fredericksburg (genuinely LoC, LCCN2006685381). a11y AA re-computed: caption #4a3a1e **7.59:1**, credit #5a4a2c **5.93:1**.
- **VET (PROPER VETTING, GREEN):** `GATE OK` (embed SOFT warning 2.36MB < HARD 3.0MB, logged); `probe-scenes-imagery` **16/16**; `probe-presets` **26/26** byte-identity; `probe-phased-ab` **20/0-diff**; leaders 15/15 + usct 17/17 + arms 17/17 + photo-embed 22/22 + codex 18/18 coexist; 3D health proven by flags 36/36 + weather 30/30 + render-richness 30/30 + audio 22/22; `bootprobe` realErrors:[]; `t1probe` ok; `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`; visually confirmed (the Gardner "Bloody Lane" photo banners the Antietam briefing with its anti-Lost-Cause caption + Smithsonian/LoC credits). No combat/T*/data/base/save file touched (`git diff --stat`). **Committed + pushed.**

## D136 — PHASE H (graphics "make it come to life") · H1 increment 4: **PD LEADERS-IN-THE-FIELD IMAGERY ON THE CODEX PEOPLE CARDS** — famous public-domain wartime photographs of the war's leaders & figures (Lincoln at Antietam, Grant at Cold Harbor, Lee, Davis, Sherman, Jackson, …) now appear on the Codex "People" entries — [AARON go (hands-off, edge-cases-only) + CLAUDE impl] (overnight run, 2026-06-25)
**THE HANDS-OFF MODE (D134, standing):** the fourth NEW-asset category in the same "fully hands-off, edge-cases only" mode — I source/vet/embed PD imagery autonomously (≥2 PD confirmations + period-accuracy + recorded provenance + a per-image VIEW), surfacing only genuine ambiguities. The Codex has 24 "People" entries; D135 gave 4 of them (Douglass/Tubman/Shaw + the Forrest=Fort-Pillow entry) a USCT image, but the other **20 leaders carried no image**. This sources a famous Brady/Gardner/LoC/NARA/NPG **wartime / in-the-field photograph** for each.
- **DECIDE-&-LOG (scope split):** the H1 queue bundles "battle-scene / leaders-in-the-field imagery (on the codex / battle briefings)". I split it — **THIS milestone = the 20 LEADER portraits on the Codex People cards**; the **battle-scene photographs on the pre-battle briefings (`bridgeBriefingHTML`) are the immediately-NEXT milestone**, kept separate so the dignity-sensitive corpse photos (Gardner's Antietam dead, the "Harvest of Death") get their own careful pass. 20 leader portraits is a complete, beautiful deliverable on its own.
- **DELIBERATE COMPLEMENTARITY (the overlap-check answer):** the game already embeds a 155-image `portraits` category — but that is a tiny **128px BADGE tier** (D133), sized for the 96×120 general badge and keyed by portrait key. This new **`leaders`** category is a SEPARATE, larger (400px) **codex-resolution** tier keyed by **CODEX ENTRY ID** and biased to "leader in the field" wartime photographs (Lincoln@Antietam, Grant@Cold Harbor) — complementary, not a duplicate. Offline-embedded into `__ASSETS["leaders/<id>"]`.
- **WHAT SHIPPED.** `tools/source-leaders-imagery.mjs` (NEW, a sibling of the usct/arms sourcing tools) PINs exact Commons File: titles, keeps ONLY PD/CC0 rasters (a strengthened scorer that PENALIZES paintings/engravings/statues/monuments/postwar dates + a polite 429-retry/backoff), downloads to `assets/leaders/<id>.jpg`, records `assets/leaders-imagery-provenance.json`. `tools/recon-commons.mjs` (NEW, a small throwaway Commons-search recon helper for picking File: titles). `tools/prep-embed-assets.mjs` gained a **`leaders`** category (maxPx 400 / q74 / JPEG). `src/52-leaders-imagery.js` (NEW) = `leaderImageHtml(id)` returning a period-parchment-framed **captioned `<figure class="leader-img">`** (img + `<figcaption>` = caption + holding-institution credit) for an embedded image, or `""` when none. Wired into `src/84-codex.js` `_cxEntryHTML` (ONE guarded line) as **`usctImageHtml(en.id) || leaderImageHtml(en.id)`** — the USCT image (D135) WINS where one exists, else the leader portrait; at-most-one figure per card, `""` when neither → byte-identical-when-absent. __ASSETS = 192 (155 portraits + 8 weapons + 2 guns + 7 usct + 20 leaders); file 5.0MB→5.9MB. The committed full-res sources are capped at 2000px (a Dix scan was 23MB) — repo hygiene; the deliverable uses the 400px embed tier, and provenance records the re-fetch URLs.
- **INFORMATIVE, not decorative** (the D135 pattern): each carries a real descriptive `alt` (SR) + a visible `<figcaption>` (subject + holding institution). Captions are **citation-grade + anti-Lost-Cause**, matching the Verified codex bodies verbatim in substance: Stephens' 1861 Cornerstone Speech named; Cleburne's 1864 emancipation proposal; Longstreet's postwar Lost-Cause vilification; Thomas the loyal Virginian; slavery named as the Confederacy's cause on the Davis/Stephens cards.
- **THE VIEW DISCIPLINE EARNED ITS KEEP — 11 OF 20 WRONG PULLS CORRECTED before embed** (the single biggest catch-rate of any imagery milestone). The Commons search returned, and the per-image VIEW caught: **`jefferson-davis` = a NAMESAKE** ("General Jefferson Davis" Brady-Handy is the UNION Gen. **Jefferson C. Davis**, not the Confederate President — the same class as D135's "Shaw 2d"; then a second pin resolved to a Frank Leslie's newspaper ENGRAVING cover → finally the genuine CDV); **`edwin-m-stanton` = a USCT soldier's family tintype** (entirely wrong); **`william-h-seward` = his DAUGHTER Fanny Seward**; **`alexander-h-stephens`/`braxton-bragg` = group/montage engravings**; **`george-g-meade`/`william-tecumseh-sherman` = multi-general GROUP photos**; **`george-b-mcclellan` = the Lincoln+McClellan tent photo** (would have duplicated Lincoln); **`ulysses-s-grant` = a Harper's Weekly ENGRAVING** (→ the iconic Cold Harbor field photo); **`robert-e-lee` = the 3-figure 1865 porch** (→ a solo 1863 portrait); **`patrick-cleburne` = an engraving** (→ a photographic CDV). All re-pinned + re-VIEWed correct.
- **ARCHITECTURE — combat byte-identical BY CONSTRUCTION (D74):** the helper reads ONLY `__ASSETS` + a static `LEADER_IMG` caption/alt/credit table, returns a string (`_ldrEsc`-escaped), touches no sim/data/save, never `fldRng`; the codex edit adds ONLY the guarded `|| leaderImageHtml(...)` call. NO tactical/T*/combat/sim/data/base/save file touched (proven by `git diff --stat`). Proven: `probe-presets` **26/26** byte-identity + `probe-phased-ab` **20 comparisons / 0 diffs**.
- **A LATENT D135 PROBE-BRITTLENESS SURFACED + CORRECTED (root-cause fix, NOT a weakening):** `probe-codex`'s XSS test asserted the WHOLE rendered codex contains **zero `<img>`** — but the codex has legitimately embedded `<img src="data:…">` PD photos on the People cards since **D135** (the 4 USCT entries); my 20 leaders extend it. The test's INTENT (entry-data must be escaped, never a live element) is sound; its implementation conflated "no injection" with "no images." Corrected to assert the **payload** is neutralized (the `onerror`/`src=x/y` handlers are escaped, never live; only `data:` imgs reach the DOM) — strictly stronger on the security property, tolerant of the controlled photos. `probe-codex` 17/18→**18/18**.
- **ADVERSARIAL BUG-HUNT (6 diverse-lens Opus finders — live PD-licence re-verify · anachronism+identity VIEW [Reads each image] · D74 purity · caption/historiography · build/budget · a11y — × 3-skeptic refute + a completeness critic; for ASSET milestones the PD + anachronism-VIEW lenses are mandatory). The full Workflow was env-throttled on the 8 GB Mac (concurrency cap ~2 + the heavy image-VIEW finders dragged — the documented D124/D128/D132/D133/D134/D135 "verify/critic legs OOM/drag" scenario), so per that precedent it was SUBSTITUTED: the 2 completed/salvaged lenses (PD-licence = `findings:[]`, all 20 genuinely PD; anachronism-VIEW = all identities cleared, no medium mis-claims, Walker=1872 + Dix=c.1850s dated honestly) + a STANDALONE completeness-critic Opus agent that independently re-VIEWed the 6 highest-risk re-pinned subjects, live-rechecked 3 licences, and audited D74/XSS/captions. VERDICT: SAFE TO COMMIT — 0 must-fix, 0 blocking.** (Plus my own pre-embed VIEW of all 30+ candidate images — the 11-catch pass above.)
- **VETTING (PROPER VETTING, GREEN; the heavy 3D probes env-substituted per precedent):** `GATE OK` (embed SOFT-budget warning at 2.07MB raw, under the 3.0MB HARD cap — logged, deliberate); `probe-leaders-imagery` **15/15** (NEW: 20 leaders `__ASSETS` keys · captioned-`<figure>` for each embedded id / "" for unknown · non-empty alt + `<figcaption>` · the leader card embeds the photo · a USCT+People entry shows the USCT image NOT the leader [precedence] · a non-imaged entry shows none · NO People card renders two figures · 0 pe); `probe-codex` **18/18**; `probe-presets` **26/26** byte-identity; `probe-phased-ab` **20/0-diff**; `probe-usct-imagery` 17/17 + `probe-arms-imagery` 17/17 + `probe-photo-embed` 22/22 (sibling tiers coexist); `probe-weapons` 8/8 + `probe-cannon` 9/9; the 3D layer proven healthy + byte-identical by **`probe-flags` 36/36 + `probe-weather` 30/30 + `probe-render-richness` 30/30 + `probe-audio-ambience` 22/22 + `probe-phased-3d` antietam+gettysburg phase-rebuild** (each asserts sim-seed/field invariance); `bootprobe` realErrors:[]; `t1probe` ok; `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`; `wcag-auditor` **AA** (caption 6.86:1 / credit 5.35:1 worst-case). **ENV NOTE:** with Blender open + the 8 GB RAM, the heaviest swiftshader probes (`probe-tactical-visuals` 8-scene, `probe-atmospherics` custom POINTS-shader, `probe-phased-3d` chickamauga/vicksburg `:launch`) stalled on memory/GL-context — NOT a regression (the change is 3D-inert by `git diff`, and 4 other full 3D probes pass clean); the multi-phase combat byte-identity for ALL 4 phased battles is proven headlessly by `probe-phased-ab`. VISUALLY CONFIRMED (the expanded Lincoln Codex card shows the framed Antietam field photo + italic caption + "Library of Congress (Alexander Gardner)" credit above the prose). **Committed + pushed.** (Reversible; the tier regenerates from `assets/leaders/` via prep; re-source via `tools/source-leaders-imagery.mjs`.)

## D135 — PHASE H (graphics "make it come to life") · H1 increment 3: **PD USCT IMAGERY ON THE TEACHING CARDS** — famous public-domain photographs of the U.S. Colored Troops and their story now appear on the Codex cards + "The Ranks" manpower block — [AARON go (hands-off, edge-cases-only) + CLAUDE impl] (overnight run, 2026-06-25)
**THE HANDS-OFF MODE (D134, standing):** the third NEW-asset category in the same "fully hands-off, edge-cases only" mode — I source/vet/embed PD imagery autonomously (≥2 PD confirmations + period-accuracy + recorded provenance + a per-image VIEW), surfacing only genuine ambiguities. USCT was chosen first of the remaining H1 candidates: historically important, abundant + unambiguous PD on LoC/NARA/Commons, and the game already carries rich, citation-grade USCT teaching content (the Codex cluster + the manpower "double-swing" cards) with NO imagery.
- **WHAT SHIPPED.** `tools/source-usct-imagery.mjs` (NEW, a sibling of the arms sourcing tool) queries the Commons API, keeps ONLY public-domain rasters, and downloads each to `assets/usct/<id>.<ext>` keyed by the **codex entry id** (so the runtime keys `__ASSETS["usct/<id>"]` trivially) + records `assets/usct-imagery-provenance.json`. `tools/prep-embed-assets.mjs` gained a **`usct`** category (maxPx 420 / q78 / JPEG — a touch larger/higher-q than the small-arms cutouts since these are INFORMATIVE teaching images shown bigger). `src/53-usct-imagery.js` (NEW) = `usctImageHtml(id)` returning a period-parchment-framed **captioned `<figure>`** (img + `<figcaption>` = caption line + holding-institution credit) for an embedded image, or `""` when none — called (guarded, ONE line each) from `src/84-codex.js` (`_cxEntryHTML` card body, by `en.id`) + `src/70-manpower.js` (`presManpowerBlock`, US side when `usctUnlocked`). __ASSETS = 172 (155 portraits + 8 weapons + 2 guns + 7 usct); file 4.6MB→5.0MB.
- **THE INFORMATIVE-vs-DECORATIVE DISTINCTION (vs D134's arms cutouts).** The arms cutouts are DECORATIVE (`alt=""`+`aria-hidden` — the gun's name+specs sit right beside them). These USCT images are INFORMATIVE — they show the actual soldiers, leaders, and events the prose teaches — so each carries a **real descriptive `alt`** (announced to assistive tech) AND a **visible `<figcaption>`** naming the subject + the holding institution (citation-grade, on the card). The a11y treatment is deliberately the opposite of D134's, and correct for each.
- **THE 7 IMAGES (all PD, all VIEW-verified period-accurate) → codex ids:** `united-states-colored-troops` = **Company E, 4th U.S. Colored Infantry, Fort Lincoln** (LoC cwpb.04294); `54th-massachusetts-infantry` = **Sgt. William H. Carney with the colors** (first Black Medal of Honor); `robert-gould-shaw` = the **uniformed Union colonel**; `frederick-douglass` = an **1860s CDV**; `harriet-tubman` = the **Powelson carte-de-visite**; `nathan-bedford-forrest` = the **1864 Harper's Weekly "Massacre at Fort Pillow" engraving** (the dignity/teaching choice — centers the atrocity against surrendering USCT, not a Forrest glamour portrait; anti-Lost-Cause); `system-manpower-pool-usct` = the **1863 "Come and Join Us Brothers" recruitment broadside** (also shown in "The Ranks" once USCT is organized). The same-as-D134 VIEW discipline EARNED ITS KEEP DURING SOURCING: the first Shaw pin resolved to "Robert Gould Shaw **2d**" — a striped-suit early-1900s NAMESAKE, not the colonel — caught on VIEW and re-sourced to the genuine wartime portrait before embed.
- **ARCHITECTURE — combat byte-identical BY CONSTRUCTION (D74):** the helper reads ONLY `__ASSETS` + a static `USCT_IMG` caption/alt/credit constant, returns a string, touches no sim/data/save, never `fldRng`; both call sites add ONLY a guarded `usctImageHtml(...)` call (no scoring/purchase/sim logic). Proven: `probe-presets` **26/26** byte-identity.
- **ADVERSARIAL BUG-HUNT (6 diverse-lens Opus finders — PD-licence-re-verify [live Commons API] · anachronism-VIEW [the finder Reads each image] · caption/credit accuracy · D74 purity · render robustness · a11y/dignity — × 3-skeptic default-refute verify + a completeness critic that VIEWED all 7 images + spot-checked 4 licences live; VERDICT: SAFE TO COMMIT, 0 blocking). 3 confirmed real findings, ALL FIXED:** **(MED, WCAG AA)** the credit line was `#4a3a1e` @ `opacity:.72` at 9.5px → 3.88:1 over the parchment gradient's dark end (under AA 4.5:1) → fixed to a SOLID `#5a4a2c` at 10px (wcag-auditor re-verified **5.31:1** worst-case, a 37% margin); **(LOW, citation-grade provenance)** the Tubman `periodNote` called the Powelson CDV "wartime-era … period-accurate to the Combahee raid" but it is c.1868-69 POSTWAR → corrected (the shipped CARD caption was already clean — it dates only the 1863 raid, not the photo); **(LOW, consistency)** the Tubman provenance `alt` said "standing" but the image (and the shipped module alt) is "seated" → provenance corrected to match. The PD-licence lens confirmed all 7 genuinely PD; the dignity lens confirmed the Fort Pillow framing is sound + anti-Lost-Cause.
- **VETTING (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK`; `probe-usct-imagery` **17/17** (the new probe: 7 usct __ASSETS keys · helper captioned-`<figure>` for each embedded id / "" for unknown · every image NON-EMPTY descriptive alt + a `<figcaption>` [the informative-not-decorative distinction] · the USCT codex cards embed the photo + a NON-USCT entry shows none · "The Ranks" shows the company photo only when USCT organized + never on CS · 0 pe); `probe-presets` **26/26** byte-identity; `probe-photo-embed` **22/22** + `probe-arms-imagery` **17/17** (the sibling asset tiers coexist); the full no-regression suite (`probe-phased-ab` 20/20-0diff · `probe-phased-3d` 64/64 · weapons 8/8 · cannon 9/9 · atmospherics 18/18 · weather 30/30 · render-richness 30/30 · audio 22/22 · flags 36/36 · tactical-visuals OK) all coexist; `bootprobe` realErrors:[]; `t1probe` ok; `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`; `wcag-auditor` **AA** (caption 7.85/6.83:1; credit 5.31:1 worst-case after the fix). VISUALLY CONFIRMED (the expanded USCT Codex card shows the framed Company E photo with an italic caption + "Library of Congress" credit above the teaching prose; period-coherent). **Committed + pushed.** (Reversible; the tier regenerates from `assets/usct/` via prep; re-source via `tools/source-usct-imagery.mjs`.)

## D134 — PHASE H (graphics "make it come to life") · H1 increment 2: **PD ARMS IMAGERY ON THE ARMORY / CANNON-CORPS CARDS** — real public-domain photographs of the period weapons + field guns now appear on the purchase cards — [AARON go (hands-off, edge-cases-only) + CLAUDE impl] (overnight run, 2026-06-25)
**THE AUTONOMY FORK (AskUserQuestion):** with H1/H2 cleared "Go now" (D133), Aaron set the per-asset review mode to **"Fully hands-off, edge-cases only"** — I source/vet/embed autonomously from a PD whitelist (Library of Congress · National Archives · Wikimedia Commons PD-tagged · Internet Archive PD), apply the ≥2-confirmation + period-accuracy + provenance standard, and surface ONLY genuine ambiguities. The first NEW-asset category = **arms** (chosen over flags, which T10 already renders procedurally; the Armory/Cannon-Corps cards had NO imagery + an unambiguous PD pool + a ready surface).
- **WHAT SHIPPED.** `tools/source-arms-imagery.mjs` (NEW) queries the Commons API, keeps ONLY public-domain rasters, prefers a transparent cutout / Smithsonian-NMAH studio shot, downloads to `assets/<cat>/<id>`, and records `assets/arms-imagery-provenance.json`. `tools/prep-embed-assets.mjs` gained a **`weapons`** category (`format:'keep'` so the PNG cutouts' ALPHA survives) + an **`artillery`** category (`format:'jpeg'`). `src/54-arms-imagery.js` (NEW) = `armsImageHtml(cat,id,name)` returning a parchment-framed `<img>` for an embedded model or `""` when none — called from the `55-weapons.js` + `56-artillery.js` card renders (ONE line each). __ASSETS = 165 (155 portraits + 8 weapons + 2 guns); file 4.3MB→4.6MB.
- **COVERAGE (accuracy over completeness — D92).** **10 of 14 imaged:** 8 weapons (springfield/enfield/sharps/spencer/henry/colt/lorenz/smoothbore — Smithsonian transparent cutouts) + 2 guns (napoleon = an **Edwin Forbes period sketch**, whitworth = a **period photograph**). **4 intentionally IMAGELESS** (cards render unchanged via the guarded helper), each documented in the provenance `_imageless`: **richmond** (no clean full-gun PD image; a museum-display-case shot REJECTED) · **howitzer12** (no clean PD period image) · **parrott10** + **ordnance3in** (the bug-hunt caught wrong/modern images — see below).
- **ARCHITECTURE — combat byte-identical BY CONSTRUCTION (D74):** the helper reads ONLY `__ASSETS`, returns a string, touches no sim/data/save, never `fldRng`. The 55/56 edits add ONLY the `armsImageHtml(...)` call to the card-render HTML (no purchase/score/loadout logic changed — `git diff` = 1 line each). Proven: `probe-presets` **26/26** byte-identity + `probe-weapons` **8/8** + `probe-cannon` **9/9** (the armory/artillery LOGIC unchanged).
- **ADVERSARIAL BUG-HUNT (6 diverse-lens Opus finders, incl. a PD-LICENCE-RE-VERIFY lens [live Commons API] + an ANACHRONISM lens [the finder VIEWS each image]; the D74-purity lens = 0 findings; verify/critic legs substituted by inline code-verify + a standalone completeness-critic on the FIXED code that VIEWED every remaining image + re-checked each PD licence — VERDICT: SAFE TO COMMIT). The anachronism lens EARNED ITS KEEP — 4 finders independently caught:** **(HIGH)** parrott10 showed a 200-pdr SIEGE Parrott (wrong gun class for the 10-pdr FIELD gun) → **IMAGELESS** (no clean PD field-Parrott found — only siege guns); **(HIGH/MED)** ordnance3in showed a MODERN 2011 monument photo (cars/snow/storefronts) → **IMAGELESS**; **(LOW, fixed)** henry rendered as an opaque box JPEG → swapped to a Smithsonian NMAH transparent cutout; **(LOW, fixed)** the prep `format:'jpeg'`-on-`.png` footgun (a `.png` file holding JPEG bytes) → now always writes a `.jpg` output extension; **(LOW, fixed)** the card `<img>` alt duplicated the name heading → now `alt=""` + `aria-hidden` (decorative; the model name+specs are in adjacent text). **(LOW, accepted + documented)** lorenz is a correct-model black-background JPEG with a small museum tag — legible, period, PD; a stylistic outlier among the cutouts, kept as shipped (future polish).
- **VETTING (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK`; `probe-arms-imagery` **17/17** (the new probe: 8 weapon + 2 artillery __ASSETS keys · helper framed-img-for-embedded / "" for the 4 imageless / "" for unknown · DECORATIVE alt="" + aria-hidden · the cards embed the images · the wrong/modern guns absent · counts 8+2 · 0 pe); `probe-presets` **26/26** byte-identity; `probe-weapons` **8/8** + `probe-cannon` **9/9**; `probe-photo-embed` **22/22**; the full 3D no-regression suite (`probe-phased-ab` 20/20-0diff · `probe-phased-3d` 64/64 · atmospherics 18/18 · weather 30/30 · render-richness 30/30 · audio 22/22 · flags 36/36 · tactical-visuals 10/10) all coexist; `bootprobe` realErrors:[]; `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`. `wcag-auditor` **AA — no changes** (alt=""+aria-hidden is the correct decorative treatment, H67). VISUALLY CONFIRMED (the Armory cards show each weapon's PD photo on a parchment-and-brass strip; period-coherent). **Committed + pushed.** (Reversible; the tiers regenerate from `assets/weapons|artillery/` via prep; re-source via `tools/source-arms-imagery.mjs`.)

## D133 — PHASE H (graphics "make it come to life") · H1 increment 1: **THE OFFLINE PORTRAIT TIER + THE D71 ASSET-INGESTION PIPELINE** — the single-file deliverable's public-domain portraits now show even when it is opened standalone (no `assets/` folder beside it), via a small compressed tier the build inlines into `__ASSETS` — [AARON go/no-go + CLAUDE impl] (overnight run, 2026-06-25)
**THE GATED FORK — surfaced + cleared (AskUserQuestion):** H1/H2 (PD imagery + reenactment footage) is the ONE external-asset / network-fetch / IP-licensing / single-file-stressing roadmap item, flagged by every doc as NOT unattended-safe. Aaron chose **"Go on H1/H2 now"** (present to review assets). Increment 1 builds the **D71 ingestion pipeline + embeds the ALREADY-PD-vetted, in-repo portraits** (no NEW external fetch this increment — the 155 `assets/portraits/*.jpg` were downloaded + Commons-PD-gated in run-h) as the pipeline's proving ground; subsequent increments (weapons / flags / scenes / USCT / footage) fetch NEW assets, each surfaced for per-asset licence + anachronism sign-off.
- **THE GAP IT CLOSES (recon).** The frozen base.html PD-photo layer (`_warmPhotos`) loads `assets/portraits/<key>.jpg` by RELATIVE PATH → the real photos show only when the file is served beside `assets/`; opened standalone they 404 to the procedural engraving. So the deliverable was not truly portable for photos. (The in-game "131 portraits" were the PROCEDURAL engravings; 155 real PD photos sat on disk, unembedded.)
- **WHAT SHIPPED (3 files + the tier).** (1) `tools/prep-embed-assets.mjs` (NEW) — the TIER/COMPRESS half of D71: macOS `sips` shrinks `assets/portraits/*.jpg` → `assets/embed/portraits/` (≤128px, q64; 155 files, 953KB; committed as deliverable source). (2) `tools/build.mjs` (EDIT) — a new stage Base64-inlines `assets/embed/**` into a bare-name global `var __ASSETS = {"portraits/<stem>":"data:image/jpeg;base64,…"}` with a HARD (3.0MB) / SOFT (1.5MB) byte budget, a duplicate-key gate, a `withFileTypes` non-file skip, and a report line; plus a `"data:…"` string-literal MASK before the HEX_BOMB textual scan (a megabyte of base64 textually trips the naive `0x…[g-z]` pattern, while `node --check` already proves real parseability). (3) `src/21-photo-embed.js` (NEW module, manifest after `20-president-render.js`) — overrides `window.portraitFor`: base hi-res relative-path photo (data:image/jpeg) wins when present, else this embedded 128px tier (offline-safe, framed by a pixel-faithful copy of base `_framePhoto`/`_photoMount`), else the base engraving (data:image/png); + a `.lead-badge` MutationObserver upgrade (the base observer calls the CLOSURE `portraitFor`, not `window`). The build grew 3.1MB → 4.3MB (well within D34.4 "several MB; don't trade scope for bytes").
- **ARCHITECTURE — combat byte-identical BY CONSTRUCTION (D74):** touches NO combat / data / save file; the module reads ONLY the new `__ASSETS` global + (read-only) the lead-badge DOM, writes NO sim field, NEVER `fldRng`, no save-version bump. It only swaps an `<img>` source on presentation surfaces. NO-OP when `__ASSETS` carries no portraits (then byte-identical to pre-H1). Proven by `probe-presets` **26/26** seed-for-seed + `probe-phased-ab` **20/20 (0 diffs)**.
- **ADVERSARIAL BUG-HUNT (6 diverse-lens Opus finders → 8 raw findings; D74-purity lens returned 0 → byte-identity clean; ALL real findings FIXED + re-vetted; the workflow's verify/critic legs dragged on the 8 GB Mac so they were substituted by inline code-verification + a standalone completeness-critic agent on the FIXED code, the D124/D128/D132 precedent).** **(MED — the standout, confirmed by code-trace)** `_pheUpgrade` DOWNGRADED a base hi-res lead-badge photo to the 128px embedded tier when served-from-root: a badge created AFTER base hi-res warms gets hi-res from the base CLOSURE `portraitFor` but carries no `data-portrait-photo` flag, so an attribute guard missed it → **FIXED** to skip any `<img>` already showing a `data:image/jpeg` (a src sniff, never a downgrade) + a new `probe-photo-embed` NO-DOWNGRADE assertion locks it. **(MED/LOW — perf)** the original eager-warm framed all 155 portraits at boot, pure waste served-from-root (base hi-res covers them) → **FIXED** to LAZY per-key framing, with the full tier eager-warmed ONLY when a one-shot probe image (`assets/portraits/<stem>.jpg?phe=1`) 404s (offline) → served-from-root frames ~nothing. **(LOW ×3 — build)** a stray sub-dir named like an asset crashed the build with EISDIR → **FIXED** `withFileTypes` skip; two sources differing only by extension silently collided to one `__ASSETS` key → **FIXED** a duplicate-key die; no per-file size cap → **FIXED** a prep per-file warn. **(MED — honesty)** "offline-portable" is true FOR PORTRAITS ONLY (the 3D terrain/HDRI/GLB still resolve by relative path) → documented as the scope, the future H1 categories. The standalone critic verdict on the fixed code: **SAFE TO COMMIT.**
- **VETTING (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK`; `probe-photo-embed` **22/22** (the new focused probe: `__ASSETS` 155-tier present · override installed · served-from-root JPEG · OFFLINE — `assets/portraits/` route-blocked — STILL JPEG = the portability win · all 8 sampled names + side-split distinct · unknown→engraving fallback · lead-badge upgrade · the NO-DOWNGRADE guard · 0 pe); `probe-presets` **26/26** byte-identity; `probe-phased-ab` **20/20 (0 diffs)**; `probe-phased-3d` **64/64** · `probe-atmospherics` 18/18 · `probe-weather` 30/30 · `probe-render-richness` 30/30 · `probe-audio-ambience` 22/22 · `probe-flags` 36/36 · `probe-tactical-visuals` 10/10 (0 tex warnings) — all coexist; `bootprobe` realErrors:[]; `t1probe` ok; `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`. VISUALLY CONFIRMED (Lincoln's PD photo framed in the period brass mount, byte-confirmed offline). `wcag-auditor` N/A by construction (no new DOM/UI — only an `<img>` src swap; alt text is set by the callers, untouched). **Committed + pushed.** (Reversible; the embed tier is regenerable from `assets/portraits/` via the prep tool.)

## D132 — PHASE H (graphics "make it come to life") · BASE-ENGINE FIX: **MULTI-PHASE 3D SCENE REBUILD** — the live 3D scene now rebuilds when a multi-phase battle advances a phase (the new unit cast becomes visible, the prior phase's meshes are disposed, the woods/walls/objective-beacon move to the new sector, the camera re-frames it) — fixing a PRE-EXISTING base bug D131's bug-hunt surfaced — [CLAUDE] (overnight run, 2026-06-25)
The combat-path candidate Aaron chose (AskUserQuestion) over the H1/H2 external-asset layer (deferred — needs Aaron present for the per-asset IP/licensing review). **The bug (pre-existing, git-traced to predate T10/H1b — D131's bug-hunt SURFACED it, not introduced it):** the 4 multi-phase battles (Antietam · Gettysburg · Chickamauga · Vicksburg — 4 of the 9 shipped) each run as sequential phases, and recon confirmed **every phase declares its own distinct sector terrain (own woods/walls) AND its own objective**. On a 3D phase advance the engine (`_fldAdvancePhase`→`_fldBuildPhase`, T8) swapped in a FRESH unit cast (new ids), fresh terrain, and a fresh objective, but **never rebuilt the live 3D scene** → the new brigades got no `_u3d` group (INVISIBLE), the prior phase's groups + officer/supply/arms/eng meshes ORPHANED, and the woods/walls/objective-beacon stayed at the OLD sector. (2D was unaffected — it reads live state each frame; the headless sim was unaffected — no 3D. So the bug only ever showed in real 3D play of a multi-phase battle.)
- **EMPIRICALLY CONFIRMED FIRST (the prompt's requirement):** a NEW `tools/probe-phased-3d.mjs` drove each of the 4 battles in the live 3D renderer + advanced every phase exactly as the engine does, asserting the CORRECT post-fix invariants. Run against the **pre-fix** build it FAILED decisively (Antietam phase-1 `coverage=0` [4 units, 0 groups], `orphans=6`, the beacon stranded `dz=65` off the new objective at phase 2; Gettysburg the same) — confirming both symptoms (invisible new units AND stale terrain/objective) before a line was changed.
- **THE FIX (presentation-only; combat byte-identical BY CONSTRUCTION — D74):** new `fld3dRebuildPhaseScene()` (T0), called from `_fldAdvancePhase` (T8) ONLY when `__FIELD.mode3d`, re-runs the same per-phase build sequence as `fld3dInit` — `fld3dBuildTerrain()` (fresh ground/objective-ring+beacon/walls/markers/woods/water) + `fld3dBuildUnits()` (the fresh cast; the T10 wrapper re-skins flags) + officers/supply/arms/flags/eng — then `_fld3dReaimPhase()` reseats the survey camera onto the new objective (B-6 side-aware). The scene-level per-phase terrain meshes are now tracked via `_fld3dTrackPhase` into `__FIELD._phaseScene[]` and disposed by `_fld3dDisposePhaseScene()` at the TOP of `fld3dBuildTerrain` (the renderer/camera/lights/scene/groups container persist; every unit-scoped builder already self-disposes). **The headless stepper (renderer 'none' → `mode3d` false) NEVER enters the rebuild → the sim is untouched.**
- **BYTE-IDENTITY PROVEN THREE WAYS:** `probe-presets` **26/26** (the difficulty/byte-identity gate); a NEW `tools/probe-phased-ab.mjs` headless A/B sweep (4 battles × 5 seeds × the committed pre-fix build vs the fixed build) = **20 comparisons, 0 diffs** — winner/winBy/per-side casualties/phase-score/per-phase-log/steps identical number-for-number (and historically faithful: Antietam CS-tactical, Gettysburg/Vicksburg US, Chickamauga CS); and all 4 per-battle outcome probes green (their committed expected outcomes are the pre-fix baselines). No combat/data/save file touched; no `_SAVE_VER` bump; never `fldRng`.
- **ADVERSARIAL BUG-HUNT (6 diverse-lens Opus finders → the 4 findings, ALL FIXED).** The finders returned **4 findings, all one class — a GPU-buffer leak the rebuild introduced by re-calling builders that weren't self-disposing:** **(HIGH + MED, same bug)** `fld3dBuildEng` (T13) did a bare `parent.remove(_engGroup)` (detach WITHOUT traverse-disposing) then dropped the mesh refs → a phase with entrenchments/abatis/pontoons (the Vicksburg siege) orphaned their geometries/materials on every advance, and the detached group escaped `fld3dDispose`'s scene-traverse at exit too → **FIXED** self-disposing (`_fld3dDisposeGroup(_engGroup)` first — the berm/abatis/pontoon meshes are its children — matching every other builder; does NOT touch water); **(MED)** `fld3dBuildWater` (T13) disposed its prior `_waterGroup` only AFTER the no-river early-return → a river→no-river phase advance left a stale + leaked river → **FIXED** by moving the dispose ABOVE the early-return; **(LOW)** the T8 rebuild call site fully swallowed exceptions, masking the very invisible-brigade failure D132 fixes → **FIXED** to `console.warn` (a real failure now surfaces + a probe's console gate catches it; the phase still advances). A **completeness self-check** then confirmed EVERY builder `fld3dRebuildPhaseScene` calls now self-disposes (terrain via `_phaseScene`, markers/beacon tracked, units/officers/supply/arms self-dispose, flags idempotent, eng + water now self-dispose) → the leak surface is fully closed. (The hunt's automated verify+critic legs were env-blocked — a hung 6th finder + an OOM on the 8 GB Mac during the focused re-run — so they were substituted by inline code-verification of all 4 findings + a standalone completeness-critic agent + the empirical re-vet, the D124/D128 substitution precedent.)
- **Re-vet after the fixes (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK`; `probe-phased-3d` **64/64** (all 4 battles, every phase: coverage=1 · 0 orphans · groupsChildren==units · the beacon re-seats on the new objective at every phase · woods rebuilt per sector · non-blank render · 0 pageerrors · 0 texture warnings) — re-confirmed AFTER the T13 fixes; `probe-presets` **26/26** re-confirmed post-fix; `probe-phased-ab` **20/20 0 diffs**; `probe-atmospherics` 18/18 · `probe-weather` 30/30 · `probe-render-richness` 30/30 · `probe-audio-ambience` 22/22 · `probe-flags` 36/36 · `probe-tactical-visuals` 10/10 (the atmospheric/flag stack coexists); `bootprobe` realErrors:[]; `t1probe` ok; `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`; VISUALLY CONFIRMED (the captured Burnside's-Bridge phase-2 shot now shows the fresh sector + visible brigades + the re-seated beacon under T17's dawn fog — previously empty). `wcag-auditor` N/A by construction (no new DOM/UI — only a camera re-aim + 3D mesh rebuild). **ENV:** `probe-engineering`/`probe-engineering-corps` are `waitUntil:'load'` → blocked by the documented THREE-CDN headless load-stall (D97/D110), NOT a regression — the eng/water 3D builders are instead exercised by the `domcontentloaded` `probe-phased-3d` (which drives them through 12 phase advances) + `probe-tactical-visuals`. **Committed + pushed only after the full empirical gate passed.** (Reversible; non-negotiables honored: D74 combat byte-identity proven 3 ways · the empirical-vet-before-push gate · base.html untouched.)

## D131 — PHASE H (graphics "make it come to life") · H1b: **FLAGS & INSIGNIA DEEPENING** — citation-grade battle-aware flag selection + corrected Kearny-patch corps-badge SHAPES (date-gated to the 1863 fields) drawn on the 2D/3D unit markers + the selected-brigade HUD, DEEPENING the existing `src/tactical/T10-flags.js` — [CLAUDE] (overnight run, 2026-06-25)
The H1b increment per `.tmp/phase-h-design.md` item 5 + memory `civilwar-idea-brigade-battle-flags`. **`T10-flags.js` ALREADY existed** (a basic flag/insignia layer shipped earlier) → H1b DEEPENS it (the D91 deepen-not-replace ethic); it does NOT add a new module. Recon of the prior code found **three real defects, all fixed here:** (1) the 2D path computed an SVG + a `new Image()` it NEVER drew → 2D flags rendered as blank solid-colour rectangles; (2) corps-badge SHAPES were never drawn (only a text label), and the shape MAP was wrong — II Corps was a circle (should be a TREFOIL), V and VI were both a generic "cross"; (3) corps badges showed on ALL battles regardless of date (an anachronism on the 1861-62 fields), and a roman-numeral substring match mis-read "II Corps" as "I Corps".
- **What shipped (the deepening):** (1) **battle-AWARE flag selection** — a new `_FLD_BATTLE_META` table (date/theater/badge-eligibility per scenario) drives the Confederate "default" flag: **First National "Stars and Bars"** at First Bull Run (the ANV battle flag did NOT exist on 21 Jul 1861 — its very creation was prompted by the smoke-confusion of the Stars and Bars with the U.S. flag THAT day) and in the early West (Shiloh), the **ANV battle flag (Southern Cross)** for the mid-war East, the **Hardee pattern** (blue field + white disc) for the native Army of Tennessee, with **Longstreet's transferred Eastern brigades (Hood/Kershaw/Law) flying the ANV flag even at Chickamauga**; (2) **NEW flag patterns drawn for real** (the doc once claimed an unimplemented "hardee") — first-national, hardee, polk (upright St-George cross, distinct from the ANV diagonal saltire), and a US cavalry guidon, all procedural canvas; (3) **corrected Kearny-patch corps-badge SHAPES drawn as glyphs** — I=disc, II=trefoil, III=diamond, V=Maltese (a flared cross pattée), VI=Greek cross (plain, distinct from V), XI=crescent, XII=star — coloured by division (1st=red/2nd=white/3rd=blue) and ALWAYS paired with a text label (CVD-safe: shape + label, never colour alone), with a light parchment **halo** so the shape silhouette reads on any background; (4) the **anachronism GATE** — badges (adopted by Hooker's Circular of 21 Mar 1863) appear ONLY at the Eastern post-Mar-1863 fields (here Chancellorsville + Gettysburg) and NEVER on Bull Run / Malvern Hill / Antietam / Fredericksburg (where the corps existed but wore no badge yet — the HUD names the adoption date there as a teaching beat); (5) a **working 2D draw** (a cached canvas blit, fog-gated) + a deepened **HUD** (the flag thumbnail + the badge swatch + a citation-grade provenance caption).
- **CITATION-GRADE CONTENT (a research+verify Workflow, 25 agents: 6 Opus historian-finders × 3-lens default-refute verifiers + a synthesis):** the verified fact-sheet CONFIRMED the core code and surfaced two refinements I applied — the **Chickamauga ANV-transplant split** (Hood/Kershaw were Longstreet's Eastern divisions, ANV flag) and **Vicksburg** flying a Western pattern (Pemberton's Western-lineage army), not the Eastern ANV cross — plus a doc-citation softening ("General Orders No. 53" → "Hooker's Circular of 21 March 1863"; the surviving primary artifact is a Headquarters AotP circular, the No. 53 label under-attested; the DATE is solid). ≥2 independent reputable sources per flag/badge/date (Encyclopedia Virginia · Smithsonian NMAH · Britannica · confederateflags.org · Tennessee State Museum · crwflags.com FOTW · Washington Times · Gettysburg Flag Works · American Battlefield Trust), anti-Lost-Cause (the battle flag's origin framed factually as a post-Manassas battlefield-recognition device, not romanticized).
- **ARCHITECTURE — combat byte-identical BY CONSTRUCTION (D74):** touches NO combat file. The render fns (`fldDrawFlags` 2D / `fld3dBuildFlags` 3D / `fldFlagHudSelected` HUD) are reached only through the EXISTING T0 `typeof` seams (probes drive the sim headlessly and never call them). Reads sim state READ-ONLY (`u.name/side/x/z/facing/alive/state`, `__FIELD.scenario/fog/units`) + `G.settings`; writes NO sim field, NEVER calls `fldRng`, bumps no save-version. The install block wraps ONLY `fld3dBuildUnits`/`fld3dDispose` (disposal of flag+corps-badge CanvasTextures, + a reinforcement re-skin) via a generic `_fldFlagKeep` that propagates any prior introspection markers (the D130 lesson) — these are NOT the T16/T17/T18/T19 marker-bearing fns, so no chain is shadowed (probe-confirmed the `._atmo`/`._wx` chain stays intact). Diff = `src/tactical/T10-flags.js` (deepened) + `tools/probe-flags.mjs` (new) + the generated HTML. **No combat / data / save file touched** → all 9 battles byte-identical seed-for-seed (probe-presets 26/26).
- **Bug-hunt (the ultracode pattern — 6 diverse-lens Opus finders [byte-identity · fog-leak · history/Lost-Cause · robustness · perf/GPU · a11y/DOM] × 3-skeptic default-refute Opus verify + a completeness critic; 46 agents; critic confidence HIGH).** **1 CONFIRMED finding (3/3) + 6 critic areas → 3 in-scope fixes applied, 1 base bug SURFACED, 2 noted:** **(CONFIRMED, MED — a PRE-EXISTING base T0/T8 bug, git-traced to predate T10 → SURFACED, NOT fixed in this presentation-only milestone):** a phased-battle 3D phase-advance (`_fldBuildPhase`/`_fldAdvancePhase` in the combat-path T8) swaps in new unit ids but never rebuilds the `_u3d` 3D groups → new-phase units get no 3D group + the prior phase's groups orphan; H1b only marginally enlarges the orphaned-mesh footprint by adding CanvasTextures. Fixing it touches the combat path (byte-identity risk) and belongs in a dedicated T0/T8 pass — recommended fix: call `fld3dBuildUnits()`+`fld3dBuildFlags()` from `_fldAdvancePhase` when `mode3d`. **(IN-SCOPE FIXES, all applied + re-vetted):** (a) a **reinforcement re-skin** — the `fld3dBuildUnits` wrapper now re-runs `fld3dBuildFlags` after the base rebuild so REINFORCEMENT arrivals (Bull Run's late brigades) also fly the proper textured flag + badge instead of the base plain-colour plane; (b) a **badge silhouette halo** — a dark 3rd-division-blue badge on the dark HUD read poorly → a light parchment halo now lifts every badge shape off any background (the CVD shape cue stays legible); (c) **fog-gate probe coverage** — the most-emphasized contract was untested → probe-flags now exercises a fog scene (fog conceals ≥1 enemy; every hidden enemy's group is invisible so its flag + badge never betray it) + a static-scan of the 2D guard. **NOTED for future H1b polish (niceties, not bugs):** batteries fly a brigade flag (artillery carried guidons); the US-guidon / CS-cavalry asymmetry. The robustness / XSS / a11y lenses were clean (no raw unit name is injected into the HUD; all dict iterations are `hasOwnProperty`-guarded; the roman parse + division lookups are integer-keyed).
- **Vetting (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓`; `probe-flags` **36/36** (battle-aware flags · the date gate · corrected shapes II=trefoil + V/VI distinct · roman parse never mis-reads II→I · division colours · CS-never-badged · the Chickamauga split + Vicksburg Western flag · CVD-safe HUD + the Mar-1863 teaching note · live 3D textured flags + badge meshes on Gettysburg · the Antietam gate builds NO badge mesh · **fog-of-war: no flag/badge betrays a hidden enemy** · byte-identity: sim seed + fields invariant across a 200-frame burst · 0 tex warnings · 0 pe); `probe-presets` **26/26** (byte-identity → **combat byte-identical**, re-confirmed post-fix); `probe-atmospherics` **18/18** (T16) / `probe-weather` **30/30** (T17) / `probe-render-richness` **30/30** (T18) / `probe-audio-ambience` **22/22** (T19) — all coexist; `probe-tactical-visuals` **10/10** (0 texture warnings); `bootprobe` realErrors:[]; `t1probe` 14/14; `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`. `wcag-auditor` AA — the new HUD DOM passes by construction (every text colour clears 4.5:1 by a wide margin, the img border clears 3:1, alt text present, CVD shape+label) → no changes. **NEXT in H:** H1/H2 PD imagery + reenactment footage (**SURFACE first** — external assets / network / IP / single-file stress; an external-audio-file layer is the same kind of fork → SURFACE first) → a per-phase-weather candidate (Antietam dawn-fog→afternoon via `_fldPhaseView`, a combat-path file → prove byte-identity) → and the discovered **phased-3D base bug** as a dedicated base-engine fix. **Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT battles LAST** (Aaron's locked queue). (Reversible; non-negotiables honored — D74 no sim write + the empirical-vet-before-push gate + D91 deepen-not-replace + citation-grade anti-Lost-Cause content.)

## D130 — PHASE H (graphics "make it come to life") · H4: **BATTLE-AMBIENCE AUDIO DEEPENING** — a default-OFF, opt-in, stereo-spatialized procedural battle-soundscape bed (rolling-fire wash + gated cannonade sub-bass + fog-gated located cannon/musket reports) UNDER the existing T9 din+SFX — [CLAUDE] (overnight run, 2026-06-25)
The H4 increment per `.tmp/phase-h-design.md` item 4 + the D91 "continue audio incrementally" decision. The base.html `dinSet` din already gives a MONO intensity bed (rumble + cannon thump + musket crackle), and T9-audio.js (D77) bridges it into the modern tactical engine. H4 DEEPENS the subsystem with a NEW layer — it does NOT replace T9. **Sub-choice decided-&-logged:** a NEW `src/tactical/T19-battle-ambience.js` module (the T16/T17/T18 wrap-by-assignment precedent) rather than editing T9 in place — lowest-risk, idiomatic, keeps the SFX bridge intact. PROCEDURAL WebAudio synthesis only ($0 / single-file — no external sound files; the linked-asset audio fork remains un-started + would be SURFACED first).
- **What shipped:** new `src/tactical/T19-battle-ambience.js` — a spatialized "great-battle" bed layered UNDER T9's din+SFX, pairing 1:1 with the T16 gunsmoke (sound comes from where the smoke rises): (1) a continuous **rolling-fire wash** (looping band-passed noise + a slow swell-LFO tremolo, gain tracking battle intensity — the "one continuous roar"); (2) a **cannonade sub-bass rumble** (looping low-passed brown noise, gated up by the count of brigades actually working their guns); (3) **located cannon/musket reports** — a cannon = sharp crack + body thump + rolling reverberant tail; a musket = a filtered-noise volley "rip" — each panned in stereo by the firing brigade's field-x (side-corrected to the camera end) and **fog-gated** (a hidden foe makes no located sound). Discoverable via a new accessible "Battle ambience" On/Off row appended to T9's audio panel; one-time captioned through T9's accessible caption.
- **ARCHITECTURE — combat byte-identical BY CONSTRUCTION (D74):** touches NO combat file. WRAPS `fld3dRender` / `fld2dDraw` (drive the per-frame decision tick, throttled) / `fldExit` (dispose) / T9's `_fldAudioPanelRows` (append the toggle row) BY ASSIGNMENT — with a generic `keep()` helper that propagates EVERY prior introspection marker (`._atmo`/`._wx`/`._t9audio`/T18's) onto each new wrapper so the sibling probes still see the chain (a regression the no-regression sweep caught mid-build — fixed before commit). OWNS a separate AudioContext (the AUDIO_SCORE / SOUND one-context-per-subsystem convention). Reads ONLY render + read-only sim fields (`u._artFlash`/`u.targetId`/`u.ammo`/`u.state`/`u.x`/`u.id`/`u.side`/`u.alive`, `__FIELD.phase/paused/fog/units`) + `G.settings`; writes NO sim field, NEVER calls `fldRng` (no randomness reaches the sim — audio texture uses `Math.random` like base.html's din), no `_SAVE_VER` (the `battleAmbience` flag is lazy-defaulted). The SCHEDULING DECISIONS (intensity, fog-gate, pan) are recorded in a `FLDAMB` state object whether or not a real audio device exists → fully testable headless. Diff = `src/tactical/T19-battle-ambience.js` (new) + `src/00-manifest.json` (T19 after T18) + `tools/probe-audio-ambience.mjs` (new) + the generated HTML. Proven by `probe-presets` 26/26 byte-identity AND a per-pure-render-window sim-seed-invariance assertion (240× `fldAmbUpdate`, seed unmoved).
- **ACCESSIBILITY / PERF (Intel UHD-617 floor):** **DEFAULT-OFF** (`G.settings.battleAmbience !== true` → zero sound, never built — the byte-identical-mix default), **fully suppressed under `reduceMotion`** (the calm default — the T16 convention), volume scaled by the existing `battleLoud` (off→silent) AND the master capped low (≤0.20, under the din's 0.26) so it never overwhelms the cues it sits under ("never jarring"). Located reports defer until the AudioContext is genuinely running (no first-gesture stampede); the bed self-silences on the 'over' screen (no idle-graph drain); source-voices node-pool bounded + all nodes disposed + context suspended on `fldExit`. CVD-safe (no colour-encoded info); the toggle row reuses T9's AA-vetted segmented-button markup (keyboard / focus / aria-pressed) → `wcag-auditor` N/A by construction.
- **Bug-hunt (the ultracode pattern — 6 diverse-lens Opus finders [byte-identity · webaudio-leaks · fog-leak · edge-robust · a11y-ux · lifecycle] × 3-skeptic default-refute Opus verify + a completeness critic; 19 agents; critic confidence HIGH; "NO byte-identity violation, NO fog leak, marker-chain correct").** **1 confirmed + 2 critic-surfaced real edge-bugs + 1 LOW refinement, ALL FIXED:** **(CONFIRMED, MED, 3/3)** `fldAmbCaptionOnce` burned the one-shot `captioned` flag BEFORE the caption rendered — but T9's `_fldAudioCaption` no-ops when captions are off (the default), so a user enabling captions mid-battle would never see the cue the layer advertises → the flag is now consumed ONLY inside the captions-on branch (re-checked cheaply each tick until shown). **(CRITIC, MED)** the six lenses only saw the probe's always-running context; in the real game the context is born SUSPENDED until a gesture, and located reports kept scheduling against the frozen clock → a stacked "stampede" the instant audio resumed → `fldAmbReports` now gates on `ctx.state === "running"`. **(CRITIC, LOW)** the live WebAudio graph idled indefinitely on the post-battle 'over' screen (master never zeroed there) → `fldAmbUpdate` now silences on `phase === "over"`. **(CRITIC, LOW)** StereoPanner nodes shared the source bag, diluting the documented voice cap + a dead `.stop()` per dispose → panners moved to a separate `pans` pool (cap counts audible voices only; panners disconnect-only). The lifecycle "stale per-phase state" finding was REFUTED 3/3 (singleton `__FIELD`; a negative men-delta clamps to 0; cooldowns expire across the interphase card — benign). Probe strengthened 19→22 steps (the suspended-ctx gate, the 'over'-silence, the panner-pool disposal).
- **Vetting (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK`; `probe-audio-ambience` **22/22** (module + 4 wrappers · opt-in activates + beds built + located reports scheduled · pan ∈ [-1,1] · separate panner pool · suspended-ctx defers (0 reports/0 errors) · 'over' self-silence · sim-seed INVARIANT across 240 pure audio ticks · `errN===0` · accessible panel row · DEFAULT-OFF silent · reduceMotion suppressed · `battleLoud="off"` master silent · fog-gated 5→0 with lift-control 3 · `fldExit` disposes beds+pools+ref · 0 pe); `probe-presets` **26/26** (byte-identity → combat byte-identical, re-confirmed post-fix); `probe-atmospherics` **18/18** (T16 + marker chain) / `probe-weather` **30/30** (T17) / `probe-render-richness` **30/30** (T18) — all coexist; `probe-tactical-visuals` **10/10** (0 texture warnings); `bootprobe` realErrors:[]; `t1probe` 14/14; `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`. (Audio can't be screenshotted — the probe verifies the WebAudio graph builds + every scheduling decision fires correctly; this is the visual-confirmation analog for a sound layer.) **NEXT in H:** H1b flags/insignia polish (extend `T10-flags.js`) → H1/H2 PD imagery + reenactment footage (**SURFACE first** — external assets / network / single-file stress) → a per-phase-weather candidate (Antietam dawn-fog→afternoon via `_fldPhaseView`, a combat-path file → prove byte-identity). **Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT battles LAST** (Aaron's locked queue). (Reversible; non-negotiables honored — D74 no sim write + the empirical-vet-before-push gate + D91 deepen-not-replace.)

## D129 — PHASE H (graphics "make it come to life") · H3-i3: **TERRAIN & UNIT RENDER RICHNESS** — procedural ground texture + woods wind-sway + banner cloth ripple + selection/march polish + casualty fade — [CLAUDE] (overnight run, 2026-06-24)
The next Phase-H increment after H3-i2 (D128), per `.tmp/phase-h-design.md` item 3. A NEW presentation-only module makes the battlefield itself feel alive — the ground grows real texture, the woods sway, the colours ripple, marching columns bob, and a fallen brigade fades from the field instead of snapping out. Continues leading H with the code-only / single-file-safe / offline procedural wins before the external PD-imagery/footage layer (H1/H2, still to be SURFACED before starting).
- **What shipped:** new `src/tactical/T18-render-richness.js` — (1) **3D ground vertex-colour enrichment** (procedural grass mottle + dry-ochre / damp-lush / bare-earth patches + tilled crop-row corduroy in a "cultivated" mask — positions untouched, colours only); (2) **woods wind-sway** (per-cone tilt-about-base on the InstancedMesh); (3) **banner cloth ripple** (per-unit flag twist/dip, ties H1b flags); (4) **selection-ring pulse** + a **march bob** for moving brigades; (5) a **casualty fade-out** on death (the group settles into the ground + fades over ~0.85s). Plus 2D-canvas equivalents (a cached soft field-grain, a pulsing selection halo, the same casualty fade).
- **ARCHITECTURE — combat byte-identical BY CONSTRUCTION (D74):** touches NO combat file. WRAPS `fld3dBuildTerrain` (enrich + capture) / `fld3dRender` (frame hook) / `fld3dSyncUnit` (per-unit polish) / `fld2dDraw` (2D) / `fldExit` (dispose) BY ASSIGNMENT (the T9/T16/T17 seam; propagates the `._wx`/`._atmo` markers so the wrapper chain stays introspectable). All variation is **analytic value-noise + wall-clock sines — NEVER `fldRng`**; reads ONLY render state + read-only sim fields (`u.x`/`u.z`/`u.alive`/`u.state`/`u.id`, `__FIELD.sel`); writes NO sim field, no `_SAVE_VER`. The ground enrichment recolors VERTICES only — units seat on the analytic `fldTerrainH`, not the mesh, so they never float/sink. **THE BYTE-IDENTICAL DEFAULT:** `renderRich="off"` (or a fresh launch with it set) leaves the un-enriched ground + unswayed woods + flat flags. Diff = `src/tactical/T18-render-richness.js` (new) + `src/00-manifest.json` (T18 after T17) + `tools/probe-render-richness.mjs` (new) + the generated HTML. **No combat/data/save file touched** — proven by `probe-presets` 26/26 byte-identity AND a new per-burst sim-field-invariance probe assertion (x/z/men/morale/facing/formation unchanged across 200 render frames).
- **ACCESSIBILITY / PERF (Intel UHD-617 floor):** every MOTION effect (woods sway, flag ripple, selection pulse, march bob, casualty fade) is suppressed under `reduceMotion` — the STATIC ground texture stays (faithful detail, not motion; the T17 precedent). `renderRich="off"` disables the whole layer (motion reverts mid-battle; the 3D ground tint reverts on the next launch — the T16/T17 apply-time pattern, documented). The woods sway recomputes each instance matrix from a cached base (no drift); `fldLow()` trims sway amplitude + grain cell size; the **2D field-grain is cached to an offscreen canvas** (built once per field/scale, blitted with one `drawImage` — no per-frame fillrate on the weakest 2D-fallback GPU). No new draw call/shader → no `gl_PointSize` concern. CVD-safe (texture carries no colour-encoded info); no new DOM/text → `wcag-auditor` N/A by construction.
- **Bug-hunt (the ultracode pattern — 6 diverse-lens Opus finders [byte-identity · render-math · perf · a11y-gating · edge-robust · lifecycle] × 3-skeptic default-refute Opus verify + a completeness critic; 37 agents; critic confidence HIGH).** **1 substantive bug + 7 real LOWs, ALL FIXED:** **(the one substantive defect, MED/HIGH across 4 lenses, 3/3 votes)** the casualty-fade `g.position.y -= (1-rec.fade)*5` was CUMULATIVE — the base `fld3dSyncUnit` early-returns for a dead unit and never re-seats its Y, so the per-frame decrement compounded to ~125 yards of sink (frame-rate dependent), plunging the fading brigade through the terrain instead of the intended ~5yd settle → **rewritten ABSOLUTE** (`g.position.y = fldTerrainH(u.x,u.z)+4 - (1-f)*5`), bounded + frame-rate-independent (probe now asserts `maxSink ≤ 8`; measured 4.96). Pure visual glitch — no sim write, D74 intact (the finders + critic confirmed). **(LOW, real)** the fade had no visibility guard → a fog-hidden enemy that died popped into view as a fading block (a fog-of-war leak) → guarded on `rec.wasVis` (the base's last-living-frame visibility) + a white-box probe test. **(critic-found LOWs)** a SELECTED unit that died kept its ring at full opacity over the fading ghost → the ring now fades with the corpse; the fade-end `transparent=false` flip corrupted the textured T10 flag's alpha (latent) → `_rrSetGroupOpacity` never flips the flag/ring transparent flag; the 2D grain was re-filled (~1300 `fillRect`s) every frame → offscreen-canvas cache; `renderRich="off"` toggled mid-battle didn't revert the motion effects → the early-return was removed so the motion-off reset paths run; the probe only proved seed-stability (not sim-field invariance) → added the x/z/men/morale/facing snapshot; the empty-catch wrappers swallowed per-unit throws silently → a counted + once-warned `FLDRR_S.errN` the probe asserts is 0. **Probe strengthened 24→30 steps.**
- **Vetting (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK`; `probe-render-richness` **30/30** (module + 5 wrappers + chain markers · ground enriched [meanAbsDelta 0.0178, 92% of vertices changed] + positions untouched · woods sway/reset · flag ripple/reset · selection pulse · casualty fade visible+opacity-in-(0,1)+BOUNDED-sink+fog-ghost-guarded+hidden-when-done · sim seed + sim-field INVARIANT · `errN===0` · `renderRich="off"` un-enriched/unswayed/flat · reduceMotion keeps texture suppresses motion · 2D paints, 0 pe); `probe-presets` **26/26** (byte-identity → combat byte-identical, re-confirmed post-fix); `probe-atmospherics` **18/18** (T16 coexists); `probe-weather` **30/30** (T17 coexists); `probe-tactical-visuals` **10/10** (all 9 battles render, 0 texture warnings); `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`; `bootprobe` realErrors:[]; `t1probe` 0 fails/0 pe. VISUALLY CONFIRMED (the field reads as real terrain with patches + tilled stripes; woods sway, flags ripple in the live battles). **NEXT in H3/H4:** H4 audio deepening → H1b flags/insignia polish → H1/H2 PD imagery + reenactment footage (**SURFACE first** — external assets / network / single-file stress). **Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT battles LAST** (Aaron's locked queue). (Reversible; non-negotiables honored — D74 no sim write + the empirical-vet-before-push gate.)

## D128 — PHASE H (graphics "make it come to life") · H3-i2: **WEATHER + TIME-OF-DAY ATMOSPHERE** — procedural sky/light re-tint + rain/snow precipitation per a citation-grade per-battle weather hint — [CLAUDE] (overnight run, 2026-06-24)
The natural successor to H3-i1 (D127). A NEW presentation-only module gives each battle a sky and light that match its history. Designed + data-scoped at `.tmp/phase-h-design.md`. Continues leading Phase H with the code-only / single-file-safe / offline procedural wins before the external PD-imagery/footage layer (H1/H2, still to be SURFACED before starting).
- **What shipped:** new `src/tactical/T17-weather.js` — re-tints the 3D `scene.background` / `scene.fog` / sun (directional) / hemisphere AND washes the 2D canvas to a resolved `{sky, time}` palette (sky ∈ clear/overcast/rain/fog/haze/snow; time ∈ dawn/morning/midday/afternoon/dusk), plus optional procedural **rain/snow precipitation** (a second `THREE.Points` cloud reusing the T16 shader pattern) and a faint 2D precip hatch. A one-time accessible **weather note** is announced at battle start via the existing role=status `fldScenarioBanner`. **Marquee visible win: Antietam's dawn Cornfield ground-fog** (the one genuinely non-clear sky in the roster); the rest carry faithful time-of-day light (warm afternoons, cool mornings).
- **ARCHITECTURE — combat byte-identical BY CONSTRUCTION (D74):** touches NO combat file. WRAPS `fld3dInit` (re-tint) / `fld3dRender` (precip + note) / `fld2dDraw` (wash + note) / `fldExit` (dispose) BY ASSIGNMENT (the T9/T16 seam; propagates T16's `._atmo` marker so the wrapper chain stays introspectable). Reads ONLY the scenario's structured `weather` hint (`_scenTop.weather` for multi-phase, else `scenData.weather`) + render state; its OWN LCG for the precip drift — **NEVER `fldRng`** (probe-asserted: the sim seed is untouched across a synchronous wx render loop); no sim write, no `_SAVE_VER`. **THE BYTE-IDENTICAL DEFAULT:** a NO weather hint — or a `clear`/`midday` hint — resolves to `null` and applies ZERO change (every currently-shipped look is preserved until a battle opts in). Diff = `src/tactical/T17-weather.js` (new) + `src/00-manifest.json` (T17 after T16) + the 9 `data/*.json` weather blocks + `tools/probe-weather.mjs` (new) + the generated HTML. **No combat/save file touched.**
- **THE CITATION-GRADE DATA (research+verify Workflow, 18 agents: 9 Opus historians × 9 default-refute Opus verifiers):** each battle's documented modeled-window weather, ≥2 real sources, anti-fabrication. The verifiers earned their keep — they REFUSED to fabricate drama: Gettysburg `haze→clear` (Rev.
