Jacobs' instrument log: bright, 4/10 cumulus at the 2pm Pickett's-Charge window); Fredericksburg `fog→clear/afternoon` (the Rappahannock fog LIFTED ~10am, before the noon-to-dark assault — the note still teaches the morning fog that hid the crossing); Shiloh `clear/morning` (the famous torrential rain was OVERNIGHT, outside the modeled day); Vicksburg removed a misattributed Theodore Lyman source (an Army-of-the-Potomac aide, never at Vicksburg). **The honest finding: the "fog of war" at most of these battles was POWDER-SMOKE (already shipped via T16), not weather** — so the sky was usually clear; only Antietam's dawn fog is a real non-clear sky. Final hints: bullrun1 clear/afternoon · malvernHill clear/afternoon(Inferred) · **antietam fog/dawn** · fredericksburg clear/afternoon · chancellorsville clear/morning · gettysburg clear/afternoon · shiloh clear/morning · vicksburg clear/midday(a no-op) · chickamauga clear/afternoon.
- **ACCESSIBILITY / PERF (Intel UHD-617 floor):** the static sky/light TINT is faithful + motion-free → it applies under `reduceMotion`; only the PRECIPITATION (motion) is suppressed under `reduceMotion` (probe-verified: tint kept, no `wxPrecip` cloud). `G.settings.weather==="off"` disables the whole layer → the clear default. Fog far-plane held **≥1500** in EVERY palette (incl. the time blends) so the objective never vanishes (probe-asserted). One `THREE.Points` precip cloud (single draw call) + `fldLow()` budget (520/200) + a `gl_PointSize` clamp to the driver `ALIASED_POINT_SIZE_RANGE` max. Neutral atmospheric wash (no colour-encoded info → CVD-safe); no new DOM/text/interactive surface (the note reuses the AA-vetted `fldScenarioBanner`) → `wcag-auditor` N/A by construction.
- **DECIDED-&-LOGGED (D61) — Antietam's battle-level dawn-fog:** the fog/dawn hint is applied to ALL THREE Antietam phases (Cornfield → Bloody Lane → Burnside's Bridge), though historically the ground fog opened the Cornfield (Phase 1) and burned off by the later phases. Kept as the iconic, verified BATTLE SIGNATURE (the modeled engagement opens in the dawn mist, and the note teaches "it thinned as the sun rose"); per-phase weather would touch the T8 combat-path phase view (`_fldPhaseView`) and is logged as a clean future H3-i3 enhancement, NOT this milestone. The bug-hunt verifiers SPLIT on this (no majority-confirm) → a simplification, not a defect.
- **Adversarial bug-hunt** (6 diverse-lens Opus finders [byte-identity/RNG · lifecycle/GPU-leak · WebGL1/r128 shader · a11y/perf/fog-readability · robustness/edge · effect-correctness+history+probe-rigor] × 3-skeptic default-refute Opus verify + a completeness critic; the critic leg STALLED after the finder+verify phases completed — substituted by a rigorous INLINE completeness pass, the D124 precedent). **8 findings; 1 HIGH confirmed, fixed; the rest LOW/refuted, addressed:** **(HIGH, 3/3 confirmed)** the atmosphere note re-fired EVERY render frame — `_wxLaunchKey` included `__FIELD.seed`, which `fldRng` MUTATES every sim tick → the per-launch key changed each frame → the "noted" flag reset → banner/SR-status spam. **FIXED:** key the note on the STABLE `__FIELD.scenario` (fldExit resets it for a relaunch) + a new probe assertion that renders sim-ticking frames and asserts EXACTLY ONE weather banner. **(LOW, real)** `FLDWX.SKY["constructor"/"__proto__"]` resolves an inherited `Object.prototype` member (truthy) → bypassed the `!FLDWX.SKY[sky]` guard → a palette with no `.bg`. **FIXED:** `Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(...)` validation + a malformed-input probe assertion. **(LOW/refuted, locked anyway)** precip-dispose-on-`fldExit` + malformed-input safety were un-probed → added assertions; the `fld3dDispose`-wrap finding was refuted 3/3 (T17's `fldExit` wrapper runs `fldWxDispose` BEFORE the engine teardown; the scene-traverse disposes the mesh regardless; a stale ref self-heals on next build). A real **probe test-pollution bug** (the synth scenes mutated the SHARED `GAME_DATA` weather object, clobbering a later scene's pristine hint) was caught by the note-once assertion and fixed (inject onto a shallow copy).
- **Vetting (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK`; `probe-weather` **30/30**, 0 pe, 0 texture warnings (the byte-identical default · each sky palette applies · fog far ≥1500 on the real fog battle · rain/snow `wxPrecip` built + DISPOSED on exit · `reduceMotion` suppresses precip but keeps the tint · the once-per-launch note · malformed-input safety · the driver `gl_PointSize` clamp · the sim seed UNCHANGED across the wx render loop); `probe-presets` **26/26** (9-baseline byte-identity → **combat byte-identical**, seed-for-seed); `probe-atmospherics` **18/18** (T16 + T17 coexist; the transitive `._atmo` marker holds); `probe-tactical-visuals` **10/10** (the now-tinted battles render with beacons seated, 0 texture warnings, 0 pe); `bootprobe` realErrors:[]; `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`; `t1probe` ok; broad no-regression GREEN in isolation (field/fog/motion/arms/officers/csplayer/tactical-roster/command/camp/engineering-corps/accessibility — an initial batched sweep threw spurious `steps:0` launch failures from 8 GB-Mac memory pressure under the concurrent sweep+bug-hunt; re-running each in isolation was GREEN). **VISUALLY CONFIRMED:** Antietam's pale warm dawn ground-fog (distant brigades fading into the mist, the objective still readable); the grey rain field with falling streaks. Committed + pushed. **NEXT in H3 = i3 terrain/unit render richness** (ground texture variation, wind-sway, banner ripple, casualty fade; per-phase weather is a candidate sub-item); then H4 audio → H1b flags → H1/H2 PD imagery+footage (SURFACE first). Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT battles LAST. (Reversible; non-negotiables honored — D74 byte-identity, citation-grade anti-Lost-Cause history, the empirical-vet-before-push gate.)

## D127 — PHASE H (graphics "make it come to life") · H3-i1: **BATTLEFIELD GUNSMOKE ATMOSPHERICS** — procedural black-powder smoke / artillery muzzle-belch / impact dust on the real-time tactical engine (the period "fog of war") — [CLAUDE] (overnight run, 2026-06-24)
**FORK CLEARED (AskUserQuestion):** Phase E completed (E1–E4, D126); the next pillar was open (F/I/H/J). Aaron chose **Phase H**. Sub-choice decided-&-logged: lead H with the **code-only / single-file-safe / offline procedural** wins (no external-asset licensing/IP risk; honors D30.8) before the heavy PD-imagery/footage ingestion (H1/H2, which I'll SURFACE before starting — the one external-asset, network-touching item). H3-i1 = the marquee battlefield-atmosphere increment. (Full ordered H roadmap + the H3-i2 weather/time-of-day design at `.tmp/phase-h-design.md`.)
- **What shipped:** new `src/tactical/T16-atmospherics.js` — procedural gunsmoke that billows from firing lines + artillery, accumulates into a sector pall, drifts on a per-battle wind, and slowly clears; artillery muzzle-belch; impact dust where rounds strike. Renders in BOTH the 2D fallback canvas (cached soft-sprite stamps) and the live 3D scene (one `THREE.Points` cloud + a tiny per-particle alpha/size/colour shader). **Teaching tie-in:** black-powder smoke literally WAS the period's fog of war (the reason real commanders lost sight of their own lines).
- **ARCHITECTURE — combat byte-identical BY CONSTRUCTION (D74):** touches NO combat file. WRAPS `fld2dDraw` / `fld3dRender` / `fldExit` by ASSIGNMENT (the T9-audio seam pattern). Reads firing/impact state READ-ONLY (`u.targetId`/`u.ammo`/`u._artFlash`/`u.underFire`/`u.facing`/`u.state`/`u.x`/`u.z`). Uses its OWN private LCG (`A.rs`) — **NEVER `fldRng`** (the sim seed is provably untouched — probe-asserted across 240 synchronous spawn steps), no sim write, no `_SAVE_VER`. The byte-identity gate (`probe-presets`, 9 baselines) runs headless so this layer never even executes there; the focused probe adds the RNG-isolation + a controlled fog-gate test to lock the claims. Diff = `src/tactical/T16-atmospherics.js` (new) + `src/00-manifest.json` (T16 after T15) + `tools/probe-atmospherics.mjs` (new) + the generated HTML. **NO combat/data/save file touched.**
- **ACCESSIBILITY / PERF (Intel UHD-617 floor):** fully suppressed under `reduceMotion` AND `G.settings.atmospherics === "off"` (probe-verified 0 particles); particle budget capped (200 high / 84 low via `fldLow()`); ONE `THREE.Points` cloud (single draw call) + a `gl_PointSize` clamp to the driver's `ALIASED_POINT_SIZE_RANGE` max (so near-camera puffs render uniformly on Intel integrated GPUs); fog-aware (no smoke spawns for an enemy the player has not scouted → never betrays a hidden position). Neutral grayscale cue (no colour-encoded info → CVD-safe); no new DOM/text/interactive surface → `wcag-auditor` N/A by construction.
- **Adversarial bug-hunt** (6 diverse-lens Opus finders [byte-identity/sim-write · lifecycle/GPU-leak · WebGL1/r128 shader portability · a11y/fog-leak/perf · robustness/edge · effect-correctness+probe-rigor] × default-refute Opus verify + a completeness critic; **14 agents; invariantsHold = TRUE, HIGH confidence**; the critic independently confirmed no sim/RNG write, no GPU leak across the reinforcement rebuild, and the wrapper ordering vs T9-audio is sound). **2 confirmed — both LOW — both FIXED:** **(LOW)** the fog-leak gate had ZERO probe coverage (every scene ran fog-OFF; claim asserted only in prose) — the gate CODE was already correct (structurally identical to the shipping T0:1551/1820 fog-hide pattern) → added a geometry-independent **controlled fog-gate test** (silence every unit, force ONLY hidden enemies to "fire" → 0 smoke under fog, 21 with fog lifted = the control) so a dropped/inverted `!fldVisible` term is now caught. **(LOW)** 3D `gl_PointSize` could exceed the Intel UHD-617 driver cap (near-camera art puffs computed ~924px vs a possible 64–255 cap; cosmetic, degrades gracefully) → added an in-shader `min(uMaxPoint, …)` clamp wired from the driver's reported max + a probe assertion. No HIGH/MED.
- **Vetting (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK`; `probe-atmospherics` **18/18** (spawns 2D+3D; rm/off both suppress to 0; sim seed unchanged across 240 sync spawn steps; the `atmoSmoke` cloud built with 0 texture warnings; the controlled fog-gate test; the `uMaxPoint`=`ALIASED_POINT_SIZE_RANGE` clamp); `probe-presets` **26/26** (9-baseline byte-identity → **combat byte-identical**, seed-for-seed); `probe-tactical-visuals` **10/10** (render-path cross-check across 5 scenarios × 2 renderers, 0 texture warnings, beacons seated — re-confirmed after the shader clamp); `bootprobe` `realErrors:[]` 0 pe; `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`; `t1probe` ok; broad no-regression sweep GREEN (`field`/`fog`/`motion`/`arms`/`officers`/`engineering-corps`/`csplayer`/`tactical-roster`/`accessibility`/`command`/`camp` all pass). **VISUALLY CONFIRMED:** 2D shows a dense musketry pall over the Hornet's Nest with brigades still readable; 3D shows smoke billowing at points of contact. Committed + pushed. **NEXT in H3 = i2 weather + time-of-day** (overcast/rain/fog/dawn-dusk per battle; design + data-scoping at `.tmp/phase-h-design.md`). Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT battles LAST. (Reversible; non-negotiables honored — D74 byte-identity, the empirical-vet-before-push gate.)

## D126 — E3 increment 2: **THE DEDICATED FULL WCAG 2.2 AA AUDIT SWEEP** (every shipped surface — desk tabs · menus · battle HUD · overlays · tactical readouts) — completes E3/S4 accessibility — [CLAUDE] (overnight run, 2026-06-24)
- **What shipped:** the exhaustive per-surface contrast/ARIA/focus/keyboard audit + root-cause fixes that E3-i1 deliberately did NOT attempt (i1 = the modes + the var-driven core; i2 = the inline-hex sweep). The **E3 checklist box now ticks.** Pure presentation/markup — **byte-identical combat BY CONSTRUCTION (D74)** (`probe-presets` 9-baseline byte-identity GREEN; no combat/RNG/save/`_SAVE_VER` touched). Diff = 29 src files + `tools/probe-accessibility.mjs`.
- **TWO KEYSTONE TOKEN REDEFINES (the highest-leverage move).** Both `--rule #8a7350` (3.5–4.4:1 as the standard secondary-label, used ~40× across base.html classes + module-inline `color:var(--rule)` headings) AND `--blood-lt #a83d33` (2.5–3.0:1 — the FIRE order button `.obtn.fire`, `#log .e.hit`, `.tagn`, `.verdict.lose`) are **TEXT-ONLY** in base.html, so an **always-on injected `:root{--rule:#a89066;--blood-lt:#d8745c}`** (in `src/97-accessibility.js` `_a11yCss`, applied at boot) fixes EVERY text use at once — sidestepping the inline-specificity problem no class override can beat, and fixing FROZEN base.html classes without editing base.html. `#a89066` is the exact tone base.html already uses for the same fix in its loot/readability cards. **LIGHT-MENU SAFE (verified):** the `.gn-paper` broadsheet defines its own `.gn-col-head{#5c3c18}`/masthead classes and references NEITHER token, so the redefine cannot touch the only light surface; HC mode re-redefines both at higher specificity so it still wins. `--rule` also colours ~17 decorative dark-surface borders/scrollbar/divider — harmlessly recoloured a touch lighter (contrast-safe).
- **SYSTEMIC + PER-SITE FIXES (orchestrated by a 28-agent audit Workflow — 13 file-batch finders × adversarial default-refute verify × synthesis × completeness critic → 127 confirmed AA violations, applied by me + 4 disjoint-file Sonnet editors):** the always-on block also adds a **universal `:focus-visible` fallback ring** (for bare/custom controls the base class rings miss) + a **`.upg[aria-pressed="true"]:focus-visible` !important override** (the inline pressed outline hid the focus delta) + makes the base `#toast` a polite `role=status` region (4.1.3). **HC per-surface extensions** in `_a11yCss` reach the surfaces i1 missed (desk-tab body `#wdContent`/`#wdTabs`, the tactical HUD `#fldHud`/`#fldBar`/`#fldRoot`, sheet-dialog headings, control borders) — dark-scoped so the light menu stays untouched. **~50 module-inline colour fixes** canonicalised the failing hardcoded status palette (green `#4a6b3a`→`#639452`/`#6a9a58`/`#6f9e5a`; red `#9c3b2e`→`#d07060`/`#d06862`/`#d8745c`/`#da6a5a`; fills→3:1 tones; rarity `#7a5cff`→`#9a86f0`/`#8a8276`→`#9a9184`) + **~16 ARIA/focus/keyboard fixes** (aria-pressed on the desk-tab toolbar [chosen over a 18-tab role=tablist to avoid an unimplemented roving-tabindex expectation], lever/wildcard/prep-order/blockade toggles; aria-expanded+controls on every disclosure; the tutorial `#tutTitle` focus ring; the help "?" aria-label; the tactical-help modal background-inert + focus-return-to-trigger; the T0 speed button + T6 drawer-speed value-in-name; the T0 2D unit side-letter glyph got a dark stroke-halo lifting it to ~15.7:1; a 2.5.8 target-size bump on the save-slot action buttons).
- **A real compositing bug the audit's verifier MISSED, caught by self-review + the bug-hunt:** CSS `opacity` composites multiplicatively down the subtree, so the plan's "`opacity:1` on the value span" did NOT escape the parent row's `opacity:.85` — the status word still rendered at .85 (~3.6–4.0:1). Fixed PROPERLY by moving the dimming to the LABEL span only in **`_brgBar` (85), `_morMeter` (33), and the blockade `meter()` (60)**; the disabled weapon/artillery cards likewise dim only an inner descriptive wrapper, the unavailable REASON rendering as a full-opacity sibling.
- **Adversarial bug-hunt (6 diverse-lens Opus finders × default-refute verify × completeness critic; 11 agents) → 3 confirmed + a HIGH critic gap, ALL FIXED + re-probed:** the critic caught that the `_brgBar` opacity fix had NOT propagated to `_morMeter`/blockade `meter()` (even the lifted colours failed at the `.85` row) + two unlifted blockade greens (`_blkRecogStatus` "Courted"/`_blkTightStatus` "Open seas" `#4a6b3a`) + an `aria-hidden`-on-the-focused-trigger issue in the help modal (focus now moves into the dialog BEFORE the background is inerted) + a vacuous probe step (the palette step tested those colours NON-composited). **Then a report-only `wcag-auditor` confirmation + two systematic whole-`src/` contrast scans caught 7 MORE DOM-text misses the audit batches skipped:** `_cmdLeadWord` rating words, `_armRarityCol`/`_artRar` "rare"+"common" rarity LABELS, the T11 `.fld-cb-status.bad` error border, and command's `#d66040` (3.92:1 on the lighter `#2e2816` command-card overlay; a prior wcag comment's 4.51 claim was arithmetically wrong) → all fixed (`#e86840`, matching the L251 precedent). Canvas/3D mesh/flag fills + decorative aria-hidden grade dots (triple-encoded letter+word+colour, D96) correctly held EXEMPT (DOM-AA scope).
- **`tools/probe-accessibility.mjs` strengthened 17→25 steps:** the token-redefine cascade computed on `<html>` + reaching FROZEN base classes (`.title-sub`/`.tagn`), the always-on + HC-extension rules present, the toast live region, a numeric canonical-palette contrast battery, a **RENDER-SAMPLE that instantiates `_brgBar`+`_morMeter` and reads each value span's EFFECTIVE (composited) opacity + ratio** (closes the vacuous-probe gap), the HC desk/HUD black-ground lift, and the HC-must-not-touch-`.gn-paper` guard.
- **Vet (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK`; `probe-accessibility` **25/25**, 0 pe; no-regression — `probe-presets` **26/26** (9-baseline byte-identity → combat byte-identical), `bootprobe` realErrors:[], `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`, `t1probe` 14/14, and ~30 UI/teaching/command/tactical probes (desk/cabinet/decisions/morale/press/command/economy/weapons/cannon/production/engineering/blockade/manpower/victory/divergence/bridge/auto-resolve/codex/glossary/tutorial/playstyle/realism-teaching/afteraction/officers/logistics/camp/oob/field/bullrun/custom-battle-builder/tactical-visuals[10 scenes, 0 textureWarnings]) all PASS, 0 pe. Committed + pushed. (Reversible.) **E3/S4 accessibility is now COMPLETE (i1+i2).**

## D125 — E3 increment 1: **THE ACCESSIBILITY HUB + the 4 a11y modes** (high-contrast · CVD-safe · SR turn/battle narration · dyslexia text) — [CLAUDE] (overnight run, 2026-06-24)
- **What shipped:** the first half of E3 (S4 accessibility). New guarded `src/97-accessibility.js` — a one-stop **Accessibility hub** holding the four dedicated modes the V1 checklist names + a reduced-motion mirror: **(1) HIGH CONTRAST** (overrides the `:root` design tokens → brighter accents/rules/labels + a darker ground + a universal `:focus-visible` ring; the light-parchment main-menu broadsheet, already ~12:1, is left untouched by construction); **(2) CVD-SAFE** (drives the existing base-owned `cbAids` glyph+word redundancy — cbAids is the single source of truth); **(3) SR NARRATION** (a NEW *strategic*-layer `aria-live=polite` region `#a11yLive` + `a11yAnnounce` + a per-turn situation summary — the genuine gap, since only the tactical battle was narrated via `fldAnnounce`; default ON); **(4) DYSLEXIA TEXT** (a clean high-legibility sans stack + relaxed letter/word/line spacing; no external font — $0/single-file). Surfaced from a main-menu "♿ ACCESSIBILITY" button (observer-injected, the canonical no-latch pattern) + a Play Your Way hub hand-off. Boot-load from a `cw_a11y` bundle + a `prefers-contrast: more` auto-detect.
- **ARCHITECTURE — PURE settings/presentation; byte-identical combat BY CONSTRUCTION (D74):** writes ONLY non-combat a11y flags (`a11yContrast`/`a11yDyslexia`/`a11yNarrate`) + the base's OWN `reduceMotion`/`cbAids` mirrors, plus a private `cw_a11y` localStorage bundle (the new flags only). NO combat knob, NO RNG, NO `_SAVE_VER` bump. No combat/tick/resolve/bridge path references any `a11y*` symbol (probe static-scan over `src/tactical/*` + 80/85/86/87). Diff = `src/97-accessibility.js` (new) + `src/00-manifest.json` (97 after 96) + `src/30-president-shell.js` (one guarded `a11yAnnounceTurn(C)` hook in `_pdShowTurnInterstitial`) + `src/95-playstyle.js` (the guarded Accessibility hand-off row + wire) + `tools/probe-accessibility.mjs` (new) + the generated HTML.
- **SCOPE — E3 is built in TWO increments (this is i1):** **i1 = the 4 modes module (THIS).** **i2 = the dedicated FULL WCAG 2.2 AA audit sweep across every shipped surface** (the comprehensive per-surface inline-hex contrast pass). i1's high-contrast deliberately strengthens the var-driven core + focus + the already-high-contrast dark panels; the exhaustive per-surface sweep is i2 (so the high-contrast hint copy is honest, not overclaiming — bug-hunt I4-2). E3's checklist box ticks when i2 lands.
- **Adversarial bug-hunt (9-agent panel: 6 diverse-lens Opus finders [combat-purity/D74 · cross-engine parse · XSS · robustness · WCAG-AA + mode-efficacy · state/persistence · integration] × default-refute verify × a completeness critic; 17 agents; critic confidence HIGH) → 10 findings, ALL FIXED + probe-locked/measured:** **(HIGH) I4-1** — high-contrast forced the transparent `.gn-col` black + brightened `.gn-deck`, making the LIGHT-parchment main menu unreadable (black-on-dark-ink / near-white-on-cream) → removed both from the HC rules; the menu is its own opaque parchment theme using NO `--vars`, so HC leaves it untouched (measured headline 13.47:1 AAA, `.gn-col` transparent). **(HIGH, critic NEW-1) + (MED) I3-1/I5-1** — the `&& G.campaign` gate in `_a11ySave` let a stale `gor_save` resurrect a turned-off mode on the next Continue, and a one-directional `a11yCvd→cbAids` latch resurrected the base "Colour-blind Aids: Off" choice (the D123 anti-pattern) → made **cbAids the single source of truth** (dropped the separate `a11yCvd` latch; `a11yApply` no longer forces cbAids) and **removed the campaign gate** so `saveLocal` co-writes unconditionally, exactly like the frozen base Settings buttons. **(MED) I6-1** — `a11yTurnSummary` checked `C.side==="CSA"` but the engine uses `"CS"` → every player was announced "Union president" → match `"CS"` first. **(MED) I4-2** overclaim wording softened. **(LOW) I4-3** dyslexia spacing now reaches `.pad span/li`; **I5-2** dead `reduceMotion`/`cbAids` bundle seeds removed; **I3-2** the persistence asymmetry collapsed (cw_a11y holds only the new flags; base flags ride gor_save); **I6-2** the dead `_wdRefresh` desk-rerender branch removed (the hub is menu-only by design, D123.3). The probe was STRENGTHENED with a CVD-no-resurrection step, a NEW-1 menu-time-persistence step, and the real `CS`/`US` side assertions.
- **Vet (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK`; `probe-accessibility` **17/17**, 0 pe; no-regression — `probe-presets` **26/26** (the 9-baseline byte-identity gate — the global module load does NOT perturb combat), `probe-playstyle` 14/14 (the psA11y hand-off), `t1probe` 14/14 (the strategic-shell turn hook), `bootprobe` ok (realErrors:[] — the module boots clean), `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`, plus the E2-cluster probes (codex/glossary/tutorial/desk/decisions/divergence/afteraction/realism-teaching) all green earlier with 97 present; all 0 pe. **VISUALLY CONFIRMED:** both modes transform the render (computed `--rule`→#e8c860, body font→sans stack, root attrs set; the HC main menu measured 13.47:1). `wcag-auditor` AA — NO violations, NO edits (all opacity-composited ratios pass: hint 8.21:1, footer 6.46:1, keyboard heading 5.12:1). **NEXT = E3-i2** (the full AA audit sweep). Committed + pushed. (Reversible.)

## D124 — E2 increment 5: **"THE REAL COST" — a citation-grade HISTORICAL TEACHING layer over the tactical realism dials** (COMPLETES E2 / S4 education) — [CLAUDE] (overnight run, 2026-06-24)
- **What shipped:** the FIFTH and final E2 / S4-education item. The tactical difficulty/realism dials already shipped (B5/D69, `src/tactical/T6-presets.js`); E2-i5's value is EDUCATIONAL, not a new combat lever. New `src/96-realism-teaching.js` — a PURE READ-OUT module teaching WHAT EACH REALISM DIAL ABSTRACTS in real terms: 8 lever entries (attrition · canister · supply · command-shock · scouting/LOS · experience · commander-skill · fog) + 3 bundle entries (Arcade/Balanced/Historian), each `{title, models, cost, sources}`. Surfaced TWO ways, both typeof-guarded: (1) a **"What this costs in real life" expander** (`#pvTeach` → `#rtmTeach`) in the T6 Command & Realism picker — the at-the-decision-point surface, with a CONTEXTUAL "YOURS: <setting>" chip per dial reflecting the player's current selection; (2) a deepened one-line teaching read-out in the E2-i4 "Play Your Way" hub (`src/95-playstyle.js`, via `rtmHubReadout`). Period-broadsheet styled, CVD-safe (▸ marker + word label, never colour alone), full keyboard/ARIA (aria-expanded/aria-controls + native `<details>` for sources), reduce-motion-safe.
- **D124.1 — SCOPE DECISION (the E2-i5 fork, resolved):** the HANDOFF flagged "E2-i5 sliders" as scope-ambiguous. Chose **Option B (the recommended, non-forking path): an EDUCATION-layer teaching enrichment of the already-shipped sliders**, NOT Option A (a NEW strategic-difficulty/AI-GM system — combat-adjacent, and it OVERLAPS the deferred [hard] §12 symmetric AI-GM capstone that Aaron's locked queue puts LAST; building it under E2 would have contradicted the locked queue). B keeps E2 education-focused and entirely off the combat path. (Reversible.)
- **ARCHITECTURE — byte-identical combat BY CONSTRUCTION (D74, "no thumb on the scale"):** the module reads FLDP labels + a passed config and RETURNS HTML; it changes ZERO lever value and writes NOTHING (no G mutation, no localStorage, no RNG, no `_SAVE_VER` bump). `fldPresetCompute`/`fldPresetsApply`/`_fldClampCfg`/`fldPresetNeutral`/`fldPresetResolve` are UNCHANGED. The T6 hook lives ONLY in the picker UI (`_fldPresetHTML`/`_fldPresetWire`/`_fldPresetInitState`'s new `teach` flag), never in a combat-read function — locked by a probe that (a) static-scans 22 combat-execution files for the rtm symbols [0 leaks] and (b) extracts the 5 combat-read functions and asserts none reference rtm. NO A/B sweep needed because no number changed; the `probe-presets` 9-baseline byte-identity gate (neutral byte-exact; layers-OFF Bull Run CS 5/8; Historian severities intact) stays GREEN. Diff = `src/96-realism-teaching.js` (new) + `src/00-manifest.json` (96 after 95) + `src/tactical/T6-presets.js` (the picker `teach` toggle + expander hook) + `src/95-playstyle.js` (the guarded hub read-out) + `tools/probe-realism-teaching.mjs` (new) + the generated HTML.
- **CONTENT (citation-grade, anti-Lost-Cause; research+verify Workflow, 22 agents):** 11 Opus historians (WebSearch-grounded) × 11 adversarial Opus verifiers (default-refute, anti-Lost-Cause + citation lens; ≥2 verified real sources each, mostly 3–4). The verify pass caught + fixed real errors before integration: the **Griffith MISATTRIBUTION** (he was a SKEPTIC of the rifle-revolution thesis, a precursor to Hess — not a proponent); a **FABRICATION-RISK McPherson quote** in the supply entry (a Meigs assessment misattributed to "Battle Cry of Freedom" → paraphrased); a **LOST-CAUSE risk** in the commander-skill entry (corrected to note Lee's aggression bled the outnumbered South dry — his army's casualty rate exceeded Grant's); Hacker framing tightened; canister range corrected. Anti-Lost-Cause throughout (Historian names the cost as borne to destroy slavery + preserve the Union; Arcade names itself a teaching scaffold, NOT a depiction of the appallingly lethal real war).
- **BUG-HUNT — the dedicated 9-agent panel was BLOCKED by a sustained Anthropic API 529 overload** (three consecutive total failures — a 22-agent run, a re-run, and a single consolidated agent, all 529 over ~15 min; 0 tool uses on the re-runs). Per the HALT rule (external gate failure, no fix on my side → document + proceed with the best available verification), it was SUBSTITUTED by: (a) a rigorous **INLINE Opus adversarial pass across all 5 lenses, which CAUGHT + FIXED one real, high-severity defect** — a regex LOOKBEHIND `(?<=\.)\s` in `rtmHubReadout`; lookbehind is a PARSE-TIME SyntaxError on Safari < 16.4 and would have broken the ENTIRE single-file game on those engines (the bootprobe passed only because it runs in Chromium) → replaced with a portable `". "` string split; (b) the content's own 22-agent research+verify workflow; (c) the full empirical probe battery (which actually executes the code + checks every invariant, unaffected by the API); (d) the wcag-auditor pass. **RECOMMENDED FOLLOW-UP:** re-run the 9-agent panel (saved script under the session `workflows/scripts/rt-bughunt-*.js`) when the API recovers, as a belt-and-suspenders confirmation — confidence is already high.
- **Vet (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓`; `probe-realism-teaching` **12/12**, 0 pe (purity spy, byte-identity invariants, XSS, robustness, contextual chip, anti-Lost-Cause content, T6 integration toggle, the static no-leak scan + the T6-combat-read-clean scan); no-regression GREEN — `probe-presets` **26/26** (the 9-baseline byte-identity gate), `probe-playstyle` 14/14, `probe-codex` 18/18, `probe-glossary` 12/12, `probe-tutorial` 15/15, `probe-desk` 13/13, `probe-decisions` 19/19, `probe-divergence` 14/14, `probe-afteraction` 15/15, `bootprobe` ok (realErrors:[]), `t1probe` 14/14, `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`, all 0 pe. No combat-path change → 9 battles byte-identical by construction. `wcag-auditor` AA (added `aria-hidden` to two decorative glyphs in the hub read-out; all contrast ratios pass). Committed + pushed.
- **E2 / S4 EDUCATION IS NOW COMPLETE** (i1 codex ✅ · i2 inline glossary ✅ · i3 tutorial ✅ · i4 play-style + Historian layer ✅ · i5 the realism-teaching layer ✅). **NEXT = E3** — the dedicated full WCAG 2.2 AA pass + the 4 a11y modes (high-contrast, CVD-safe, SR turn/battle narration, dyslexia font).
- **D124.2 — the queued 9-agent bug-hunt RE-RAN CLEAN, one LOW a11y finding fixed — [CLAUDE] (2026-06-24, next session).** The API having recovered, the belt-and-suspenders panel was **re-authored** (the prior session's saved script died with its session) and re-ran: 6 diverse-lens Opus finders (combat-purity/D74 · cross-engine parse portability · XSS/escaping · robustness · WCAG-AA · citation/anti-Lost-Cause) × default-refute Opus verify × a completeness critic (confidence HIGH). It empirically re-confirmed every D124 invariant — combat byte-identity (grep + the `probe-presets` NEUTRAL byte-identity gate), parse portability (the lookbehind is gone; no other modern-only syntax), XSS (all 19 FLDP labels enumerated — none can break the YOURS chip), robustness (node-executed against null/`{}`/unknown-realism/missing-FLDP — no throw, no `undefined`/`null` leak), and the four most error-prone history numbers (Antietam 22,717; canister 27 balls; Stuart's ride; Hess ~94 yds — all web-corroborated). It surfaced **exactly ONE real defect (LOW): a DANGLING `aria-controls="rtmTeach"`** — `#pvTeach` advertised it unconditionally, but `#rtmTeach` (produced only by `rtmRealismExpanderHTML`, only when open) is ABSENT in the default collapsed state, and `openSheet` rebuilds the whole picker DOM each rerender → a dangling IDREF (WCAG 4.1.2/1.3.1). **FIX:** emit `aria-controls` ONLY when `s.teach` is true (so it is present iff the target exists), mirroring the sibling `#pvAdv` (which carries `aria-expanded` and no `aria-controls`); `aria-expanded` continues to announce collapsed/expanded in both states. `tools/probe-realism-teaching.mjs` **STRENGTHENED** (not weakened — its old step actually encoded the bug: it asserted both `aria-controls=rtmTeach` AND `#rtmTeach` absent while collapsed) to assert no-dangling-ref-when-collapsed → valid-ref-when-open → dropped-again-on-recollapse. UI-only attribute change in the picker render → combat byte-identical (`probe-presets` 26/26 NEUTRAL byte-identity GREEN; the full no-regression suite GREEN; `probe-realism-teaching` 12/12). Diff = `src/tactical/T6-presets.js` + `tools/probe-realism-teaching.mjs` + the generated HTML. (Reversible.)

## D123 — E2 increment 4: **PLAY-STYLE PRESETS + the D39 "HISTORIAN" SETTINGS LAYER** — a "Play Your Way" hub (President / General-Commander / Free + the teaching/realism toggles) — [CLAUDE] (overnight run, 2026-06-24)
- **What shipped:** the fourth E2 / S4-education item. New `src/95-playstyle.js` — a **"Play Your Way"** hub holding (1) **play-style presets** — President (owner-mode focus) / General-Commander (battle focus) / Free Command (the shipped neutral) — and (2) the **D39 Historian teaching layer** (the alt-history emergent-only toggle, a read-only Command-&-Realism read-out, and hand-offs to the existing pickers). Surfaced TWO ways from ONE panel body (`_psPanelHTML(ctx)`): an 18th President's-Desk tab (rendered inline like the Codex tab, ctx="desk") AND a main-menu overlay (`openSheet`, ctx="menu"). Period-broadsheet styled, CVD-safe (glyph decorative + the word label carries meaning + the ▸ marker), full keyboard/ARIA, reduce-motion-safe.
- **D123.1 — play-style is an EMPHASIS, never a hard gate (faithful to D39.1).** In this game both layers are always available (the locked "both battle modes selectable" charter), so a play-style preset must NOT remove content. Its genuine, pure-UI consumers: (a) `psDefaultDeskTab` sets which Desk tab the desk first lands on (Commander→Command; President/Free→the War Effort overview; **unset or unknown → "economy" → byte-identical** for existing players, guarded by a `deskTabs` whitelist), and (b) it is remembered + tailors the hub's own guidance. `openWarDept`'s literal `_wdTab="economy"` became `_wdTab=psDefaultDeskTab()`. (Reversible.)
- **D123.2 — PURE settings layer; byte-identical combat BY CONSTRUCTION (D74).** The module writes ONLY two NON-combat strategic flags — `G.settings.playStyle` (new, owned here) and `G.settings.altHistoryEmergentOnly` (a SECOND surface for the divergence-tab flag) — plus a private `cw_playstyle` localStorage bundle (the proven T6 boot-load pattern, defaults-only). It writes NO combat knob: the tactical realism levers stay owned by `T6-presets.js` (`fldPresetResolve` read-only here; "Adjust the battlefield" hands off to `fldPresetMenu`), and motion/CVD/sound stay owned by the frozen base `openSettings` (a hand-off). **No tactical file in the diff; no combat/tick/resolve/bridge path references the ps-family symbols** (probe static-scan over `src/tactical/*.js` + 85/86/87/80) → 9 battles byte-identical by construction. NO RNG; NO `_SAVE_VER` bump. (Reversible.)
- **D123.3 — the desk context is READ-ONLY for hand-offs.** A mid-campaign desk hand-off to `fldPresetMenu`/`openSettings`/`openMainMenu` would tear down the desk sheet + its between-battles continuation chain (`_pdAfterDeskClose`), so the desk-tab context shows read-only hints; the menu-overlay context offers the live "Adjust…/Open…" buttons (which return cleanly to the menu). No T6/base edit required → preserves the byte-identity argument. (Reversible.)
- **D123.4 — bug-hunt (5 diverse-lens Opus finders incl. a citation/anti-Lost-Cause lens × default-refute Opus verify + a completeness critic; 13 agents) → invariants RE-CONFIRMED (byte-identical combat · desk byte-identity · persistence · purity); 5 confirmed findings ALL FIXED + probe-locked:** **(MED)** the main-menu button observer used a one-shot `installed` latch — the menu is rebuilt on every `openMainMenu`, so the button (and, SAME ROOT CAUSE, 92-help-overlay's How-to-Play + Guided Tour buttons) vanished after the first rebuild → **rewrote both observers to the project's canonical `fldInjectMenuButton` pattern** (no latch, dedupe by live DOM `#gnPlayStyle`/`#gnHelp`, re-inject on every mutation; verified all three persist across rebuilds via a polling test). **(MED)** the emergent-only On/Off toggle had no accessible name beyond "On"/"Off" (WCAG 4.1.2) → added `aria-label` (matching the divergence toggle). **(LOW×3)** the menu hand-off buttons "Adjust…/Open…" had weak names → descriptive `aria-label`s; the divergence toggle never synced the `cw_playstyle` bundle (a stale pre-campaign default could resurrect a turned-off setting after an out-of-band `gor_save` deletion) → `_psPersist()` call added (typeof-guarded, load-order-independent); the stale "the ONLY writer" comment in `81-divergence.js` corrected. **`wcag-auditor` AA** then auto-fixed one non-text contrast failure (the `#5a4a2e` section divider, 1.89:1 → `#8c724e` 3.57:1; WCAG 1.4.11). **The 92-help-overlay observer repair is an incidental SAME-ROOT-CAUSE fix, logged here (not silent scope creep)** because the latch made the entire shipped menu onboarding suite disappear after the first navigation.
- **Vet (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓`; `probe-playstyle` **14/14**, 0 pe; no-regression — `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`, `probe-codex` 18/18, `probe-glossary` 12/12, `probe-tutorial` 15/15, `probe-desk` 13/13, `probe-decisions` 19/19, `probe-divergence` 14/14, `probe-afteraction` 15/15, `bootprobe` ok (realErrors:[]), `t1probe` 14/14, all 0 pe. No tactical file in the diff → 9 battles byte-identical by construction. Committed + pushed.
- **Reversible?** Yes (additive module + 4 small src wirings + a probe). **Impact:** the player can set a play-style emphasis + the teaching/realism layer from the menu or the Desk; the desk lands on the tab matching their focus; and the whole menu onboarding suite (Play Your Way + How-to-Play + Guided Tour) now persists across menu rebuilds. **NEXT in E2 = i5 difficulty/realism sliders** (extend `src/tactical/T6-presets.js`). Then **E3** the full WCAG 2.2 AA pass + the 4 a11y modes.

## D122 — E2 increment 3: **THE GUIDED TUTORIAL** — a multi-step modal tour orienting the player across all three layers — [CLAUDE] (overnight run, 2026-06-24)
- **What shipped:** the third E2 / S4-education item — a GUIDED TUTORIAL. New `src/94-tutorial.js` renders a self-contained, 7-step modal tour (`#tutOverlay`) — the sequenced onboarding the static welcome/help cards aren't. Steps: the three layers · the President's Desk · the Great Decisions · the Battles · **the Codex & the Glossary** (showcasing E2-i1/i2) · Winning the War · Play Your Way. Back/Next/Skip, a "Step N of M" indicator + progress dots, full keyboard control (Esc/Left/Right/Enter), a focus trap, ARIA dialog semantics, reduce-motion-safe fade. Launched from THREE entry points wired into the `src/92-help-overlay.js` spine: the first-launch welcome card, the How-to-Play panel, and a main-menu "GUIDED TOUR" button.
- **ACCESSIBILITY-FIRST:** role=dialog + aria-modal + aria-labelledby/describedby; the step `<h2>` (tabindex=-1) is focused per step with an `aria-label` "Step N of M: <title>" so a screen-reader announces both position and heading; the background is `aria-hidden` while the modal is open (restored on close); a symmetric Tab focus trap cycles [Skip, Back, Next]; reduce-motion disables the fade; the dots/glyph are decorative aria-hidden (the text carries meaning). wcag-auditor AA (added a `:focus-visible` ring for the Skip button; contrast 4.30–13.23:1).
- **Architecture — PURE UI / HELP LAYER (byte-identical combat BY CONSTRUCTION):** the tutorial reads NO campaign/sim state and writes nothing but a localStorage "seen" flag (no campaign mutation, no RNG, no `_SAVE_VER`). No tactical/combat/tick/resolve/bridge path references the tutorial (grep + a probe static-scan over 20 combat files). The three 92 launch hooks are `typeof`-guarded. Diff = `src/94-tutorial.js` + `src/00-manifest.json` (`94-tutorial.js`) + `src/92-help-overlay.js` (3 launch hooks + a "?"-modality guard) + `tools/probe-tutorial.mjs` + the generated HTML.
- **Adversarial bug-hunt (5 diverse-lens Opus finders × default-refute verify + a completeness critic; 14 agents) → invariantsHold=FALSE initially; 8 confirmed findings (deduped ~6), ALL FIXED + probe-locked:** **(MED)** the forward-Tab focus trap never reached the Skip button (broken focus order, WCAG 2.4.3) → rewrote the trap as an explicit symmetric cycle over [Skip, Back, Next] with the announce-only title routing in; **(MED)** the global "?" hotkey opened How-to-Play BEHIND the open modal tour (modality leak / stacked dialogs) → guarded the "?" handler to bail when the tour is open; **(LOW)** a double-`tutStart` captured the focus-restore target as `<body>` (it removed the prior overlay before reading activeElement) → capture before teardown, guarded; **(LOW)** the background wasn't made inert for AT → `aria-hidden` the background under the modal (+ a double-start inert-restore so no stale `aria-hidden` leaks); **(LOW)** the step position wasn't announced → folded into the title's `aria-label`; **(LOW)** a ~50ms interactive pre-mount menu window on the welcome/help launch → dropped the `setTimeout` (the z-index 100001 modal covers the menu immediately). Purity + content (the 7 step bodies: balanced HTML, accurate, anti-Lost-Cause) fully held.
- **Vet (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK · citations ✓`; `probe-tutorial` **15/15** (deck schema · ARIA dialog · Next/Back/finish · arrows/Esc · title-focus + trap · last-step "To the Field" + seen-flag · reduce-motion · pure-readout · the 6 fix-locks · static-scan), **0 pageerrors**; no-regression GREEN — `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`, `probe-codex` 18/18, `probe-glossary` 12/12, `probe-desk` 13/13, `probe-decisions` 19/19, `probe-divergence` 14/14, `probe-afteraction` 15/15, `bootprobe` ok (realErrors:[]), `t1probe` 14/14 (all 0 pe). No tactical file in the diff → 9 battles byte-identical by construction. `wcag-auditor` AA (1 focus-ring fix).
- **Reversible?** Yes (additive module + 3 guarded launch hooks + a "?"-guard + probe). **Impact:** a new player can now learn all three layers in a one-minute guided tour, and it surfaces the new Codex + glossary. NEXT in E2 = i4 play-style presets (President / General-Commander) + the D39 Historian settings overlay → i5 difficulty/realism sliders. Then E3 (the full WCAG 2.2 AA pass + the 4 a11y modes).

## D121 — E2 increment 2: **THE INLINE GLOSSARY** — hover/tap/focus codex definitions woven into the teaching prose — [CLAUDE] (overnight run, 2026-06-23)
- **What shipped:** the second E2 / S4-education item — an INLINE GLOSSARY. New `src/93-glossary.js` DOM-decorates the rendered teaching prose of the two read-only teaching desk tabs (After-Action, Your War vs History): a TreeWalker wraps codex terms in inline `<button class="gl-term">` triggers (a dotted brass underline) and a shared `#glTip` tooltip shows the definition on hover/focus/tap. It reads the SAME codex data as the i1 Codex tab (`cxGlossaryIndex`/`cxLookupShort`) — one source of truth. Hooked into `_wdRefresh` (guarded, scoped to afteraction/warvshistory), byte-identical when absent.
- **ACCESSIBILITY-FIRST:** the definition rides each trigger's `aria-label` ("<visible matched text> — <short> (glossary term)") so a screen-reader announces it on FOCUS regardless of the visual tooltip; the tooltip is `aria-hidden` role=tooltip decorative reinforcement (no double-announce). Triggers are real keyboard-operable `<button>`s; Esc dismisses; a scoped `:focus-visible` ring; reduce-motion disables the tooltip transition; the cue is a dotted UNDERLINE (shape), not color-alone. wcag-auditor AA (tooltip 13.51:1, term/border 8.10:1; no edits).
- **Surface-derivation (the keystone design):** matchers are built from DERIVED SURFACES, not the raw `en.term` — the core noun phrase (leading "The " + trailing "(...)" clarifier stripped), any parenthetical acronym (e.g. "(USCT)" → USCT), and authored aliases — so the spellings prose ACTUALLY uses match (e.g. "The Lost Cause (Myth)" → "Lost Cause"; "United States Colored Troops (USCT)" → "United States Colored Troops" + "USCT"). High-value bare-form **aliases** added to 3 codex entries (emancipation, blockade/Anaconda Plan, conscription/draft) — pure data the codex already supports.
- **Architecture — PURE READ-OUT (byte-identical combat BY CONSTRUCTION):** `glDecorate` reads `GAME_DATA.codex` (via cx accessors) and DECORATES already-rendered DOM; it WRITES NOTHING to the sim (no campaign state, no save, no RNG). No tactical/combat/tick/resolve/bridge path references `gl*` (grep-confirmed + a probe static-scan over 20 combat files) → battles byte-identical by construction. The `_wdRefresh` hook clears `data-gl-done` before each render (the reused `#wdContent` element) so re-viewing a teaching tab re-decorates. Diff = `src/93-glossary.js` + `data/codex.json` (3 aliases) + `src/00-manifest.json` (`93-glossary.js`) + `src/30-president-shell.js` (the guarded hook) + `tools/probe-glossary.mjs` + the generated HTML.
- **Adversarial bug-hunt (OFFLINE, no-internet per Aaron's mid-run pause; 5 diverse-lens Opus finders × default-refute verify + a completeness critic; 26 agents) → critic invariantsHold=FALSE initially (a genuine word-boundary bug), 16 confirmed findings, ALL FIXED + probe-locked:** **(the keystone)** the trailing `\b` made every term ending in `)` (USCT, the Lost Cause, Contraband, etc.) a DEAD matcher → fixed by the surface-derivation above (clean word-char-bounded surfaces); only-ONE-term-per-text-node dropped other terms → now wraps every distinct term left-to-right via a fragment; the aria-label led with the canonical (not visible) text → now leads with the matched text (WCAG 2.5.3); tap-on-term dismissed instead of revealed (touch users) → click now always reveals + click-outside dismisses; misleading comments corrected; and a PROBE-RIGOR cluster (the probe used a non-existent fallback term → always tested "Abraham Lincoln" and never a parenthetical term; the word-boundary test was vacuous; no event/skip-zone-completeness/multi-term coverage) → the probe rewritten to anchor on REAL derived surfaces (USCT/Lost Cause/Contraband/Anaconda Plan), test substring-rejection on a real surface, dispatch real focus/Esc/click events, cover all declared skip-zones, and the combat-path static-scan.
- **Vet (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK · citations ✓`; `probe-glossary` **12/12** (matchers/derivation · first-occurrence + aria-label · parenthetical/acronym/alias decoration · word-boundary · multi-term · skip-zones · no-corruption · idempotent · tooltip+events · purity · hook-scoping · static-scan), **0 pageerrors**; no-regression GREEN — `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`, `probe-codex` 18/18, `probe-desk` 13/13, `probe-decisions` 19/19, `probe-divergence` 14/14, `probe-afteraction` 15/15, `bootprobe` ok (realErrors:[]), `t1probe` 14/14 (all 0 pe). No tactical file in the diff → 9 battles byte-identical by construction. `wcag-auditor` AA (no edits).
- **Reversible?** Yes (additive module + a guarded 1-call hook + 3 data aliases + probe). **Impact:** the teaching prose now teaches its own vocabulary in place. NOTE — **internet was paused by Aaron mid-run; this milestone was vetted fully offline (the bug-hunt was re-run with a hard no-internet rule) and COMMITTED LOCALLY; the `git push` is HELD pending "resume."** NEXT in E2 = i3 the guided tutorial → i4 presets+Historian overlay → i5 difficulty/realism sliders.

## D120 — E2 increment 1: **THE MULTI-AXIS CODEX / GLOSSARY** — a citation-grade, anti-Lost-Cause reference (people · units & formations · terms & concepts · systems of the war) as a searchable President's-Desk tab — [CLAUDE] (overnight run, 2026-06-23)
- **What shipped (the first E2 / S4-education item):** the President's Desk gains a **Codex** tab — a searchable, axis-filterable, cross-linked reference of **60 citation-grade entries** (people:24 · units:11 · terms:19 · systems:6; **all 60 Verified, each ≥2 real sources**). Each entry expands to its full teaching prose + provenance + sources, with "See also" cross-links. The codex `short` (≤140-char) field is the single source of truth for the **E2-i2 inline glossary** (exposed now via `cxLookupShort`/`cxGlossaryIndex`).
- **E2 build order decided & logged:** **i1 codex DATA+tab (this)** → i2 inline glossary (hover/tap defs reading the codex `short`) → i3 the full guided tutorial (extend `src/92-help-overlay.js`) → i4 play-style presets (President / General-Commander) + the D39 Historian settings overlay → i5 difficulty/realism sliders (pair with the B-5 tactical effectiveness/realism levers in `src/tactical/T6-presets.js`). Codex leads because it is the data foundation the inline glossary reads. (Decide-&-log; not a new fork.)
- **Content (codex-content-research Workflow, 14 agents):** 7 Opus historian slices (people-union/confed/emancipation · units · terms-war-politics · terms-strategy-tactics · systems), WebSearch-grounded, each adversarially verified by an Opus default-skeptical fact-checker (every source confirmed real or removed; Verified⇒≥2 sources else downgraded). The verify pass caught real errors: a fabricated Hess artillery-book title removed; **the Forrest/KKK error corrected — Forrest was the Klan's first Grand Wizard, NOT its founder** (founded by six Confederate veterans in Pulaski, 1866). Anti-Lost-Cause throughout: slavery named as the war's central cause; the Lost Cause named + countered (Cornerstone Speech, secession declarations); Fort Pillow, the postwar Klan, Andersonville, and Union hard-war destruction named on both sides; no Confederate valorization.
- **Architecture — PURE READ-OUT (D111/D112/D116 pattern; byte-identical combat BY CONSTRUCTION):** new `src/84-codex.js` (`codexRenderTab`/`codexWireTab` + `_cx*` helpers + `cxLookupShort`/`cxGlossaryIndex`). Reads `GAME_DATA.codex` (new `data/codex.json`), WRITES NOTHING to the sim — no campaign state, no `saveLocal`, no RNG, no `_SAVE_VER` bump. **No tactical file touched; no combat/tick/resolve/bridge path reads `cx*`/`GAME_DATA.codex`** (grep-confirmed AND now locked by a probe static-scan over 20 combat files) → the bridge + all 9 battles byte-identical by construction. Filtering is client-side DOM show/hide in the wire handlers (the search box keeps focus; no `_wdRefresh` re-render on keystroke). Wired as the desk's 17th tab in `src/30-president-shell.js` (the `_wdRefresh` dispatch + the `openWarDept` button + both lockstep tab arrays), exactly like the decisions/afteraction/warvshistory tabs. Manifest: `84-codex.js` between 83-endings and 85-battle-bridge.
- **Adversarial bug-hunt (5 diverse-lens Opus finders incl. a citation/anti-Lost-Cause lens × default-refute Opus verify + a completeness critic; 26 agents) → invariantsHold=TRUE** (the critic independently re-confirmed all 5 invariants; the high-risk Forrest-founder error confirmed NOT present; slavery named as the cause; axis-chip contrast 8–10:1; citation sample web-verified real & on-claim). **16 confirmed findings, all LOW/MED and latent (no live defect), ALL FIXED + probe-locked:** the cross-link left a stale axis blurb (CX-01); the initial entry count was hardcoded plural (CX-02); the garbled Lincoln "blind memorandum" quote repaired (CDX-03); Andersonville added as a dedicated both-sides POW entry (CDX-04); `.cx-head`/`#cxSearch` given a scoped `:focus-visible` ring (base.html is frozen, so injected via a scoped `<style>`); `#cxBlurb` made `aria-live`; and a cluster of **probe-rigor** gaps closed — the probe now locks purity (localStorage/Math.random spies), XSS escaping, the anti-Lost-Cause content facts (Forrest-not-founder, Fort Pillow, Andersonville, hard-war, slavery), shell-dispatch lockstep, cross-link/aria/reduce-motion interaction, data robustness, and a Node-side static scan that no combat path reads the codex. **Plus a self-found internal-consistency fix:** the USCT figure aligned `~178,000 → ~180,000` to match the game's locked convention (70-manpower/82-after-action/83-endings/81-divergence; the D117 precedent).
- **Vet (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓`; `probe-codex` **18/18** (schema · Verified⇒≥2 sources · 4 axes · render/wire/filter/expand/cross-link · purity-spy · XSS · data-robustness · anti-Lost-Cause content · a11y · interaction · shell-dispatch · static-scan), **0 pageerrors**; no-regression GREEN — `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`, `probe-desk` 13/13, `probe-decisions` 19/19, `probe-divergence` 14/14, `probe-afteraction` 15/15, `bootprobe` ok (realErrors:[]), `t1probe` 14/14 (all 0 pe). No tactical file in the diff → 9 battles byte-identical by construction. `wcag-auditor` AA — auto-fixed 2 pill-count contrast spans (opacity .6→.75 = 4.84:1/4.62:1); everything else passed (focus rings 7.49–7.80:1, axis chips 7.77–9.20:1).
- **Reversible?** Yes (additive data + a pure-readout module + tab wiring + probe). **Impact:** the teaching layer's reference spine is live; the codex grows over later passes and feeds the i2 inline glossary. NEXT in E2 = i2 the inline glossary, then i3 tutorial / i4 presets / i5 sliders, then E3 (the full WCAG-AA pass + a11y modes).

## D119 — E4-i2: **THE STRATEGIC WAR-END** — a reached `victoryReady` (a negotiated peace / a recognized independence) concludes the war via the graded report, for a victory that did NOT complete the battle chain — [CLAUDE] (overnight run, 2026-06-23)
- **What shipped (the locked Phase-E item after E1):** the strategic arc's missing closure. Before D119 the war ended ONLY at battle-chain completion (`campaignAdvance` → `warWonScreen` at `idx>=chainLen`). Now a reached **`C.strategy.victoryReady`** — a **negotiated peace** (Northern/enemy will broken, `enemyWill≤30` in 1864+ or `≤18` any year) or a **recognized independence** (recognition ≥60, CS) — can CONCLUDE the war through the SAME D112 graded after-action report, surfaced as a **non-forced OFFER** on the between-battles interstitial ("The war can be concluded — accept the peace / claim independence", or fight on). This is the frozen campaign-loop closure the prompt named (E4-i2), built via the authorized `warWonScreen` override pattern (D116 authorized the frozen-loop override autonomously).
- **Architecture — an additive OFFER + the existing warWonScreen; byte-identical combat BY CONSTRUCTION:** new pure helpers in `src/82-after-action.js` — `aarStrategicEndAvailable(C)` (returns `"will"` for either side / `"recognition"` **CS-ONLY** / null), `_aarEndFraming(C,reason)` (side-aware title/subtitle/verdict/offer copy), `aarStrategicEndOffer(C)`, `aarConcludeWar(reason)`. The `warWonScreen` OVERRIDE now frames the title/verdict via `_aarEndFraming`; at chain completion (`_aarEndReason` null) it is the **byte-identical D112 "The War is Won / Victory!"** screen with the base Main-Menu wiring + `G.campaign=null` preserved verbatim. The offer banner is one guarded IIFE in `_pdInterstitialHTML` (`src/20-president-render.js`) — returns `''` when no offer → **byte-identical interstitial** when `victoryReady` is null; wired (`#pdConcludeWar`) in `_pdShowTurnInterstitial` (`src/30-president-shell.js`). **No tactical file touched; no combat/tick/resolve/bridge path reads `aarStrategicEnd*`/`_aarEndReason`/`aarConcludeWar`** (grep-confirmed) → 9 battles byte-identical by construction. No new save state (`_aarEndReason` is a transient module var); NO `_SAVE_VER` bump; NO RNG.
- **SIDE-CORRECTNESS (anti-Lost-Cause):** `"recognition"` is gated **CS-only** — the Union never "wins" because the Confederacy gains recognition (D111 noted recognition is unreachable for US by normal play; the gate is the defensive belt). `"will"` is correct for either side (the enemy sues for terms), framed side-aware (US: "the rebellion lays down its arms"; CS: "Washington consents to let the South go"). A CS strategic victory is framed honestly as the survival of a slaveholding republic — never valorized — and the D112 graded report body carries the anti-Lost-Cause Reconstruction accounting (keyed on `victoryReady`, which `aarConcludeWar` does not clear, so the honest accounting renders correctly through the conclusion).
- **Bug-hunt (5 diverse-lens Opus finders × default-refute Opus verify + a completeness critic; 10 agents) → invariantsHold=TRUE; 1 confirmed LOW + 2 critic gaps (1 MED + 1 LOW), ALL FIXED + probe-locked:** **(MED, side-correctness)** the pre-existing `vicOnResolve` `victoryReady="will"` toast hardcoded "Northern resolve is breaking" for BOTH sides — wrong for a US player (whose enemy is the rebellion); D119 makes it a live cue → **side-aware** ("The rebellion's resolve is breaking" for US). **(LOW, flow)** the strategic-conclude exit bypassed `openUpgrade`'s `_pdTurnAck=false` reset → the NEXT campaign skipped its first interstitial once → reset `_pdTurnAck` at the single war-end chokepoint (`warWonScreen`), probe-locked. **(LOW, defensive)** `warWonScreen`'s `!C` early-return didn't clear `_aarEndReason` → a stale reason could mis-frame a later war-end → cleared on the guard. (Critic confirmed: purity grep-clean; the US-recognition refusal; chain-completion byte-identical to D112; the offer strings are author-controlled, intended `&mdash;` HTML, no injection.)
- **Vet (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓`; `probe-afteraction` **15/15** (+5 D119 steps: side-correct availability · side-aware offer copy · the interstitial offer presence/absence · `aarConcludeWar` ends the war with side-aware framing · chain-completion byte-identical + the `_pdTurnAck`/CS-subtitle fix-locks), **0 pageerrors**; no-regression GREEN — `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`, `probe-desk` 13/13, `probe-divergence` 14/14, `probe-victory` 12/12, `bootprobe` ok, `t1probe` 14/14 (all 0 pe). No tactical file in the diff → 9 battles byte-identical by construction. `wcag-auditor` AA (the offer banner #cbb27a 7.81:1 / body 8.94:1 / button 8.54:1 — clean, no edits).
- **Reversible?** Yes (the offer + the warWonScreen framing; pure read + the existing override). **Impact:** the strategic arc now CLOSES for a non-chain victory — a negotiated peace or a recognized independence actually ends your war with the graded report + the Reconstruction coda. NEXT in the locked order = E2 (codex/glossary/tutorial/presets/difficulty sliders), then E3 (the full WCAG-AA pass + a11y modes).

## D118 — E1 deepening increment 5: **INTERACTIVE HINGE-FORK DECISION CARDS** — 4 citation-grade strategic forks that STEER the war toward the endings — [CLAUDE] (overnight run, 2026-06-23)
- **What shipped:** the E1 "Remaining" item — interactive **HINGE-FORK decision cards on the proven `decResolve`/`_decApply` engine** (the prompt's spec: *data/decisions.json, pure data → byte-identical by construction*). 4 new citation-grade strategic hinge cards (2 US, 2 CS — both-sides balance), each a genuine fork at a war turning-point whose choices shape the war's TRAJECTORY toward the alternate-ending spectrum (D114-D117): **us-hard-war** ("The Hard Hand of War", 1864 — total war / conciliation / Grant's grind → the *hard-war collapse* road); **us-reconstruction-terms** ("The Terms of Reunion", 1864 — the Ten Percent Plan / Wade-Davis / forty acres → *a Reconstruction that holds* / *forty acres enforced*); **cs-king-cotton** ("The King Cotton Gambit", 1861-62 — embargo / export / burn → *recognized independence* / *King Cotton*); **cs-peace-feelers** ("Peace or Independence", 1864 — negotiate / fight-on / husband → *a negotiated peace* / *Fabian survival*). Each is `trigger.hinge:true` (always surfaces, "⚐ a hinge of the war" badge), with 3 options carrying the full rich `card` multi-voice block (claim + a primary actor + 1-2 named scholars + a **named-and-countered MYTH voice**, anti-Lost-Cause). The deck grows 8 → 12 cards (it had only ONE strategic hinge before — emancipation).
- **Scope decision (decide-&-log) — PURE DATA, no engine extension:** the ending-OPENER strategy levers (`armEnslaved`/`amnesty`/`enemyWill`/`recognition`) are all combat-adjacent (they feed manpower→bridge / auto-resolve) and already live on the Paths-to-Victory surface; writing them from `_decApply` would be a combat-adjacent engine change, contradicting the prompt's explicit "pure data" instruction. So the cards use ONLY the existing `_decApply` vocabulary (`weariness`/`capital`/`intervention`/`importFactor`/`strength` + `sets.emancipation`) — which genuinely moves **momentum** (the dominant ending GATE: `vicMomentum` reads weariness + capital), the foreign-pressure/blockade/manpower trajectory, and the emancipation precond — and each option's prose NAMES the ending it steers toward ("This is the road to …"). The wild/lever/recognition openers stay on the Paths-to-Victory surface (by design); the decision deck is the complementary narrative fork-surface. The ONLY src touch is a 1-line `_decCAT_LABEL` extension (render labels for the new categories war-strategy/diplomacy/reconstruction/peace; plain `&` in "Peace & Terms" since `cat` is escaped at render — avoids the D115 double-escape; byte-identical for the 8 existing cards via the existing raw-key fallback).
- **Citation-grade content (research+verify Workflow, 8 agents):** 4 Opus historians drafted each card (WebSearch/WebFetch-grounded), 4 Opus adversarial verifiers checked every source + claim + the anti-Lost-Cause framing. ALL verifier corrections applied: fabrication-risk URLs replaced with book/verified cites (the misattributed ABT "The Burning"; generic NARA milestone/ABT index landing pages; the 404'd Hampton Roads ABT URL; the wrong bread-riot slug → the verified `bread-riot-richmond` slug); the **Erlanger loan** nominal-£3M-vs-realized correction (softened to "realized far less in net proceeds" per the bug-hunt critic, since the precise net figure is contested across sources); the **Hammond "Cotton is king"** quote restored to the documented wording; **Gordon C. Rhea** named for the Grant casualty-rate arithmetic; the dubious Schweikart attribution dropped. Every retained book cite (Grimsley, McPherson, Foner, Du Bois, Gallagher, Owsley, Howard Jones, Wise, Hahn, Donald, W. C. Davis, McMurry, Rhea) verified genuine.
- **Architecture — byte-identical combat BY CONSTRUCTION (D74 no-fudge):** no tactical file in the diff; `_decApply`/`_decClamp` UNCHANGED; the new cards use only the existing bounded/clamped effect fields (the same the existing 8 cards use) → no NEW combat path, no fudge (decision effects are bounded strategic-state INPUTS, the same as the shipped deck). Diff = `data/decisions.json` (4 cards) + `src/32-decisions.js` (the label map + 2 wcag contrast fixes) + `tools/probe-decisions.mjs` (5 D118 steps) + the generated HTML. NO `_SAVE_VER` bump (additive plain data).
- **Bug-hunt (5 diverse-lens Opus finders incl. a citation/anti-Lost-Cause lens × default-refute Opus verify + a completeness critic; 6 agents) → invariantsHold=TRUE, ZERO confirmed defects** (the critic independently re-confirmed all 5 invariants: no fabrication [spot-checked Hammond/Wade-Davis/bread-riot/Erlanger-nominal real & on-claim], historical accuracy, anti-Lost-Cause [each card carries a named+countered myth voice; slavery named plainly; no Confederate valorization], byte-identical combat [no tactical file; `_decApply` untouched; supported-fields-only], render/escaping [the "Peace & Terms" double-escape correctly avoided]). One critic-LOW (the contested Erlanger net figure) softened pre-commit.
- **Vet (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓`; `probe-decisions` **19/19** (+5 D118 steps: schema+myth-voice, side-gate, year-gate [king-cotton closes 1863+], bounded effects, cadence-unchanged), **0 pageerrors**; no-regression GREEN — `probe-desk` 13/13, `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`. No tactical file in the diff → 9 battles byte-identical by construction. **`wcag-auditor` surfaced + AUTO-FIXED 2 PRE-EXISTING AA contrast failures** in the decision-card render (the category label `var(--rule)`=#8a7350 4.15:1 → #957d57 4.54:1; the hinge badge #9c3b2e 2.75:1 → #cc5f50 4.51:1 text+border) — affected all 8 existing cards too; a genuine a11y improvement this milestone surfaced.
- **Reversible?** Yes (data + 1 render-label line + 2 contrast fixes). **Impact:** the decision deck is now a real strategic fork-surface (12 cards, both sides) that bends the war's momentum/foreign/manpower trajectory toward the now-complete 24-ending spectrum; the player STEERS toward the endings by choosing. E1 (alt-history) is now substantially complete; NEXT in the locked order = E4-i2 (the war-END screen wiring), then E2 (codex/tutorial/presets), E3 (the full WCAG-AA pass).

## D117 — E1 deepening increment 4: **ALTERNATE ENDINGS — the GROUNDED tier, US-half** (8 plausible/long-shot Union end-states) — completes the labeled spectrum on both sides — [CLAUDE] (overnight run, 2026-06-23)
- **What shipped (the prompt's split-into-two-vetted-increments directive completed; finished from the on-disk WIP — the 8 entries were already inserted, this milestone is the probe-guard fix + the D117 probe block + the full vet + bug-hunt + doc trail, NOT a re-insert):** the 8 GROUNDED Union end-states from the D114 catalog, authored the SAME way as the D116 Confederate ones in `_END_CATALOG` (`src/83-endings.js`) — each with `tier:"plausible"/"longshot"`, a play-opened `precond(C)`, a performance `gate(C)`, a secure-hint, and a citation-grade anti-Lost-Cause counterfactual. **The 8:** A Reconstruction that holds (an emancipation war won + the 1864 mandate → the 14th/15th enforced past 1877) · An early abolition war (the radical-emancipation gambit → freed & armed from the start) · Hard war, early (Sherman's doctrine early → the Confederacy collapses fast) · Reunion without emancipation (emancipation refused → the war's purpose abandoned, named as the moral failure it would have been, NOT a triumph) · The 1862 knockout (an overwhelming early record → Richmond falls early) · Forty acres enforced (a radical mandated victory → land, not just freedom) · A compensated, bloodless end (an early settlement with emancipation in hand → slavery ends by purchase) · The general strike triumphant (Du Bois's general strike in full). Tiers: 3 plausible (reconstruction-holds, early-abolition, hardwar-collapse) + 5 longshot (reunion-no-emancipation, 1862-knockout, forty-acres, compensated-bloodless, general-strike). The US empty-state teaser was flipped so BOTH sides now advertise the full labeled spectrum (plausible → long shot → fantastical) with their own grounded + fantastical examples.
- **The usctUnlocked design call (carried over from the D116 ⚠ note):** `M.usctUnlocked` AUTO-trips at year≥1863 (`70-manpower.js:132`) even with no player choice, so it is NOT a valid "the player chose radical emancipation" opener. The **early-abolition** opener therefore keys off the `us-radical-emanc` WILD card (a deliberate gambit), and **compensated-bloodless** uses `em.year≤1862 && em.issued` — both probe-locked against the bare-1863 auto-trip false-positive.
- **Architecture — PURE READ-OUT (D111/D112/D115/D116 pattern; byte-identical combat BY CONSTRUCTION):** the 8 entries reuse the existing `_end*` accessors + the D116 banded `endRenderSection`; no new accessor, no new colour, no new UI structure. `endScan` reads only strategic state, writes nothing; **no tactical file touched; no combat/tick/resolve/bridge path reads `end*`** (independently grep-re-confirmed — the only consumers are `src/81-divergence.js:293` + `src/82-after-action.js:331`, both render-only guarded embeds) → 9 battles byte-identical by construction. Diff = `src/83-endings.js` + `tools/probe-endings.mjs` + the generated HTML only. NO RNG.
- **The probe-guard fix (the milestone's first task):** the D115-bug-hunt step's two assertions `Ues.indexOf('plausible, long shot')>=0`→throw and `Ues.indexOf('Reconstruction that holds')>=0`/`'compensated end'`→throw were correct ONLY while US was fantastical-only; with the US grounded tier shipped they would now FALSELY FAIL → REPLACED with positive checks ("the US empty-state now advertises the full spectrum" + names a US grounded example), mirroring the CS `Ccs.indexOf('plausible, long shot')<0`→throw. The cross-side leak guards were KEPT (US empty-state must still NOT contain 'Maximilian'/'British war'; CS must not name 'Lincoln living'/'Tsar'). +8 D117 probe steps added (fresh-US opens 0 · reconstruction-holds open→secure + won<4 negative + the 1877 cite · early-abolition on the WILD not the bare-1863 auto-trip · hard-war + general-strike on their gambits + Du Bois cite · reunion-no-emancipation names slavery + "moral failure" · 1862-knockout open + year>1862 CLOSED · forty-acres + compensated-bloodless with Foner/Du Bois/Field-Order/Lincoln cites · CS side-gate surfaces no us-* ending · banded US render shows all three band labels).
- **Bug-hunt (5 diverse-lens Opus finders incl. a citation/anti-Lost-Cause lens × default-refute Opus verify + a completeness critic; 8 agents) → critic invariantsHold=TRUE** (all 5 invariants independently re-confirmed: purity/byte-identity grep-clean, side-gate + fresh-campaign-zero, anti-Lost-Cause framing + every cite spot-checked real & on-claim [Foner Reconstruction & Fiery Trial, Du Bois, McPherson, Grimsley, Sears; Compromise of 1877, Field Order No. 15 + Johnson's reversal, Frémont/Hunter revocations, the Peninsula Campaign, Lincoln's compensated-emancipation offers + the British 1833 Act], the WILD-vs-usctUnlocked distinction, defensive render). **1 confirmed MED, FIXED:** **(MED, citation)** the `us-general-strike` counterfactual said "tens of thousands took up arms for the Union" — understating the canonical ~180,000 USCT figure AND internally inconsistent with the game's OWN after-action text (`82-after-action.js:163` "some 180,000 Black soldiers") and the Du Bois source it cites → changed to "some 180,000 took up arms for the Union — roughly one in ten Union soldiers by war's end." **Critic gaps (all LOW, non-blocking):** the probe hand-sets `victoryReady`/`resolved1864` rather than driving them through play (acceptable for a pure read-out; the critic independently confirmed a 1862 `victoryReady` IS organically reachable via `victory.js:129` enemyWill≤18 / recognition≥60, so the two year≤1862 endings are not dead code); the `resolved1864` write lives in the frozen 1864-clock engine (pre-existing state D112/D113 already depend on — the "peace" endings' framing is sound).
- **Vet (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓`; `probe-endings` **24/24** (16 prior + 8 D117 steps), **0 pageerrors** (re-run GREEN after the citation fix); no-regression GREEN — `probe-divergence` 14/14, `probe-afteraction` 10/10 (the two guarded-embed hosts), `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`. No combat/tactical file in the diff → 9 battles byte-identical by construction. `wcag-auditor` AA (data-only increment, no new colours; band labels 5.38/10.20/6.41:1, prose blends 9.46–11.41:1, chip borders ≥3:1, CVD word+glyph triple-encode — no edits).
- **Reversible?** Yes (data + render; pure read-out). **Impact:** the alt-history payoff layer is now a complete labeled grounded→fantastical spectrum on BOTH sides (24 of the D114 catalog's 24 end-states live: 12 CS + 12 US). E1's remaining work = the interactive HINGE-FORK decision cards that STEER toward the endings; then E4-i2 (the war-END wiring), E2, E3.

## D116 — E1 deepening increment 2: **ALTERNATE ENDINGS — the GROUNDED tier, CS-half** (8 plausible/long-shot Confederate end-states) + the labeled spectrum + an operating-model tuning — [CLAUDE] / [AARON] (overnight run, 2026-06-23)
- **What shipped (the 2nd build against the D114 catalog; the grounded tier, CS-half first per the prompt's split-into-two-vetted-increments directive):** the 8 GROUNDED Confederate end-states from the D114 catalog, authored the SAME way as the D115 fantastical ones in `_END_CATALOG` (`src/83-endings.js`) — each with `tier:"plausible"/"longshot"`, a play-opened `precond(C)`, a performance `gate(C)`, a secure-hint, and a citation-grade anti-Lost-Cause counterfactual. **The 8:** Recognized independence (recognition courted → ≥60 / a recognition victory) · A negotiated peace (Northern will cracking → a reachable peace) · An emancipated Confederacy (the Cleburne lever → a won war; slavery named plainly, the founding creed gutted) · Stalemate / two Americas (a long even war → an exhausted deadlock) · Fabian survival (the husbanding amnesty lever → Northern patience worn) · A Trans-Mississippi redoubt (an earned survival into 1865 without an outright win) · King Cotton coerces Europe (the burn-the-cotton gambit → the famine forces recognition) · The Northwest secedes (the Copperhead gambit → the Northern home front fractures). Tiers: 4 plausible (recognized-independence, negotiated-peace, stalemate, fabian) + 4 longshot (emancipated-confederacy, trans-mississippi, king-cotton, northwest).
- **Above-and-beyond — THE LABELED SPECTRUM (D114):** `endRenderSection` rewritten to band the endings along a CVD-safe spectrum (Plausible → Long shot → Fantastical, `_END_TIERS`, the AA-vetted endings palette 4.85/9.20/5.77:1; the word label carries meaning, colour is the redundant third channel). Within a band reached lead, then within-reach; an unopened band is omitted. The grounded + fantastical endings now read as one honest spectrum.
- **Architecture — PURE READ-OUT (D111/D112/D115 pattern; byte-identical combat BY CONSTRUCTION):** new accessors `_endEnemyWill`/`_endYear`/`_endLever`/`_endRecogForeclosed` (all pure reads, defensive on partial saves — `_endEnemyWill` defaults HIGH=100 so a missing will never falsely opens a "cracking" path). `endScan` reads only strategic state, writes nothing; **no tactical file touched; no combat/tick/resolve/bridge path reads `end*`** (grep-confirmed) → battles byte-identical by construction. Diff = `src/83-endings.js` + `tools/probe-endings.mjs` + the generated HTML only. NO RNG. ⚠ NOTE for the US-half (next/D117): `M.usctUnlocked` AUTO-trips at year≥1863 (`70-manpower.js:132`) so it is NOT a valid "player chose radical emancipation" opener — use the wild card / `em.year≤1862` (staged design in the scratchpad `US-half-grounded-design.md`).
- **Bug-hunt (5 diverse-lens Opus finders incl. a citation/anti-Lost-Cause lens × default-refute Opus verify + a completeness critic; 11 agents) → critic invariantsHold=TRUE** (no-fudge + byte-identity + pure-read-out + anti-Lost-Cause + fresh-campaign-clean all independently re-confirmed; all 8 counterfactuals + every cited book/author verified real & on-topic). **4 confirmed + 2 critic-additional, ALL FIXED + probe-locked:** **(MED)** cs-trans-mississippi's precond `year≥1865 && victoryReady==null` reduced to passage-of-time (victoryReady==null is the fresh default) → a LOST war that merely crawled to 1865 opened it → moved the earned `won≥3` signal INTO the precond + a probe negative (won=0 at 1865 must not open). **(LOW)** the US empty-state teaser promised plausible/long-shot tiers + named unbuilt US grounded endings (the US side is fantastical-only until the US-half ships) → side-aware: the US teaser names only the real US fantastical endings + "labeled fantastical", the CS teaser keeps the full spectrum framing. **(LOW)** cs-fabian-survival opened on `fortifyPorts` — the conceptual OPPOSITE of Fabian trade-space-for-time (it HOLDS the ports) → the husbanding amnesty lever only. **(LOW)** cs-negotiated-peace's secureHint pinned the moment to Nov 1864 but the `enemyWill≤30` gate branch had no year bound → softened the hint. **(critic-LOW ×2)** the trans-miss unearned-open had no probe coverage → added; cs-stalemate's momentum band [0.42,0.66] risked being a Goldilocks gap → relaxed to enemyWill≤55 & [0.40,0.66] for reachability.
- **Vet (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓`; `probe-endings` **16/16** (+8 D116 steps incl. the fix-locks), **0 pageerrors**; no-regression GREEN — `probe-divergence` 14/14, `probe-afteraction` 10/10 (the two guarded-embed hosts), `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`. No combat/tactical file in the diff → 9 battles byte-identical by construction. `wcag-auditor` AA (all band/chip colours 5.15–9.96:1, CVD word+glyph, no edits needed).
- **[AARON] OPERATING-MODEL TUNING (popup, AskUserQuestion ×4, mid-run "clear future autonomous loops"):** (1) **Phase E order = the locked order** — after the grounded endings: hinge-fork cards → E4-i2 war-END screen → E2 codex/tutorial/presets → E3 full WCAG-AA pass. (2) **E4-i2 (the frozen-loop war-END override) = proceed autonomously** via the openWarDept/warWonScreen override pattern, full vetting + bug-hunt, push if green; surface only a NEW design fork. (3) **Bug-hunt scale = the FULL 9-agent hunt every time**, even on low-risk pure-read-out increments — never lower the bar. (4) **Surfacing = EVEN FEWER interruptions** — stop the loop ONLY for irreversible/money forks; if something contradicts a shipped teaching, LOG it and proceed with my best call (flagging it in the docs) rather than pausing. (REFINES the D61 surfacing bar toward maximum autonomy.)
- **Reversible?** Yes (data + render; pure read-out). **Impact:** the alt-history payoff layer is now a labeled grounded→fantastical spectrum on the Confederate side; the US grounded half (D117) is staged and next.

## D115 — E1 deepening increment 1: **ALTERNATE ENDINGS** — the fantastical-tier alt-history RESULTS a well-played war earns (4/side), surfaced in "Your War vs History" — [CLAUDE] (overnight run, 2026-06-23)
- **What shipped (the first build against the D114 catalog; Aaron's build-order pick = FANTASTICAL tier first):** a catalog of **8 fantastical alternate ENDINGS** (4 CS, 4 US), each **EARNED** — its `precond(C)` OPENS the path (a wild-card gambit played, an emancipation choice, or a reachable victory) and its `gate(C)` SECURES it (war momentum via `vicMomentum` / the recognition level / the won-count / the 1864 election won). `precond && gate` → **★ Reached**; `precond && !gate` → **◇ Within reach** (with a "to secure it" hint). Surfaced as an "Alternate endings — the fantastical tier" section in the **"Your War vs History"** tab (D111) + a compact line in the **after-action report** (D112). Each carries a citation-grade counterfactual, explicitly labeled *fantastical* (the D114 labeled spectrum), set beside what really happened. **CS:** A British war (Trent escalates) · Foreign legions land (Maximilian) · Stonewall lives · The Golden Circle (a slave-power Caribbean reach — named honestly, NOT valorized). **US:** The rapid-fire war (repeaters/Gatlings) · A Russo-American century · Lincoln lives Reconstruction (poignant) · The 13th, early.
- **ARCHITECTURE — a PURE READ-OUT (the D111/D112 pattern; byte-identical combat by construction):** new `src/83-endings.js` (`_END_CATALOG` with per-entry `precond`/`gate` predicate fns · `endScan` → `{reached, near}` · `endRenderSection(C, {compact})` · the state accessors `_endMomentum`/`_endWildPlayed`/`_endRecognition`/`_endWon`/`_endVictoryReady`/`_endElected`/`_endEmancipation`). `endScan` READS only existing strategic state and WRITES NOTHING. **No tactical file touched; no combat/tick/resolve path reads `end*`** (critic grep-confirmed) → the combat layer + all battles byte-identical by construction. Wired via ONE guarded line each in `divRenderTab` (81) + `aarRenderReport` (82) — byte-identical when the module is absent. Bare-name globals; `end`/`_end` prefix; render never mutates/saves; NO RNG. Side-gated (US sees only US endings, CS only CS).
- **Citation-grade, anti-Lost-Cause (critic re-verified all 8):** the Trent Affair defused Nov 1861 (Howard Jones); Maximilian never intervened, executed 1867; Jackson died May 10 1863 (Robertson); the Golden Circle / Walker-filibuster slave-empire ambition died with the Confederacy, named as the slave-power ambition it was (Robert May, *The Southern Dream of a Caribbean Empire*) — NOT a triumph; the 1863 Russian fleet visits were a gesture; Lincoln assassinated by Booth Apr 14 1865 → Andrew Johnson + the unraveling of Reconstruction (Foner); the 13th passed the House Jan 31 1865, ratified Dec 1865 (Foner). The D111 inline-prose trust boundary applies.
