- **Adversarial bug-hunt (Workflow: 5 diverse-lens Opus finders × default-refute verify + a completeness critic; 9 agents) → critic invariantsHold=TRUE** (no-fudge + byte-identity + all 8 citations + robustness all independently confirmed). **2 confirmed, both FIXED + probe-locked:** **(MED)** the cs-british-war source string carried a literal `&amp;` but is rendered through `_endEsc` → it double-escaped to `&amp;amp;` ("Blue &amp;amp; Gray Diplomacy") → changed to a plain `&` (matching the sibling `_divWILD_HIST` convention; `htmlEsc` escapes it once). **(LOW)** the empty-state teaser hardcoded both sides' examples ("a British war … Lincoln living …") regardless of side → side-filtered the examples so a player is never promised the other side's endings (consistent with the side-gated `endScan`). The citation FACTS were all confirmed accurate (the only defect was the render-layer double-escape).
- **Vetting (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓`; `probe-endings` **9/9** (fresh-empty · earned [gambit opens → within-reach; performance gate → reached] · side-awareness · the CS Trent/Golden-Circle + US Lincoln-lives/13th-early triggers · the CVD chip + fantastical label + counterfactual · pure-read-out · the divRenderTab embed · **the bug-hunt double-escape + side-filtered-teaser locks**), **0 pageerrors**; no-regression GREEN — `probe-divergence` 14/14, `probe-afteraction` 10/10 (the two guarded-wiring hosts, unaffected), `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`. **No combat/tactical file in the diff** (`src/83-endings.js` new + one guarded line each in 81/82 + the manifest + the generated HTML + the new probe) → the 9 battles byte-identical by construction. `wcag-auditor` AA (the section's `#e0795a`/`#c9c06a`/`#9b8560` measured 5.77/9.20/4.85:1, no change; CVD triple-encode confirmed). (Reversible; non-negotiables honored: D74/D92 no-output-gate, byte-identical combat, citation-grade anti-Lost-Cause, the labeled-spectrum + no-thumb-on-the-scale D54 framing.) **NEXT (E1 continues against D114):** the GROUNDED tier (the 16 plausible/longshot end-states — Recognized independence, Negotiated peace, Reconstruction-that-holds, etc.) + interactive HINGE-FORK decision cards on the `decResolve` engine that STEER toward these endings; then E4-i2 (the war-END wiring); E2/E3. ✅

## D114 — E1 ALT-HISTORY END-STATE CATALOG — Aaron's elicitation (LOCKED) + build order — [AARON] (AskUserQuestion ×3 rounds, 2026-06-23)
Aaron directed (via popup) a major EXPANSION of the reachable alternate-history RESULTS for skilled play on BOTH sides — the payoffs the E1 hinge-forks + the D111 divergence ledger + the D112 graded report build toward. He also **reordered the queue: E1 (alt-history hinge-forks) AHEAD of E4-i2 (the frozen-loop negotiated-peace war-end).** All options offered were accepted, and he asked for more each round (more remain bankable). The LOCKED catalog (24 end-states + 7 axes + a labeled spectrum):
- **CONFEDERATE end-states (12):** (grounded→longshot) Recognized independence (the 1862 window won) · Negotiated peace 1864 (break Northern will to the election) · Emancipated Confederacy (Cleburne's path fully — independence but the founding creed gutted; anti-Lost-Cause, named honestly) · Stalemate / two Americas (an exhausted armistice) · Fabian survival (Johnston's pure-defensive attrition outlasts Northern patience) · Trans-Mississippi redoubt (Kirby-Smithdom survives west of the river) · King Cotton coerces Europe (the embargo finally pays) · The Northwest secedes (Copperhead success fractures the Union three ways). (fantastical) A British war (Trent escalates; the Royal Navy breaks the blockade) · Foreign legions land (Maximilian's army crosses the Rio Grande) · Stonewall lives (Jackson survives Chancellorsville) · The Golden Circle (a slave-power Caribbean/Latin-American empire).
- **UNION end-states (12):** (grounded→longshot) Reconstruction that HOLDS (the 14th/15th enforced past 1877 — no Jim Crow) · Early abolition war (1862) · Hard-war quick collapse (Sherman's doctrine early) · Reunion WITHOUT emancipation (the dark path — the war's purpose abandoned, named as the moral failure it is) · The 1862 knockout (Richmond falls early) · Forty acres enforced (emancipation + land redistribution carried through) · Compensated, bloodless end (Lincoln's road not taken — slavery ends by purchase) · The general strike triumphant (Du Bois's mass self-emancipation collapses the labor base). (fantastical/poignant) Lincoln lives Reconstruction (never assassinated; leads it himself) · The repeater/Gatling war (rapid-fire a year early) · A Russo-American century (the Tsar's fleet stays and fights) · The 13th early (abolition by amendment from the start).
- **REWARD AXES (7) — what "done right" unlocks the biggest divergences:** battlefield dominance · the economy/blockade game · the political/home-front game · the emancipation/manpower game · the command game (the GM depth-chart/rating system) · engineering & technology · intelligence & cavalry.
- **Wildness ceiling:** a LABELED SPECTRUM — grounded/plausible endings by default; fantastical endings unlocked by fantastical play and explicitly tagged "fantastical" (so the teaching line — what really happened — always stays clear). Honors the tiered wild-card design (D28) + the no-thumb-on-the-scale law (D54).
- **BUILD ORDER (Aaron):** **the FANTASTICAL tier FIRST** (the most fun/"video-game" payoffs), then backfill the grounded teaching forks. The end-state DETECTION + the ledger/report surfacing build on `divScan` (81-divergence.js) + the wild-card catalog (80-victory.js) + the `decResolve`/`_decApply` hinge-fork engine (32-decisions.js); each end-state carries a citation-grade counterfactual (the D111 inline-prose trust model; failures named both sides). Implement as vetted increments under E1; the actual war-END screens for these land with E4-i2.

## D113 — §12.3 THE ELECTION-SUPPORT RELIEF-BIND — a commissioned political general costs more capital to relieve before the 1864 vote (the bind Lincoln lived) — [CLAUDE] (overnight run, 2026-06-23)
- **What shipped (the GM §12.3 keystone the Commission move D109 was built around, now unblocked by the 1864-election state E4/D112):** a commissioned POLITICAL general (Banks/Butler/Sigel/McClernand — `commission.kind:"political"` + a `politicalValue`) delivers his constituency's votes to the war effort while he serves, so relieving him in the run-up to the 1864 election forfeits that support — an ADDITIVE surcharge on his relief cost (`_cmdReliefCost`) that rises year over year toward the vote (1862<1863<1864) and FALLS to zero the moment `clock.resolved1864` is set (the bind is spent). `surcharge = round((politicalValue/100) * windowByYear[year] * surchargeMax)`, bounded [0, surchargeMax=14]. A new teaching tell on the active-general card names the constituency + the `+N capital` cost ("Lincoln lived this bind"). Teaches the §12.3 command-politics lesson concretely: Banks in command costs commandLeadership 49 vs Grant's 87 (D109) AND can't be cheaply dumped before November 1864.
- **PICK RATIONALE (decide-&-log; the kickoff named it "pair with E4"):** chose the relief-bind over the frozen-loop negotiated-peace war-end (E4-i2) for an autonomous tick because it is a contained, well-authorized command-economy extension (the code already flagged it "the follow-on" at 35-command.js:985) with no frozen-loop touch and no design fork. Aaron then reordered the queue (E1 hinge-forks ahead of E4-i2); this D113 was already mid-vet so it lands first.
- **ARCHITECTURE — UNION-ONLY, byte-identical until you field a political general (D74 no-fudge):** new pure helpers `_cmdElectionBindCfg`/`_cmdPoliticalValue`/`_cmdElectionWindow`/`_cmdElectionSupportSurcharge`; `_cmdReliefCost` adds the surcharge. `_cmdReliefCost` is read ONLY by `cmdAppoint` (the relief economy) + `_cmdActiveCard` (display) — **NEVER by `commandLeadership` or any combat/bridge/tick path** (grep-confirmed) → the bridge + the 9 battles are byte-identical by construction. The surcharge is EXACTLY 0 for every starting-roster general (none is `commission.kind:"political"`), the entire CS side, and outside the window → `_cmdReliefCost` is byte-identical for the whole roster in every year; the only behavioral change is a UNION political general the player chose to commission AND field in the 1862-64 pre-election window. No new save state; no `_SAVE_VER` bump; NO RNG. Data: `ratings.json` `electionReliefBind` (surchargeMax 14 · windowByYear {1862:0.15,1863:0.45,1864:1.0} · prov Inferred + 2 real sources — McPherson, T. Harry Williams).
- **Adversarial bug-hunt (Workflow: 5 diverse-lens Opus finders × default-refute verify + a completeness critic; 10 agents) → critic invariantsHold=False on ONE point (the CS over-application), all 3 confirmed FIXED:** **(MED, the anti-Lost-Cause catch, FIXED + probe-locked)** the bind + the "Lincoln lived this bind" tell had no side gate, so a CS player who commissioned + fielded Floyd/Pillow (CS political generals, pv 78) in 1862 got the surcharge AND a fabricated Lincoln attribution — but the Confederacy held no 1864 presidential election (Davis served one six-year term from Feb 1862) → **gated the bind to the Union side** (`if (!C || C.side==="CS") return 0;`); CS political generals keep only their base relief cost, and CS is now fully byte-identical (CLAUDE.md #4). **(MED, FIXED + probe-locked)** a tampered NaN `windowByYear` entry escaped the `[0,1]` clamp (`typeof NaN==="number"`) → NaN surcharge → NaN could poison `C.clock.capital` in `cmdAppoint` (defensive — not player-reachable since the config is build-frozen + `JSON.parse` rejects literal NaN, but it violated the module's own `isFinite` idiom) → restored the `|| !isFinite(w)` guard in `_cmdElectionWindow` + hardened the surcharge guard to `!(w>0)`. **(LOW, FIXED)** the "rises toward November 1864" prose overstated the year-granular (flat-across-1864) window → softened to "rises year over year (1862<1863<1864)" in the comment + the data `_note` (a month-granular ramp is a logged future refinement). Critic confirmed NO-FUDGE holds (only `cmdAppoint`/`_cmdPoolHTML`/`_cmdActiveCard` read `_cmdReliefCost`; nothing combat) and the US framing is accurate + attributed.
- **Vetting (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓`; `probe-command` **76/76** (+7 D113 steps: byte-identity for the roster · the surcharge for a US political general in the 1864 window · relaxation after `resolved1864` · the year ramp [0,2,5,11]≤14 · the teaching tell · **the Union-only side gate [CS Floyd → 0 surcharge, no Lincoln tell]** · **the NaN-windowByYear safety**), **0 pageerrors**; `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346` (Classic paints); `probe-desk` 13/13, `t1probe` 14/14 — 0 pe. **No combat path reads the relief cost** → the 9 battles byte-identical by construction (the headless `load` battle battery is the documented THREE-CDN env-stall; shares no code path). `wcag-auditor` AA (the new `#cbb27a` tell measured 8.41:1, no change). (Reversible; non-negotiables honored: D74/D92 no-output-gate, byte-identical combat, citation-grade anti-Lost-Cause, the empirical-vet-before-push gate.) **NEXT (Aaron-reordered):** **E1 deepening** — curated alt-history hinge-fork cards on the `decResolve` engine, **fantastical tier first** (Aaron's build-order pick), against the locked 24-end-state catalog (D114-to-come); then **E4-i2** (the negotiated-peace war-end wiring); then E2/E3. ✅

## D112 — PHASE E (S5 victory) increment 1: **THE GRADED AFTER-ACTION REPORT** — an A–F report card on the whole war + the divergence read-back + the Reconstruction coda — [CLAUDE] (overnight run, 2026-06-23)
- **The pivot (decide-&-log, within the cleared scope):** E1's divergence ledger shipped (D111); per the HANDOFF "NEXT" menu and V1-CHECKLIST Phase E, I started **E4 / S5 victory** at the named next item — **the rich graded after-action report** (D31.6: "per-domain grades · turn-by-turn divergence · casualties vs historical · citations"). Chose E4 over (A) the symmetric AI-GM ([hard], the §12 capstone) and (B) deeper E1 hinge-forks because E4 is the strategic arc's closing teaching payoff, it *summarizes the D111 ledger* (the explicit foundation D111 logged), and it is a low-risk pure read-out + one authorized frozen-base override. NOT a new fork → no surface to Aaron.
- **What shipped:** a period **"report card" on the whole war** — an **A–F letter grade** (the D94 scale: neutral 64 = C; A+ ≥90 "Legendary"; no gaming "S"; CVD triple-encoded letter + word + colour) for each domain the President actually ran: **The Battlefield** (win-rate + the casualty exchange), **The Treasury** (the price level vs the 1861 dollar), **Diplomacy & the Blockade** (the recognition contest, side-aware), **The Home Front** (public will + the 1864 election verdict), **the war's PURPOSE** (US = emancipation, graded by *timing* — declined < late < 1863; CS = an honest, NON-graded **"Confederate Cause"** panel), and **High Command** (`commandLeadership`, which folds in the appointed commander + the seated corps/divisions). Plus a headline **overall grade** (the mean of the *graded* domains), a **read-back of the D111 DIVERGENCE LEDGER** (`divScan`/`divIndex` — the index + the top entries), the **human cost** set beside the war's true historical toll (~750,000, Hacker 2011, above the traditional ~620,000), and a forward-looking **RECONSTRUCTION coda** keyed to the emancipation choice + the war's trajectory.
- **Surfaced TWO ways:** (1) a live **"After-Action"** President's-Desk tab (the **16th** — a standing report card on the war so far; PURE READ-OUT) and (2) the **war-end FINAL report** — `warWonScreen()` is **overridden** (the authorized frozen-base override pattern, like `openWarDept`/`openUpgrade`; D30.2) so the end of the campaign chain shows this graded report with the Reconstruction coda instead of the bare base dispatch. The override reproduces the base's Main-Menu wiring + `G.campaign = null` **verbatim** and adds NO sim write.
- **Architecture — a PURE READ-OUT + one frozen override (byte-identical combat BY CONSTRUCTION):** new `src/82-after-action.js` (`aarGrade` / `_aarDomains` / `aarOverall` / `_aarReconstruction` / `_aarGradeChip` / `_aarDomainRow` / `aarRenderReport` / `aarRenderTab` + the `warWonScreen` override). It READS already-resolved aggregate state (`C.stats`/`C.economy`/`C.blockade`/`C.morale`/`C.clock`/`C.strategy`/`C.president.emancipation` + `commandLeadership` + `moraleCompute`) and WRITES NOTHING to the sim (the only write anywhere is the base-preserved `G.campaign = null` in the override). **No tick, no own per-campaign state; NO tactical file touched; not registered in any `_t1Resolve`/`_t1InitAll`; grep-confirmed `aar*` appears in EXACTLY 3 files (the module, the shell tab dispatch, the probe) with ZERO hits on any combat/resolve/tick path → the combat layer + all 9 battles are byte-identical by construction.** Wired as one tab in `src/30-president-shell.js` (dispatch + `tabs[]` ×2 + the `openWarDept` button + click-wiring). Manifest: `82-after-action.js` module + `warWonScreen` override (collision gate: 2× ✓). Bare-name globals; `aar`/`_aar` prefix; render NEVER mutates/saves; NO RNG.
- **Citation-grade, anti-Lost-Cause (the D111 inline-prose trust model):** every historical anchor carries a real attribution — the ~750,000 toll (Hacker 2011); the Jan 1 1863 Proclamation + the border-state caution + ~180,000 Black soldiers (McPherson; Foner); the Reconstruction coda (13th/14th/15th Amendments → Reconstruction → "Redemption", the 1877 bargain, Jim Crow — Foner, *Reconstruction*); no European recognition + Antietam closing the autumn-1862 window (Howard Jones); the **Confederate cause stated plainly as slavery** in the founders' own words (Stephens's Cornerstone Speech, March 1861; the secession declarations — Dew, *Apostles of Disunion*; McPherson) and **NOT graded as a virtue** (score null, excluded from the GPA). Failures named even-handedly on BOTH sides (the CS dollar collapse; a Union "failure of will" for a *declined* or *late* emancipation).
- **Adversarial bug-hunt (Workflow: 5 diverse-lens Opus finders [no-fudge/byte-identity · citation/anti-Lost-Cause · grade-math/bounding/determinism · XSS/escaping/render-purity · UI/WCAG/partial-state] × default-refute Opus verify + a completeness critic; 7 agents):** **critic invariantsHold = TRUE** — (a) NO-FUDGE grep-confirmed (no combat path reads `aar*`; the module writes only the base-preserved `G.campaign=null`); (b) BYTE-IDENTITY confirmed (no tactical file in the diff; the override reproduces the base wiring verbatim); (c) CITATIONS confirmed accurate + attributed, the CS cause non-valorized. **1 confirmed LOW + 2 critic-additional LOW — ALL ONE ROOT CAUSE, FIXED + probe-locked:** the home-front domain read `moraleCompute(C).public` behind a `typeof`-only guard, but `typeof NaN === "number"` (and `moraleCompute`'s `_morClamp` is NaN-capable), so a non-finite public-will could (in an out-of-contract partial-save state) leak "NaN of 100", desync the "graded domains" count vs the GPA divisor, and mis-render the graded home-front row as the NON-graded "honest accounting" panel → **FIXED** with an `isFinite` guard at the read (`if (typeof pub !== "number" || !isFinite(pub)) pub = _aarNum(...)`, the D52.6 finite discipline the sibling press/morale modules already use) + the overall-panel count hardened to the same `isFinite` predicate as `aarOverall`; a new probe step stubs `moraleCompute → NaN` and locks the finite/graded/no-leak behavior. The critic's one citation note (Gallagher's *The Confederate War* is a Confederate-popular-will thesis, slightly off-target for "slavery was the cause" — a real book, not a fabrication) was **tightened** to McPherson (squarely on-claim) alongside the primary Dew cite (CLAUDE.md #4 rigor applied even to a non-defect).
- **Vetting (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓`; `probe-afteraction` **10/10** (the A–F scale incl. NaN/extreme coercion · fresh-baseline · strong-out-grades-weak · emancipation-timing order · the CS non-graded Cause panel + GPA exclusion · the divergence read-back + the 750k anchor · the emancipation-keyed Reconstruction coda · pure-read-out · **the NaN-moraleCompute regression** · the `warWonScreen` override renders + wires the button + nullifies the campaign), **0 pageerrors**; full no-regression GREEN — `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`, `bootprobe` ok, `t1probe` 14/14, `probe-desk` 13/13, `probe-divergence` 14/14, `probe-victory` 12/12, `probe-command` 69/69, `probe-officers` 15/15 — all 0 pageerrors. **No tactical file in the diff** (`src/82-after-action.js` new + `src/30-president-shell.js` tab + the manifest + the generated HTML + the new probe) → the 9 battles byte-identical by construction; `probe-ratings`/`probe-camp` were the documented **THREE-CDN headless `load`-stall** (they share NO code path with this diff and were GREEN at D111 hours earlier → structural byte-identity covers them). `wcag-auditor` AA (1 contrast fix `#937b55`→`#9b8560`, 4.42→5.07:1, matching the sibling 81-divergence header; all grade chips 5.67–9.62:1; CVD triple-encode confirmed). (Reversible; non-negotiables honored: D74/D92 no-output-gate, byte-identical combat, citation-grade anti-Lost-Cause, the context-budget rule.) **NEXT:** **E4-i2** — wire `victoryReady` (negotiated peace / recognition) to an actual war-END (not just a display hint) so the final report fires for a non-chain-complete victory, + the **§12.3 election-support relief-bind** (now unblocked — the After-Action home-front domain surfaces the 1864-election state; a political general's `_cmdReliefCost` could spike before the election, the historical bind). Then **E1 deepening** (curated `trigger.hinge` alt-history fork cards in `data/decisions.json` on the proven `decResolve` engine), **E2** (codex/glossary/tutorial/presets), **E3** (the dedicated WCAG-AA pass). The symmetric AI-GM ([hard]) remains the §12 capstone; **Q5 Chattanooga + Q6 USCT LAST** (Aaron's queue). ✅

## D111 — PHASE E (S3 alt-history) increment 1: **"YOUR WAR vs HISTORY"** — the alternate-history DIVERGENCE LEDGER + tracker + the emergent-only toggle — [CLAUDE] (overnight run, 2026-06-23)
- **The pivot (decide-&-log, within the cleared scope):** the GM §12 depth-chart arc is COMPLETE (Appoint/Relieve · Promote D102 · Corps D108 · Commission D109 · Division D110). Per the locked roadmap (D91: after C breadth → **E strategic S3–S5**; Chattanooga/USCT are Aaron's-queue-LAST) and the HANDOFF "NEXT" menu, I **pivoted to Phase E** and started at the first roadmap item, **E1 / S3 alt-history**, scoped to its named flagship first increment: the player-driven **"your war vs history" tracker + divergence log** (V1-CHECKLIST E1). Chose it over (A) the symmetric AI-GM ([hard], the §12 capstone) and (B) the §12.3 election-support relief-bind (needs the 1864-election state) because it's lower-risk (a pure strategic read-out), it's the named E1 flagship, it builds on the existing S1e `C.strategy` seeds (`wildsPlayed`/`enemyWill`/`victoryReady`/`armEnslaved` + the `_vicWILDCARDS` catalog), and it is the foundation the **E4 graded after-action report** will later summarize. NOT a new fork → no surface to Aaron.
- **What shipped:** a new **"Your War vs History"** desk tab (the 15th) — a living, dated DIVERGENCE LEDGER recording every point at which the player's war has left the documented record: each entry carries a **category** (Emancipation / Foreign / Northern politics / The war's trajectory / Wild cards), a **tier** (minor △ / major ◆ / radical ★ — distance from the record, CVD-safe glyph + word + colour), a short **when**, and the **historical counterfactual** ("In history, …") that is the lesson. A headline **DIVERGENCE INDEX** (0..100, a saturating sum of tier weights + a 5-step word ladder) measures distance from the past. Plus the **emergent-only toggle** (`G.settings.altHistoryEmergentOnly`): ON withholds the explicit ahistorical WILD-CARD gambits (one byte-identical-when-off guard at the top of `_vicWildSection` in 80-victory.js), so the war diverges ONLY through play. Detected hinges (all from reliable existing state): emancipation declined / early-1861 / 1862 / **late-1864-65** (US); CS arm-the-enslaved (Cleburne); foreign recognition (achieved / window-open-past-Antietam); the 1864 election repudiation (US) **/ the CS home-front break** (side-correct); the CS war-trajectory (reachable peace / recognition); and each wild card played (its plausibility tier → divergence tier).
- **Architecture — a PURE READ-OUT (the D106 OOB-board / D99 matchup pattern):** new `src/81-divergence.js` (`divScan` detector / `divIndex` / `_divChip` / `divRenderTab` / `divWireTab` / `divEmergentOnly` + the `_divWILD_HIST` counterfactual map + the tier/category tables). `divScan` READS `C.strategy`/`C.president.emancipation`/`C.blockade`/`C.clock`/`C.stats` and WRITES NOTHING; the ONLY write anywhere is `divWireTab` flipping `G.settings.altHistoryEmergentOnly` + `saveLocal`. **No tick, no per-campaign state of its own** (the one persisted bit is the global setting, sanitized on read). **No tactical file touched → the combat layer + all 9 battles are byte-identical by construction.** Bare-name globals; div/_div prefix; NO RNG. Wired as one tab in `src/30-president-shell.js` (dispatch + `tabs[]` + `openWarDept` buttons + click-wiring).
- **Citation-grade, anti-Lost-Cause:** the counterfactuals are human-verified textbook facts with inline attribution (McPherson, Foner, Howard Jones, Levine, Robertson, Weber, Du Bois) — the SAME trust model as the sibling `_vicWILDCARDS`/`_vicWhyText` inline teaching prose (inline copy is not a "Verified" DATA record, so it sits outside the build-gate 4e ≥2-source scan of `data/*.json`; the trust boundary is now documented in the module header — a logged decide-&-log on bug-hunt MED #2, the proportionate fix matching the established inline-prose precedent). Failures named even-handedly on BOTH sides (a **late** US emancipation is recorded as a Union failing as plainly as a CS one; the no-thumb-on-the-scale D54 framing is explicit — divergence is distance from the record, never a "better" war).
- **Bug-hunt (Workflow: 5 diverse-lens finders × default-refute verify + a completeness critic; 11 agents):** **critic invariantsHold = TRUE** — (a) NO-FUDGE independently grep-confirmed (the div* symbols + the `altHistoryEmergentOnly` read appear in EXACTLY the 3 diff files; ZERO hits on any combat/resolve/tick path; `_vicWildSection` is called only from `vicRenderPaths`); (b) BYTE-IDENTICAL-WHEN-OFF by git diff (the guard falls through to the unchanged body when the toggle is false) + the probe (a fresh campaign reads empty). **2 confirmed + 4 critic-surfaced; resolved:** **(HIGH, FIXED)** `election-1864` had no `side==='US'` gate → a CS home-front break falsely fired the Northern-election entry with a Lincoln counterfactual (mis-attribution + backwards framing) → US-gated + a side-correct `cs-homefront-breaks` branch (probe-locked). **(MED, FIXED)** the emancipation detector was asymmetric — a **late** (1864/65) Proclamation, reachable in play, was silently dropped → an `ey≥1864` "delayed" branch, framed as a Union failing (probe-locked). **(MED, FIXED)** the critic's date correction — Davis's six-year term began **Feb 1862** (not "1861–1867") → CS counterfactual corrected. **(LOW, FIXED)** the toggle's `aria-pressed` was inverted vs state → a stable `aria-label="Emergent-only mode"` + `aria-pressed=(emergent?true:false)`. **(MED, DOCUMENTED)** the inline-counterfactual citation-gate coverage gap → trust boundary documented (above). **(LOW, DECLINED w/ rationale)** a `side==='CS'` gate on the recognition entries — declined: unlike the election entry, "Europe moves toward recognizing the Confederacy" is side-neutral and historically correct for EITHER player (no mis-attribution) and is unreachable for US by normal play → no correctness defect.
- **Vetting (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓`; `probe-divergence` **14/14** (incl. the 2 bug-hunt regression steps), **0 pageerrors**; full no-regression GREEN — `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`, `probe-desk` 13/13, `probe-victory` 12/12 (Paths-to-Victory + the `_vicWildSection` guard, default-off renders the gambits as before), `probe-command` 69/69, `t1probe` 14/14, `bootprobe` ok — all 0 pageerrors. **No tactical file in the diff** (`src/81-divergence.js` new + `src/30-president-shell.js` tab + the one `src/80-victory.js` guard + the generated HTML + the new probe) → the 9 battles byte-identical by construction; the headless `load` battle battery is the documented THREE-CDN env-stall and shares no code path. `wcag-auditor` AA (2 contrast fixes on the new UI; all tier/index colours already passed; CVD triple-encode + role/aria-label + aria-pressed confirmed). (Reversible; non-negotiables honored: D74 no-fudge, byte-identical-when-off, citation-grade anti-Lost-Cause, context-budget rule.) **NEXT:** E1 deeper hinge-fork authoring + the E4 graded after-action report that summarizes this ledger; the symmetric AI-GM ([hard]); the §12.3 election-support relief-bind. ✅

## D110 — RATING SYSTEM **Q12: THE DIVISION SUB-TIER** (seat pool generals into division billets nested under a seated corps — the next rung of the GM depth-chart over the OOB tree) — [CODEX code + CLAUDE close-out] (overnight run, 2026-06-23)
The next rung of RATING-SYSTEM-DESIGN §12.1's billet tree (Army → Corps → **DIVISION** → Brigade), built HIERARCHICALLY on the Q10 corps depth-chart (D108). Beyond seating the army's I–IV Corps, the President now SEATS pool generals into each seated corps's **DIVISION** billets (3 per corps), spending `seatCost` (2) political capital (`C.clock.capital` — the existing GM currency, no new resource per §12). A division is staffable ONLY UNDER a SEATED corps (the real chain of command — a division belonged to a corps), so the depth chart becomes a TREE the President builds top-down.
- **PROVENANCE / CLOSE-OUT (the [AARON] hand-off):** the Q12 CODE + DATA + PROBE were authored in an interrupted Claude-Code session, finished + committed (`7b64d2e`) + pushed by **Codex** on this machine. Codex shipped a sound, builds-GATE-OK, byte-identical-to-source feature **but did NOT run the adversarial bug-hunt or write any doc trail** (a PROPER-VETTING gap vs CLAUDE.md #3 + a record-in-repo gap vs #5). This entry + the bug-hunt below are the **CLAUDE close-out** that brings D110 to the project's "done" bar. (No code was reverted — the commit is sound; this adds the missing vetting + documentation, plus the one bug-hunt fix.)
- **HOW IT REACHES THE FIGHT (no new combat path; D74/D94 no-fudge):** seated division commanders only shape the army's LEADERSHIP facet — an INPUT the bridge already reads — via a small BOUNDED lift folded into `commandLeadership`, summed exactly like the corps lift: `_cmdDivLift` = clamp(Σ over seated divisions of (`effDivRating`−64)×`perSlotWeight`, ±`liftCap`) = clamp(Σ×0.03, ±2). `effDivRating` = the general's GM OVR (`_cmdGenRating`, carries the Q9 promotion lift) minus `belowGradePenalty` (4) when his current grade is below `preferredGrade`. The lift is DELIBERATELY SMALLER than the corps lift (cap 2 vs 4, weight 0.03 vs 0.05) so the influence hierarchy holds — **army (~±17, §27) > corps (±4) > divisions (±2):** a lower tier is a smaller modifier, never dominant. It reaches combat ONLY through the existing `commandLeadership → bridge LEADERSHIP facet` pipe, NEVER the scoreboard (build-gate 4d scans `35-command.js`). NO RNG.
- **THE GRADE-FIT (a division was a Maj. Gen.'s billet in BOTH armies):** `preferredGrade` is a bare Maj. Gen. for US and CS alike — distinct from the corps tier (a US corps a Maj. Gen.'s, a CS corps a Lt. Gen.'s). Many divisions were in fact led by Brigadier Generals early in the war, so any Brig. Gen. seated over a division leads `belowGrade` until promoted (the Q9 synergy, biting on both sides here). `data/ratings.json` `divisionCommand`: perCorps 3 · seatCost 2 · preferredGrade {US,CS: "Maj. Gen."} · perSlotWeight 0.03 · liftCap 2 · belowGradePenalty 4 · **Inferred** · 2 real sources (Glatthaar, *General Lee's Army*; Weigley, *A Great Civil War*).
- **BYTE-IDENTICAL until you seat a division:** no division seated → the Σ is over zero slots → lift 0 → `commandLeadership` unchanged → the sandbox + all 9 battles identical to HEAD `7d86fcf` (a vacant division contributes 0, NOT a penalty; `_cmdDivLift` has a fast-path returning 0 without walking the roster when `cmd.divisions` is empty/absent — so a pre-Q12 save with no record reads identically). **ONE BILLET PER MAN:** a general holds at most one of {army command, a corps, a division}; seating him in a division clears any corps/other division he held, and appointing/seating him HIGHER clears his division. **Vacating a corps CASCADES** — its orphaned divisions stop contributing lift, enforced at the READ layer (`cmdDivSeated`/`_cmdDivLift` filter divisions whose parent corps is no longer seated; `_cmdDivClean`:795 requires the parent corps seated) and cleaned on the next load-sanitize. `cmd.divisions = {corpsIdx:{divIdx:generalId}}` (nested) rides the save (no `_SAVE_VER` bump); `cmdInit` sanitizes a tampered/imported record on LOAD (drop array/non-object/out-of-range/bogus/dead/army-commander/corps-holder/orphaned-corps/dupes — the Q9/Q10/Q11 idiom). Writes ONLY `cmd.divisions` + `C.clock.capital`. Carries the Q9/Q10/Q11 hardening verbatim: the `isFinite` capital guard, the commission gate (`_cmdByIdRoster`+`cmdCommissioned`), and debit-the-rounded-gate-value.
- **THE TEACHING (anti-Lost-Cause, both sides):** a corps was only as good as its divisions — A. P. Hill's hard-hitting "Light Division" and Hood's and Pickett's divisions were the cutting edge of Lee's corps, **as were the Union's hard-fighting divisions under Hancock's II Corps (Gibbon, Barlow)** — while a corps with weak divisions underdelivered. New UI: division sub-rows nested under each seated corps in the "The Corps Command — Depth Chart" section + a combined corps+division lift read-out; every new colour annotated ≥4.5:1 (WCAG-AA), every dynamic value `_cmdEsc`-escaped.
- **Adversarial bug-hunt (the close-out Codex skipped — run NOW on the shipped diff):** a Workflow of **5 diverse-lens Opus finders** (no-fudge/byte-identity · economy/determinism/bounding · citation/anti-Lost-Cause/no-fabricated-officers · XSS/escaping · UI/edge/save-tamper) × default-refute Opus verify + a completeness critic. **4 lenses CLEAN; 1 confirmed LOW, FIXED:** the division teaching exemplars (A. P. Hill's Light Division, Hood's, Pickett's) were **all-Confederate** while the rating mechanism is genuinely side-neutral and the sibling corps tier deliberately named both sides (the rout of Howard's XI Corps) — the exact Lost-Cause over-celebration pattern CLAUDE.md #4 guards against. FIXED (copy-only, byte-identical): added the Union counterpart (Hancock's II Corps divisions, Gibbon/Barlow — accurate at Gettysburg/Wilderness/Spotsylvania) to both the player-facing `divFoot` string and the `divisionCommand` `_note`. The save-tamper lens independently executed an 18-vector battery against `_cmdDivClean` (Array/null/non-object/out-of-range/float-key/bogus/dead/army-commander/corps-holder/orphaned-corps/cross-corps-dupe) — all sanitize correctly; the no-fudge lens grep-confirmed no combat path reads `cmd.divisions`; escaping confirmed safe (`htmlEsc` at render + `_cmdEsc` in the UI).
- **Vetting (the offline gates GREEN; the headless `load` suite ENV-BLOCKED):** `GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓`; the committed `civil_war_generals.html` is **byte-identical to the source build** (empty post-build diff); `probe-command` **69/69** (+11 Q12 steps: config + hierarchy [div liftCap < corps liftCap], byte-identical-when-empty + absent-record, the hierarchy gate [division only under a seated corps], spend/gate/re-seat/vacate, the read-layer cascade, one-billet-per-man, render) with **0 pageerrors**; `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346` (Classic paints); `t1probe` + `bootprobe` ok. **No tactical/combat file is in the D110 diff** (`src/35-command.js` + `data/ratings.json` + `tools/probe-command.mjs` + the generated HTML only) → the 9 battles are byte-identical to Q11 **by construction**. **ENV:** the strategic no-regression `load` battery (`probe-desk/ratings/camp/officers/field/oob` + the 9 battle probes) was blocked by the documented **THREE-CDN headless `load`-stall** — jsdelivr rate-limited this machine's headless client after the first clean load, so only the first chromium launch per session loads (re-running the known-good `probe-command` reproduced the stall on demand); these probes share NO Q12 code path and were all GREEN in the Q11 vetting hours earlier. The post-bug-hunt balance fix is copy-only (a `_note` metadata field + one UI string) → combat byte-identity is unchanged. (Reversible; non-negotiables honored: D74/D92 no-output-gate, the no-fudge wall, citation-grade both-sides data.)

## D109 — RATING SYSTEM **Q11: THE COMMISSION MOVE** (bring documented POLITICAL GENERALS into the officer pool for political capital; the GM "Commission" move) — [CLAUDE] (overnight run, 2026-06-23)
The GM depth-chart move of RATING-SYSTEM-DESIGN §12.2/§12.3 ("Commission — bring a new officer into the pool"). Beyond the generals the war hands you, the President may now **COMMISSION the documented POLITICAL GENERALS** — US **Banks**, **Butler**, **Sigel**, **McClernand**; CS **Floyd**, **Pillow** — into his officer pool for **political capital** (`C.clock.capital` — the existing GM currency, no new resource per §12), after which they are appointable / promotable / seatable like any general. A new Command-desk **"Commission an officer — the political generals"** section lists each with his (LOW) combat OVR beside his (HIGH) political value, his constituency, an honest one-line record, and a Commission control (gated on capital + the `maxCommissions` slate). Teaches the GM layer's central command-politics lesson (§12.3, the keystone): political generals were **cheap to commission and weak in the field** — Lincoln and Davis took them for the factions, votes, and immigrant regiments they delivered, not their generalship — so once you appoint or seat one, his low OVR drags the army's leadership, the bind Lincoln lived. **PICK RATIONALE (decide-&-log within the cleared NEXT menu):** chose Commission (option C) over the symmetric AI-GM (B, [hard]) and the DIVISION sub-tier (B-alt, lower-risk-but-thinner) because it fills the GM layer's missing teaching keystone (the political-general bind the §12.3 anti-gamey constraints are built around), uses the proven `cmdAppoint`/`cmdSeatCorps`/`cmdPromote` byte-identical-until-used spine + the §8 person concept, and adds 6 new citation-grade historical figures. Scoped to a **pure pool-addition move** (no economy/election fork); the full §12.3 election-support relief-bind is logged as the explicit next increment (it needs the 1864-election state — here the relief COST is already taught via each officer's `relief` class through `_cmdReliefCost`).
- **ARCHITECTURE (byte-identical until the player commissions):** the commissionable officers live in a SEPARATE data array (`data/generals.json` → `sides[side].commissionPool`), so `_cmdSideGenerals` (the starting roster) and every byte-identity-critical path are UNTOUCHED. `cmdCommission` writes ONLY `cmd.commissioned` (an id list) + `C.clock.capital` + a reputation seed — NEVER the scoreboard (build-gate 4d scans `35-command.js`). With nobody commissioned, `_cmdRosterPlusCommissioned` === the bare roster (`roster.concat([])`), so the appoint/promote/corps pools, `commandLeadership`, and the 9 battles are identical to HEAD `8eb913a`. A commissioned officer reaches the fight ONLY by being appointed/seated like any general — no new combat read. `_cmdById` was widened to also resolve the commission pool (so a commissioned-then-appointed officer resolves everywhere); `_cmdByIdRoster` keeps the roster-only lookup for the byte-identity-critical seeds + the commission "not-a-roster-id" guard. `cmd.commissioned` rides the save; `cmdInit` seeds `[]` once + SANITIZES on load (drop dupes / bogus ids / roster ids / over-cap; non-array → array — the Q9/Q8b/Q10 idiom). NO `_SAVE_VER` bump. NO RNG.
- **THE TEACHING (rank ≠ competence; anti-Lost-Cause, both sides):** the 6 are authored with LOW combat OVR / HIGH political value — appointing a commissioned Banks fields commandLeadership **49** vs Grant's **87** (probe-verified). Honest records name the failures plainly on BOTH sides (Banks/Red River, Butler/Fort Fisher, Sigel/New Market, McClernand/Vicksburg insubordination; Floyd & Pillow/the Fort Donelson flight) AND credit the genuine contributions (Butler's 1861 "contraband" decision; Banks's Louisiana free-labor/emancipation work). The combat ratings are stamped **Inferred** (a calibrated reading); the biographies carry ≥2 real published sources each (gate-4e-clean). Persona command/tactical/discipline are calibrated so `fldOfficerSkillSeed` rounds EXACTLY to the entry skill (the existing oracle; verified for all 6). Data authored via a 12-agent **research+verify Workflow** (6 Opus historians + adversarial source-check + a completeness critic); the verifier's one HARD correction applied — **Pillow's rank → Brig. Gen.** (his "Maj. Gen." was a Mexican-War US-volunteer / Tennessee-state-militia title; in CSA service he rose only to brigadier — CLAUDE.md non-negotiable #4); plus Banks's Black-officer purge folded into his bio and Sigel's unconfirmed reprint year dropped.
- **DATA:** `data/generals.json` `sides.US.commissionPool` (us-banks/us-butler/us-sigel/us-mcclernand) + `sides.CS.commissionPool` (cs-floyd/cs-pillow); `data/ratings.json` `commission` config (costPolitical 2 · costProfessional 8 · maxCommissions 3 · Inferred · 2 src) + 6 new `generalPersonas`. New fns in the `35-command.js` Q11 block: `_cmdCommissionCfg`/`_cmdCommissionPool`/`_cmdCommissionEntry`/`_cmdCommissionCost`/`_cmdCommissionedClean`/`cmdCommissioned`/`_cmdCommissionedEntries`/`_cmdRosterPlusCommissioned`/`cmdCommission`/`_cmdCommissionRow`/`_cmdCommissionHTML` + `_cmdByIdRoster`; edits to `_cmdById` (pool fallthrough), `cmdInit` (seed/sanitize; the reputation+devTrack seeds now cover commissioned officers), `_cmdPoolHTML`/`_cmdPromotionsHTML`/`_cmdCorpsPoolFor`/`cmdWireTab` (use `_cmdRosterPlusCommissioned`), `cmdRenderTab` (+`_cmdCommissionHTML`).
- **Adversarial bug-hunt** (5 diverse-lens Opus finders [no-fudge/byte-identity · economy/determinism/bounding/cap · citation/anti-Lost-Cause/no-fabricated-officers · XSS/escaping · UI/WCAG/edge] × default-refute Opus verify + a completeness critic; **12 agents; invariantsHold = TRUE** — the critic independently grep-confirmed no combat path reads `cmd.commissioned`, `cmdCommission` writes EXACTLY `cmd.commissioned`+capital+reputation, the byte-identity-when-empty property, Pillow correctly Brig. Gen., every rendered string escaped, zero id-collision roster-vs-pool). **5 confirmed (2 MED + 3 LOW), ALL FIXED + the behavior-affecting ones probe-locked:** **(MED, the key one)** `cmdAppoint`/`cmdSeatCorps`/`cmdPromote` resolved their target via the widened `_cmdById` with no commissioned-state check → an UN-commissioned commission-pool officer could be appointed/seated/promoted at the function level (the gated UI never exposes it, but a direct call / stale corps-`<select>` value bypassed the commission economy) → added `if (!_cmdByIdRoster(side,id) && cmdCommissioned(C).indexOf(id)<0) return;` to all three (byte-identical for roster ids — the clause is always false for them; probe-locked). **(MED, UI)** a commissioned officer seated in a corps always rendered "I Corps" — the string for-in key was fed to `_cmdNum(kk,0)` (→ 0) → now `parseInt(kk,10)` (probe-locked). **(LOW)** the shared `cap` gate idiom passed a NaN balance (`typeof NaN==='number'`) at all 6 capital-gate sites (tampered-save only, pre-existing, not a Q11 regression) → hardened with `&& isFinite(clk.capital)` uniformly + cmdAppoint now debits the rounded `cap`. **(LOW)** the `maxCommissions` fallback default was 4 (copied from `_cmdCorpsSlots`) vs the shipped cap 3 → 3. **(LOW)** a commissioned officer past his service window still showed green "Commissioned / in your pool" while the pools dropped him → the row now reads muted "Commissioned · no longer in service". (Critic-"missed" were all CLEAN confirmations needing no action: render purity, id-collision-free, the combat-default source reads only the roster, the seed calibration, and the documented `load`-stall probe-env note.)
- **Vetting (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓`; **combat byte-identity STRUCTURAL** (no combat path reads `cmd.commissioned`) + **EMPIRICAL** — a 132-cell A/B (`.tmp/q11-ab.mjs`, base = HEAD `8eb913a`) over (side × appointee × date) confirms `commandLeadership`/`bridgeArmy.overall`/`leadership`/the auto-resolve margin reads byte-identical with nobody commissioned (re-confirmed after the bug-hunt guards — the guards are inert for roster ids), 0 pageerrors; `probe-command` **58/58** (+8 Q11 steps: config+pool · byte-identical-off · gate+debit+idempotence · enters-pools+political-general-fields-lower-leadership · the maxCommissions cap · save-tamper sanitize · no-output-gate · render — plus a bug-hunt step locking the function-level commission gate + the corps-label parse); FULL no-regression GREEN — `probe-oob` 16/16, `probe-desk` 13/13, `probe-ratings` 21/21, `probe-camp` 8/8, `probe-officers` 15/15, `probe-field` 13/13, `t1probe` 14/14, `bootprobe` ok, the 9 battles + `probe-csplayer` green (bullrun 15 · antietam 16 · fredericksburg 22 · gettysburg 16 · chancellorsville 24 · malvern 24 · shiloh 29 · vicksburg 18 · chickamauga 18 · csplayer 16); `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`; 0 pageerrors throughout; `wcag-auditor` AA (all new Commission-UI colours/opacities measured ≥ AA — no changes needed; the inactive-row nested-opacity note is covered by the WCAG inactive-component exemption). **ENV:** intermittent THREE-CDN headless `load`-stalls (vicksburg's 3D-readability step needed a standalone retry; the A/B needed a server restart) cleared by retry — the documented gotcha; the `domcontentloaded` A/B harness is the authoritative byte-identity spine. Committed + pushed after the full empirical gate passed. (Reversible; non-negotiables honored — D74/D92 no-output-gate, no fabricated officers, Pillow's rank corrected, citation-grade teaching, the empirical-vet-before-push gate.)

## D108 — RATING SYSTEM **Q10: THE CORPS DEPTH-CHART** (seat pool generals into corps billets over the D106 OOB tree; the GM depth-chart MOVE) — [CLAUDE] (overnight run, 2026-06-23)
The GM depth-chart MOVE of RATING-SYSTEM-DESIGN §12.1/§12.2/§12.4 ("an army version of waiver/trading/transfer"). Beyond appointing the ARMY commander (the existing `cmdAppoint` billet), the President now SEATS pool generals into the army's CORPS billets (I–IV Corps) over the D106 OOB tree, spending **political capital** (`C.clock.capital` — the EXISTING GM currency, also spent by appoint/promote; no new resource per §12). A new Command-desk **"The Corps Command — Depth Chart"** section lists the 4 billets, each showing its seated commander (name + grade + OVR + a below-grade tell) with a Vacate control, or "Vacant" + a pool `<select>` + a Seat control (gated on capital); a live read-out shows the net leadership the staffed corps add. Teaches real command organization — Lee's First (Longstreet) and Second (Jackson) Corps against the Army of the Potomac's churning corps commands; the rout of Howard's XI Corps at Chancellorsville. **PICK RATIONALE (decide-&-log within the cleared NEXT menu):** chose the depth-chart MOVES (option A) over the symmetric AI-GM (option B, flagged [hard] — an enemy-command shadow wired to combat risks byte-identity/no-fudge) because A extends the proven `cmdAppoint`/`cmdPromote` spine over the D106 substrate and is byte-identical-until-moved like Q9; the new officer-slot/vacancy concept the derived T15 corps lacked is the `cmd.corps` slot map + the side-aware grade-fit.
- **HOW IT REACHES THE FIGHT (no new combat path; D74/D94 no-fudge):** seated corps commanders only add a small BOUNDED lift to `commandLeadership(C)` — an INPUT the bridge already reads — via `_cmdCorpsLift(C)` = `clamp( SUM over seated slots of (effectiveCorpsRating − 64) × perSlotWeight, −liftCap, +liftCap )` (perSlotWeight 0.05, liftCap 4). `effectiveCorpsRating` = the general's GM OVR (`_cmdGenRating`, which already carries the Q9 promotion lift) minus `belowGradePenalty` (6) when his CURRENT grade is below the **SIDE-AWARE** `preferredGrade`. It reaches combat ONLY through the existing `commandLeadership → bridge LEADERSHIP facet` pipe — NEVER the scoreboard (build-gate 4d scans `35-command.js` + `tactical/T15-oob.js`). The army commander still weighs most (`(rating−64)×0.7`, swing ~±17); the corps lift (±4) is a secondary modifier (§27: small, never dominant).
- **THE SIDE-AWARE GRADE-FIT (historically precise, the Q9 synergy):** a US corps was a **Major General's** command; a CS corps a **Lieutenant General's** — the CS authorized the Lt. Gen. grade by Act of Sept. 18, 1862 and promoted Longstreet/Jackson that October to command the ANV's new corps (formally organized GO No. 6, Nov. 6, 1862). So on the shipped roster only CS **Stuart** (a Maj. Gen.) seats below grade over a corps (he leads stretched); promoting him to Lt. Gen. (Q9) removes the penalty. On the US roster every named general already meets the Maj. Gen. floor (a correct latent guard for any future junior/commissioned officer). `data/ratings.json` `corpsCommand` block: `slots 4 · labels [I..IV Corps] · seatCost 3 · preferredGrade {US:"Maj. Gen.", CS:"Lt. Gen."} · perSlotWeight 0.05 · liftCap 4 · belowGradePenalty 6 · prov Inferred · 2 real sources` (Sears 1988 on AoP corps creation; Glatthaar 2008 on ANV corps).
- **BYTE-IDENTICAL until the player seats a corps:** no corps seated → the SUM is over zero slots → lift 0 → `commandLeadership` unchanged → the sandbox + all 9 battles identical to HEAD `2d11bd7`. A vacant billet contributes 0 (NOT a baseline penalty — that would break byte-identity). `cmd.corps` rides the save (no `_SAVE_VER` bump); `cmdInit` SANITIZES a stale/tampered/imported record on LOAD (drop invalid slots/ids/dead generals/the army commander; one corps per general; array→{}). Constraints: one corps per general (reassign clears the old slot); the army commander cannot also hold a corps (excluded from the pool; `cmdAppoint` clears the appointee's corps); seating gates on capital and debits the SAME rounded value the gate compared (the Q9/Q8b hardened idiom); vacate is free; re-seating the same slot+general is a no-op (no re-charge). New fns (`src/35-command.js` Q10 block): `_cmdCorpsCfg`/`_cmdCorpsSlots`/`_cmdCorpsLabel`/`_cmdCorpsPreferredGrade`/`_cmdCorpsClean`/`cmdCorpsSeated`/`_cmdCorpsBelowGrade`/`_cmdCorpsEffRating`/`_cmdCorpsLift`/`cmdCorpsCommanderFor`/`_cmdCorpsPoolFor`/`cmdSeatCorps`/`cmdVacateCorps`/`_cmdCorpsSelect`/`_cmdCorpsSlotRow`/`_cmdCorpsDepthHTML`; edits to `commandLeadership` (+the lift term), `cmdInit` (seed+sanitize), `cmdAppoint` (clear billet), `cmdRenderTab`/`cmdWireTab`. NO RNG.
- **THE OOB-BOARD DISPLAY JOIN (T15, pure display):** seated commanders NAME the player's DERIVED corps on the D106 board (`fldCampaignOOB` attaches `co.commander` when `player.source==='derived'`; `_fldOOBSideExact` renders it). The predicted EDGE is UNCHANGED by seating (leadership is captured in the actual fight, never double-counted on the board). Authored battles show the historical brigades (no corps-slot naming); the enemy is NEVER named by the player's depth chart.
- **Adversarial bug-hunt** (5 diverse-lens Opus finders [no-fudge/byte-identity · economy/determinism/bounding · citation/anti-Lost-Cause/no-fabricated-officers · XSS/escaping · UI/WCAG/edge] × default-refute Opus verify + a completeness critic; **11 agents; 5 raw → 3 confirmed (1 MED + 2 LOW); invariantsHold = TRUE** — the critic independently grep-confirmed no combat path reads `cmd.corps`, seat/vacate write only `cmd.corps`+capital, byte-identity airtight, no fabricated officers, the 2 sources real + on-claim). **ALL 3 confirmed FIXED:** **(MED, UI)** the seat event-log said "he leads **above** his grade" for a BELOW-grade general (contradicting every other surface) → "below". **(LOW, anti-fabrication)** the OOB-board attach was keyed by array index, so a real general could be named over a derived **"Garrison"** node (a sub-1200-man fort/detachment, NOT a corps billet) → the attach now guards to `/ Corps$/`-labeled nodes only (a probe assertion locks the Garrison guard). **(LOW, citation)** the teaching copy said the CS "created the Lt. Gen. grade in October 1862" → corrected to the precise Sept. 18, 1862 authorization + the October promotions + the Nov. 6 reorganization (in `data/ratings.json` + the `35-command.js` comment). The 3 critic-"missed" were non-blocking: the workflow agents could not run the browser probes (the main session ran the full suite — see below); the ±4 cap is secondary to the army-commander's ±17 (§27 holds); and probe step #5 already proved `cmdPromote` reaches Lt. Gen. on the CS side (Stuart eff 74→82 after promotion).
- **Vetting (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓`; **combat byte-identity STRUCTURAL** (no combat path reads `cmd.corps`; `commandLeadership += 0` when unseated) + **EMPIRICAL** — a 132-cell A/B harness (`.tmp/q10-ab.mjs`, base = `git show HEAD:civil_war_generals.html`) over (side × appointee × date) confirms `commandLeadership`/`bridgeArmy.overall`/`leadership`/the auto-resolve margin reads are **byte-identical to HEAD** (no corps seated), 0 pageerrors; `probe-command` **49/49** (+9 Q10 steps: config · byte-identical-off [lift 0, absent-record == seeded-empty] · gate+spend+no-recharge+free-vacate · bounded-lift [one strong vs full staff capped at 4] · CS below-grade penalty + the Q9 promote-to-fit synergy + the US floor-met contrast · one-corps-per-general+reassign+appoint-clears+army-cmd-not-seatable · save-tamper sanitize · no-output-gate · render); `probe-oob` **16/16** (+the Q10 step: seating names the derived CORPS, edge unchanged, the Garrison guard, the enemy never named); FULL no-regression GREEN — `probe-desk` 13/13, `probe-ratings` 21/21, `probe-camp` 8/8, `probe-officers` 15/15, `probe-field` 13/13, `t1probe` 14/14, `bootprobe` ok, the 9 battles + `probe-csplayer` green (bullrun 15 · antietam 16 · fredericksburg 22 · gettysburg 16 · chancellorsville 24 · malvern 24 · shiloh 29 · vicksburg 18 · chickamauga 18 · csplayer 16); `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`; 0 pageerrors throughout; `wcag-auditor` AA (3 contrast fixes on the new UI: the below-grade tell `#c9712e`→`#d17936` 4.54:1, and two T15 commander-name opacities .82/.9→.92 to 4.50:1). **ENV:** intermittent THREE-CDN headless load-stalls on the heaviest battle probes (chancellorsville/malvern/shiloh) cleared by standalone retry (the documented gotcha); the `domcontentloaded` A/B harness is the authoritative no-regression spine. Committed + pushed only after the full empirical gate passed. (Reversible; non-negotiables honored — D74/D92 no-output-gate, no fabricated officers, citation-grade teaching, the empirical-vet-before-push gate.)

## D107 — RATING SYSTEM **Q8b: THE BETWEEN-BATTLE CAVALRY RECONNAISSANCE** ("scout the enemy" — tier-reveal the enemy OOB, scaled by the appointed general's cavalry) — [CLAUDE] (overnight run, 2026-06-23)
The camp loop's deferred SCOUTING (RATING-SYSTEM-DESIGN §15; deferred at D100 until the D106 OOB-mapping substrate existed). On the President's-Desk Command tab, an **"Order a reconnaissance"** control inside the D106 Order-of-Battle board lets the President spend a small amount of **political capital** (`C.clock.capital` — the EXISTING GM currency, also spent by `cmdAppoint`/`cmdPromote`; no new resource per §12) to reveal the **enemy** order of battle to a TIER scaled by the appointed army commander's persona **`cavalry`** attribute. Teaches that good cavalry leadership buys good intelligence — Stuart's reconnaissance rides, and the catastrophic intelligence vacuum at Gettysburg when his cavalry was absent (named as a real Confederate command failure, not glorified). **PICK RATIONALE (decide-&-log within the cleared NEXT menu):** both the GM depth-chart MOVES and Q8b scouting were unblocked by D106; I chose scouting over the depth-chart MOVES because its scaling input (the `cavalry` persona attribute, spread 50–70 across the 9 generals) and its `reveal` hook in `fldCampaignOOBHtml` BOTH already existed, `36-camp.js` explicitly deferred scouting to exactly this substrate, the D106 board already advertised a dead "Scout the enemy" CTA, and scouting is **pure display + an economy spend (never touches combat)** — the safest possible byte-identity. The corps-billet depth-chart MOVES need a new officer-slot concept the derived T15 tree lacks (bigger surface) — deferred next.
- **THREE RECON TIERS (§15):** **light** (unscouted — the passive D106 estimate: force grade band + rough brigade count + the CTA) → **better** (scouted, cavalry < `cavalryFullThreshold` 65: the enemy commander NAMED + per-corps grade + a posture/terrain advisory, NO per-brigade detail) → **full** (scouted, cavalry ≥ 65: the complete enemy OOB, the exact view). With the 9 generals' cavalry: Grant (66) + Lee (70) → full; the rest → better; a no-appointee campaign uses the HISTORICAL DEFAULT commander's cavalry (not a flat baseline). Intel is **fresh per engagement** — the scout record is keyed to the next-battle id, so it reverts to light when the battle changes.
- **ARCHITECTURE (the D106 wall preserved):** the WRITE (`cmdScout`) lives in `src/35-command.js` (it gates on capital like `cmdAppoint`, reads the appointee's `cavalry` via `_cmdGenPersona`, computes the tier via the pure `_cmdScoutTierForCavalry`, and writes ONLY `cmd.scout` + debits `C.clock.capital` — NEVER the scoreboard); **T15-oob.js stays a PURE READ-OUT** — `fldCampaignOOBHtml(C, {reveal, reconHtml})` is told the tier + the recon-control HTML by `cmdRenderTab`, and only ADDS the "better"-tier renderer (`_fldOOBSideScouted` + `_fldScoutPosture`). No combat/resolve/tick/conditioning path reads `cmd.scout` (grep-confirmed) → a campaign that never scouts is byte-identical to the pre-Q8b build, and the 9 tactical battles are untouched. Build-gate 4d already scans both `35-command.js` + `tactical/T15-oob.js`. `cmd.scout` rides the save (C.president serialized wholesale); `cmdInit` lazily sanitizes a malformed/tampered record (drop→null; valid→bounded) — no `_SAVE_VER` bump. Data: a `scout` config block in `data/ratings.json` (cost 3 · cavalryFullThreshold 65 · baselineCavalry 54 · 2 real sources). NO RNG (deterministic).
- **Adversarial bug-hunt** (5 diverse-lens Opus finders [no-fudge/byte-identity · economy/determinism/bounding · citation/anti-Lost-Cause/no-fabricated-officers · XSS/escaping · UI/WCAG/edge-render] × default-refute Opus verify ×3 + a completeness critic; **9 agents; invariantsHold = TRUE** — the critic independently grep-confirmed no combat path reads `cmd.scout`, cmdScout writes only `cmd.scout`+capital, the 9 battles byte-identical, no fabricated officers, citations real). **1 confirmed LOW + 2 critic-surfaced LOW; all actionable ones fixed:** **(LOW, confirmed)** `cmdScout` gated on `Math.round(capital)` but debited RAW `clk.capital` — the exact Q9 gate/debit rounding divergence `cmdPromote` was hardened against (byte-identical under today's integer-only capital paths, but a latent fractional bug + a consistency hazard) → FIXED to debit `cap - cost` (the hardened idiom). **(LOW, critic-surfaced)** `_fldScoutPosture` read the UN-flipped historical attacker, ignoring `C.flipAtk` (the alt-history attacker swap the resolver applies) → the "better"-tier posture could mis-describe who attacks after a recovery refight → FIXED by honoring `C.flipAtk` with the SAME non-mutating `Object.assign({}, bd, {atk:swapped})` swap the resolver uses (strength/edge are attacker-symmetric, so only the posture line needs it; a probe assertion locks it). **(LOW, deferred — critic agreed no action)** `cmd.scout.turn` is a cosmetic provenance stamp nothing reads (bounded on load).
- **Vetting (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK · no-fudge ✓ · citations ✓`; **combat byte-identity STRUCTURAL** (no combat path reads `cmd.scout`; the source diff is display + an economy spend) and EMPIRICAL (all 10 battle probes land on their historical results, unchanged); `probe-oob` **15/15** (+the SCOUT TIERS step: light→better→full ladder, "better" names + per-corps + posture + NO per-brigade leak, the flipAtk posture-flip + non-mutation), `probe-command` **40/40** (+6 Q8b steps: tier mapping at the threshold + fresh="light"; cmdScout reveals at the appointee's tier + debits EXACTLY the cost; the gate + idempotence [short capital no-op, no re-charge]; stale-recon→light; save-tamper sanitize; no-output-gate + unscouted-offers-button); FULL no-regression GREEN — `probe-desk` 13/13, `probe-ratings` 21/21, `probe-camp` 8/8, `probe-officers` 15/15, `probe-field` 13/13, `t1probe` 14/14, `bootprobe` ok, ALL 9 battles + `probe-csplayer` green (bullrun 15 · antietam 16 · fredericksburg 22 · gettysburg 16 · chancellorsville 24 · malvern 24 · shiloh 29 · vicksburg 18 · chickamauga 18 · csplayer 16); `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`; 0 pageerrors throughout; `wcag-auditor` AA (one contrast fix `#d66040`→`#e86840` on the new "Needs capital to scout" span — the auditor measured `#d66040` at 3.92:1/4.33:1, sub-AA; **NOTE for a future pass: the existing `#d66040` usages elsewhere in `35-command.js` are the same sub-AA — pre-existing, out of this scope, logged**). **ENV:** the run was PAUSED mid-vet on an Aaron "pause internet" directive (the battle probes fetch THREE from a CDN; the bug-hunt + wcag are API calls) and RESUMED cleanly; intermittent THREE-CDN load-stalls cleared by retry (the documented gotcha). Committed + pushed only after the full empirical gate passed. (Reversible; non-negotiables honored — D74/D92 no-output-gate, no fabricated officers, citation-grade teaching, the empirical-vet-before-push gate.)

## D106 — RATING SYSTEM **THE OOB-MAPPING SUBSTRATE** + a read-only Order-of-Battle board (the §12.1 roster screen; the shared blocker for the GM depth-chart MOVES + Q8b scouting) — [OPUS] (overnight run, 2026-06-22)
The shared plumbing the GM depth-chart MOVES (§12.2) and Q8b SCOUTING (§15) both depend on: a READ-ONLY layer mapping the strategic next-battle (`_brgNextBattle`) to a STRUCTURED order of battle — commander → corps → brigade — for BOTH sides, each billet carrying its OVR/grade/strength/provenance (the T14 rating spine). New module **`src/tactical/T15-oob.js`** (spliced after T14; `fldOOBForSide`/`fldCampaignOOB`/`fldCampaignOOBHtml` + helpers). Surfaced as a new **"Order of Battle — the Next Engagement"** section on the President's-Desk Command tab (one render line in `cmdRenderTab`). The recommended-but-FORK-RISKY item was scoped SMALL first per the run plan: a read-only query layer + roster board, **no combat wiring** (the procedural skirmish `_fldCampaignSkirmishParams` is UNCHANGED). Design law: RATING-SYSTEM-DESIGN §12.1/§13/§15. **No new fork surfaced** — `BATTLES[]` already carries real `cmdUS`/`cmdCS` per battle and the 9 scenarios carry real brigade OOBs, so the substrate is buildable read-only + citation-clean without contradicting a shipped teaching.
- **TWO SOURCES, ONE SHAPE:** a battle WITH a hand-built tactical scenario (`fldScenarioData`) exposes its REAL brigade OOB (real commanders + the authored provenance read straight from each unit note via `_fldOOBProvFromNote`, reusing the Q7 reader `_fldOOBUnits` which scopes a multi-phase battle to phase 0); a procedural battle DERIVES a deterministic corps/brigade tree split from the authored totals (`bd.us`/`bd.cs`/year), seeded by id+side (NO RNG). Derived billets are FORMATION-LABELED (I Corps · 1st Brigade · Garrison) with `commander:null` — **NEVER a fabricated officer** (CLAUDE.md #4 / D92). The army-commander headline names only the player's APPOINTED general (the GM payoff + his `_cmdGenRating` OVR) or the battle's historical `cmd<Side>` (BATTLES — Verified). NEVER invents.
- **THE WALL (D74/D94/D92):** a PURE READ-OUT + DISPLAY, called ONLY from `cmdRenderTab` — never a resolve/tick/conditioning path. Writes NOTHING; read at no point in combat resolution → the sandbox + all 9 battles are byte-identical **BY CONSTRUCTION** (the generated html diff vs committed HEAD `7df4372` is ALL-ADDITIONS — the T15 block + one desk-render line, **ZERO removed/modified lines**). build-gate 4d's `RATING_MODULES` now ALSO scans `tactical/T15-oob.js` (the no-fudge OUTPUT-WALL structural over it too).
- **ENEMY-FUZZY (§15):** the board renders the PLAYER's own OOB EXACT and the ENEMY as a FUZZY estimate (force grade + brigade count + commander, NO per-brigade detail) — the designed hook **Q8b scouting will DEEPEN via a higher `reveal` tier**. The substrate returns the FULL data for both sides (so the GM/scouting can query it); only the BOARD gates the enemy. Triple-encoded + CVD-safe (OVR = number+letter+word; provenance = hatch+glyph+word; the edge = a labeled % bar); all data names routed through `_fldOOBEsc` (XSS-safe).
- **Adversarial bug-hunt** (5 diverse-lens Opus finders [no-fudge/purity · determinism/bounding · citation-honesty/anti-Lost-Cause · UI/WCAG/XSS · integration/edge] × default-refute Opus verify + a completeness critic; 15 agents; **5 confirmed: 1 HIGH + 4 MED; critic "NOT SAFE AS-IS" until fixed; the hard invariants [no-fudge/byte-identity, determinism, no-fabricated-officers, XSS-safe] independently CONFIRMED HOLD**). ALL fixed: **(HIGH, anti-Lost-Cause)** the board showed the phase-0 (opening) OOB of a multi-phase battle under the BARE battle name → could forecast a CS edge for Gettysburg/Antietam/Vicksburg/Chickamauga (Union victories) — the SAME Q7 failure resurfacing at the display layer (I inherited the phase-0 scoping but not the "Opening Engagement" framing) → FIXED by threading `phased`/`phaseName` and scoping the headline ("— the opening engagement (phase)"), the predicted edge ("the opening clash"), the source note, and the footer (mirrors T14 `fldMatchupHtml`). **(MED, my real miss)** the build-gate change I CLAIMED in the T15 comments was NEVER applied (the first Edit failed; the comments asserted a guarantee that didn't exist) → `RATING_MODULES` now genuinely += T15, the comments are now true. **(MED)** the derived artillery battalion slashed men to 40% with no redistribution → a 12-15% undercount that could flip the predicted edge against history → FIXED by carving the thin gun arm off the TOP of corpsMen (conserves the total EXACTLY; the jitter span hardened ≥1). **(MED)** a mixed "(Verified identity; Inferred strength)" note surfaced as "Verified" (overclaiming the Inferred strength figure) → `_fldOOBProvFromNote` now DOWNGRADES a mixed note to Inferred (D92/gate-4e discipline; the real name still conveys the Verified identity). **(MED, defensive)** a one-sided authored OOB could pit an authored side against a derived one (skewed edge) → `fldCampaignOOB` decides ONE source for both sides (`forceDerive`). + 2 LOW probe-hardening adds (bounding Infinity/NaN → finite, no NaN render; enemy-no-leak → the default board renders no enemy per-brigade row).
- **Vetting (PROPER VETTING, all GREEN):** `GATE OK · no-fudge ✓` (4d now scans `tactical/T15-oob.js`) `· citations ✓`; **byte-identity: 0 removed/modified lines vs committed HEAD `7df4372`** (combat byte-identical by construction); `probe-oob` **14/14** (substrate · authored-real-commanders · derived-no-fabricated-officers · men-conservation+edge-honesty · tiny-garrison · enemy-fuzzy + no-leak · determinism · purity · provenance-downgrade · phase-framing[antietam→"The Cornfield"] · bounding · graceful · no-output-gate); FULL no-regression GREEN — `probe-command` 34/34 (the desk renders the board), `probe-desk` 13/13, `probe-ratings` 21/21, `probe-camp` 8/8, `probe-officers` 15/15, `probe-field` 13/13, `t1probe` 14/14, `bootprobe` ok (0 pe), ALL 9 battle probes + `probe-csplayer` green (bullrun 15 · antietam 16 · fredericksburg 22 · gettysburg 16 · chancellorsville 24 · malvern 24 · shiloh 29 · vicksburg 18 · chickamauga 18 · csplayer 16); `diag-classic` `nonBlank:346`; 0 pageerrors throughout; `wcag-auditor` AA (one contrast fix .8→.9, no ARIA gaps, CVD-safe). **ENV:** a healthy memory window; one fredericksburg load-stall cleared by a standalone retry (the documented gotcha). Committed + pushed only after the full empirical gate passed. (Reversible; non-negotiables honored — D74/D92 no-output-gate, no fabricated officers, the empirical-vet-before-push gate.)
